Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With Marketing Cloud Intelligence you connect, harmonize, visualize, and act on your marketing data to
optimize performance within campaigns, discover insights in real-time, and then act on them. Learn
more about each in the associated help sections.
If you’re new to the Salesforce Help site and want to see how to navigate and find information, click here
to view a short instructional video.
Note Did you know there’s a new way to stay up to date on Marketing Cloud Intelligence features?
Check out Updates Released by Month—your monthly guide to the latest enhancements, new
connectors, and feature updates, all in one place.
Tip Did you know there’s a new and improved Marketing Intelligence? Built natively on the
Salesforce Platform, Marketing Intelligence harnesses the power of Data 360 and Agentforce to
boost campaign performance and reduce wasted spend. Check out the Marketing Intelligence
Quick Look.

=== PAGE 7 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Create a Support Ticket for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Log a support case to enable permissions or if you are experiencing technical difficulties.
Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can integrate all your data from marketing and advertising
platforms, web analytics, CRM, e-commerce, and more. You can then use this unified view of your data
to optimize campaign performance and discover and act on insights in real time.
Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data from a variety of sources with different data types. Each one of
these different data types has its own unique combination of dimensions and measurements. The
dimensions are made up of entities that have a certain relationship, or hierarchy, between them. It’s
important to maintain this relationship in Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s database, otherwise the data
can get aggregated incorrectly.
Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
One of the most crucial steps when working with Marketing Cloud Intelligence, is uploading data.
Since Marketing Cloud Intelligence supports numerous types of data, you can choose from many
different ways to upload it. During the upload stage, you can map your data to determine which fields
appear in Marketing Cloud Intelligence, and you can even create custom calculated fields to get the
most out of your data. All these actions are done in the Connect & Mix tab.
Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Granular Data Center is a premium feature designed for ingesting raw data into the system. Due to this
data’s granularity, row count can be hundreds of millions or even billions of rows. As opposed to other
data stream types, usage and pricing don’t go by the row count but rather by terabytes of storage
space. Some examples of data types that work best with Granular Data Center data streams are
keyword level data, event level data, log data, and granular geodata.
Dimensions
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, a dimension is a qualitative non-numerical attribute that provides
information about your dataset, for example, campaign name and campaign ID. A dimension can’t be
counted, even when it includes a number. For instance, a campaign ID consists of numbers but you
can’t calculate them.
Measurements
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, measurements are quantitative numerical data that you can count,
for example, number of clicks and emails opened. Measurements are used to set KPIs and analyze
your data.
Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers many tools so you can get a real-time view of all your marketing
data in one place. You get everything from standard key performance indicator (KPI) reporting to
complex visualizations required for audience segmentation, customer journey analytics, and predictive
modeling. And it’s all done in the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers many tools for analyzing your data. All of these tools are found in
the Analyze and Act tab.
Harmonize Your Data
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers multiple tools for harmonizing your data. Because you can upload
3

=== PAGE 8 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence
data from various sources, you need a way to merge the data and provide a complete view of your
marketing activity. In most cases, no single data source holds all relevant data on a marketing event or
campaign. The data is shared by the multiple platforms that digital marketers use to do things like
promoting the campaign on multiple channels. To analyze the full picture, harmonize data from all
sources into one. If data isn’t harmonized, you must analyze each source individually—making it a
challenge to gain insights quickly and efficiently.
Media Transparency Center
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center allows you to ingest, unify, and analyze
Media Plan and Delivery data to pace, track costs, and effectively optimize your media budgets and
campaigns.
Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To enhance your marketing intelligence solutions, check out the apps and tools offered on the
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace. Anyone can access the Marketplace, but depending on
your user role, your access might be limited.
4

Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can integrate all your data from marketing and advertising
platforms, web analytics, CRM, e-commerce, and more. You can then use this unified view of your data
to optimize campaign performance and discover and act on insights in real time.
To get started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you set up an account and create workspaces to
integrate your data.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers additional functionality at an extra cost, such as Granular Data
Center, Sandbox, and Media Transparency. You can view the available features on the Add-on Features
tab in the Marketplace.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Updates Released by Month
Learn about recent updates to Marketing Cloud Intelligence (MCI), including changes to data
coverage, new metrics, and feature enhancements.
Get Ready for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Before we start, explore the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform. Then check that you are using one
of Salesforce’s support browsers. Finally, familiarize yourself with the user roles in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.
Account Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is divided into workspaces. An account can contain multiple
workspaces. Account settings allow you to manage and view data, user actions, and analytics for your
8

=== PAGE 13 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
account. In the account menu you can find account settings, such as account name and logo, number
of allowed workspaces, and rows, and all the way to more advanced settings, such as currency, and
password permissions.
Workspace Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Each Marketing Cloud Intelligence account can have multiple workspaces. Each workspace has its own
data streams, dashboards, and reports. You typically use workspaces to separate clients, business
units, channel views, internal views, or role-based views—whatever best fits your needs.

•
Meta has updated click-through attribution in
Connectors
Meta Ads Manager. Meta now separates link
clicks from non-link interactions (likes, shares,
comments). Non-link interactions are now
classified as Engaged-Through conversions
rather than Click-Through.
This change may result in a decrease in reported
click-through conversion rates. This reflects a
change in reporting methodology, not a drop in
performance.
•
MCI now supports the engaged-through
attribution setting. This setting isn't mapped by
default but is included in total conversion
metrics. To report on it separately, map the
engaged-through attribution setting in your
configuration.

been upgraded to v19 to ensure continued
compatibility and access to the latest platform
capabilities.

Get Ready for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Before we start, explore the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform. Then check that you are using one of
Salesforce’s support browsers. Finally, familiarize yourself with the user roles in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.
Location of all Help Topics
Beginning May 1, 2022, you can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence help documentation and training
materials in Salesforce Help. The Datorama Success Center is no longer available.
Navigate the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Platform
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform is divided into four tabs. Here you can explore the different
tabs.
Set Up Your First Workspace in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After your Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is provisioned, you can now start setting up your
workspace. A Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is divided into workspaces. Each workspace is a
13

=== PAGE 18 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
separate working environment with its own permissions. When you make changes, the changes are
applied only to the workspace that you’re working in.
Supported Browsers
You can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence from a browser on a Windows, macOS, or Linux
computer.
User Roles and Permissions
Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three types of user roles: viewer, power user, and admin. A user
role defines which actions a user can perform. Each user role has a default set of permissions, but you
can assign users with the same role different permissions. For example, one user with the viewer role
can view unpublished pages but another user with the viewer role can’t.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Videos
Use this video resource to learn more about Intelligence features.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Glossary
Familiarize yourself with Marketing Cloud Intelligence and other marketing terminology.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Security
Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses a variety of industry-standard and state-of-the-art security
technologies and procedures to help protect your information from unauthorized access, use, or
disclosure. All users must have a registered MFA method in order to access the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence platform directly. You can decide on which methods best suit your needs. Session timeout
is another form of security and determines the amount of inactive time after which a user is logged
out of the platform. The session timeout period can be set in the account settings. For security
purposes there is a forced timeout after five days regardless of inactivity. Keeping up with the evolving
security landscape is an ongoing, joint partnership between Marketing Cloud Intelligence and our
customers. As security threats grow more common, it’s important to implement stronger measures to
protect your business and customers.
IP Addresses for Inclusion on Allowlists in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Sometimes you need to set up a data stream via an SQL service provider or an FTPS. If you have a
firewall in place, you must allow specific IP addresses to ensure data is retrieved.

Navigate the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Platform
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform is divided into four tabs. Here you can explore the different
tabs.
•
Visualize — (1) Visualize your data. Create collections, with dashboard and app pages, bring the data to
life with widgets and customize the views with filters.
•
Analyze & Act — (2) Analyze your data, and share the actionable insights through reports. Measure
KPIs with goals and actions.
•
Connect & Mix — (3) Import your data via data streams so you can classify and manipulate your data.
Here you can use any of the offered methods: API connectors, TotalConnect, LiteConnect. After you
import the data you can manipulate it with the various features available in the Connect & Mix tab.
•
Marketplace — (4) An ecosystem with Marketing Cloud Intelligence apps that offer a wide range of
solutions developed based on real use cases and customer feedback.
When you click a new tab, the left menu bar changes to display the available actions for the tab you
selected. For example, when you click the Visualize tab, the left menu bar shows your collections,
dashboard pages, and templates.
Set Up Your First Workspace in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After your Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is provisioned, you can now start setting up your
workspace. A Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is divided into workspaces. Each workspace is a
separate working environment with its own permissions. When you make changes, the changes are
applied only to the workspace that you’re working in.

USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To set up a workspace: Admin
By default, a workspace can have up to 50 million rows. If you exceed this amount, an error message
appears, and you can’t create the workspace. To increase the number of rows, contact your Marketing
Cloud Intelligence representative.
11.. Log in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence with your username and password. The first time you log in, the
Welcome screen appears.
22.. To add your first workspace, click the Click here link.
33.. Enter these details.
aa.. Name–Enter your workspace name. (1)
bb.. Vertical–Select the targeted market that’s relevant to your workspace. (2)
cc.. Currency–The default behavior is to inherit the currency settings from the account. If you require a
16

=== PAGE 21 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
different currency, select it from the dropdown list. Cost data uploaded to the workspace is
converted according to the workspace’s currency. (3)
dd.. First Day of Week–Set the first day of the week. By default, it’s set to Monday. (4)
ee.. Culture–Select the country to define how numbers are displayed in the platform. For example,
100,000 for United States, 100.000 for Germany. (5)
ff.. Timezone–The time zone affects notifications and automatic scheduling, like report scheduling.
Data stream updates are performed at 6:00 AM in the workspace’s time zone. (6)
gg.. Apply Blind Periods–Blind periods are time periods when users with analyst and viewer roles can’t
see the updated data in the workspace. If you’re using a blind period, enter the number of days the
period lasts. (7)
44.. Save your workspace. You can now add users and upload your data.

Supported Browsers
You can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence from a browser on a Windows, macOS, or Linux computer.
To access Marketing Cloud Intelligence on a mobile device, your mobile browser must meet these
minimum requirements:
•
Screen resolution of at least 1024 x 768 (when browsers are maximized)
•
Cookies enabled
•
JavaScript enabled
If you use a browser other than the ones listed below, you aren’t able to log in to Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.
•
Google Chrome
•
Firefox
•
Internet Explorer
•
Microsoft Edge
•
Safari
Browser-Specific Compatibility
In general, Marketing Cloud Intelligence supports the current and prior major releases.
•
Chrome: Latest stable version supported.
•
Microsoft Internet Explorer:
- Version 11
- Compatibility View isn’t supported.
Note Microsoft Internet Explorer is no longer supported. If bugs occur with IE11, they can’t be

User Roles and Permissions
Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three types of user roles: viewer, power user, and admin. A user
role defines which actions a user can perform. Each user role has a default set of permissions, but you
can assign users with the same role different permissions. For example, one user with the viewer role can
view unpublished pages but another user with the viewer role can’t.
You can also create user groups. With a user group, all permissions in the group are applied to all the
users in the group. For more information how to create user groups, see Create a User Group
Each account has a pre-set number of user roles based on your account contract. For more information
about editing user roles, see Add a User in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Take a look at the table below to see which actions are permitted per user role.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Glossary
Familiarize yourself with Marketing Cloud Intelligence and other marketing terminology.
Term Description
Ad Operations Agency team that deals with the technical aspects
of launching and maintaining an advertising
campaign. Duties include: trafficking, placing
pixels, and QA tag errors.
Ad Server Computer system, which stores, maintains, and
serves advertising banners for one or more
websites. Ad servers program, track, and report
several statistics about website visitors, which are
used by advertisers to custom tailor ads and offers
to suit different categories of visitors.
Ad Targeting The practice of targeting ad content to user and
contextual data.
Adtech Advertising technology. A term that can apply to
anything from DSPs to Data Management
Platforms that focus exclusively on advertising.
Advertiser Company, organization, or individual who pays for
advertising space or time to present an
announcement or persuasive message to the
22

=== PAGE 27 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Term Description
public. They typically do so in order to achieve a
goal such as enhance brand awareness (branding),
influence a consumer to make a purchase (direct
response), and so on.
Agency A company that advises advertisers on how and
where to advertise. They’re responsible for
managing the advertiser’s media plan and are
expected to deliver success and provide reports to
prove their performance.
ARR Annual Recurring Revenue. The amount of
revenue a company is planning to consistently
bring in from deals every year.
Attribution Identifying a set of user actions (“events” or
“touchpoints”) that contribute in some manner to
a desired outcome.
Attribution Window The number of days that agencies or brands
designate when a user action is considered for
conversions.
Campaign A set of media that have a shared theme and
ideas to market a specific brand, product, or
concept. Most campaigns also include criteria
such as Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), a
specific start and end date, daily or overall
budgets, frequency restrictions, and targeting
based on user or inventory data.
Click Active user interaction with an ad that drives users
to a designated landing page.
Conversion When a user performs a desired action designated
by an agency or brand. For example, sign up,
purchase, or install app.
Cookie Information stored on a user’s computer by a
website.
Creative The actual graphic element that is used to fill a
placement or media buy.
Collection A collection is where you house pages, similar to
how a folder contains files.
Data Stream A connection to a data source in Marketing Cloud
23

=== PAGE 28 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Term Description
Intelligence - anything from a Google Analytics
API connection to a customized flat-file
connection.
Data Warehouse A central repository for all or significant parts of
the data that an enterprise's various business
systems collect. Example: AWS
Dimension Non-numerical values, which put the numerical
data in context. Qualitative values.
DMP Data Management Platform. Piece of software that
houses cookie level details about users relevant to
the given advertiser so that they can create
audience profiles for ad targeting purposes.
Examples: BlueKai, Adobe, and Lotame
DSP Demand-Side Platform. Helps advertisers buy ad
space (Display, Video, and so on), in an automated
or programmatic fashion. Examples: MediaMath,
Rocketfuel, and DBM.
Entity Any object in the platform that users want to
model and store information about. Entities are
recognizable concepts, either concrete or abstract,
such as people, places, things, or events, which
have relevance to the database. Every entity in use
MUST have a Key (defines unity) mapped.
ETL Extract, Transform, Load. Three database
functions that are combined into one tool to pull
data out of one database and place it into another
database.
Exchange A technology platform that facilitates buying and
selling from multiple ad networks.
Executive Workspace A collection of workspaces that exist in an
account. An accumulation of data across the
matching dimension values.
Granularity The level or depth of detail present in a set of
data.
Granular Data Center A storage repository that holds a vast amount of
raw data in its native format until it ‘s needed.
While a hierarchical data warehouse stores data in
24

=== PAGE 29 ===

Term Description
files or folders, a Granular Data Center uses a flat
architecture to store data.
Impression Number of times when an ad is loaded onto a site.
Log Level Data The collection of an abundant set of data points
gathered at a low level of granularity. This can
include granular information about bids or PII.
Mapping The process of correlating data source fields into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence fields.
Media Buy/Placement A specific place on a site where an ad is shown, or
a specific tactic to show ads on a site.
Measurement Numerical values, on which mathematical
operations are performed. Quantitative facts.
Page A page is created within a collection It can contain
several visual elements to represent data, which
are called widgets.
Pixel A tracker placed by an agency or brand that tracks
actions. For example, ads served, clicks,
conversions, landing page views.
Publisher The actual website where an advertisement is
served. Publishers are referred to as the “sell side”
of the business, their inventory being placements.
Reimmersion (of data) Taking data that’s calculated within the platform
post-ETL (for example, calculated dimensions or
calculated measurements) and mapping it into a
data stream to make it part of the data model.
Retargeting/Remessaging A form of online targeted advertising that’s
targeted to consumers based on their previous
internet actions.
Site The website from which media buys are acquired
and where advertisements are published.
SSP Supply Side Platform. A platform used on the
publisher’s side that sells ad space to buyers.
Complimentary to a DSP.
Strategy A plan of action for advertising, which is meant to
optimize performance.

Term Description
Widget A visual element representing data. This
visualization can be of various types. Examples:
Bar Graph, Donut Chart, or Table.
Workspace Each Account can contain several workspaces,
whereas each workspace has its own data
streams, calculations and Analyses, as well as
visualizations. Workspaces can represent different
advertised brands, product categories, or
geographical regions.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Security
Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses a variety of industry-standard and state-of-the-art security
technologies and procedures to help protect your information from unauthorized access, use, or
disclosure. All users must have a registered MFA method in order to access the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence platform directly. You can decide on which methods best suit your needs. Session timeout is
another form of security and determines the amount of inactive time after which a user is logged out of
the platform. The session timeout period can be set in the account settings. For security purposes there
is a forced timeout after five days regardless of inactivity. Keeping up with the evolving security landscape
is an ongoing, joint partnership between Marketing Cloud Intelligence and our customers. As security
threats grow more common, it’s important to implement stronger measures to protect your business and
customers.
To learn about our trust and compliance information, see:
•
Salesforce Trust and Compliance Documentation
•
Certifications and documents
•
Privacy Statement
Multi-Factor Authentication in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) requires users to enter two or more pieces of evidence, or factors, to
prove identification. One factor is something the user knows, such as a username and password
combination. Other factors are verification methods that the user has in possession, such as an
authenticator app or security key. All users must have a registered MFA method in order to access the
Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform directly.
Single Sign-On (SSO) in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
If you’re the administrator of a Marketing Cloud Intelligence account, you can grant team members
access by signing into a central identity provider. SSO) makes access easier and more secure for
everyone. You can put the identity provider that you already trust in charge of authentication, while
team members can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence without another password to manage.
What Are Cookies?
Cookies temporarily store a small amount of information in a user’s browser. For example, logging in to

a website, preferences for particular content, capping how often a user sees ads, or tracking what a
user does online. Cookies are set at the user level, allowing every user to customize their own cookie
settings.
Multi-Factor Authentication in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) requires users to enter two or more pieces of evidence, or factors, to
prove identification. One factor is something the user knows, such as a username and password
combination. Other factors are verification methods that the user has in possession, such as an
authenticator app or security key. All users must have a registered MFA method in order to access the
Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform directly.
Note If you use single sign-on (SSO) to access the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform, you’re
required to use MFA for your SSO identity provider. See Salesforce Multi-Factor Authentication FAQ.
Supported Verification Methods in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
When a user logs in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the first time using Multi-Factor
Authentication, the user must select at least one verification method. It’s recommended to provide
users with several verification options.
Manage and Reset Multi-Factor Authentication From Your Profile in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can reset MFA from your profile. After MFA is reset, you are prompted to select a verification
method the next time you log in.
Manage and Reset Multi-Factor Authentication Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence at the
User Level
All users must have a registered MFA method to access the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform
directly. Admins can reset an MFA recovery process for users that lost, want to change or add an
authentication method. Users can also reset MFA from their Profile.
Supported Verification Methods in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
When a user logs in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the first time using Multi-Factor Authentication,
the user must select at least one verification method. It’s recommended to provide users with several
verification options.
Email and SMS text messages aren’t allowed as a second factor for MFA.
Supported verification methods:
Salesforce Authenticator
A Salesforce mobile app that provides secure and fast MFA via push notifications. It’s highly
recommended that users set up a PIN or biometric requirement on their mobile device to
ensure that unauthorized parties can’t access Salesforce Authenticator. For more information,
see the Salesforce Authenticator web page.
27

=== PAGE 32 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
One-time password generator
A third-party authenticator app, such as Google Authenticator or Authy, that generates specific
time-based, one-time passcodes that users enter when they log in.
Security key
A small physical device, such as a Yubikey or Google Titan Key, that users connect to their
computers when logging in.
Built-in authenticator
A service, such as Windows Hello or Touch ID, that verifies a user’s identity with a fingerprint, iris,
or facial recognition scan. This method is bound to a specific device, and users must register a
built-in authenticator on each device that they log in from.
Recovery code
We recommend registering this method as a backup option only. Generates a list of 10 codes
that you can copy or download. Each code can be used only once. This method doesn’t require
additional devices.

Single Sign-On (SSO) in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
If you’re the administrator of a Marketing Cloud Intelligence account, you can grant team members
access by signing into a central identity provider. SSO) makes access easier and more secure for
everyone. You can put the identity provider that you already trust in charge of authentication, while team
members can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence without another password to manage.
When you set up SSO as the default setting, your identity provider authenticates end users. When a user
wants to access Marketing Cloud Intelligence, all processes, policies, and security features that were set
up with your identity provider are applied. Security features that Marketing Cloud Intelligence provides,
such as two-step verification or the ability to reset passwords, are no longer in effect because your
identity provider handles all aspects of authentication.
Note RelayState redirection within SSO settings isn’t supported. The interaction between Marketing
Cloud Intelligence and SSO providers is only via authentication. After users are authenticated,
they’re directed to the relevant workspace and to the default login page in the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence platform.
After SSO is turned on, users can log in to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform in two ways.
Using your identity provider portal (SSO vendor)
Idp initiated login
by clicking the Marketing Cloud Intelligence app.
Using the Users Direct Login page, which enables
SP initiated login you to go directly to your Marketing Cloud
Intelligence account online if you’re logged in to
30

=== PAGE 35 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
your identity provider. You could be redirected to
the Idp login page. You can find this URL on the
Single Sign-On Account Setting page under User
Direct Login URL.

Use these configuration settings to connect to Marketing Cloud Intelligence:
•
When configuring the Identifier ID and Reply URL, use the URL of the application that you use to log
into Marketing Cloud Intelligence:
- AWS US https://app.datorama.com
- AWS EU https://app-eu.datorama.com
- Azure US https://app-us2.datorama.com
- Azure EU https://app-eu2.datorama.com
•
When configuring the Sign-on URL field, enter the URL according to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence
application that you use: (this is the Users Direct Login Page)
- AWS US https://app.datorama.com/services/auth/sso/<account ID>
- AWS EU https://app-eu.datorama.com/services/auth/sso/<account ID>
- Azure US https://app-us2.datorama.com/services/auth/sso/<account ID>
- Azure EU https://app-eu2.datorama.com/services/auth/sso/<account ID>
If you’re using SAML2, follow these guidelines:
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses SAML2 with the HTTP Redirect binding for SP to IdP and expects
the HTTP Post binding for IdP to SP.
•
You can find the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Customer URL (also called the Assertion Consumer
Service URL / ACS) in your Intelligence account on the Single Sign-on setting page.
•
The NameID must contain the user’s email address:
Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified"
•
Your identity provider might ask if you want to sign the SAML assertion, the SAML response, or both.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence requires the SAML response to be signed. You can choose signed or
unsigned for the SAML assertion.
What Are Cookies?
Cookies temporarily store a small amount of information in a user’s browser. For example, logging in to a
website, preferences for particular content, capping how often a user sees ads, or tracking what a user
does online. Cookies are set at the user level, allowing every user to customize their own cookie settings.
Cookie preferences in Marketing Cloud Intelligence are updated yearly. For security reasons, cookies are
sent only in requests to the domains that are associated with the cookie.
First-party cookies are stored by the domain (website) that you are directly visiting. First-party cookies let
website owners collect analytics data, remember language settings, and perform other useful functions
that help provide a good user experience.
Third-party cookies are created by domains other than the one that you are directly visiting. Third-party
cookies are used for cross-site tracking, retargeting, and ad serving.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence splits its cookies into three categories:
32

=== PAGE 37 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Necessary for basic website functionality. For
example, session cookies needed to transmit the
Required website, authentication cookies, and security
cookies. These cookies are always active and can’t
be disabled.
Required to open support tickets and, Screen
Functional Guides. These cookies are optional and can be
disabled.
Help improve Marketing Cloud Intelligence by
sending anonymous data regarding platform
Usage
usage. These cookies are optional and can be
disabled.

Important Information
•
Azure uses Azure Storage Service Endpoints in all subnets to optimize and secure access. When
accessing Azure Storage, the traffic comes from Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s internal Azure IP
addresses. When using a firewall on the Azure Portal, you can only allow private IP address ranges and
VNETS that belong to the same account. One possible workaround for this issue, is to set up an SFTP
server in your VNET and have Marketing Cloud Intelligence send reports to that server. See Microsoft
Learn: Azure virtual network service endpoints. Another workaround is to run a command in Cloud
Shell in the Azure Portal or locally (requires Azure CLI).
The subnet ID for Azure US is:
az storage account network-rule add --account-name
<inputaccountname> --subnet /subscriptions/009fdd36-efea-4a4a-a0ec-173c31f61
22a/resourceGroups/Networking-RG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetwork
s/Prod-VNET/subnets/aks-prod-us2-green

Reserved External IP - If you’re in the Azure environment, allow these IP addresses as well, due to
future updates to the Azure IP addresses.
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence relies on a technology called websockets to be enabled in order for the
platform to work correctly. Some IT departments restrict communication via websockets, and this can
lead to dashboards not loading correctly or at all. To test if websockets are enabled, navigate to
https://websocketstest.com/. If for some reason they aren’t working correctly, contact your IT
department. If you encounter problems despite websockets being allowed in your environment, then
contact Marketing Cloud Intelligence support.
•
For Amazon S3 services, when the client's bucket is located in the same region as Marketing Cloud
Intelligence’s bucket, the client should whitelist the VPC endpoints instead of the public IP's (as
described in the table above). For more information, see Controlling Access from VPC Endpoints with
Bucket Policies
These are the available regions:
- (AWS US) is in Amazon region us-east-1
- AWS EU) is in Amazon region eu-central-1
Account Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is divided into workspaces. An account can contain multiple
workspaces. Account settings allow you to manage and view data, user actions, and analytics for your
account. In the account menu you can find account settings, such as account name and logo, number of
allowed workspaces, and rows, and all the way to more advanced settings, such as currency, and
password permissions.
After you receive your Marketing Cloud Intelligence login information, a Salesforce representative creates
an account for you.

Manage User Groups in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can create a user group to apply a set of content permissions and workspace user filters to users
who need access to specific accounts or workspaces. A user role determines what a user can and can’t
do, while a user group determines where the user can apply these permissions. You can assign a user
to more than one user group.
Change Your Marketing Cloud Intelligence Platform Language
You can change the default language for a user or just for the current session at login. This is done per
user at the account level.
Switching Between Accounts in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Switching between accounts can be done through the account settings. This enables you to view
another account’s settings, account users, workspaces, data streams, and collections.
Account Deactivation in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers two types of customer accounts: Paying and POC. If an account
expires and no renewal contract has been issued, a deactivation process is automatically initiated.
Account Analytics in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Measure user engagement and analyze user actions. Get a holistic view of page views and where activity
is high or low.
The report is available both at the account and workspace level. Account level data can be viewed for all
your workspaces in the associated account.
If you are an admin and have access to all the workspaces in your account, you can view both Account
and Workspace Analytics.

Data Row Usage in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
When you upload a file into Marketing Cloud Intelligence, each row of data in the file occupies a row
of data in the Intelligence database. Row usage is calculated as the sum of rows in the database for a
specific account or workspace. Each Intelligence account has a set number of rows in the database
that can be divided between the workspaces in the account.

Account Analytics measures page views and user activity.

are viewed most. You can also monitor:
•
Page views for both licensed and anonymous users. Anonymous users can be users that open
dashboard pages shared with them via Share & Embed.
•
When a page is refreshed or clicked.
•
Each time a page is shown.

user, the user must perform one of the following actions:
•
Log in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
View a page
•
Switch accounts or workspaces.
Note If an entity is deleted, for example a dashboard page is deleted, the deleted entity is still
measured in the report, but you can’t see it in the breakdowns.

Data Row Usage in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
When you upload a file into Marketing Cloud Intelligence, each row of data in the file occupies a row of
data in the Intelligence database. Row usage is calculated as the sum of rows in the database for a
specific account or workspace. Each Intelligence account has a set number of rows in the database that
can be divided between the workspaces in the account.
Monitor Marketing Cloud Intelligence Database Row Usage
You can view your overall and current row usage at the account and workspace levels. Row usage is
refreshed every hour.
Monitor Marketing Cloud Intelligence Database Row Usage
You can view your overall and current row usage at the account and workspace levels. Row usage is
refreshed every hour.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To monitor row usage Admin
11.. To the right of the navigation bar, click the dropdown arrow, and click the Account Settings gear icon.
22.. In the account menu, select Data Rows Usage.
33.. View live row usage, and a breakdown of workspaces by row usage.
These system measurements reflect the total row count of a data stream in a workspace:
•
Data Stream Job Source Rows—Represents the number of rows in the original files. This number is
close to the true number when there’s no Media Transparency Center or data load rules in the
workspace.
•
Data Stream Job Added Rows—The number of added rows for each job the data executed.
•
Data Stream Effective Total Rows— This measurement reflects the actual number of rows the data
stream holds for the specified date range. Row count is broken down by Data Date with Media
Transparency and factors included.
•
Data Stream Total Rows — Row count is broken down by Data Date without any factors.
You can query these metrics in pivot tables and page widgets.
Note It’s recommended to use the Data Stream Effective Total Rows measurement because it
accounts for special cases like Media Transparency Center’s row factoring.

Manage Users in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Admins with Manage Account Users permissions can view and manage users across their account and
workspaces from account settings in the Manage Users tab.
There are three Manage Users tabs. In each tab you can modify the table view from the settings icon to
add or hide table columns, or reset table settings:
•
Account Users — View and manage all users that exist in the account. Edit, delete, copy, enable, and
disable users. View user information, for example, user role, last login Date, and the workspaces a user
has access to.
•
Access Requests — View and manage all the access requests for your account and workspaces.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence users such as support agents or success architects are required to
request access and receive admin approval in order to access an account or workspace in Marketing
Cloud Intelligence. Without admin approval, access isn’t possible. When an access request is made,
the admin receives an email notification with the access request details.
Access can be approved or denied directly from the email, or from the Request Access tab.
Access is always temporary—between 24 hours and 90 days, and can be revoked at any point.
View the access request information, for example, access time, workspace, request status, if the
request was made by a single user or a user group, and access reason.
•
Access Invitations — Invite Intelligence users to gain access to your workspace using the access
invitation tab. View and manage your list of invitations, revoke existing invitation, and send new
invitations. Users receive an email invitation where they can accept or decline the invitation within two
weeks.

take and which features they have access to.

=== PAGE 52 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
When a user creates an entity, that user is the entity owner. If an entity owner is deleted, the entities
created by that user become unowned. Some entities, such as actions, report schedulers, and data
streams with specific retrieval settings, require an owner to function properly. When a user is being
deleted, Marketing Cloud Intelligence checks whether the user owns entities that require an owner.
You can then assign the entity to a new owner to keep the entities functioning properly.
Re-enable a Disabled User in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A Marketing Cloud Intelligence Admin can re-enable a user or another admin. Users can be manually
disabled. You can also disable users automatically if they’re inactive for a certain number of days.

Invite Intelligence users to gain access to your workspace using the Access Invitation tab. Users have
two weeks to accept or decline invitations. An email confirmation is sent to you when the invitation is
accepted. You can view the invitation statuses in the Access Invitation tab.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses unique tokens to identify users and grant access to API resources. A
user generates a token on the intelligence platform to be included in subsequent API calls. Account
admins can enable or disable the Token API generator for specific users.

Admins can set permissions to control access to collections for user groups, power users, and viewers. If

columns are collection, workspace, account, and permissions. You can show or hide additional
columns in the table settings.

Delete a User in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
When a user creates an entity, that user is the entity owner. If an entity owner is deleted, the entities
created by that user become unowned. Some entities, such as actions, report schedulers, and data
streams with specific retrieval settings, require an owner to function properly. When a user is being

44.. Select a new owner.

••
If you don’t want to delete a user you can disable a user. Disabled users can’t take any actions,
but the entities still function properly. Disabled users are counted in the number of permitted
users in the account.
••
You can assign each entity a different owner. When selecting a new owner for an entity the list
of users will only include eligible owners.

Token API and Authorization Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses unique tokens to identify users and grant access to API resources. A
user generates a token on the intelligence platform to be included in subsequent API calls. Account
admins can enable or disable the Token API generator for specific users.
A token is valid for 24 months from the time it’s generated. The generated token is the only copy of the
private key, and the user is responsible for storing it securely. When a user generates a new API token, the
previous token expires immediately.
The token doesn’t display authentication data. The approved client authentication method is a JSON
web token.
51

=== PAGE 56 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The process for creating and authorizing API tokens is as follows:
•
Step 1:Create an API Service Account
•
Step 2: Generate a JSON web Token
•
Step 3: Use the Access Token
Note API access via JWT is now available, and will be enforced starting in Dec 2023. Until then,
existing (old) tokens will still be supported.
Give Users API Access in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
An API token is required to perform actions via the Intelligence API. An admin must enable API access
for a user to generate a token.
Create an API Service Account in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can create an API service account from your profile if you have API access. The client ID and
private key are valid for 24 months.
Generate a JSON Web Token (JWT)
To obtain an access token, you need to generate a JWT with specific claims, and sign it using the
RS256 algorithm with your private key.
Use the Access Token
After you have obtained the access token from the token request response, you can use it to make
authenticated API calls to other endpoints.
Give Users API Access in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
An API token is required to perform actions via the Intelligence API. An admin must enable API access
for a user to generate a token.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To give users API access: Admin with Manage Users permissions
11.. To the right of the navigation bar, click the dropdown arrow and select Manage Users.
22.. Select the user, and click the pencil icon.
33.. Under Permissions, select the type of access allowed for this user.
••
Enable API Access—The user can access an external service account. When enabled, the license
model is enforced and validated. If you disable access, the Token API is automatically deactivated.
••
API Access Only—The user can’t generate a token. Only an account admin with the Manage Users
permission can generate the user’s external token.
Create an API Service Account in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can create an API service account from your profile if you have API access. The client ID and private
key are valid for 24 months.
52

=== PAGE 57 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED

settings.

The API service account’s details are download in a text file locally on your machine. Store this file
securely.
Note Generate/Refresh generates the new API service account details. It does not generate the
Access Token. The old string tokens are a separate entity and will remain supported until their
deprecation date.
The downloaded file looks like this:

=== PAGE 58 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The token provides this information:
••
serviceAccountId—Represents your client ID that you use to generate an access token.
••
privateKey—Your RSA private key you use to sign your private key JWT (JSON Web Token).
••
discoveryEndpoint—Use this URL to retrieve the access token generation endpoint and the issuer
address.
Generate a JSON Web Token (JWT)
To obtain an access token, you need to generate a JWT with specific claims, and sign it using the RS256
algorithm with your private key.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To generate an API token: User with Enable API Access
Step: 1 Get the token request endpoint URL
You first need to get the token request endpoint URL.
11.. Insert the discoveryEndpoint URL you obtained when generating the API token in Intelligence into
your browser’s search bar.
22.. Press Enter.
33.. Copy the returned value for the field “token_endpoint”.
Example for AWS US (aka US1):
Example
54

=== PAGE 59 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
"token_endpoint": "https://idp.intelligence.salesforce.com/us1/token"
Step 2: Sign the JWT
In order to sign the JWT, you must use the private key provided when generating the API Token in
Intelligence to encode the payload.
Using Python the necessary modules we need are:
import jwt
import requests
import uuid
import time
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization
from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default_backend
The Intelligence platform supports the JWT module - jwt 1.3.1
The JWT needs to be generated with specific claims and signed using the RS256 algorithm with your
private key.
JWT Claims (payload):
{
"iss": "your-client-id",
"aud": "https://idp.intelligence.salesforce.com"",
"sub": "your-client-id",
"iat": 1690129371, (time when the token was issued)
"jti": "45911cd010b846f9810b150c969b0007"
}
"iss" and "sub": Equivalent to the serviceAccountId
"iat": Epoch time in seconds.
"jti": a random UUID without "-".
Example of JWT
# env can be us1 / us2 / eu1 / eu2
app = 'us1'
service_account_id = ''
private_key_str = '''-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----\nMIIEogI....REDACTE
D.....xweWOhhutft/sA=\n-----END RSA PRIVATE
55

=== PAGE 60 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
KEY-----'''
private_key = serialization.load_pem_private_key(
private_key_str.encode(),
password=None,
backend=default_backend()
)
private_key_jwk = jwt.jwk_from_pem(private_key_str.encode())
payload = {
"iss": service_account_id,
"aud": "https://idp.intelligence.salesforce.com"",
"sub": service_account_id,
"iat": int(time.time()),
"jti": str(uuid.uuid4()).replace('-','')
}
jwt_object = jwt.JWT()
jwt_token = jwt_object.encode(payload, private_key_jwk, alg='RS256')
Step 3: Use the JWT to obtain a Platform Token
The Token Endpoint is available in the discoveryEndpoint URL obtained from your API client information.
Note The endpoint used in the example of AWS US instance may change.
"token_endpoint": "https://idp.intelligence.salesforce.com/us1/token""
Request Parameters
Parameter Type Description
client_id string Your client ID
(serviceAccountId).
client_assertion string Your JWT
client_assertion_type string The type of client assertion.
(Fixed value:
'urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-
assertion-type:jwt-bearer')
grant_type string The grant type. (Fixed value:
'client_credentials')
56

=== PAGE 61 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Request structure
[token_endpoint_obtained_from_the_beginning_of_Step_2]?grant_type=client_credentials&client_assertion=[your_signed_jwt_token]&client_id=[your_client_id]&client_assertion_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-
assertion-type:jwt-bearer
Example Response
{
"access_token": "eyJraWQ...",
"expires_in": 300
}
•
“access_token”—The issued access token you use to make API calls.
•
“expires_in”—The access token life time is 5 minutes (300 seconds).
Use the Access Token
After you have obtained the access token from the token request response, you can use it to make
authenticated API calls to other endpoints.
As an example, let's use the access token to make a POST request to get your user details:
POST https://app.datorama.com/services/admin/user/find/{user_id}
You should add an Authorization Header:
Authorization: Bearer [your access token]
Replace [your access token] with the actual access token obtained from the token request
response (field: “access_token”) .
Example Response:
Note This is just an example, and the actual endpoints and data for your specific use case may
differ.
Example
{
"id": 123,
"name": "John Doe",
"email": "john.doe@example.com"
...
}

Manage User Groups in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can create a user group to apply a set of content permissions and workspace user filters to users who
need access to specific accounts or workspaces. A user role determines what a user can and can’t do,
while a user group determines where the user can apply these permissions. You can assign a user to
more than one user group.
For example, you can create a user group for all users in the EMEA location and one for the marketing
team. And because a user can be assigned to multiple user groups, a marketeer in EMEA can be in both
the EMEA and marketing team user groups. This user receives all the content permissions and workspace
user filters available in either group, even if one group has fewer permissions.
You can create or or assign user groups when creating or editing a user.
Create a User Group
Create a user group to apply content permissions and workspace filters to a group of users who need
access to specific accounts or workspaces.
Create a User Group
Create a user group to apply content permissions and workspace filters to a group of users who need
access to specific accounts or workspaces.

Collection Permissions for User Groups
You can apply collection permissions to a user group. If the group includes users with different user

Collection Permissions for User Groups
You can apply collection permissions to a user group. If the group includes users with different user roles,
such as admins, power users, and viewers, the collection permissions don’t extend the individual user
role permissions.
For example, if you give Edit permissions to a user group that includes viewers, those viewers can only
view the collection and not edit it. Similarly, if you set Restricted permission to a user group that includes
admins, they can still edit the collection.

Workspace Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Each Marketing Cloud Intelligence account can have multiple workspaces. Each workspace has its own
data streams, dashboards, and reports. You typically use workspaces to separate clients, business units,
channel views, internal views, or role-based views—whatever best fits your needs.
Your work environment displays one workspace at a time. When changes are made, they are only applied
to the workspace you are working in. You can control your workspace settings from the workspace
settings tab, found in your profile. Each workspace is defined with a default maximum number of
allowed rows (50M). If you exceed this amount, an error message appears informing you that the
workspace cannot be created. To extend the default amount, contact your Marketing Cloud Intelligence
representative.

Workspace Analytics Report in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Workspace Analytics provides information about your workspace and helps you measure and improve
user engagement. The report is available at both the account and workspace level. The data in the
workspace report applies to the specific workspace. At the account level, you can view the data for all
the workspaces in the account.
Data Row Usage for Workspaces in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data row usage is calculated as the sum of rows in Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s database for a
specific account or workspace. The row count is mainly affected by the lowest level entity in your data,
multiplied by the number of days for which there is measurement data.

Data Row Usage for Workspaces in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data row usage is calculated as the sum of rows in Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s database for a specific
account or workspace. The row count is mainly affected by the lowest level entity in your data, multiplied
by the number of days for which there is measurement data.
An account can store a set number of rows in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence database. You can divide
the number of rows between the workspaces in the account. At the workspace level, you can view its row
usage and the number of rows distributed between the different data streams.

Archiving in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
If you have a lot of data that you no longer use but want to keep, you can archive it . You can pull up
archived data from previous time periods to use in reports.
Note The archiving feature is a premium feature available for purchase.
Archiving is based on a time window in which data is available to the user (called hot data). Data prior to
the time window (called cold data) is archived and isn’t available in queries. For example, if you set a
time window of 730 days, data from over two years ago is archived.
Note Archived rows are calculated separately from regular rows (hot rows) and don’t affect the hot
row limit.
66

=== PAGE 71 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
How Does Archiving Affect Data?
With the exception of reports, archived data isn’t available in queries on the platform or through the
Marketing Cloud Intelligence API.
Updating archived data via data streams is available for a limited number of uploads per day. Updating
occurs automatically when the data stream contains the relevant dates. When the workspace exceeds
the number of uploads allowed, it stops updating it until the next day.
The archiving window looks for the actual date range, not the processing date range. Therefore, you can’t
include additional filters. Dates above the time window are disabled. For example, if your archiving
window is defined as 365 days, and today’s date is 1/1/2020, you can’t choose dates before 1/1/2019.
When your archived data reaches the limit for the workspace, the data streams that are trying to update
archived data fail. Data streams that are updating hot data work as expected.
Include Archived Data in a Report
If you’re an Admin, you can run reports to retrieve archived data.
Include Archived Data in a Report
If you’re an Admin, you can run reports to retrieve archived data.
Note The archiving feature is a premium feature available for purchase.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To edit account settings Admin
You can create reports that query archived data, but they’re limited to a certain number of queries per
day. (A query is equivalent to one sheet in reports). Make sure that reports can query archived data.
Select Include Archived Data in Report when setting up the report.
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, select Reports.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Create a report.
44.. Expand Configuration, and select Include Archived Data in Report.
Each report sheet processed is counted against the daily limit of reports containing archived data.

Date Ranges in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use a date range to display specific data. You can set a date range for a pivot table, report, and
dashboard. You can select a predefined date range from the date picker, such as Last 30 days or This
Month, or you can enter a range. When you set a date range, your data updates according to the dates
selected.
When setting a date range, the following rules apply.
•
When the date range is a calendar segment (day, week, or month), Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses
the beginning of the calendar period and doesn’t count the days back from the current date. For
example, if you select Previous Week in the date picker, it isn’t counted as today minus seven days.
Instead, it goes to the week previous to the current week and starts the range on the first date that the
previous week started from.
•
The first day of the week is Monday, so Sunday is considered part of the previous week. You can
change this preference using the Custom Calendar feature.
This table lists the Marketing Cloud Intelligence date range options.
Date Range Description
Ongoing The data is relevant from the selected start date to the present day
(data is valid to yesterday).

=== PAGE 76 ===

Date Range Description
Yesterday Refers to the day before today.
Week to date
From the beginning of the current week to the current day.
Bi-week to Date Includes information from the beginning of the calendar bi-week.
Bi-week divides the entire year into two-week periods. This refers to
a specific two weeks, depending on the count from the beginning
of the year.
Month to date Includes information from the beginning of the calendar month.
For example, if you set this date range to April 15, it pulls data from
the beginning of April and shows 15 days worth of data.
Quarter to date From the beginning of the current quarter to the current day.
Year to date From the beginning of the year to the current day.
Previous Week Refers to the week before the current week.
Previous Bi-Week The previous bi-week to date. Bi-week divides the entire year into
two-week periods, starting from the beginning of the year.
Previous Month The entire month before the current month.
Previous Quarter The entire quarter before the current quarter.
Previous Year The entire year before the current year.
Last 7 Days The past 7 days, not including today.
Last 14 Days The past 14 days, not including today.
Last 30 Days The past 30 days, not including today.
Last 90 Days The past 90 days, not including today.
This Week The week in which the current date is in.
This Bi-week
The bi-week in which the current date falls in.
Bi-week divides the entire year into two-week periods, starting from
the beginning of the year.
This Month The month in which the current date is in.
This Quarter The quarter in which the current date is in.
This Year The year in which the current date is in.

Executive Workspaces in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
An executive workspace incorporates data from other workspaces, so you view and analyze data as if it’s
one data pool. Executive workspaces can also function as a regular workspace allowing you to create
data streams, and analyze data by creating rules, goals, calculated dimensions, and measurements.
Note Executive workspace is a premium feature. If you’re interested in purchasing contact a
Marketing Cloud Intelligence representative.
You can upgrade a workspace to an executive workspace when purchasing a Marketing Cloud
Intelligence license. For example, if you purchase two workspace licenses and you want an executive
workspace license as well, then you must purchase an additional license for the executive workspace.
When using an executive workspace, make sure the custom attributes, measurements, and dimensions
are mapped and constructed with similar logic and consistency. For example, if custom classification 1 is
defined differently under different workspaces, you can’t slice the data from these workspaces using the
custom classification 1 dimension.
Note Calculated dimensions and measurements aren’t inherited from workspaces. If you want to
use them in an executive workspace you have to either recreate them or push them.
Executive workspaces act as a separate unit. Data streams don’t appear under the executive workspace's
Data Streams tab, and changes made in the executive workspace don’t apply to the included
workspaces. For example, if you change a dimension or measurement display name in the executive
76

=== PAGE 81 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
workspace, the change isn’t applied to the included workspaces. Furthermore, events added in the
included workspaces don’t appear in the executive workspace as well.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Sandbox Testing Environment
You can use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence sandbox to test setup configurations and explore how new,
modified, or deleted entities could impact and change live data before implementing them to
production. Sandbox is enabled at the account level, so all workspaces under the account have access to
the sandbox environment.
Note Sandboxes are for testing and development use only, and not for production use.
After testing in sandbox, you can deploy the sandbox environment to production. Sandbox supports the
following entities:
•
Data streams
•
Generic data stream types
•
Dashboards
•
Reports
•
Pivot tables
•
Calculated dimensions
•
Calculated measurements
•
Data fusions
•
Classification Rules
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Sandbox Use Cases
Consider these common use cases when working in sandbox mode.
Sandbox Mode in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After enabling sandbox, the sandbox icon is available in the top right.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Sandbox Use Cases
Consider these common use cases when working in sandbox mode.
•
Adding an entity that doesn’t yet exist in production.
- The created entity only appears in sandbox.
- When deployed to production, the entity loses its sandbox status and becomes a production entity.
- The new production entity is now accessible in both sandbox and production modes, like any other
production entity.
•
Modifying an existing production entity.
- A copy of the entity is created in sandbox.
- Only the copied version is accessible in sandbox while only the original is accessible in production.
- When the modified version is deployed to production, it replaces the existing entity in production.
- The updated entity is now accessible in both sandbox and production modes, like any other
production entity.
77

=== PAGE 82 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Removing an existing production entity.
- When you remove an existing entity in sandbox, it no longer impacts anything in sandbox. The
entity is still visible as a deleted entity in case you want to reinstate it.
- When sandbox is deployed to production, the entity is deleted from both sandbox and production.
Sandbox Mode in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After enabling sandbox, the sandbox icon is available in the top right.
When you’re in sandbox mode, the screen has a purple background so that you can easily distinguish
between your production and sandbox environments.
Note Only Power Users and Admins can access sandbox.
Each entity that has been created, edited, or deleted in sandbox mode is marked with a purple triangle
(1).
Click Show Only Sandbox Entities (2) to see only sandbox entities in your list.
Click Show Deployment History (3) to see the history of the entities deployed from sandbox to
production.
The Sandbox Management Center (4) shows all your sandbox entities in one location.
You can revert changes (5) you made in sandbox mode.
Click Deploy All (6) to push all changes you made in sandbox mode to production.
Manage Sandbox Entities in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Because sandbox is shared at the workspace level, all admins have access to the same sandbox mode.
However, you can choose to let users see only the sandbox entities that they created.
Deploy or Revert Actions in Sandbox Mode in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
In sandbox mode you can deploy or revert changes.
Resolve Dependencies in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Sandbox
Sometimes an entity is dependent on another entity. For example, if you create a calculated
measurement in sandbox that is based on another calculated measurement also created in sandbox,
the first measurement is dependent on the second to work correctly.
View Deployment History in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Sandbox
All your deployment activity is logged and can be viewed in the Sandbox Deployment History dialog.

The following happens when reverting:
If an entity was added, revert removes the entity.
If an entity was edited, revert cancels the editing changes and brings the entity back to its original
settings.
If an entity was deleted, revert cancels the deletion, and the entity returns to sandbox.

must deploy the related classification rule.

measurement in sandbox that is based on another calculated measurement also created in sandbox, the
first measurement is dependent on the second to work correctly.

Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data from a variety of sources with different data types. Each one of
these different data types has its own unique combination of dimensions and measurements. The
dimensions are made up of entities that have a certain relationship, or hierarchy, between them. It’s
important to maintain this relationship in Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s database, otherwise the data
can get aggregated incorrectly.
For this reason, for each of the different data types, Marketing Cloud Intelligence has emulated the
unique relationship, which exists between these entities. This means that the corresponding Marketing
Cloud Intelligence entities within its database follow the same relationship. Modeling the data by these
relationships for the various data types is what’s collectively known as the Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Model.
The legend used by the diagrams that follow:
80

=== PAGE 85 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
There are two possible relationship types between entities – one-to-many and many-to-many:
One to Many – For example, the connection between Site and Media Buy. One Site can be linked to
many Media Buys, however, one Media Buy can be linked to one Site only.
Many to Many – For example, the connection between Media Buy and Creative. One Media Buy can be
linked to many Creatives and vice versa, and one Creative can be linked to many Media Buys.
The Main Entity in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model considers one of the entities from each data type to be the
main entity. The main entity is the entity around which everything else revolves or is associated with
within a given data type. In the data stream type diagrams below, all main entities appear in a circle. For
example in the Ads data stream type, Media Buy is the main entity.
The different main entities are:
Overarching Entities in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Overarching entities exist in all data stream types and allow you to link different data streams by these
entities. This enables slicing different data types by one (or more) overarching entity. For example,
delivery and social data can both be sliced by the overarching entity ‘Product’ (as seen in the following
image):
In the overarching entities in the following diagram, the entities on the-right hand side are in fact
'Attributes' of the main entity. They are in a one-to-many relationship with the main entity, so they can
each only hold one value for each main entity value. For example, in the ‘Ads Data Stream Type’,
'Product' is an attribute of 'Media Buy' meaning that there can only be one 'Product' value for each
'Media Buy' value. The entities on the left-hand side are in a many-to-many relationship with the main
entity and as such, they can each hold multiple values for each main entity value. So for example, in the
‘Ads Data Stream Type’, there can be multiple 'Device Category' values for 'Media Buy' value.
Note that Channel isn’t a mappable entity.
81

=== PAGE 86 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Stream Custom Attributes in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Stream Custom Attributes behave like Attributes to the Data Stream Entity. Data Stream Custom
Attributes are updated with a slight delay, so they might not appear in your workspace data immediately
after being added.
Cross-Connectivity Between Data Stream Types in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence
A relationship between entities doesn’t have to be via a direct link but can also be through a ‘third party’
Entity. For example, Campaign and Site aren’t linked directly one to another. However, since each one of
them is linked to Media Buy in a one-to-many relationship, the relationship between Campaign and Site
is many-to-many indirectly.
Data Stream Types in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The following diagrams show which entities are included in each data stream type. Not all entities must
be mapped in each data stream, however it’s advised that you always map the main entity. The main
entity appears in a circle so you can distinguish it from the other entities. Entities that aren’t mapped by
the user are given a default value by the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform. For example, in the
following widget (representing one data stream) the ‘Media Buy Entity’ is mapped, but the ‘Campaign
Entity’ isn’t, hence the ‘Campaign Entity’ gets a default value.
Note If the existing Data Stream types don’t match your data use case, you can use the Generic
Data Stream Type.
In the Data Models below, the largest circle highlights the Main Entity.
The main entity is determined per data stream type. For example, ‘Media Buy’ is the main entity in the
‘Ads Data Stream Type’, but it isn’t the main entity in the ‘Conversions Data Stream Type’. In the data
models that follow, the largest circle highlights the main entity. You can see that some data stream types
include entities from other data stream types. For example, in the data model for the ‘Conversions Data
Stream Type’, you can see entities from the ‘Ads Data Stream Type’.
The types of data streams are:
•
Ads
•
Ads Verification Blocking
•
Buy Data
•
Buy Data Conversions
•
CRM Leads

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence

Competitive

Conversion Tag
•
Conversion Tag with Keywords

Ecommerce

•

•

Web Analytics
