Apply Unnest Transformers in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The unnest transformer enables you to transform concatenated values into separate rows. The
measurement values linked to the nested row are duplicated to each new row. For example, If there are
three values, comma-separated, and one field must be broken out into individual rows of data, this
transformer creates individual rows for each value in that field.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To apply transformers: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Data Streams, and then Data Streams List.
22.. Create a data stream, or edit an existing one.
33.. In the Preview Source screen, click Edit Transformers.
44.. In the Add Transformers area, next to the Unnest Transformer, click the blue plus icon.
55.. In the Column field, select the column that you want to transform.
66.. In the Delimiter field, enter the delimiter that is used in the selected column data to separate
between the values to be unnested.
77.. In the Unnest Column Name field, enter the name of the new column to be created in the file. The
column specifies the number of values included in the nested row. If you want to divide the
measurement values between the new rows, set a function in the mapping process that divides the
measurement value by the number specified in this column.
88.. To indicate which column that you want to initiate the process, select the By Column Index checkbox.
(If necessary, you can apply multiple unnest transformers to the same column.)
99.. Click Done.

Mapping Your Source Data in TotalConnect
Mapping connects the source fields in your uploaded file to the corresponding Marketing Cloud
Intelligence fields. Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses machine learning algorithms to map your data. The
mapping process is similar regardless of which method you use to upload your data, with some minor
differences. If you’re using TotalConnect, Intelligence identifies the most suitable data stream type
according to your data and offers an initial mapping suggestion.
For more information about mapping, see Mapping Your Source Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Troubleshoot Total Connect Data Streams
Errors can occur that prevent data streams from being created or processed. You can view errors using
the Log button. The log contains important technical information and can help you diagnose issues.
The log for TotalConnect data streams stores all files that entered the data stream, and each file has its
own secondary log.
Here are some common errors that occur and possible solutions:
Error: MISSING_FIELD "A mandatory field is missing in the uploaded file"
A mandatory field is missing in your data stream. Two scenarios can cause the Missing Field error.
•
A column is missing from the file.
•
A column header has been renamed or misspelled.
Confirm the Error:
•
Download the file causing an error from the data stream’s log and compare mapped fields in the data
stream to the downloaded file fields. If fields are missing, contact an admin who is familiar with the
files.
•
Look for header names that are different from the previous files that were processed without the error.
Possible Solution:
138

=== PAGE 143 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Apply a mapping formula using the COALESCE function to accommodate all header names—both past
and current versions.
•
Upload the file to a new data stream. Issues concerning the headers aren’t always visible in the original
file—uploading the file to the platform in a separate data stream allows you to see how the platform
processes the file.
Error: DATE_FORMAT_IS_INVALID "Invalid date format"
Dates are mandatory for uploading measurement data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. An invalid date
format causes the data stream to fail. Two scenarios can cause an Invalid Date Format error.
•
The date field is being read as a Text and not as Date.
•
Dates are formatted differently than in previous files.
Possible Solution:
•
Apply a PARSEDATE function to the Day field in the mapping, including all possible variations of the
date formats in an ‘if’ statement.
•
Revert the file back to its original settings—the original file extension or original date format. If you
can’t revert the file, contact your account executive for assistance.
Error: INVALID_DATE_RANGE "Invalid date range"
An invalid date range error occurs when the date range of a file includes a large number of years. The
platform recognizes that the date range is unreasonable and doesn’t upload the file. In Marketing Cloud
Intelligence you can only process data within a 15-year time period. Any data older than 15 years results
in an invalid date range error.
Possible Solution:
•
Reduce the date range to include data within the last 15 years only.
•
A large date range error can also result from a date format error—it’s recommended to also check the
date format in the failed streams.
Error: FORMULA_EVALUATION_FAILED "Formula evaluation failed"
A formula that doesn’t function as intended can cause a data stream to fail. The scenario that causes the
Formula Evaluation Failed error:
•
The formula that is applied to the data stream can’t run on all the data it’s applied to. For example,
some of the data in the formula divides in a null value.
Confirm the Error:
•
Check the failed jobs log and determine which formula is failing.
•
Check the raw data files and see what data the failed formula is applied to.
139

=== PAGE 144 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Possible Solution:
•
Edit the formula and reprocess the files.
Error: MAPPING_VALIDATION/Mapping Warnings
A mapping validation or mapping warning highlights specific issues with the relationships between the
fields in the uploaded file. For example, there are multiple campaign name values associated with the
same campaign key. Mapping validation and Mapping warning can cause aggregation issues.
Possible Solutions:
•
Every mapped attribute field has to have its key field mapped as well. For example, if you map the
Social Post Domain field, Social Post Key must be mapped as well. Check the failed jobs in the logs
and determine which dimension is problematic.
•
Confirm that entities that are mapped in a one-to-many relationship. For example, Campaign and
Media Buy— the 'one' field, in this case campaign, has only one value corresponding to the 'many' field
(Media Buy). You can check this using a pivot table:
•
Confirm that each entity, for example Media Buy Key, has only one value under each attribute field—for
example Media Buy Size. In the image you can see that there are two attributes associated with the
entity, causing a MAPPING_VALIDATION or Mapping Warnings error.
•
In datasets that only include Many to Many relationships, you can create a concatenation in the
mapping to concatenate several fields to create a viable key.
Error: Security Error: invalid value "...path may contain malicious code"
The invalid value error occurs when a page path is identified as code—and for security reasons it’s
blocked.
Possible solution:
•
The path isn’t malicious code. You can prevent it from being blocked by using this formula:
STRIP_HTML(csv['page_path']) in page path and its subcategories (Page Path Level 1 and 2).
•
After applying the formula, reprocess the failed days.
Error: The formulas contain a cyclic reference of Marketing Cloud
Intelligence fields: cyclic reference detected
A cyclic formula reference error commonly occurs when using VLOOKUP functions and the wrong type of
field is used.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence references these field types in formulas:
140

=== PAGE 145 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
CSV—Fields in the uploaded file. Transformers affect these values, but formulas don’t.
•
Dat—A data stream is manipulating a field in the "transformed" file. Formula manipulation affects these
values.
•
Data Model fields (appears as [Media_Buy_Name])—Fields in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence
database that contain values loaded by existing data streams to the platform.
When using VLOOKUP it’s common to search and retrieve values from fields uploaded by other data
streams in the workspace. Data Model fields with square brackets are used to retrieve the values allowing
you to access values that exist in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence database.
Possible Solution:
•
Make sure that the VLOOKUP formula uses the right type of fields in the second and third parameters,
searchDimension, and returnDimension.
Error: The workspace exceeded total rows or The account exceeded total
rows
When a workspace or account exceeds its total rows limit, data streams can’t process and fail. Every
account has a limited number of rows. This number is divided between the account's workspaces. The
error in question appears when either the account or one of its workspaces has reached their row limit.
When an account reaches its row limit, 'The account exceeded total rows' error appears.
When a workspace reaches its row limit, 'The workspace exceeded total rows' appears.
Confirm the Error:
•
Admins can track the row usage from the account or workspace settings under Data Row Usage.
Possible Solution:
•
Delete data from the workspace.
Note Changes can take up to 30 minutes to apply. To increase the account or workspace row
limit, contact your account executive.
Problem: Zero rows are uploaded when a new file is uploaded.
To see the number of rows added in the last update:
•
On the Connect & Mix tab, expand Data Streams, and then click Data Stream List.
•
In the Last # Of Rows Added column, you can see the number of rows that were added.
If 0 rows were added, check to see if a measurement header is missing, or if it appears under a different
name in the recent file. These issues can result in rows not being uploaded.
141

=== PAGE 146 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Possible Solution:
•
Apply a mapping formula using the COALESCE function to accommodate all header names for both
past and current versions.
•
Upload the file to a new data stream. Sometimes, issues related to headers aren’t visible in the original
file. Uploading the file to the platform in a separate data stream allows you to see how the platform
processes the file.
Problem: The log displays "NO FILE" under the 'File' column and "No files
were retrieved" under 'Reason'
This situation occurs when the data stream's retrieval settings are enabled, but no new files are available
during the retrieval. This message means that the data stream ran, but no new data was present.
Data upload limits apply to TotalConnect data streams. This table lists the limits.
File Type File Size Row Limits
Direct Upload 40 MB 15 Million Rows
Email 30 MB 15 Million Rows
Retrieval 500 GB 15 Million Rows
Additional Notes:
•
Up to 15 jobs can be processed simultaneously in each workspace. All other jobs that start at the same
time are added to a queue and given a Pending status until they’re processed.
•
When creating a scheduled report in order to feed a data stream, the associated email address can be
entered in the To, CC, or BCC fields.
Creating Data Streams with API Connectors in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
API connectors enable you to easily connect data sources such as Google Ads or Facebook to Marketing
Cloud Intelligence. When you connect an API connector, an API data stream is created that
automatically maps the data fields the vendor provides to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence data model,
allowing you to access your information.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers various API connectors. Connecting API connectors to Intelligence is
a dynamic process that changes according to the API connector you use. As a result, not all fields are
available for each connector.
Create a Data Stream with an API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use an API connector to create an API data stream. You can choose from one of thousands of API
connectors, or you can go to the Marketplace and install connectors.

Update Data in API Data Streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You have two options to update the data in an API data stream after it’s been created: Re-process and Re-
run.
Re-process Data in API Data Streams
Re-process is a common action that updates the data within a selected data stream. The most popular
use for Re-process is when a dataset changes on the platform’s side, such as Facebook, and the user
wants to pull the update back to Marketing Cloud Intelligence. This action proactively pulls the new
data instead of waiting for a scheduled update to take place (if applicable).
Re-run Data in API Data Streams
Re-run doesn’t initiate the retrieval of new data into the data stream, it reruns the data stream’s
existing data.
Re-process Data in API Data Streams
Re-process is a common action that updates the data within a selected data stream. The most popular
use for Re-process is when a dataset changes on the platform’s side, such as Facebook, and the user
wants to pull the update back to Marketing Cloud Intelligence. This action proactively pulls the new data
instead of waiting for a scheduled update to take place (if applicable).

new changes made to it.

Troubleshoot API Data Streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Errors can occur that prevent API data streams from being created or processed. For example, it’s
possible you used invalid credentials and processing failed. You can view errors using the Log button. The
log contains important technical information and can help you diagnose issues.
The log for API Connections doesn’t store individual files, but each line of the log analyzes each time the
API was called to process a given set of dates. The same technical information used to determine why
the call failed is available via the Show More option.
Here are some common errors that occur and possible solutions:
Error: INVALID_CREDENTIALS "Invalid Credentials"
Credentials are the security tokens most APIs use to authenticate access to the source systems. Two
scenarios can cause the Invalid Credentials error:
•
The security token is expired and requires re-authentication.
•
The credentials have changed password values.
Possible Solution:
The fix is the same for both scenarios.
11.. Navigate to the Connect & Mix tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams, and then click Data Source Authentication.
33.. Select the credentials that are being used in the failed API stream, and click Refresh Token, and then
enter your credentials.
146

=== PAGE 151 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Error: ‘Provider Internal Error’
As the name indicates, this is an error on the provider’s side. This error can be due to any number of
reasons.
Possible Solution:
Because this is a provider error, Marketing Cloud Intelligence support can’t do anything to resolve the
issue. Typically, waiting a few minutes and processing the data stream’s failed date ranges resolves the
issue. Sometimes you see some dates process successfully and other dates fail again. In this case, wait an
additional 5–10 minutes, and then process the remaining failed days.
Error: Security Error: invalid value "...path may contain malicious code"
This error occurs when a page path is identified as code and blocked for security reasons.
Possible Solution:
Because this isn’t malicious code, you can prevent it from being blocked by using this formula:
STRIP_HTML(csv['page_path']) . After applying the formula, reprocess the failed days.
Error: FORMULA_EVALUATION_FAILED "Formula evaluation failed"
This error occurs when a formula applied to the data stream can’t run on all the data it’s applied to.
Possible Solution:
Edit the formula, and try reprocessing the data stream again.
Error: The workspace exceeded total rows or The Account exceeded total
rows
Every account has a limit to the amount of rows it can use. This amount is divided between the account’s
workspaces. The error in question appears when the account or one of its workspaces reaches its row
limit. When the account reaches its row limit first, the ”The account exceeded total rows” error appears.
When the workspace reaches its row limit first, the ”The workspace exceeded total rows” appears. After a
row limit is reached, data streams fail and data isn’t uploaded to the workspace or account. Admins can
track the row usage under the Settings menu in the Account/Workspace Usage Data section.
Possible Solutions:
•
Delete data from the workspace using the delete data feature. Changes can take up to 30 minutes to
apply.
•
To increase the account/workspace’s row limit, contact your Account Manager. Changes in the row
limit can take up to 30 minutes to apply.
147

=== PAGE 152 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Error: INVALID_DATE_RANGE "Invalid date range"
This error occurs when the date range of a file includes a large number of years. The platform recognizes
that the date range is unreasonable and doesn’t upload the file. Sometimes a wide date range is the
result of a problem with the date format. When this error occurs, it’s advisable to check the date format
in the failed data streams.
•
Only dates within 15 years of the current year can be processed. Anything beyond this period results in
the data stream failing with the “Invalid date range” error.
•
Fifteen jobs can be processed simultaneously per workspace. All other jobs starting at the same time
are added to a queue and set to Pending until processed.
Error: Rate Limit Exceeded
This error occurs when the rate limits defined on the API provider's side are exceeded. Please note that
this is a limitation on the provider's end and the Marketing Cloud Intelligence support team can’t do
much to overcome this error.
Rate Limits are defined on the provider's side and vary from API to API - every API has different
limitations. In addition to this, the limitation might be specific to your credentials that are linked to the
provider in question.
They can also vary in type - for example, there might be a Daily Limit on how many requests your account
can make to the provider for a given day. The provider may limit the number of requests from a specific
account, so it only allows a few to pass successfully while others are rejected due to Rate Limits.
These limitations may be related to the specific credentials used to access the Provider's Database (the
credentials used inside the API stream).
Possible Solution
It’s recommended you wait a day, and then process the data streams with failed jobs the next day. Rate
limits are typically reset each day.

Adform API Connector
The Adform API connector retrieves Leads data. The data is aggregated and can only be seen at the
campaign level.
You might see some discrepancies in the Media Cost data, due to a difference between the API report
and the report sent to the user.
•
Channel: Display
•
Cost Data lagging: 1 Day back
Prerequisites
•
A valid Adform username and password with user level API access enabled. To enable API access, go
to your user profile screen and check the 'External API' checkbox
•
If your username and password don’t grant you access to the API, contact your Adform representative.
•
Adform has password complexity requirements for API users. If these requirements aren’t fulfilled, an
‘Invalid credentials’ error appears and you won’t be able to create the OAuth.
•
Password requirements:
- Minimum 6 symbols
- Upper and lower case letters
- Numbers
- At least 1 Special character
Additional Details
•
The maximum date range accessible through the API is 365 days from today. For example, last year's
data is available through the Adform API.
•
If the same credentials used to set this API are used in an additional Workspace, make sure to set the
Level to 'Account' through the Authentication edit window.
Extended Properties
•
Cost Type – Set the Cost type to match the Cost Type set in your Adform account. You can see the cost
type set in your Adform account be clicking the Cost column.
•
Get Banner Data – This checkbox pulls Creative Level Dimensions from Adform. After choosing this
checkbox, the following Dimensions are pulled to the Data Stream:
- Creative Key (AdID)
- Creative Name (AdName)
- Creative Category (bannerFormat)
- Creative Format (AdType)
- Creative Custom Attribute 01 (AdWidthxAdHeight)
159

=== PAGE 164 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Creative Custom Attribute 02 (AdFileSize)
•
Get Conversion Data – This checkbox pulls Conversion Data to the ‘Conversion Tag’ Bucket. The
following Dimensions and Measurements are pulled to the Data Stream:
- Conversion Tag Key (pageID)
- Conversion Tag Name (page)
- Total Conversions (conversions)
- Post Impression Conversions (PostImpressionLeads)
- Post Click Conversions (PostClickLeads)
- Post Impression Revenue (PostImpressionSales)
Adglow API Connector
Adglow is a Social Media Advertising Campaign Management Tool.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type: Ads and Conversion Tag
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Details
•
To retrieve data via the Adglow API, your campaigns must be listed within Adglow’s billing system.
Contact your Adglow representative to list your campaigns in their billing system.
•
Currently, Marketing Cloud Intelligence only supports Facebook data.
Prerequisites
A valid Adglow username and password required. Contact your Adglow representative to receive these
credentials.
QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in Adglow for the required date range. Only Facebook reports can currently
be retrieved. Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date
range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.

Adobe Analytics Custom API Connector
The Adobe Analytics Custom API Connector allows you to retrieve a custom set of Measurements and
Dimensions from the Adobe Analytics API into Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
The standard Adobe Analytics API connector is preset to be accurately mapped to the Data Model, while
the Custom connectors require manual setup. Use the Adobe Analytics Custom Connectors to retrieve
unique measurements, data from the Data Warehouse reports, or if you want to retrieve calculated
metrics and segments from the Analytics reports.
•
Channel: Web Analytics
•
Data Stream Type:
162

=== PAGE 167 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Ads Conversion Tag
- Generic
- Web Analytics
- Web Analytics Events
- Web Analytics Tags
- Web Analytics Pages
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to Adobe Analytics, you need valid OAuth credentials (Client Id + Client Secret).
General Setup
Enter your credentials and select the Profile you want to pull data for.
•
Account – Select the Adobe Analytics account you want to retrieve data for. This correlates to the
Report Suite in Adobe Analytics.
•
Segments – Select the Segment you want to retrieve data for. This section is optional and you can only
select one segment per Data Stream; however, you can add multiple Segment values within the
Extended Properties (see below).
•
Fields – Select the fields you require. You can select up to 3 Dimensions per Data Stream and up to 30
Measurements. Dimension fields are identified with the prefix (ELEMENT) and Measurements are
prefixed with (METRIC).
Extended Properties
•
Override Segments – Insert the Segment IDs of the segments you want to pull. This is only relevant if
you want to query more than one segment per Data Stream. Separate each segment ID with a
comma.
Note The values inserted here override the segment selected in the General Setup section (see
above).
•
Date Granularity – Select the date granularity you want to retrieve your data for:
- Daily
- Aggregated
Note If Aggregated is set, the data is retrieved without a Day Dimension, so you’ll need to apply
a Date in the Mapping Screen.
•
Data Stream Type – Select one of the available Data Stream Types you want to map your data to. You
can only select one type of Data Stream Type in any given Adobe Analytics Custom API Data Stream.
You can map into the following Data Stream Types:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
- Generic

Adobe Analytics Data Warehouse API Connector
Adobe Analytics is a Web Analytics service that tracks and reports website traffic and interaction.
This connector retrieves data from the ‘Data Warehouse’ reports section in Adobe Analytics. Pulling data
from Data Warehouse means that you can’t retrieve Calculated Metrics —— you can only retrieve data for
a selection of supported Segments.
•
Channel: Web Analytics
•
Data Stream Type:
- Web Analytics
166

=== PAGE 171 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Web Analytics Pages
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to Adobe Analytics, you need valid OAuth credentials (Client Id + Client Secret).
Details
When Authenticating Adobe Analytics, you’re required to enter a Username and the Secret Key. For the
Username value, you have to combine your User Name and Company Name values in the following
format: Username:Company and the Secret Key, is your Shared Secret value in Adobe
If you’re not sure how to find your Shared Secret value in Adobe, go to the User Settings and under the
Web Service section, you’ll see a string that is referred to as your Shared Secret.
General Setup
Select your Account. Account correlates to the ‘Report Suite’ value in Adobe Analytics. Only after
segment can be selected at a time. To add more than one segment see the Extended Properties.
In the Fields dropdown list, all Dimension fields are identified with the prefix (ELEMENT) and metrics are
prefixed with (METRIC). Calculated fields can’t be selected.
Extended Properties
•
Override Segments – This setting is only relevant if you need to query on more than one Segment, as
it allows you to enter up to 4 Segment values (which you can’t do from the Segment dropdown at the
top). Enter the segment values ids separated by commas. Be advised that the values entered in the
Override Segments section overrides any Segment selection you have made in the dropdown at the
top. To obtain the Segment ID values from within the Adobe platform, edit a certain Segment and
retrieve the ID value from the URL component.
•
Data Stream Type – The data being pulled is unmapped, so select the Data Stream Type to which the
retrieved fields should be mapped to.
Additional Information
•
The Segment List in Marketing Cloud Intelligence populates ALL the available segments from Adobe,
some Segments might not be compatible with Data Warehouse Report Type (as per Adobe’s internal
limitation). Thus, if you select a Segment that isn’t supported in Data Warehouse, the Data Stream
processing fails with an error message to that effect. If you’re unsure which Segments are supported in
Data Warehouse, then as best practice we suggest you verify this from within the Adobe platform prior
to creating the Data Stream. To do so, simply check which Segments were populated in the list of
Segments in the DW Report Builder itself.
167

=== PAGE 172 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
As for your selection of Dimensions (identified with the prefix ELEMENT) note that you can only
combine Dimensions from within the same ‘Group’ (or ‘Type Breakdown’) again, as per an Adobe
limitation. The two available groups are ‘Traffic’ and ‘Commerce’. You can’t for example have both
'browserheight' (Traffic) and 'category' (Commerce) as 2 Dimensions queried within the same Data
Stream, as each belongs to a different group and would, therefore, cause the Data Stream processing
to fail.
•
Not all Dimensions are supported in the Data Warehouse Report Type. To find out which Dimensions
are supported, drag & drop a Dimension (Element) into the Segment Builder’s Definitions field to find
out about its Report Type compatibility.
•
In the Fields dropdown list, due to security reasons, all HTML tags are removed from the field names.
As a result, field names that have HTML tags (such as < >) won’t match perfectly to their names in
Adobe.
•
The reporting UI (Adobe Workspace) doesn’t produce a flat CSV report, rather it provides a drill-down
report with a limit of only 400 rows. This can be challenging when trying to compare data with
maximum granularity.
Pulling Unique Measurements in Adobe Analytics
The Unique Measurement values will only match those on the Adobe platform for the exact same
combination of Dimensions (including Date), but not at any other level and not as a total of these rows of
data.
QA Your Data: Regular Measurements
•
Generate a report in the Adobe Data Warehouse Report Builder with the exact same set of Dimensions
and Measurements that exist in the Data Stream query, for a certain date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and measurements for the same date range.
•
Compare the measurement totals and make sure they’re aligned.
•
Make sure that you’ve mapped correctly in terms of entity relationships. Filter the Adobe report for a
certain dimension value and make sure that the list of associated Dimensions seen in the Adobe
report corresponds to that seen in Marketing Cloud Intelligence when the same filtering is applied.
•
Make sure that the metric values are aligned.
QA Your Data: Unique Measurements
•
Generate a report in Adobe DW Report Builder that has the exact same set of Dimensions including
Date and unique measurements that exist in the Data Stream query.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and unique measurement/s, for the same date range.
•
Instead of comparing the Measurement Totals, compare each line measurement.

Extended Properties
•
Date Breakdown
- Day - This is the default Date Breakdown and aggregates the data by a daily Date Breakdown.
- Week - Selecting this aggregates the date by a weekly Date Breakdown.
- Aggregated - Selecting this setting means that the data won’t be aggregated on a Daily or Weekly
level. Instead, the data is assigned a Data Date based on the day of its retrieval. This setting is most
useful for viewing Unique Measurements, such as Reach and Frequency without having a Daily or
Weekly aggregation applied to them.
•
Placement Display Mode - From here you can determine whether to retrieve Placements, Folders or
both:
- Leaves - Selecting this retrieves Placements
- Nodes - Selecting this retrieves Folders
- All - Selecting this retrieves both Placements and Folders. Selecting this option could lead to
171

=== PAGE 176 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
duplications of the Measurements data.
•
Get Conversions - This is checked by default and pulls Conversions data to the Conversion Tag Data
Stream Type.
•
Get Creative Interactions - Selecting this pulls Video Interaction Measurements (such as Video
Completions) into the Ads Data Stream Type.
•
Get Full Placement Path - Retrieve the full directory path of the Placement in a Dimension called
placementFullPath unmapped in Ads and Conversion Tag buckets.
In AdOcean’s UI, Creative Interactions are represented as different values under a Dimension called
‘Creative Interaction’, where the quantity of each type of Creative Interaction is placed under a
Measurement called ‘Interactions’, as shown below:
Creative Campaign Creative Interaction Interactions (#)
Y X 25% Viewed 5
Y X 50% Viewed 2
Y X Fully Viewed 1
For this data to appear correctly in Marketing Cloud Intelligence, Marketing Cloud Intelligence performs
a process similar to a Pivot Transformer, whereas the Dimension and Measurement are parsed as four
separate Measurements - Video Views 25%, Video Views 50%....etc. As illustrated below:
Creative Campaign 25% Viewed 50% Viewed Fully Viewed
Y X 5 2 1
Known Issues
Checking the ‘Get Creative Interactions’ box in the Extended Properties retrieves an additional report
from AdOcean. This means that every time the Data Stream is processed, it will be processing an
additional report - potentially adding to the processing time. Additionally, due to the structure of the
Creative Interactions data, as detailed above, Marketing Cloud Intelligence may not automatically parse
some Creative Interaction Types.
QA Your Data
•
Within Marketing Cloud Intelligence, create a Pivot Table for the same Date Range and Client as the
Report you generated in AdOcean.
•
Since the AdOcean UI is limited to showing only three Dimensions at a time, your view in Marketing
Cloud Intelligence should only include three Dimensions to match what you see in AdOcean.
•
Match the Date Range of your view in Marketing Cloud Intelligence to the Date Range you’re looking at
within the AdOcean UI.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals per row to ensure the data is correct.
172

Adthena API Connector
Adthena provides search competitive data.
•
Data Stream Types: Unmapped
•
Data Lag: 2 Days
General Setup
Select the dates for which you wish to process the Data, under Initial Data Fetch.
173

=== PAGE 178 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Extended Properties
•
Data Stream Type - Select the Data Stream Type for the Data you wish to retrieve.
•
Reports Type - Select one of the available Report Types: Market Share, Market Trends, Top Ads, Google
Shopping (or Top PLAs, which is called ‘Google Shopping’ on the Adthena UI (Product Listing Ads -
Google Shopping in the Adthena UI).
•
Results Filter - Whole Market filters the report by general market terms, My Terms filters the report
according to your search terms.
•
Device Breakdown - Select By Devices or Totals. By Devices means that Marketing Cloud Intelligence
adds a device breakdown via the Device Dimension. Totals returns the totals without the device
breakdown.
Note
•
The Top Ads and Google ShoppingTop PLA Reports always includes Device Breakdown, regardless
of the setting you apply here.
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence allows you to retrieve all the available Measurements at once.
Additional Details
•
Adthena has four different types of Reports:
- Market Share
- Market Trends
- Top Ads
- Top PLAs (Product Listing Ads) - Google Shopping in Adthena UI
•
For the Market Share and Market Trend reports, Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls the actual
percentage Measurements.
- It’s recommended to change the Measurement format to percentage for ease of analysis.
- With Percentage Measurements it’s also recommended to consume the Data at the granularity with
which it was pulled as viewing it at any other granularity could result in discrepancies.
Advanced Settings
•
The Market Trends Report is retrieved with a Daily Breakdown.
•
Other reports can’t be retrieved with a Daily Breakdown. For these Reports Marketing Cloud
Intelligence queries Adthena for 7-day batches and receives the aggregated Measurement values for
that duration. The End Date of the 7-day period is the value that is mapped to the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence ‘Day’ value.
•
The ‘Statistic Span days’ is what determines the number of days in the processing batch while the
‘Sliding Window’ setting determines the number of days queried on each of the Stream’s automatic
daily runs. In light of this, the ‘Sliding Window’ setting should also be configured to the same number
as the ‘Statistic Span Days’ to allow the Stream to query the source for the number of days that it is
configured to process. Should your processing Date Range consist of a sum of days that can’t be
174

=== PAGE 179 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
divided by the number set as the Statistic Span Days - in this example, 7 - any days that are left over
will be aggregated for their own Duration Period.
•
If you wish to create a Daily Breakdown for the Data Stream you create, set both the ‘Statistic Span
Days’ and ‘Sliding Window’ to 1 day.
QA Your Data
•
Create the relevant Adthena Report Type for a certain Date Range.
•
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, create a Pivot Table for the same Date Range as the Report you
generated in Adthena.
•
Compare the Measurement values per row. Be sure not to compare the total Measurement values for
more than one row at a time, as the fact that Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls the Percentage values
rather than the raw Measurement values that will lead to a difference between the two views.
QA Market Share, Top Ads and Google Shopping
•
When creating one of these Reports in the Adthena UI, you’ll have to comply with at least one of the
processing batches’ Date Ranges and compare your data based on that Date Range. To view the Batch
Date Range for which you’ll need to generate the report in the Adthena UI, refer to the relevant Data
Stream’s Execution Log.
•
When creating the Pivot Table in Marketing Cloud Intelligence, filter it for one Date value - the last day
in the Adthena Report’s Date Range.
•
As in the Marketing Trends Report, any comparisons for Percentage Measurements should be
performed per row, rather than for several rows at a time.

no Measurements data. Dates with no Measurements data are not processed in Marketing Cloud

Amazon Advertising API Connector
This API connector retrieves data for Amazon Campaign Types Sponsored Products and Headline Search
Ads
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Types:
- Ads (for Delivery Data)
- Conversion Tag (for Conversions Data)
- Search Keywords (for Keywords data with Delivery Data)
- Conversion Tag with Keywords (for Keywords data with Conversions Data)
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
A valid User Name and Password as a ‘Merchant’ account with access to Amazon’s Seller Central is
required.
Details
Data retrieval is only available for the last 60 days.
Extended Properties
This connector always retrieves datasets for both Amazon Campaign Types—Sponsored Products, and
Headline Search Ads regardless of what is configured in the Extended Properties of the Data Stream.
•
Get Conversions - retrieves additional Conversions and Revenue Measurements (including many
Conversions Measurements that are unmapped by default), and that data are mapped into the
Conversion Tag Data Stream Type. Despite it being Conversions data, it won’t be retrieved with a
Conversion Name/Tag Dimension, but rather with the same set of Dimensions used in the Ads Data
Streams type
•
Get Keywords - retrieves an additional Keyword Dimension, and that dataset is mapped into the
Search Keywords Data Stream Type. Beyond the Measurements already retrieved into the Ads Data
Stream Type, no additional Measurements are retrieved within the Keyword dataset.
QA Your Data
•
Compare data for each Campaign Type separately.

make sure that the Measurement totals are still aligned.

information about sales and traffic, consumer behavior, and operations.
You can view both order and item-level data using products’ Amazon Standard Identification Number

This API connector brings in consumer data, but if you want inventory data, see Amazon Vendor Central
178

=== PAGE 183 ===

Inventory API Connector.
Important Due to Amazon’s new authentication process, you must re-authenticate all Amazon
accounts once a year to ensure your data is up-to-date. For more information about this workflow,
see Amazon Authentication Workflow.
Note Because of an Amazon limitation, you can fetch only the past 7 days of your data. After
connecting your data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, the API brings in data according to the set
schedule. The default is daily, and it accumulates in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
More details about the Amazon Vendor Brand Analytics API connector:
•
The connector brings in the product’s ASIN as shown in Amazon Seller Central and maps it as Product
Key.
•
You can connect more than one marketplace to the connector. However, you must create a separate
data stream per marketplace.
•
Amazon sometimes requires your approval to share data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Amazon Vendor Central allows you to query only up to the past 7 days of data. After you connect your
data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, the API continues bringing in data according to the specified
cadence, which is by default is daily.

Account Analytics API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics connector, you can monitor engagement, page
views, audit changes, and entity creation logs. You can use this data to create customized views, add
dynamic dashboards, and pull actionable reports. The connector includes logs for both unpublished and
published pages.
To create an API data stream for Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics, you need access to the
workspace that you’re requesting data for.
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics data stream type is Lite Connect, and data lagging is
1 day.
Users are account level entities. When selecting the user entity from the extended properties, you see
data for all users in the account and not the specific workspace.

44.. In the General section, select the account and workspaces from the dropdown.
55.. Set the time period for the initial data fetch. You can process data up to the past 180 days.
66.. In Extended Properties, select the entities that you want to audit from the dropdown.
77.. To change the mapping, click Mapping. You can also access mapping by selecting Map to Model at
the bottom of the Create New Stream page.
88.. To define advanced settings for the data stream, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, click
Advanced Settings.
99.. Click Create.
1100.. Quality check your data. Compare the data pulled from the connector to the data in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Account Analytics.
AOL One Video API Connector
The ONE by AOL API connector retrieves data related to Video Ads that are displayed on mobile devices.
In using our API you'll able to retrieve standard Ads and Conversions data, such as Campaigns, Media
Buys, Impressions, Clicks, and Video Measurements.
Additional Measurements and Dimensions, including Viewability Measurements, are also retrieved and
are available unmapped. Their relevance depends on your data configuration.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
•
Valid Client ID & Secret – Contact your ONE by AOL representative to receive these credentials. Ask for
a Production Client ID and Secret. The Authentication Process is performed by the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Platform.
•
Organization ID – The ID is available on the top right menu of the ONE by AOL UI. By clicking your
organization name, you’re able to see its ID.
Extended Properties
Continent and Time-Zone – To generate reports within ONE by AOL, specify the Continent and Time-
Zone for the account. This applies to using the API as well and setting the wrong values could lead to
discrepancies in the data.
Default values are set (North America, ETC/UTC), so the Data Stream is created without changing these
values.

How to QA Your Data
Generate a report from the Reports section under the ‘Analytics’ menu, including all of the relevant
dimensions and measurements. Make sure to set the correct date range, Continent, and Time-Zone.
AppNexus Advertiser API Connector
AppNexus is a global internet technology company that operates an independent marketplace for digital
advertising, and enterprise technology for buyers and sellers of digital ads. AppNexus Advertiser is used
to retrieve performance, revenue, and profit data across a selected advertiser's creatives and campaigns.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to AppNexus Advertiser, you need a valid AppNexus user with API access enabled on the user
level. If your username and password don’t grant you access to the API, contact your AppNexus
representative.
General Setup
After you’ve entered your credentials, select the Profile you want to pull data for.
Extended Properties
•
Location Breakdown - Select this to add a further breakdown to the data retrieved by the API, resulting
in a higher level of granularity on which the Measurements can be sliced. The following Breakdown is
available when pulling data from the AppNexus Advertiser API, in addition to the standard data being
pulled:
- Country - Select this to pull country data and add the Dimension ‘Country’ to the dataset.
- TimeZone Breakdown - Select the time zone for which you want to pull data. (This is only available
for the Geo Analytics Report. Billing data is always be based in the UTC Time Zone.)
•
Get Conversions Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve Conversion and Revenue data in addition to
the Delivery data retrieved by the API by default. The following fields are added and mapped to the
Conversion Tag Data Stream Type:
- Total Conversions
- Revenue
- Post Impression Revenue
- Post Impression Conversions
- Post Click Revenue

=== PAGE 191 ===

- Post Click Conversions
- Conversion Tag Key
•
Get Creative Level Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve creative level data in addition to the Delivery
data retrieved by the API by default. The following fields are added and mapped to the Conversion Tag
Data Stream Type:
- Creative Name
- Creative Key
- Creative Format
•
Get Video Metrics - Select this checkbox to retrieve video metric data in addition to the Delivery data
retrieved by the API by default. (Note that Video Measurements can’t be retrieved along with Billing
Period Values). The following fields are added and mapped to the Ads Data Stream Type:
- Video Plays
- Video Fully Played
- Video Competitions 75%
- Video Competitions 50%
- Video Competitions 25%
Known Issues
AppNexus stopped supporting Campaigns in Augmented Line Items (ALI) in August 2019. Instead, they
use Splits. Since Campaign ID and Name fields are mapped by default, these fields return empty for ALIs.
Split ID and Name are available unmapped.

and Measurements for the same report and date range.

Extended Properties
•
Report Type - Select the report you want to pull data for. The following Report Types are available:
- Buyer Segment Performance Report - This report provides buyers with segment performance across
campaigns and multiple advertisers.
- Device Analytics Report - This report enables you to retrieve buy-side and sell-side performance data
based on devices where impressions were served.
- Geo Analytics Report - This report allows you to break down campaign delivery and performance by
geographic area.
- Network Site Domain Performance Report - This report shows performance and optimization data
by domain for all of a network's advertisers.
- Data Usage Report - This report provides details related to your usage of data provided by third
parties (for example, user segment providers), the costs of data usage and line items/campaigns in
which that data was used to target users.
- Buying Billing Report - This report shows billing data across direct advertiser impressions (bought
across direct publishers and real-time inventory).
- Seller Billing Report - This report shows billing data across direct publishers (sold across direct
advertisers and other platform buyers).
•
TimeZone Breakdown - Select the time zone for which you want to pull data. (This is only available for
the Geo Analytics Report. Billing data are always based in the UTC Time Zone.)
•
Data Stream Type - Select one of the available Data Stream Types you want to map your data to. You
can only select one type of Data Stream Type in any given AppNexus Reports API Data Stream. You can
map into the following Data Stream Types:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
- Generic

Apple News Publisher API Connector
Apple News Publisher places ads in Channels in the Apple News App on iPhone and iPad.
•
Channel: Display
190

=== PAGE 195 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
•
Access Token
•
Certificate
•
Private Key
To obtain the these credentials from the Apple News platform, go to the ‘API’ tab on the ‘Account’ page.
Then, click Create Key to generate a new API Key. After a key has been created, you will receive a ZIP file
containing an Access Token, a Certificate, and a Private Key. We recommend opening the files in a Text
Editor and then copying and pasting the text as is, into the corresponding text boxes in our platform.
You can also use an existing API Key Name but note that in case you download an existing Key Name
that has already been used, the existing Access Token, Certificate, and Private Key will expire. In such a
case, you’ll have to replace the existing prerequisites in our platform with the updated ones.
Details
The API connector retrieves Campaign Performance data including Line and Ad level data. The data
includes Delivery, Conversion, and Video Measurements.
QA Your Data
Apple News platform allows you to see the performance of Ads that are associated with a single
Campaign in a single view. Our platform allows you to see the performance of Ads from different
Campaigns in a single view. Therefore, when comparing your data between the platforms make sure that
both of your views are filtered accordingly.
Apple Search Ads API Connector
The Apple Search Ads API retrieves data related to Search Campaigns advertised on Apple App Store. By
using this API, you’ll be able to retrieve standard Display data. The Dimensions and Measurements that
are pulled through the API match the data that’s displayed on each of the Ad Groups views within the
Apple Search UI.
•
Channel: Search
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
A valid Apple user with admin level permissions to an Ads Account.

=== PAGE 196 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Extended Properties
•
Available Breakdowns – The Breakdowns that are available in the API match the options that are
available on the left menu under the ‘Reports’ view in the Apple Search UI.
Known Issues
•
Search Keyword data is currently unavailable.
•
When a Breakdown is selected, Delivery Data might be missing for rows that are below Apple's privacy
threshold. For example, Search Terms must reach at least 10 Impressions, otherwise data won’t
appear in the reports and won’t be retrieved by the API connector. Media Cost Value is returned
regardless of Delivery Data volume.
AppsFlyer API Connector
The AppsFlyer API connector retrieves App Analytics data. Through our API, you can retrieve the data
that’s available in the Geo Daily Report in the AppsFlyer Reports section. The report includes Campaigns
data, broken down by Countries and Media Sources.
The Total Conversions measurement represents the App Installs data. Currently, we provide an
aggregated report only, therefore Event and User level data isn’t available through the API.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
•
Valid Account owner API Key – Contact your AppsFlyer representative in order to receive your Key
details.
•
App ID – The App ID represents the App for which you’re retrieving data, and can be copied from the
Apps list in AppsFlyer, or from the URL in a specific App View. App ID should be in the format
idxxxxxxxxxx .
Known Issues
AppFlyer applies API rate limit at both the Account level and the App level. We recommend that you
process AppsFlyer Data Streams gradually whenever changes are made and re-creations are required.
See AppsFlyer Help Center: Report generation quotas (rate limitations).
Automation Studio for Exporting Data from Marketing Cloud
When you’re unable to use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence API to grab granular data or a specific
dimension and then connect that data to a Granular Data Center, try Automation Studio. With
192

=== PAGE 197 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Automation Studio (a Marketing Cloud app) you can extract the granularity of data you need so you can
analyze your data with ease.
You must have a Marketing Cloud account to access Automation Studio.
•
Channel: Social, Competitive
For more information, see Automation Studio
To use Automation Studio:
11.. Log into Marketing Cloud.
aa.. Make sure you have an FTP user enabled in your account. Go to Settings > Setup > Data
Management > FTP Accounts. If you don’t have an FTP user, then add one.
22.. Create a Data Extension
In this step you need to create a data extension that is a table containing your data. There are
predefined Data Views you can use to help get a data extension.
aa.. Go to Audience Builder > Contact Builder >Create.
bb.. Name your data extension.
cc.. Add the attributes: Measurements and Dimensions.
dd.. After the data extension is created, copy the “External Key” (the value you’ll get after you’ve
completed the previous step).
33.. Create an Automation Activity
aa.. Go to Journey Builder > Automation Studio > New Automation to set-up the export of the desired
data extension to the FTP.
bb.. Select the following activities: “Schedule”, “SQL Query”, “Data Extract”, and “File Transfer”.
cc.. Define the schedule options (hourly, daily, weekly... timezone).
dd.. Refresh the data extension information.
ee.. Extract the data to a secure folder that isn’t accessible to anybody.
ff.. Transfer this file from this folder to the FTP.
44.. Define the Schedule
55.. Configure the properties of the “SQL Query” Activity
aa.. Select Create New Query Activity
bb.. Enter a name and then click Next.
cc.. Create the Query.
dd.. Select your data extension then select the Overwrite radio button.
66.. Configure the Properties of the “Data Extract” Activity
aa.. Select Create New Data Extract Activity.
bb.. Enter a Name.
cc.. Select the file naming pattern and make sure you add a valid file format to the end (valid formats:
csv or txt).
dd.. From the Extract Type dropdown list, select Data Extension Extract. Make sure you copy the File
Naming Pattern as you’ll need it in the next step.
ee.. Add the file delimiter and then select the Headers checkbox and add the “DECustomer
Key”/“External Key” (this is the key you copied earlier).
77.. Configure the Properties of the “File Transfer” Activity
193

=== PAGE 198 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
aa.. Select Create New File Transfer Activity.
bb.. Enter a Name.
cc.. In the File Action area, select the Move a File From Safehouse radio button. This is the internal
Marketing Cloud folder.
dd.. Enter the same File Pattern Name.
At this stage you have finished configuring Automation Studio. Now you have to configure the Data
Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
88.. Configure the Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
aa.. Go to Connect & Mix > Data Streams.
bb.. Select your Data Stream and edit it.
Continue to configure your Data Stream with all the parameters you defined in steps 1–6:
cc.. Define the Host/Port (this is the Marketing Cloud FTP URL).
dd.. Enter your Credentials (your User and Password.)
ee.. Set the Directory as: /export/ (this is the folder where Automation Studio generates files to).
ff.. Enter the file name you defined in step 6 in the File name wildcard match.

corresponds to what you see in Intelligence when the same filtering is applied.

Create a Bing Ads Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To track and view your Pay-Per-Click advertising on Bing and Yahoo!, create a Bing Ads data stream. The
sliding window for the Bing Ads API connector is 3.

USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an API data stream: Admin
To create a Bing Ads API data stream, you need a valid Bing username and password.
11.. On the Connect and Mix tab, expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the Bing Ads connector.
44.. In the General section, enter your credentials and select the profile that you want to pull data for.

77.. In the Extended Properties section, you can set these properties:
aa.. To add breakdowns for increased granularity, from the Location Breakdown dropdown, select the
breakdown. You can select only one type of location breakdown for each Bing Ads API data stream.
Each selection pulls the previous listed dataset. For example, if you select Region, the data stream
retrieves Country and Region, and if you select DMA/Metro, the data stream retrieves Country,
Region, and DMA/Metro. If you select a location breakdown, Ad and Keyword data isn’t pulled.

- In CallRail go to Reports > Acquisition > Calls by landing page then define your date range.
•
All fields retrieved are unmapped by default. After you save the data stream you’ll be directed to the
mapping screen. Map according to usage, for example - the connector retrieves UTM fields, however, if
these don't match your use case then there's no need to map them.
•
The data stream type you choose should correspond to the result you wish to achieve. If you wish to
analyze your calls data alone, map the data to the Generic data stream type. If you wish to view the
data along side your Paid Search data you can either map it to the Generic Data Stream type and use
Data Fusion to match it with the Paid Search data, or you can use the Conversions Data Stream type
and map the data using Custom Measurements.
•
The Date in CallRail data streams is the call date.
•
There are two call measurements - 'Total Calls' and 'Calls'.
- Total Calls - Displays all the calls made from a number in a LIFETIME format. To view this
measurement it’s best to either map it as a Dimension (with 'Update Entities Attributes' enabled) or
map it as a Measurement and display it for a specific day and a specific number. This Measurement
shouldn’t be viewed aggregated.
- Calls - The basic summable measurement.
Extended Properties
•
Get Caller Details - This checkbox is unchecked by default. After it is checked it retrieves personal data.

View Percentage type Measurements in a Media Buy granularity (or Creative, if you choose to retrieve
data at the Creative level instead of the placement). Beyond that level the Measurement won’t
aggregate correctly. The total value of these type of measurements will not match the value in the
source platform. If you want to view these measurements on other levels other than the Media Buy/
Creative level, create them as Calculated Measurements in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.

Details
•
API service in Coosto is a paid service, that can be purchased complementary to a Coosto Enterprise
license. For the API connection to work, you must have a paying account in Coosto.
•
There are 2 payment plans according to the number of requests available per 10 minutes, a request is
translated into a query in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. For each query, data is retrieved for the last 30
days.
•
Custom Measurements are used in the Coosto connector's mapping:
- Social Page Custom Measurement 10 is mapped to the 'Post' Measurement in Coosto
- Social Page Custom Measurement 11 is mapped to the 'Sentiment' Measurement in Coosto
•
You should change these measurements’ Display name for a more convenient use and display.
•
For the Sentiment Measurement mentioned previously to aggregate correctly, its aggregation method
should be changed to average. This is done in the measurements sub tab, in the Aggregation column.
•
If Social Page Custom Measurements 10 & 11 are already mapped in the Workspace, note that the
changes to their display name and aggregation method affects all their appearances in the Workspace.
If this is the case map the 'Post' and 'Sentiment' Measurements to a different custom measurement,
via manual mapping.

Data 360 CI Connector
Data 360 allows you to unify all your data - from anywhere - for a complete view of your customers. The
Data 360 API connector allows you to pull either your calculated insights from Data 360 or use Marketing
Cloud Intelligence predefined insights in order to get advanced insights with a click of a button.
Note A new version of the Data 360 connector was released. This version may appear if you
previously connected to Data 360 either via the connector or the app. If you connected via the
connector, you will still see the connector in the Connect & Mix tab. It’s advised you use the new
version to avoid discrepancies.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type: LiteConnect
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Log in to Data 360, and then allow access to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
11.. Log in to Data 360 as an Admin user.
22.. From Setup, click OAuth custom scopes.
33.. Create two custom scopes named
cdpquery
and
205

=== PAGE 210 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
cdpprofile.
44.. If you see a “This site can’t be reached” message, then you need to return to the setup screen for the
org you’re logged into.
55.. Click Allow.
66.. Navigate to Salesforce Setup.
77.. From Setup, under App manager, clickOAuth and OpenID Connect Settings and then make sure
Allow Agent-Session Linking is off.
88.. From Setup, under App manager, click Manage OAuth Custom Scopes in Customer Data Cloud.
99.. Click Install next to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
1100.. Click Install to confirm.
Details
The connector pulls data from Data 360 in two forms:
•
Calculated Insights (CI) - After you enter your credentials, the “Source” dropdown populates with all
your calculated insights from Data 360. Select the calculated insights you wish to pull into Marketing
Cloud Intelligence. The data stream automatically builds the CI schema and pulls all of the CI’s data
into Intelligence.
•
Predefined Insights- Automatically create interesting views on top of your data, for the following 3
predefined insights:
- Demographics - The demographics predefined insight provides information on your audience by
different attributes.
- Fields - Day, Age Group, Children Count, Yearly Income Range, Country, City, Count of Individuals,
and Count of Unified Individuals.
- Engagement - The engagement predefined insight pulls different KPIs of your engagement data
model objects, like Email, Device Application and Message.
- Fields - Day, Channel, Campaign, Total Engagements (broken by engagement type), and Total
Engaging Customers (broken by engagement type).
- Conversion - The conversion predefined insight presents aggregated information on orders made.
- Fields - Day, Product, Product Category, Total Number of Orders, Total Revenue, and Number of
customers.
General Setup
11.. After entering your credentials, the Source dropdown is populated with the predefined insights and
calculated insights.
22.. Select the CI/Predefined insight you’d like to create the data stream with. There can be a delay of up
to a few hours from the time calculated insights are created in the Customer Data Platform until the
data becomes available in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
33.. In the Initial Data Fetch section, select the dates for which you want to pull data. You can only process
from the last 180 days. If you must process older data then contact Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Support.

=== PAGE 211 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
••
If you select a Demographics Predefined Insight or a Calculated Insight, Intelligence automatically
pulls all available data, regardless of the selected date range.
••
If you choose Engagement/Conversion Predefined Insights, Intelligence pulls data according to the
selected date range.
Notes
•
Not available in Government Cloud. See Data 360 edition availability
•
The Data Cloud API Connector allows you to view the data in your Calculated Insights and include
data in your Intelligence dashboards and reports. You can’t map the data retrieved from Data 360 to
your Intelligence data model.
•
To ensure that deleted Data 360 records are also deleted from Marketing Cloud Intelligence, make
sure that your data stream load mode is set to Total Replace. This setting ensures the replacement of
all data coming from Data 360 on a daily basis. If you choose to set your data stream load to a
different mode, deletions aren't automatically passed across apps, and you must manually delete any
data that you would like deleted from Intelligence using the Delete Data functionality in Intelligence.
•
Predefined Insights are complicated queries that scan large amounts of data. In case the data stream
process fails due to a timeout, you can create a similar calculated insight, which will do the processing
in advance, and pull the CI into Intelligence via the connector.
QA Your Data
Use the Data Explorer in Data 360 to compare the data in your calculated insights and the data pulled
into Marketing Cloud Intelligence.

Facebook Ads API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Facebook Ads is a platform on which users can purchase and manage advertising space on Facebook.
Facebook Ads is a Display channel connector. The data stream type is Ads and Conversion Tag, and data
lagging is 1 day.
Additional information about the Facebook Ads connector:
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence retrieves the reach measurements, Reach and Social Reach, from
Facebook as part of the Facebook Ads API. It’s best practice to use Facebook Ads Custom to retrieve
unique measurements because the values in Marketing Cloud Intelligence might not match the
aggregation seen in the Facebook Ads manager platform.
•
Intelligence maps the Facebook Ads Measurements 3-Second Video Views to the Measurement Video
Views. Video Views is the sum of 3-Second Video Views [1 Day After Viewing] and 3-Second Video
Views [7 Days After Clicking].
•
Data is retrieved in the time zone set in Facebook.
Create a Facebook Ads Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To pull data and create reports for Facebook ads, create a Facebook Ads data stream.
Define Reach Data in Facebook Ads Custom API Connector
208

=== PAGE 213 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Define how to calculate reach data in the Facebook Ads custom API. Facebook uses user-level data to
calculate reach. When a date or field is adjusted in Facebook, the granularity changes, and Facebook
recalculates the reach value based on the new parameters. Processing historical reach data streams
requires unique date ranges for each process. Set a unique reach value for each day based on the
granularity. Every date must be processed separately to return the aggregated value for that date.
Mapping Reach Data for Facebook Ads Custom API in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To report reach data correctly, you must map the data correctly. Consider these best practices for
optimal results.
Set Up Age and Gender Breakdowns for Reach in Facebook Ads Custom Data Streams
To retrieve Age and Gender breakdowns properly for reach data streams you must add a formula in
the mapping.
Troubleshooting Reach Data for Facebook Ads Custom
After you set up reach, ensure that all parameters in the Facebook platform match the parameters and
granularities set in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.

Define Reach Data in Facebook Ads Custom API Connector
Define how to calculate reach data in the Facebook Ads custom API. Facebook uses user-level data to
calculate reach. When a date or field is adjusted in Facebook, the granularity changes, and Facebook
recalculates the reach value based on the new parameters. Processing historical reach data streams
requires unique date ranges for each process. Set a unique reach value for each day based on the
granularity. Every date must be processed separately to return the aggregated value for that date.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and edit an API data stream: Admin
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the Facebook Ads Custom connector.
44.. Under Extended Properties:
aa.. Set Date Resolution to Aggregated.
bb.. For the level of granularity that you want to report on, select Dimensions.
cc.. Don’t edit Measurements. Reach measurements are automatically populated.
dd.. Select the breakdowns that you want to include in the report, for example, Age and Gender.
55.. Under the Advanced section:
aa.. In the Data Refresh window, click the edit icon and set the frequency.
bb.. Set the maximum number of days a single processing statistic can span. For example, for a 30-day
retrieval and a 10-day span, a total of three statistics are created.
cc.. Set the sliding window —the number of days back that data is retrieved on a scheduled run starting
yesterday.
Mapping Reach Data for Facebook Ads Custom API in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To report reach data correctly, you must map the data correctly. Consider these best practices for optimal
results.
•
Set up an individual data stream for each granularity of reach that you want to report on.
•
Don’t include non-reach measurements because they duplicate the data.
•
Don’t map frequency because it appears incorrectly at an aggregated level. Frequency data is at the
user level and can’t be pulled at an aggregated level. To calculate frequency, create a calculated
measurement outside of the mapping for any granularity of reach that you set up. For example,
Impressions/Reach.
•
Always select lifetime as the aggregation function.
•
Map reach to a delivery custom metric that you aren’t using to easily know which granularity of reach
211

=== PAGE 216 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
you’re reporting.
Set Up Age and Gender Breakdowns for Reach in Facebook Ads Custom Data Streams
To retrieve Age and Gender breakdowns properly for reach data streams you must add a formula in the
mapping.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and edit an API data stream: Admin
11.. In the Connect & Mix tab, select your Facebook Ads custom data stream.
22.. On the Mapping page, click Strategy key or Exchange key.
Make sure the keys are not being used elsewhere in your setup.
33.. Click the Add Formula icon, and enter: csv['fb.age']+csv['fb.gender']
44.. Click Save.
55.. To map Age, select a key custom attribute, click the Add formula icon and enter: csv['fb.age'] .
66.. Click Save.
77.. To map Gender, select a key custom attribute, click the Add formula icon and enter:
csv['fb.gender']
88.. Click Save.
Troubleshooting Reach Data for Facebook Ads Custom
After you set up reach, ensure that all parameters in the Facebook platform match the parameters and
granularities set in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Date Range—Facebook reach is the number of unique users reached based on the parameters that
you set. When comparing the data, select the correct parameters in Facebook. For example, if you set
up a 31-day sliding window in Intelligence, select the 31 days before this date in Facebook. For
example, if you select April 2, select 31 days before—March 2 through April 2. Facebook displays how
many people you’ve reached by April 2, counting 31 days back.
•
Dimensions & Granularities —Select the data stream granularity to match the granularity set in
Facebook. For example, if you set up a data stream to report reach at a campaign level, select
campaign in Facebook.

Facebook Ads Custom API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use the Facebook Ads Custom API connector to retrieve a custom set of measurements and dimensions
from the Facebook Ads API into Marketing Cloud Intelligence. The Facebook Ads Custom API connector
facilitates the retrieval of unique measurements, such as Reach. These measurements cannot be
aggregated. This custom connector requires manual setup. The Facebook ads custom is a display channel
connector, the data stream type is ads, conversion tag, and generic. The data lagging is one day.
Additional Information for the Facebook Ads Custom Connector:
•
This API connector can also be used when creating a Granular Data Center.
•
Data is retrieved in the timezone set in Facebook.
•
Not all the attributes for all entities are available through the Facebook Ads Custom API connector, for
example, img_url.
•
The sliding window for the Facebook Ads Custom connector is 10
Create a Facebook Ads Custom Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To create a Facebook ads custom report, create a Facebook ads custom data stream.

=== PAGE 218 ===

Create a Facebook Ads Custom Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To create a Facebook ads custom report, create a Facebook ads custom data stream.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an API data stream: Admin
To create a Facebook Ads Custom API data stream, you need a valid Facebook user account with ‘View’
or ‘Manage’ access to the Ads Account.
11.. On the Connect and Mix tab, expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the Facebook Ads Custom connector.
44.. In the General section, enter your credentials, and select the account that you want to pull data for.
55.. To add a data source authentication, click Add New and enter your Facebook credentials.
66.. To add a template, select a template from the dropdown menu.
77.. Set the time period for the initial data fetch. You can process data up to the past 180 days.
88.. In the Extended Properties section, you can set these properties:
aa.. Select the data stream type you want to map your data to. Only one data stream type is selected for
a Facebook Ads custom API data stream.
bb.. Set the granularity from the Level dropdown. The level setting determines the lowest level of
granularity you want to retrieve. (Granularity affects the measurement values, in relation to the
Facebook Ads manager view.)
cc.. Set the Date Resolution granularity. When selecting Aggregated, the data is retrieved without the
Day dimension, so add a date in the mapping screen.
dd.. Select the custom dimensions you want to retrieve, separated by a comma. For more information
on the syntax and limitations, see Meta Developer Documentation: Insights API.
Note The lowest level of granularity is set in the Level section, and any custom dimensions
added on a lower level of granularity won’t affect the data. For example, if you set Campaign-
Level as the lowest level of granularity, adding Ads attributes to the list of dimensions won’t
have any effect, since the measurements retrieved are on the campaign level.
ee.. Select the custom measurements you want to retrieve, separated by a comma. Some fields indicate
that there are a group of measurements listed under one name. For example, actions. The
measurements are listed in the list link.
To retrieve specific measurements from a group, place the names inside curly brackets ({}) after the
group name and separated by a semicolon (;).
To retrieve all the available data for the specified measurement group, omit the curly brackets.
Select at least one dimension and any related dimensions to the selected measurements. For
example, if you’re retrieving the Cost or Revenue measurements, add the currency dimension so the
currency measurements are converted correctly.

If you want to retrieve offline purchases, you can use the following syntax:
"actions{offline_conversion.purchase}"
ff.. When retrieving conversion and revenue measurements, the API pulls data for 1 day, 7 day and 28

attribution window you set in Facebook Ads.
gg.. To add a breakdown, enter the breakdown fields you want to apply to the data. For example, to see

information see the Facebook Developer page Combining Breakdowns.
hh.. To apply an Action Breakdown to your data, for example: action_type, enter the action breakdown.

in the measurements section.

jj.. To retrieve all archived entities in Facebook Ads, select Get Archived Entities (Disabled with Adset

Conversion metrics in Marketing Cloud Intelligence are transposed. For example, in Facebook Ads
platform, there is a measurement for each conversion type: Lead, Purchase, View Content. In
Marketing Cloud Intelligence, to align with the Data Model and other platforms, the type of the
conversion is returned as a value of a Conversion Tag Category Dimension, while the metric values
of all conversions are available in the Total Conversions metric.
There isn’t a view on the Facebook platform that matches the Conversions in the Conversion Date
report. To quality-check your data, compare the totals of Entities from Campaigns that have ended.
Facebook Page Insights API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use Facebook Page Insights to track user interaction on your Facebook Fan Page. Facebook Insights is
seen by all the Admins of your page to help you track the number of active users so you can better
understand your page performance. Facebook Page Insights is an organic social channel connector, the
data stream type is social objects, and the data lagging is three days.
215

=== PAGE 220 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Additional Information for the Facebook Page Insights Connector
•
Retrieving many pages results in a large amount of data, and can cause a timeout. If a timeout occurs,
reduce the number of pages per data stream.
•
Facebook Page Insight's has limitations, and you could see discrepancies in your data. For more
information on the limitations See the Facebook Developer Insights page.
•
Facebook tends to change the lagging days. If you notice missing metrics, we recommend waiting a
few days.
•
Discrepancies could occur in the "Fans(Lifetime)" Measurement when the Data Stream is set to
retrieve location breakdown. The discrepancies occur when a fan was attributed to a country, and is
now attributed to a different country.
•
If you can’t see all of the pages you manage, complete these steps in your Facebook user Settings:
- Select Business Integrations.
- Next, under Active Business Integrations, select Datorama Landing Zone.
- Under Show a list of the Pages you manage, select all the pages you want to appear.
- Save your selections.

Facebook Post Insights API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use the Facebook post insight connector to understand data on how users engage with your Facebook
post so you can make informed decisions on the content you post. Facebook post insights is social
channel connector, the data stream type and there’s no data lagging. Facebook post insights pulls data
from Graph API, and Insights.
Additional Information on Facebook Post Insights
•
The connector shows Lifetime type measurements. Lifetime measurements display the accumulated
value of the measurement for the current date.
•
When a Facebook data stream is created, all posts from the creation date of your Facebook profile are
retrieved.
•
Data streams in Lifetime aggregation do not allow for any failed retrievals to be reprocessed after a
failure.
•
Entries in the data stream log appear under the processing date of the extraction date (‘Today’).
•
There’s no option to select a specific date when processing the data stream. All data for your posts is
retrieved up until the processing date and assigned a date value that is identical to the day of
processing.
•
Measurement data retrieved for all posts is accurate as of the processing date.
•
After data is deleted from a Facebook data stream, the deleted data is not accessible for retrieval
through reprocessing.
•
To reprocess data that was retrieved in the past without deleting any data, use the Re-Run button in
the log in the data stream management menu.
Create a Facebook Post Insights Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To track and view Facebook Post insights, create a Facebook Post Insights data stream.
