Gartner API Connector
Gartner is a global research and advisory firm providing information, advice, and tools for leaders in IT,
finance, HR, customer service and support, communications, legal and compliance, marketing, sales, and
supply chain functions.
•
Channel: Search
•
Data Stream Type: Search Keywords
219

=== PAGE 224 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Prerequisites
In order to connect to Gartner connector, you need a valid username, password, and API key. Contact
your Gartner representative to receive an API key.
General Setup
After entering your credentials, select the relevant profile you want to retrieve data for. You can then
select the channel you want to retrieve data for or leave the Channel box empty to retrieve data from all
channels.
QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the Gartner platform for the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.
Google Ads API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use the Google Ads API connector to retrieve a predefined set of dimensions and measurements from
the Google Ads API. Google Ads is a display and search channel connector. The data stream type is Ads,
and the data lagging is one day.
Additional information on the Google ads API connector:
•
When Get Keywords is selected, conversions in Marketing Cloud Intelligence can show a higher value
than the Google Ads platform. Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls the Display Network and Search
Network keywords, whereas the reports in Google Ads include only the Search Network keyword as
part of the keyword breakdown.
Note Display Network keywords receive a Google Display Network site name.
•
The connector retrieves paused and inactive fields. It doesn’t retrieve deleted fields.

Google Ads Custom API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use the Google Ads Custom API connector to retrieve a custom set of measurements and dimensions.
You can also use the Google Ads Custom connector when creating a Granular Data Center. Google Ads
Custom is a display and search channel connector, and the data lagging is one day.
Additional information on the Google Ads Custom API Connector:
•
The Google Ads Custom connector can also be used when creating a Granular Data Center.
•
The connector requires manual setup.
•
When retrieving all Search Impressions Share measurements, Marketing Cloud Intelligence treats
values, such as greater than (>) and less than (<), as a single number, to support aggregation. For
example, > 90% is treated as 90%, and <10% is 10%. Configure these special values differently in
the mapping section.
•
Deleted fields can’t be retrieved. Paused and inactive fields can be retrieved.
•
The sliding window for the Google Ads Custom API connector is 7.
•
To get weekly and monthly aggregation for unique measurements, such as Reach, contact your
Marketing Cloud Intelligence success manager.

To create and manage Google Ads reports, create a Google ads data stream.

Google Analytics API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Google Analytics is a web analytics service offered by Google that tracks and reports website traffic.
Google Analytics is a web analytics channel connector, the data stream type is web analytics, and the
data lag is one day.
Additional information for the Google Analytics connector
•
When the number of sessions exceeds 500,000, Google Analytics bases its reports on a sample of the
data. For maximum accuracy, Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses Higher Precision settings. Maximum
sample size provides the most precise representation of your data set when retrieving data from
Google Analytics.
•
This API pulls in data at the site level by default. For more detailed data, use Extended Properties.

Add Commerce Fields to Google Analytics Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Add commerce fields to your Google Analytics connector to view Product and Order level
data—displaying engagement and performance data alongside analytics data. With this view you can
learn more about your commerce marketing spend and generated revenue.

Add commerce fields to your Google Analytics connector to view Product and Order level
data—displaying engagement and performance data alongside analytics data. With this view you can
learn more about your commerce marketing spend and generated revenue.
The ecommerce fields are added to your Google Analytics mapping. The campaign and order ID are
connected in Google Analytics, allowing you to view a direct correlation of marketing spend to revenue
generated.
To retrieve ecommerce product and order level data, select Get Product Level Data in the Extended
Properties section of the Google Analytics connector.

Google Analytics Custom API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use the Google Analytics Custom API connector to retrieve custom dimensions and metrics. Use custom
dimensions to collect and analyze data that Google Analytics doesn’t automatically track. You can
combine Analytics data with non-Analytics data using the custom connector. Google Analytics Custom is
a web analytics channel connector, and the data lagging is one day.
Additional information on the Google Analytics Custom connector:
•
Google Analytics Custom supports Google Analytics 4 properties.
•
You can use theAPI connector when creating a Granular Data Center.
•
When the number of sessions exceeds 500,000, Google Analytics bases its reports on a sample of the
data. For maximum accuracy, Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses Higher Precision settings when
retrieving data from Google Analytics.
•
The Google Analytics Custom connector doesn’t support data with transformers, which affects its
ability to retrieve goals data. This type of data requires transposing because the goal dimension
(referred to in the platform as tag/goal) isn’t available in the core API. Goal data in the regular Google
Analytics connector is retrieved using a series of actions that are applied in the background, including
accessing a source in addition to the Google Analytics API. These actions can’t be executed as part of
228

=== PAGE 233 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
the custom connector. Instead, you can use a scheduled report from Google Analytics that contains
the data you need and upload it using the TotalConnect connector.
•
According to the specifications of the Google Analytics API, the Google Analytics Custom connector
can bring in a maximum of seven dimensions and nine measurements.
Create a Google Analytics Custom Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To create and manage Google Analytics custom reports, create a Google Analytics custom data stream.

To create and manage Google Analytics custom reports, create a Google Analytics custom data stream.

You can retrieve up to seven dimensions and nine measurements per data stream connection.
You must be able to query the dimensions and measurements that you’re retrieving together. To
confirm that you can query them together, go to the GA API Explorer. Select the dimensions and
measurements that you want to retrieve one at a time. If you can select all of them, they can be
queried together.

Google Ads Manager API Connector
Google Ad Manager, formerly known as DoubleClick for Publishers (DFP).
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Details
The best practice is to map Google Ads Manager data to a workspace that is dedicated specifically to
publisher data. Avoid mapping sell-side data, and buy-side data in the same Workspace. Mapping sell-
side data in a different Workspace allows you to apply specific settings to that type of data only, for
example, publisher terminology (cost to revenue, media buy to placement, etc.)
Extended Properties
•
Country Breakdown - After the Country breakdown is chosen, creative and video data won’t be pulled
from Google Ads Manager.
•
Browser Breakdown - After the Browser breakdown is chosen, creative and video data won't be pulled
from Google Ads Manager.
•
Get Creative Level Data - This retrieves creative data from Google Ads Manager as long as the Get
Conversions Data checkbox is selected, it includes the following fields: Creative Click URL, Creative
Format, Creative Key, Creative Name, Creative Size.
•
Get Conversions Data - This retrieves conversion data from DFP, it includes the following fields:
Conversion Tag Category, Conversion Tag Key, Conversion Tag Name, Post Click Conversions, Post Click
Revenue, Post Impression Conversions, Post Impression Revenue, Total Conversions.
•
Get Rich Media Metrics - This retrieves Rich Media Metrics from Google Ads Manager it includes the
following fields: Rich Media Backup Images, Rich Media Display Time, Rich Media Expansion Time,
Rich Media Full Screen Impressions, Rich Media Interactive Impressions.

Google Ad Manager Custom, formerly known as DoubleClick for Publishers (DFP). Use the Google Ad

Manager custom API connector to create the dimension and metric combinations that work best for

Google Analytics Attribution API Connector
The Google Analytics Attribution tool is available on the Google Analytics platform. It enables a view of
the conversion path a client goes through before the conversion takes place. Tracking starting in the
client's first interest until the conversion takes place, across different marketing channels.
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Prerequisites
Google Analytics credentials
General Setup
The General, Advanced, and Data Stream Custom Attributes sections setup is the same as other API
connectors.
Extended Properties
Under the Dimensions and Measurements fields enter the Google Analytics fields you wish to retrieve.
Separate the fields separated by a comma ',' and match the syntax specified in the GA API Explorer. You
can retrieve only 7 Dimensions per Data Stream connection. According to the fields chosen, select the
appropriate data stream type, you can choose either one of the Web Analytics data stream types or an
existing generic data stream type. You can’t create a new generic data stream type while setting up the
connector.

Google Campaign Manager Custom API Connector
Google Campaign Manager Custom, is a web-based ad management system for advertisers and
agencies, which helps you manage digital campaigns across websites and mobile platforms.
Google Campaign Manager Custom API connector allows you to retrieve a custom set of Measurements
and Dimensions from the Google Campaign Manager API into Marketing Cloud Intelligence. The Google
Campaign Manager Custom API connector enables the retrieval of Measurements that aren’t available
through the standard API, such as fields from the Floodlight Report, Landing Page URL, Dynamic
Elements, etc.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
- Conversion Tag with Keywords
- Generic
- Search Keywords
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Details
•
Removed entities aren’t retrieved by the API.
•
Multiple Advertisers are retrieved in a single Data Stream.
•
If you’re defining a Reach report you must select the Advertiser Dimension (both in the Google
platform and Intelligence API).
•
Due to limitations on the provider’s end, Google Campaign Manager Floodlight Reports can only be
retrieved up to 62 days back.
•
If you’re defining a Floodlight report you must make sure you have a Floodlight CID, see the following
for more information.
•
To create a Dimension only Data Stream, you must do the following: select at least one Measurement
in the Fields section under the General section and select the Only Update Dimensions checkbox
under the Advanced Settings section. Otherwise, if only Dimensions are selected without a
Measurement and without checking the checkbox, the Data Stream will fail.
•
This connector doesn’t support fetching floodlight variables. If you require floodlight variable
consumption use a TotalConnect data stream instead. For more information, see Create a Data Stream
with TotalConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
235

=== PAGE 240 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
General Setup
After an Account is selected, choose the Report Type you want to retrieve. The following Report Types are
available:
•
Floodlight
- If your Floodlight ID isn’t shared between different advertisers, then you must make sure your
Floodlight CID is the same as the Advertiser's ID to avoid any issues. For more information about
Floodlight IDs and configurations, visit Google Support.
•
Reach
•
Standard
After you’ve selected the relevant Report Type, select the fields you want to retrieve from the dropdown.
Extended Properties
•
Data Stream Type - Select one of the available Data Stream Types you want to map your data to. You
can only select one type of Data Stream Type in any given Google Campaign Manager Custom API
Data Stream. You can map into the following Data Stream Types:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
- Conversion Tag with Keywords
- Generic
- Search Keywords
•
Advertisers - Insert the Advertiser IDs you want to retrieve, separated by a comma. If you want to
retrieve all Advertisers, then leave this box empty. Note: Retrieving many Advertisers could create a
large amount of data and cause a timeout. If this happens, reduce the number of Advertisers per data
stream.
•
Floodlight Configuration ID - Insert the Floodlight ID for which you want to retrieve data. This field is
mandatory if you want to retrieve the Floodlight report.
Known Issues
•
Because the Floodlight Report is a large report, it can take longer to process. Selecting many fields or a
larger date-range can result in the query timing out and displaying an error such as: "Provider Report
Timeout in the Data Stream Log".
•
If you have large amounts of data, break it down into smaller retrievals, either by selecting smaller
groups of Dimensions or by reducing the Statistic Span days. Contact Support if you want to
implement this.
How to QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the Google Campaign Manager platform for the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.

Google Display and Video 360 API Connector
Google Display & Video 360 is a Demand-Side-Platform (DSP) providing global Display Media Buying
across Ad Exchanges. Google Display & Video 360 (DV360) was formerly known as DoubleClick Bid
Manager (DBM).
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type: Ads, Conversion Tag
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Prerequisites
To connect to Display & Video 360, you need a valid DV360 username and password with at least ‘Read
Only’ permissions or higher.
Details
Note The API connector retrieves only campaigns that have associated creative data. Campaigns
without creative data are not included.
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data from the ‘General Report’ in DV360.
•
This connector doesn’t retrieve Display & Video 360 Trueview data. Intelligence retrieves all available
Cost Measurements, however, only the Revenue Measurement is automatically mapped, the
remaining Measurements must be mapped manually.
•
Cost Measurements are retrieved by default according to the advertiser’s currency and not according
to the partner’s currency. You can retrieve Cost Measurements in the partner’s currency by selecting
the relevant checkbox in the Extended Properties
General Setup
After entering your credentials, select the relevant Partner you want to retrieve data for.
•
To retrieve all Advertisers, leave the Advertiser box empty.
•
If you want to retrieve data for a specific Partner or Advertiser then select the relevant Partner or
Advertiser from the respective list. If either list doesn’t populate automatically, or you can’t find the
Partner or Advertiser that you want to retrieve data for, enter the Partner and/or Advertiser IDs into
the Override Partner Id and/or Override Advertiser Ids section under Extended Properties.

•

Override Advertisers Ids - Enter the Advertiser Ids you want to pull data for. Use this section if the

data for under the General Settings section. (see General Setup). Insert multiple Advertisers IDs by
separating them with a comma.

added Dimensions.

partner’s currency in addition to the advertiser’s currency. These Measurements are unmapped by
default. You can map them using Manual Mapping. The following Cost Measurements are added:
- partnerCurrency

- totalMediaCostPartnerCurrency

- Conversions Total

- inventorySourceType

added to the Ads Verification Data Stream Type:
- Active View: Viewable Impressions
- Active View: Measurable Impressions
•
Get Video Metrics - Select this checkbox to retrieve video data. The following fields are added to the

- First-Quartile Views (Video)

Google My Business API Connector
The Google My Business API connector retrieves data related to listings of businesses that are advertised
on Google.
By using this API, you are able to retrieve both your Business details, including your business’ name,
photos, phone number and more, and your listings performance - such as the following actions that
were made, including calls, driving directions requests and more.
Note According to a recent update from Google, their API now provides a more precise
representation of data, showcasing solely the number of unique impressions and engagements.
This can cause a discrepancy in your data.

•
There can be a discrepancy in Total values when querying the Top Results entity. This is because the
Google Search Console only displays 1000 results in their platform, whereas Marketing Cloud
Intelligence retrieves all results. The value per row will be identical, but the Total value could be
different.

aggregation) vs. the Page Report.

and ‘Device’ to the dataset.

- Page and Query - Display your data by both your page and query entities.
- Page - Display your data by your page entities.
- Query - Display your data by your query entities.
- None - Display your data not aggregated by any reporting entity.

How To QA Your Data
Whenever you are creating a report in the UI in order to compare it with the data in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence, be sure that you are using the same Dimensions as to what has been defined for the
relevant Data Stream in Intelligence.
11.. Click on ‘Reports’ to the left of the page
22.. Select any Report from the list (‘Daily Report’ and ‘Advertisers’ is recommended)
33.. Ensure all the relevant Dimensions and Measurements are selected and match the data you are
retrieving via the Has Offers Data Stream in Intelligence.
44.. Next create a Pivot Table in Intelligence that has the same Date Range.
55.. Compare the Measurement Totals between the Has Offers Report and the Intelligence Pivot Table -
applying relevant filters to both views and making sure the Totals still match.
Hubspot API Connector
Hubspot is a marketing automation tool, Marketing Cloud Intelligence currently supports only Email
Marketing data.
•
Channel: Email Marketing
Prerequisites
Hubspot Credentials
245

=== PAGE 250 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Details
•
Data is retrieved at the Campaign and Email Subject level
•
The measurements in Hubspot are all LIFETIME measurements
•
Since the report available in Hubspot displays only the most current data, you won’t be able to
reprocess past dates in Hubspot streams. Processing past date retrieves the most current data.
•
To validate your data go to 'Email' section under the 'Content' tab, then navigate to the 'Analyze'
section, scroll down to the second segment under 'Email Performance' and switch to the 'Campaigns'
view. You can validate the data at the overview level or look into a specific campaign.
•
Be aware when validating the data that having more than one Campaign with the same Email Subject
affects the data view.

Instagram by Facebook Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The Instagram by Facebook API connector retrieves lifetime post data from Instagram using Facebook’s
247

=== PAGE 252 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
graph API. This connector pulls a larger variety of measurements, such as organic impressions. Instagram
by Facebook is a social channel connector, the data stream type is social objects, and there’s no data lag.
Additional Information Regarding Instagram by Facebook
•
Only Instagram business accounts are supported, personal accounts can't be used.
•
The connector shows LIFETIME type measurements. LIFETIME measurements display the
accumulated value of the measurement as of the current date.
•
When an Instagram data stream is created, all posts from the creation date of your profile are
retrieved.
•
Entries in the data stream log appear under the extraction date’s processing date, which is listed as
Today.
•
When processing the data stream, there’s no date selection because data for all posts are retrieved
until today and assigned a date value that matches the processing day.
•
Measurement data retrieved for all posts is accurate as of the processing date.
•
To prevent potential data inaccuracies, data streams in LIFETIME aggregation don’t allow for any failed
retrievals to be reprocessed post-failure.
•
After data is deleted from an Instagram data stream, the deleted data isn’t accessible for retrieval
through reprocessing.
•
To reprocess data retrieved in the past without actually deleting any data, for example, to
accommodate changes in mapping, use the Re-Run Files option.
Create an Instagram by Facebook Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To create and manage Instagram reports, create an Instagram by Facebook data stream.
Create an Instagram by Facebook Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To create and manage Instagram reports, create an Instagram by Facebook data stream.

USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED

To create an Instagram by Facebook data stream you need a valid Facebook username and password and
Instagram and Facebook accounts linked to each other.

The API only reports organic interaction metrics—interactions on ads containing a media object are

stream.

Instagram Public API Connector
The Instagram Public (competitive) API allows you to get basic metadata and metrics for other
Instagram Business Accounts.
•
Channel: Social, Competitive
•
Data Stream Type: Competitive
Prerequisites
A valid Facebook Username and Password connected to a Facebook Page linked to an Instagram
Business Account. See Meta Developer Documentation: Instagram APIs.
Details
•
Not all fields retrieved via the API are mapped automatically. If you wish to include a certain field that
isn’t mapped by default, use Manual Mapping
•
All the Measurements in the Instagram Public API are aggregated as LIFETIME (LIFETIME aggregation
means that Measurements hold the total accumulated value up until the current date.)
•
Since this connector only retrieves the data for the date of retrieval, you can’t reprocess past dates in
Instagram Public Data Streams.

The Instagram API Connector can only retrieve data for Instagram Business Accounts and not private

The account’s profile includes the 'Email', 'Directions', or 'Call' buttons.

Integral Ad Science API Connector
IAS is an Ad Verification Vendor displaying Viewability, Ad Safety, and Ad Fraud data.

250

=== PAGE 255 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Extended Properties
•
Report Type - Select the IAS report you want to retrieve data for. You must have permission to generate
the report in the Excel Reports section in IAS for it to be available to you in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.
- The following reports are supported for Campaign Monitoring:
- 3MS Viewability - MRC mandated data set that uses measured impressions as the denominator,
providing clients with a tangible in-view number.
- Campaign Viewability - Report that shows the data behind the ability for a digital ad to be seen by
an actual consumer. Extrapolated report that uses data science to provide clients and with an in-
view percentage across all unblocked impressions. This report includes custom metrics to help
provide additional detail.
- Adsafe Monitoring - Brand Safety level reporting that reports back on Risk Level analysis of a
campaign and its ads.
- Country Compliance - Ads Tracked by Country Location in which they were served.
- DMA Compliance - Ads Tracked by Sub Region in the Country’s in which they were served. (Sub
Regions vary by Country. (that is, USA’s Sub Regions are States)).
- Device Type - Ads Tracked by device on which they were served (that is, Laptop/Desktop, Mobile,
Tablet).
- Traq Score - TRAQ (True Advertising Quality) score. TRAQ combines critical variables - Ad Fraud,
Viewability, Brand Safety, Ad Clutter, and Professionalism - to offer the most comprehensive
appraisal of media quality. You can target web pages based on scores ranging between 250 and
1,000, with higher scores meaning higher quality.
- Suspicious Activity - Suspicious Activity Report is an aggregated report that provides data on
suspicious imps, non-suspicious, their percentages, and the different types of bot impressions
seen per campaign for a given time period.
- Facebook Display Viewability - Detailed analysis for advertisers who want to see Facebook
campaign performance in terms of Display Viewability.
- Facebook Video Viewability - Detailed analysis for advertisers who want to see Facebook
campaign performance in terms of Video Viewability.
- Overview - A summary view of metrics from various detailed reports. Overview Report doesn’t
report on any new metrics, all metrics are from Fraud Report, Brand Safety Report, or Viewability
Report.
- Viewability - IAB and MRC reporting that shows a detailed analysis of data with the removal of
fraudulent data from ads that weren’t shown to a human due to bot activity, pixel stuffing, could
never be in view, etc.
- Brand Safety - Reporting that provides a detailed analysis of ads categorized by the 7 Brand Safety
Content categories.
- Ad Fraud - Reporting that provides a detailed analysis of any deliberate activity that prevents the
proper delivery of ads to the intended people, at the right time, and in the right place.
- The following reports are supported for Firewall:
- Facebook Display Viewability
- Facebook Video Viewability
- Overview
- Viewability
251

=== PAGE 256 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Brand Safety
- Ad Fraud
•
IAS Product - Select the IAS product you want to retrieve data for. The following products are
supported:
- Campaign Management
- Firewall Note that if the selected product doesn’t correspond to the team's settings, the Data Stream
processing will either fail or retrieve zero rows, depending on your user permissions.
Known Issues
•
The IAS vendor operates in US hours. US hours may not be compatible with your time zone processing
hour, which can lead to incomplete data for the previous day. The following run the next day should
retrieve the full data for the incomplete day.
•
Some ‘Firewall’ reports have been deprecated from both the IAS platform and API, therefore when
trying to process these deprecated Data Streams the following error message appears:
*PROVIDER_INTERNAL_ERROR,Message=The report isn’t supported in Firewall Product*
How to QA
•
To QA your data, generate a compatible report in the 'Excel Reports' section located under the
'Overview' menu.
•
Create the desired report in the platform for the required date range. The 'Cut Off' field should be set
to zero. It’s recommended that you select all the 'Delivery Environment' checkboxes and the
'Campaign' and 'Placement' checkboxes to ensure the view in IAS will be identical to the view in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Intelligence containing the exact same Dimensions
and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.

33.. Input your Organization ID and API Key into the Authentication box.
Setup Requirements
The Integrate connector is a report-based connector. Select a report from the ‘Report’ dropdown,
populated with report name values that are of actual reports already created in Integrate. You can find
the Reports in Integrate by navigating to Analytics > Reports
Extended Properties
Data Stream Type - Select the Data Stream Type into which the data will be mapped. You can map into
the following Data Stream Types:
•
Ads
•
CRM
•
CRM Leads
•
Conversion Tag
•
Generic
Details
The names of the Unmapped File Fields match the names in the report file you download from Integrate.
It isn’t compulsory to map all the available fields - you can map only the fields you need.
How to QA
•
Create the desired report in the Integrate UI for the required date range and then process the relevant
Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the exact same date range. Not all reports are subject
of date range - some provide lifetime data. For example, the Leads Report provides lifetime leads data.
In such instances follow the same steps, but note that no matter which date range you process, the
same data is retrieved.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.
•
To ensure that you have mapped correctly in terms of Entity relationships, filter the Integrate report for
a certain Dimension value and make sure that the list of associated Dimensions seen in the Integrate
report corresponds to that seen in Intelligence when the same filtering is applied. Under the above
filtering, make sure that the Measurement values are aligned as well.
Kenshoo API Connector
Kenshoo provides digital marketing solutions in Search, Social, and Display.
•
Channel: Search, Social, Display
253

=== PAGE 258 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Provide a valid username and password, and a Kenshoo Account ID (also known as KS ID or Server
Number).
The Server Number can be found within the URL string used to log into Kenshoo. For example, if your
URL string is KS1234.kenshoo.com, your Server Number is 1234. There are occurrences where Kenshoo
provides their customers with "masked URLs" (for example, name.kenshoo.com). In these situations
contact your Kenshoo representative to obtain your Server Number.
Setup
The Kenshoo connector is a report based connector meaning that upon set up you’ll need to select a
report from the ‘Report’ drop-down populated with report name values. The report name values will be
of actual reports already created on Kenshoo.
Next, under the ‘Extended Properties’ section, choose the Data Stream Type with which to map your
data, as well as enter all the Field Names (or Column Headers) separated by a comma, as they appear in
Kenshoo for the selected report.
To get a list of the field names for the above report quickly and accurately, download the report from the
Kenshoo UI, copy the column row, replace the tabs with a comma and paste it back in the above box.
When the date range is selected for the Initial Data Fetch (or in routine processing thereafter) Marketing
Cloud Intelligence will process the report in question for the relevant date ranges using the API.
How to Map
When in the mapping stream, there are two entities that to pay special attention to: Date, and Currency
For the date value to be parsed by Marketing Cloud Intelligence correctly, use the following formula in
the mapping: PARSEDATE(csv[‘datefield'],'yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss')
Another potential date format could be PARSEDATE(csv[‘kn.day'],'MM/dd/yyyy')
If you want the platform to consider the source currency to make any currency conversion calculations
correctly, make sure that this field is included in the list of fields and make sure you map it to the
Intelligence ‘Currency’ field. Alternatively, search for the unmapped ‘Currency’ field, then assign the
currency value from the Default Value dropdown to select the appropriate currency value and click ‘Set’
How to QA Your Data
For a certain report, process it in the Kenshoo UI for the required date range and then process the
relevant stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the exact same date range. Then create a Report/
254

=== PAGE 259 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Table Widget/Pivot table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact same Dimensions and
Measurements for the same date range. Start off by simply comparing the Measurement totals and make
sure those are aligned. Next, to make sure that you’ve mapped correctly in terms of Entity relationships,
filter the Kenshoo report for a certain Dimension value and make sure that the list of associated
Dimensions seen in the Kenshoo report corresponds to that seen in Intelligence when the same filtering
is applied. Also, under the above filtering, make sure that the metric values are aligned as well.
Known Issues
•
This connector might be a bit slow to process (as per Kenshoo’s API processing time) however this isn’t
indicative of any issues and actual stream failure rates should be relatively low.
•
Keep in mind that if the source report has more than one tab, the platform will always take the first tab
therefore use CSV reports or single sheet reports.

MailChimp API Connector
MailChimp API Connector.
•
Channel: Email Marketing
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Valid username and password.
Details
•
The data retrieved in the first run includes campaigns sent between the specified date range, and 30
days before that date range's start date.
•
In following retrievals, data updates always include campaigns sent in the last 30 days.
•
Measurements' data aggregate starting from the Stream's creation date, meaning that the first data
date will be the stream's creation date.
•
All the Measurements retrieved in the connector are LIFETIME Measurements.
•
The connector only retrieves campaign level data.
•
The connector retrieves Email Recipient Key & Name and the Email Recipients List fields. However,
Email Recipient Key and Name are populated with default values concatenated with the Email
Recipients List value.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Connector for B2C Commerce
Use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Connector for B2C Commerce to pull aggregated orders or
product-level data from your B2C Commerce site. B2C Commerce gathers data from your realm and
configures it into logical datasets called dashboards.
By connecting Marketing Cloud Intelligence to B2C Commerce Cloud, you can import your B2C
Commerce data into Intelligence and create custom views. You can analyze order and product data to
gain insights into business performance details and product performance. For example, find out how
many orders were placed during a promotion, or identify the top-three order channels used by registered
shoppers.
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Connector for B2C Commerce data stream type is ecommerce, and
data lagging is one day.

revenue over time without the details of specific products.

A digital advertisement search engine. Measures ad viewability on Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube.
•

•

•

Details
•

•

•

here.
General Setup
After you’ve logged in with your credentials, you can now select the Brand ID for which you’re pulling
data.
Extended Properties
•
Get Placement Level Data - This checkbox is selected by default, and retrieves a Placement Level
report in addition to the data retrieved by the API by default. This added report is mapped to the Ads
Verification Data Stream Type.
•

the data retrieved by the API by default. This added report will be mapped to the Ads Verification Data
Stream Type.
•

retrieved in addition to the data retrieved by the API by default. This added report is mapped to the

Known Issues
•
The following MOAT Entities are automatically mapped to the following Fields within Marketing Cloud
260

=== PAGE 265 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Intelligence:
- level1 - Campaign
- level2 - Site
- level3 - Placement (when Get Placement Level Data is checked)
- level4 - Creative (when Get Creative Level Data is checked)
- slicer1 - unmapped (when Get Domain Level Data is checked)
•
Due to limitations on the provider's side, the naming of MOAT fields isn’t consistent. Therefore, if you
believe there’s a discrepancy in your data, check the MOAT API documentation here (note that an API-
access set of credentials is required to access this link) and check the significance of the relevant MOAT
field to verify that it has been mapped correctly in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. If you find a field that
has been incorrectly mapped, simply unmap it and remap it to the correct Marketing Cloud
Intelligence field within the MOAT Data Stream.

Marketo API Connector
Marketing automation platform.
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day

•

263

=== PAGE 268 ===

'New' > 'View Details' displays the credentials .
•
Identity - You can find the Identity under 'Admin' > 'Web Service' > copy the 'Identity for rest API'
Details
•
Reports from Marketo might not retrieve measurements data. In order to query data successfully in
the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform, a measurement must be queried along with the
dimensions. To make sure this is available to you, we advise that when mapping Marketo data you
map a measurement field with a st value, for example, '1'. When visualizing Marketo data, query this
measurement as well.
•
The 'Day' field should be mapped according to your desired view. You can use the Lead or Opportunity
modified date, map it as TODAY(), or any other relevant date.
Setup Process
After you enter your credentials into the Data Stream General Settings, a list of reports will be available
from the Report Type drop-down. Note that report names may slightly differ from how they appear in the
Marketo UI. One of the Report Types available is the ‘Email Marketing Campaign Summary’ report. If you
intend to create a Data Stream based on this report, start with that (before creating additional Data
Streams based on other Marketo Report Types) as it is more straightforward and easier to work with than
the other Report Types.
Furthermore, in selecting this Report Type, we recommend you select the ‘Email Marketing’ Data Stream
Type under the Extended Properties Section, as doing so will mean that all fields in this report will be
automatically mapped correctly in the Email Marketing Data Stream type.
If you intend to create a Data Stream based on one of the Leads reports, you have three options to
choose from: ‘Leads Report’, ‘Leads by List’ and ‘Leads by Program’. Out of the three, we strongly
recommend that you select the Leads Report as using this particular Report Type will assure much better
Data Stream performance, and furthermore - the ‘Leads by List’ and ‘Leads by Program’ Report Types are
planned to be deprecated in the near future.
In some reports, you’ll have the option to choose additional fields to retrieve in the report.

MOZ API Connector
MOZ API is a Search Engine Optimization tool, which among other things, provides different competitive
views. By using our API, you’re able to select Domains, and retrieve a variety of Measurements related to
them.
•
Channel: SEO / Competitive

Details
The data that is retrieved through the API matches the view that is available under the ‘Links’ tab, which
can be found in the Campaigns Dashboard section in Moz Pro’s UI
Information on the provided Measurements can be found here.
Two Measurements that should be addressed differently are ‘MozRank’ and ‘MozTrust’, of type Rank.
These Measurements aren’t easily aggregated, and we recommend mapping them either as
Measurements with an Average aggregation type, or as a Dimension. There are 3 AVG type metrics
named “Rank #” within the Competitive Data Stream Type designated for that purpose.

data.

•

depending on your Moz use-case.
•
Domains – Select the Domains that you would like to track.
Known Issues
The data provided from Moz is aggregated by Lifetime, and thus are always be assigned to ‘Today’. There
are 2 levels of access – Free and Paid. Each one of them enables to different data-sets (Dimensions and
Measurements).

NetBase API Connector
Netbase is a Social Listening Platform. They develop Natural Language Processing technology that is used
to analyze social media and other web content.
•
Channel: Social
267

=== PAGE 272 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Data Stream Type: Social Listening
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Provide a valid Netbase username and password to an Account that has API access. To enable API
access, contact your Netbase representative.
General Setup
•
Enter your Netbase credentials, and select the Account you wish to pull data for from the drop-down.
•
The Topics for the selected Account appears in a drop-down. Choose at least one topic you wish to
pull data for. Not that each Data Stream retrieval is limited to three Topics, to retrieve data for more
than three Topics, it’s recommended that you create additional Data Streams.
Details
•
The Netbase Dimension ‘Topic’ maps to the Entity called ‘Monitor’, which is the Main Entity in the
Social Listening Data Stream Type.
•
The Netbase Dimension ‘Documents’ maps to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Entity called ‘Social
Mention’.
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls Netbase’s data at the ‘Mention’ level, which is the most granular
level possible, as it’s the actual social media post. This allows for all the data pulled to be aggregated at
that level in the background, while providing you with the flexibility to create the aggregations you
wish to see within Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Known Issues
•
Due to Twitter’s legal limitations, which block the transfer of their post content to third party
applications, you won’t be able to retrieve the content of Twitter Mentions into Marketing Cloud
Intelligence - but their associated Measurements are retrieved normally.
•
Due to policy agreements between Twitter and NetBase, Tweet IDs won’t be included in the "docID"
field returned from the API by default. To retrieve these IDs, contact your NetBase representative and
request the IDs to be returned for "retrieveDocuments" and "retrieveSentences" endpoints.
•
Netbase’s API has a low rate limit, meaning that Marketing Cloud Intelligence can only complete one
data retrieval per minute, potentially causing delays or processing errors, such as: “Rate Limit” or “Too
Many Requests”.

Oauth2 Authentication
Oauth2 is an authentication method commonly used as a way for users to authorize websites or
applications to access their information on other vendors using authentication tokens, without giving
them the credentials to the vendor. Oauth2 is used when the connection to a vendor is done through a
mediator app.
When generating an authentication token, the mediator app contacts the relevant vendor and trigger it
to send the user an authentication request. When the user enters their credentials, a token will be
created and saved for future use. This token is the authentication method for connecting to the vendor
from now on. You can see a use of oauth2 in Marketing Cloud Intelligence in some of our API
connectors. For example, when creating a Google Analytics data stream the authentication screen is a
Google authentication screen triggered by Marketing Cloud Intelligence. When the user enters their
credentials, an authentication token is saved in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for future use.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence enables the use of Oauth2 authentication in HTTP retrieval settings, for
example when retrieving data from vendors that require this kind of authentication like Hubspot. To
apply an Oauth2 authentication choose Oauth2 as the Authentication Type in the retrieval settings of the
Data Stream. Then enter the relevant details required by the vendor to generate an authentication token.
•
Callback URL - Enter this URL in the mediator app you use, the callback URL is used by the mediator
app to direct the response back to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Token Name - This is an internal name that is used only in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Auth URL - This is the URL of the vendor's authentication window, as provided by the vendor.
•
Access Token URL - This is the URL of the token that becomes available after the authentication
process is complete. It should be provided by the vendor.
•
Client ID - A part of the mediator's app credentials when accessing the vendor, as provided by the
mediator app you’re using.
•
Client Secret - A part of the mediator's app credentials when accessing the vendor, as provided by the
mediator app you’re using.
The following fields are optional. Some vendors might require you include them in the request. The
vendor's requirements should be included in their documentation.
•
App ID - A part of the mediator's app credentials, as provided by the mediator app, and is optional and
not required for all Vendors.
•
Request Token URL - Also referred to as 'Refresh Token URL', Some vendors have different URLs for
generating an authentication token and refreshing an authentication token. If this is the case, enter
the URL used for refreshing the authentication token.
•
Scope - Some vendors allow you to request only specific data. If this is the case, enter the scope details
269

=== PAGE 274 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
as required by the vendor.
•
Grant Type - Currently, Marketing Cloud Intelligence supports only the 'Authentication Code' type.
•
After you’re done entering the authentication form click Generate Token. Enter your credentials in the
vendor's authentication pop-up window. When using Oauth2 you must generate a token to pull data.
•
Access token type - After a token is generated, the Access Token Type determines how it’s used. Make
sure that this setting matches the vendor's settings.
Order Management System API Connector
Order Management System (OMS) is a central hub for handling all aspects of the order life cycle,
including order capture, fulfillment, shipping, payment processing, and services.
The data stream type is ecommerce, and data lagging is 1 day.
Here is some additional information about the Order Management System API Connector:
•
If your Commerce Cloud setup is connected to OMS, your Commerce Cloud data appears in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence when connecting to the API.
•
Intelligence pulls both order and item-level data using the order key. The order key is in the URL of the
order in OMS and appears at the end. For example, …/OrderSummary/10s4L000000kFIYSAU/view
•
Intelligence maps the OMS order number to a custom field named Order Number.
Prerequisites for Creating an API Data Stream Using the Order Management System Connector
To create an API data stream for OMS, you enable using multiple currencies. Then, when products
have different currencies, Marketing Cloud Intelligence converts the currency to the currency selected
in Intelligence.

Pinterest API Connector
Pinterest is an online pinboard for collecting visual pieces of multimedia and sharing the content with
other users within the platform.
272

=== PAGE 277 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Channel: Social
•
Data Stream Type: Social Objects
Prerequisites
Provide a valid Pinterest username and password. After it is authenticated, under Profile select the Profile
you want to retrieve.
Details
•
Due to Pinterest API limitations, the connector retrieves posts from the last 90 days, therefore all posts
90 days prior aren’t retrieved.
•
Entries in the Data Stream Log appear under the processing date of the extraction date (‘Today’).
•
When processing the data stream, there’s no way to define a start and end date, since data for posts is
retrieved until today and assigned a date value identical to the day of processing.
•
Measurement data retrieved for all posts is accurate according to the date when processing takes
place.
•
The connector shows LIFETIME type Measurements. LIFETIME Measurements display the
accumulated value of the Measurement as of the current date.
•
Data Streams with LIFETIME Aggregation don’t allow for any failed retrievals to be reprocessed post-
failure, in order to prevent potential data inaccuracies. For example, if a data stream retrieval failed on
January 1, 2019 at 8 AM. Even a minute or two later - the data will be updated and refreshed, and
what is available for retrieval on the platform will no longer reflect what was attempted to be retrieved
a minute or two prior - not to mention how much the data may change by January 2, 2019.
Reprocessing that failed retrieval on January 2nd would mean that data that didn’t exist in the
platform on January 1 would be attributed to January 1 - a misrepresentation, which could lead to
serious inaccuracies.
•
Similarly, after data is deleted from a Pinterest data stream, the deleted data won’t be accessible for
retrieval through reprocessing, because of the nature of LIFETIME Measurements.
•
In order to reprocess data retrieved in the past without actually deleting any data - for example, to
accommodate changes in mapping - the Re-Run Files option can be used, which is located under the
‘Log’ button in the Data Streams Management menu.
QA Your Data
11.. Create the desired report in the Pinterest UI for the required date range.
22.. Then, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
33.. Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
44.. Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.

conversion type, Search, Signup, Watch-Video, etc. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, to align with the
Data Model and other platforms, the type of the conversion is returned as a value of a Conversion Tag
Category Dimension, while the Measurement values of all conversions are available in the Total
Conversions Measurement

Ads account. If you aren’t comparing Totals, ensure that you compare the Conversion Measurements
in Pinterest with the corresponding attribution window set in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Python Data Stream Retrievals
Python scripts are an effective, fast growing and easy to use technology, and they are now supported in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence as a Retrieval Method for TotalConnect Data Streams. Although in some
cases the same results can be achieved using HTTP requests, Python retrievals are more robust while still
being flexible, powerful, and relatively easy to set up.
Setup
Being a data source connection method, the same procedure is followed as when connecting any new
data source, which means you’ll first have to make sure you’re in the Connect & Mix tab. Under the Data
Stream List section, click Add New.
Scroll down to the Technical Vendors section and start the Data Stream setup process from there.
275

=== PAGE 280 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Alternatively, you can also start the setup process by choosing to create a TotalConnect Data Stream and
then setting Python as the Retrieval Method.
What Can It Be Used For?
Python can be useful for retrieving data from different sources in different formats. The fact that Python
is a script makes it flexible in dealing with varying formats, requirements, and scenarios. Below are some
examples of use cases that are best addressed by using a Python-based connector:
•
Retrieving data via an API connection where multiple HTTP calls are required in order to handle any of
the following cases:
- Obtaining an OAuth 2.0 token.
- Retrieving Entities.
- Looping Through Calls.
- Paginating through the response.
•
Parsing the data during the retrieval process itself (prior to having it available in the mapping screen)
so that it’s easier to work with within Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Technical Implementation
Execution Requirements
•
Version: Python 3.9
•
Processing Limit: Maximum 10 minutes.
•
Memory Limit: Maximum 1.5 GB
•
Output: The script must generate a dataset compatible with a Data Stream.
Blocked Libraries
These libraries can’t be used. Attempting to import these results in script failure:
•
os
•
subprocess
•
shutil
•
pty
•
sys
•
importlib
•
ctypes
•
socket
•
pickle
•
marshal
•
code
•
runpy
•
multiprocessing
•
signal
276

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
commands
•
pdb
•
webbrowser
•
antigravity
•

glob

Blocked Functions & Patterns
These built-in functions and access patterns are restricted:
Functions: exec() , eval() , and compile()
Patterns: importlib.import_module() __import__() (direct use) and __builtins__
We recommend that you develop your script and test it externally (in your own Python environment) so
that it’s easier for you to validate and debug. You can use the publicly available Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Package to build your script and test it. Use the “pip install” command to install the
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Package on your local environment. Note that using ‘save_csv’ and ‘save’ in
your local environment will print the results instead of saving them to Marketing Cloud Intelligence. Do
the following:
•
Start by Importing the relevant Packages and include the Marketing Cloud Intelligence package.
•
Build the logic as per your requirements using the Supported Packages.
•
Use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Package to programmatically write rows to a csv file, which will
be uploaded to the Data Stream (see below a table with a summary of the functions supported in our
Package).

There are two different ways to write Python scripts:
•
Internal - The Python module is read via Marketing Cloud Intelligence data streams.
•
External - The script is run via a local IDE.
The examples below show how to write internal scripts. If you are writing external scripts, you must write
the scripts as follows:
•
Call the datorama module via “from datorama.datorama import *” instead of “import datorama”
•
Remove “dataorama.” from the beginning of each function.
Here you can see a live example of how this looks:
from datorama.datorama import *
#Generating a list of values for our headers' row
row_values_list = ['date','campaign','impression','clicks']
# Writing a new row to the csv input file from values list
add_row(row_values_list)
Example 1 - using add_row() and save():
import datorama
#Generating a list of values for our headers' row
row_values_list = ['date','campaign','impression','clicks']
# Writing a new row to the csv input file from values list
datorama.add_row(row_values_list)
#Generating two more lists containing row values
278

=== PAGE 283 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
#Note that the values of a specific column are in the same position in their r
ow's list as the position of their header in the headers' row
row2 = ['01-12-2017','Campaign 1',15,6] row3 = ['02-12-2017','Campaign 2',20,1
0]
# Creating a list of the two rows we want to write at once
multiple_rows = [row2,row3]
# Writing several rows at once to the csv by sending a list of multiple value
s' lists
datorama.add_rows(multiple_rows)
# Saving the csv file to upload it to our data stream
datorama.save()
Example 2 - using save_csv():
import datorama
#Generate the exact same file by providing a csv formatted string of the fil
e's content
csv_string = '''"date","campaign","impression","clicks"\n\ "01-12-2017","Campa
ign 1", 15, 6\n\ "02-12-2017","Campaign 2", 20, 10'''
#Saving the csv file, based on the input string
datorama.save_csv(csv_string)
Both scripts will produce the following csv file:
Date Campaign Impressions Clicks
01-12-2017 Campaign 1 15 6
02-12-2017 Campaign 2 20 10

using a Package that is not currently supported, please log a Feature Request with your CSM so that the

This script retrieves publicly available data from Twitter for different Screen Names, with Metrics such as

import datoramaimport tweepyimport datetimeimport jsonimport
pandas as pdscreen_names = ["NASCAR", "F1", "PGATOUR", "MotoGP", "IndyCa
r", "ufc", "WWE"]
report_fields = ["screen_name", "statuses_count","followers_count","frien
ds_count","listed_count","favourites_count" ]
#Twitter API
credentialsconsumer_key = ""consumer_secret = ""access_key = ""access_sec
ret = ""
#Calculating the date of the run
now = datetime.datetime.now()
date = now.strftime("%d-%m-%Y")
#authorize twitter, initialize
tweepyauth = tweepy.OAuthHandler(consumer_key, consumer_secret)
auth.set_access_token(access_key, access_secret)
api = tweepy.API(auth)
full_report = pd.DataFrame()
#Pulling the report from Twitter per screen namefor screen_name in scree
n_names:
get_user = api.get_user(screen_name = screen_name)
#Using pandas DataFrame to manipulate the report
df = pd.DataFrame(get_user._json)
#Filtering the report only on requested fields
requested_df = df[report_fields]
#Adding the screen name to the data set
requested_df["account"] = screen_name
#Appending the current screen name's data set to the full report of all s
creen names
full_report =full_report.append(requested_df, ignore_index=True)
#Adding the run date in the "date" column of the data
setfull_report["date"] = date
#Creating a csv string of the data
setcsv_string = full_report.to_csv()
#Saving the csv
datorama.save_csv(csv_string)

Quora Ads API Connector
Quora is an online community that allows users to share knowledge (by asking and answering
questions). Quora is one of the highest-quality user-generated content platforms.
Click here for more information about Quora.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type: Ads, Conversion Tag
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Provide a valid Quora Ads username and password.
General Setup
In the Credentials area, if you have already set up your Quora user, you can select the name from the
dropdown list. If not, click Add new to define a new user.
Extra Information
The attribution window of the Conversion data is set to the default, 28 days click and 1-day view.
Known Issues
•
The connector supports all Conversion Types available in Quora but unfortunately, due to limitations
on Quora’s end, this doesn’t include any new Conversion Types that might be added by Quora in the
future. If you’re missing a Conversion Type in your data, contact Support.
•
There may be discrepancies with the total of data that is retrieved at the Ad level in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence compared to the totals in Quora. This is because Marketing Cloud Intelligence displays the
total of data based on a run-time calculation that doesn’t include deleted Ads, where Quora includes
deleted Ads in their calculation (although they aren’t displayed).
QA Your Data
This section outlines the steps to take to make sure your Data is working in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
11.. Create the desired report in the UI for the required date range.
22.. Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
33.. Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
44.. Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.
282

=== PAGE 287 ===

Compare the Conversions Data as follows:
11.. Go to the Ads Manager in Quora
22.. Configure filters.
33.. Click Download Ads.
44.. Compare Quora conversions to Marketing Cloud Intelligence conversions.
55.. Contrary to how it’s displayed in the UI, the Conversion Metrics in Marketing Cloud Intelligence are
transposed. For example, in the UI - there’s a Measurement for each conversion type, Generic, App
Installs, Purchase etc. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, to align with the Data Model and other
platforms, the type of the conversion is returned as a value of a Conversion Tag Category Dimension,
while the metric values of all conversions are available in the Total Conversions metric
Radarly API Connector
Radarly by Linkfluence, is a social listening platform that uses Artificial Intelligence (AI) to turn social data
into valuable insights. With Radarly, users can leverage social data and gather valuable consumer insights
online.
•
Channel: Social Listening
•
Data Stream Type: Social Listening
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to the Radarly API, provide a valid Radarly username and password with API access.
General Setup
After selecting a Project, you can choose to retrieve a single or several Queries, or leave the field empty
to retrieve all. Retrieving too many Queries could result in a large amount of data, causing a timeout. If
this happens, reduce the number of Queries per data stream.
Known Issues
•
Some platforms don’t allow Radarly to return all the content through their APIs. For example, Twitter
restricts their content to be distributed through APIs only to Tweet IDs, Direct Message IDs, and/or
User IDs.
•
Token limitation - A Radarly user can only use a single data source authentication. This means that in
order to use it in different Workspaces, the data source authentication must be shared to the entire
Account.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud Data Extensions
Salesforce Marketing Cloud is a platform for delivering relevant, personalized journeys across channels
and devices - enabling marketers to deliver the right message at the right time throughout all phases of
the relationship.
The SFMC Data Extensions connector enables you to pull in data extensions, which are custom tables
with fields of data about your contacts. They can be standalone or can be related to other data
extensions.
Note
•
Don’t edit the data extension after creating a data stream. Removing or renaming any columns
after creation, causes issues with automation. If for some reason you must edit the data
extensions, contact Marketing Cloud Support.
•
Make sure your data extension doesn’t contain these columns: date_id, location_id,
dat_account_currency, dat_exchange_rate, pacing_period, pacing_end_date, row_count, or
brand_id.
•
Make sure the data extension doesn’t use the same column name more than one time.
•
Only data extensions with less than 50M records are supported.
•
Sendlog DEs aren’t supported.
•
Your user must have Enterprise-level access.
285

=== PAGE 290 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Your user must also be an Admin and Marketing Cloud Admin.
•
This API connector supports the following:
- Enterprise 1.0
- Enterprise 2.0
- Core or Connect
•
You must make sure your user has access to the parent member, otherwise the authentication
fails.
•
If for some reason the connection to the data extension failed, try deleting the automation of the
data extension in Automation Studio. If this doesn’t help, contact Marketing Cloud support.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type: Generic
•
Data Lagging: 1 day
Details
•
The data stream runs on an hourly basis, but usually pulls data once a day. The data is pulled after it
becomes available from Marketing Cloud.
•
Usually Marketing Cloud data isn’t available upon the creation of the data stream, and is only available
24–48 hours later. Therefore, if no data is pulled in during the first process, it’s recommended you
check the data stream a day or two later. After initial provisioning the data stream is processed as
expected, and data becomes available daily.
Prerequisites
To connect to the Salesforce Marketing Cloud API, you need a valid username and password with API
access.
•
Your user must have Enterprise-level access.
•
It’s advised you check your Business Unit Marketing Cloud time zone. If it differs from Central Standard
Timezone (Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s default time zone), you could see discrepancies in your data.

Admin.

Authentication

22.. Under Account, select Installed Packages and click New.
33.. In the New Package Details screen, enter a Name and Description.

55.. Click Save.

77.. More details can be found here.
For Marketing Cloud Intelligence you need:
•
Client ID
•
Client Secret
•
Instance ID
If you’re not sure what the Instance ID is, you can find it within the URL string when logging into
Marketing Cloud: it is the S(#) value in the URL.
Setup
When setting up the Data Stream there are 3 dropdowns that you need to consider under the ‘General’
tab. The ‘Credentials’ is where you would select the username on the Marketing Cloud platform. Each
Workspace can have more than one username for which data is pulled. The dropdown is therefore
populated with all the usernames that have been authenticated. Each username can be associated with
more than one Account on Marketing Cloud and the dropdown will display all the Accounts associated
with the respective username. The Business Unit selection is optional, it populates with all the Business
Units associated with this Account.
This connector pulls LIFETIME measurements and every processing action pulls data for all existing
Campaigns. This means that there’s no ‘Initial Data Fetch’ configuration in the Data Stream settings, as in
other API connectors. Every time the Data Stream is processed, whether at creation or otherwise, it pulls
all Campaigns data regardless of how far back such data exists historically. This means that configuring
dates for Initial Data Fetch or for routine processing is irrelevant.
The Platform assigns the Processing Date as the Data Date for the retrieved data, since the LIFETIME
values of the Measurements pulled (in every such instance) are true to that particular date.
To keep data for Failed Days at Date level, Failed Days must be reprocessed on the day that they failed.
Reprocessing data on any other day would assign the new Processing Date to the reprocessed data.
Similarly, if you delete data for dates prior to the current day, reprocessing assigns the Reprocessing Date
to that date, overriding whatever dates it had before.
To avoid confusion, here’s a list of the Source Measurements and the Intelligence Measurements to
which they’re mapped:

Push Device Type Dimension Unmapped
Push Geofence Name Dimension Unmapped
Push Message Subtitle Dimension Unmapped
Push Message Title Dimension Unmapped
Push Operating System Dimension Unmapped
Push Page URL Dimension Unmapped
Push Message Failures Measurement Message Failures
Push Sends Measurement Message Sends
Push Opens Measurement Message Total Opens
Push Opt-Ins Measurement Opt In
Push Opt-Outs Measurement Opt Out
Push Time in App Measurement Time in App
Push Time in App Open Measurement Unmapped
Known Issues and Limitations
Email meta-data and engagement data, stored in the Marketing Cloud database is only accessible to
third party apps, such as Marketing Cloud Intelligence through daily extracts. Marketing Cloud, exports
the data to an Amazon S3 bucket on a daily basis. The Marketing Cloud Intelligence API then reads the
data from the S3 bucket, aggregates it, and stores it on the Marketing Cloud Intelligence data model.
The data in the S3 bucket is deleted after 90 days (to comply with the GDPR regulations) the deletion
295

=== PAGE 300 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
causes some of the limitations listed below:
•
First Time Creating a Data Stream - The first time you create a Data Stream with your Enterprise
account, a background provisioning process takes place enabling your Salesforce Marketing Cloud
account to access this data. This process could take a couple of hours to complete and can cause the
Data Stream to fail during initial data retrieval with a specific error - Initial Retrieval Timeout. You can
either reprocess your data stream manually after a few hours, or it will reprocess and fix itself the next
day.
•
Send Date Dimension - The Send Date dimension for triggered (by journey or API) emails will not be
available if more than 90 days have passed since the original send date. For example, if you send out
an email for a specific campaign on January 1, 2021, and it was opened or clicked on April 1 2021, the
send date dimension will not be available, so any engagement data from that email campaign will not
have a send date since 90 days have passed since the original send date (January 1, 2021). Note: This
limitation doesn’t affect standard emails.
•
Campaigns Dimension - There are several limitations to the campaign dimension as follows:
- Engagement data for campaigns created over 90 days ago won’t have the campaign information.
- In Marketing Cloud you can assign several campaigns, while in Marketing Cloud Intelligence the
data model only allows for one campaign, if you assigned several campaigns Marketing Cloud
Intelligence selects the first one.
- If the campaign code changes after the first time it’s set, Marketing Cloud Intelligence won’t reflect
those changes.
- Campaign data won’t populate if the campaign is assigned to the email after the email was sent.
•
MobileConnect Message Definition - Messages can be created more than 90 days before the
message is sent, in a case like this, the message information attached to the measurements won’t be
available. For example, the message information attached to the measurement, such as Message
Name won’t be available.
•
MobileConnect and Journey - Journey data is not pulled for MobileConnect data. All Journeys
appears as 'Default Journey' or 'N/A'
•
Geolocation - The geolocation is derived from the IP address. Consumer rights (GDPR) can block the
reporting of the IP address, making the data you have on geolocation incomplete. Another common
issue with the accuracy of geolocations is the use of VPNs and Proxy Servers that cause the IP address
to be unreliable and inaccurate in determining the geolocation.
Note Geolocation is only available for engagement metrics.
•
Content with Special Characters - When files contain special characters (non-ASCII) for example
Spanish or Japanese, any data that contains multiple lines, could fail during parsing. When using non-
English characters it’s recommended to check the Disable Multiple Parsing and Enable non-ASCII
parsing checkbox. This checkbox parses non-English characters but can’t parse multiline, so it only
pulls the first line of each field.
How to QA Your Data
For Intelligence Reports for Engagement, complete the following steps:
•
Go to the Marketing Cloud Report Builder.
•
Navigate to: Analytics Builder > Marketing Cloud Intelligence Reports > Pivot Tables
296

=== PAGE 301 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Compare the totals of specific emails between Marketing Cloud Intelligence and Intelligence Reports
for Engagement.
Marketing Cloud Configuration Best Practices
For an optimal view of your Marketing Cloud Intelligence data, consider these best practices:
•
Avoid special characters. Special characters and emoji in Marketing Cloud aren’t supported. They’re
replaced with the replacement character
•
Create a unique Campaign Code. The Campaign Code from Marketing Cloud will be mapped to
Campaign Key in Marketing Cloud Intelligence and the key must be unique to ensure proper data
aggregation
•
After a Campaign Code is set, don’t change it. Campaign Code in MC is a freeform field that can be
manually changed after it is entered. If the Campaign Code changes after the time its first set, the
change won’t be reflected.
•
Assign the Campaign Key and Name prior to sending the email. Campaign data doesn’t populate if the
campaign is assigned to the email after it was sent
•
Assign only one campaign per message. The Marketing Cloud Intelligence data model can only link
one message to one Campaign Key.
•
Because of the Campaign Name nuances, it’s best to leverage the Message Send Name for any naming
convention.
Salesforce Account Engagement API Connector
Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot) is Salesforce’s B2B marketing automation solution. If
you’re a business that sells to other businesses (or B2B), Account Engagement can automate your
marketing activities and unite your marketing and sales departments so that they can work better
together.
•
Channel: Email Marketing, Lead Generation, Sales, Forms
•
Data Stream Type: Messaging (for Account Engagement Email Marketing data); CRM Leads (for
Account Engagement Prospects data); CRM (for Account Engagement Opportunities data);
Conversions (for Account Engagement Forms data)
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
•
New Account Engagement data streams - To connect to a new data stream you must use the
credentials of an SSO-enabled Salesforce user who has Account Engagement access.
- In cases where the organization section displays “Manual Business Unit ID Input”, insert your
Business Unit ID in the extended properties section. To find your Business Unit ID:
- Log into Salesforce using the same account you use to log into Account Engagement.
- Go to Setup > Account Engagement > Business Unit Setup.
- The business unit ID (BUID) begins with "0Uv" and is 18 characters long.
•
Existing Account Engagement data streams - will continue to work using the legacy authentication
297

=== PAGE 302 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
method (API Key & Account Engagement User name). However, we strongly recommend that you
reauthenticate using the new approach, leveraging the BUID (if needed) as described earlier.
Details
Data is retrieved according to a Daily breakdown and not LIFETIME. In order to retrieve LIFETIME data,
process the Data Stream from the Campaign start date.
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, data is tracked by the day, therefore a measurement such as 'Opens'
increases over time as more people open the email. For example, if you filter on the send date of Jun 14,
you’ll see a discrepancy between Marketing Cloud Intelligence and Account Engagement.
General Setup
In this section, after entering your credentials, select the relevant Organization you want to retrieve data
for.
Advanced Settings
This section allows you to define advanced settings for your API connector. The settings are the same for
all API connectors.
Extended Properties
•
Get Email Name - Select this checkbox to retrieve the email name when pulling data from Pardot.
Note Retrieving the Email Name dramatically increases the number of API calls that could result
in you reaching your profile’s rate limit (see ‘Known Issues’).
•
Get Prospect Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve prospect data as well as email marketing data.
•
Get Opportunity Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve opportunity data as well as email marketing
data.
•
Business unit ID - In cases where the organization section displays “Manual Business Unit ID Input”,
insert your Business Unit ID as described earlier.
•
Get Forms Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve forms data as well as email marketing data.
Known Issues
Account Engagement has a rate limit for API calls on the profile level that could lead to processing
failures when processing failed statistics or large sets of days. Before engaging in bulk processing, check
your concurrent API calls rate limit under the Pardot User Profile to avoid failures. For more information
about Account Engagement API, see the Account Engagement API documentation.

=== PAGE 303 ===
