How to QA Your Data
This section describes how to make sure your Data is working in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Create the desired report within Marketing Assets in the Salesforce Account Engagement platform for
the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table or Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.
Deleting Account Engagement Data
Deleting your Account Engagement connector only deletes email marketing data and not CRM leads or
CRM data. This is because the Prospects and Opportunities data is retrieved from Account Engagement
via the CRM data stream types that upload data at the workspace level and not at the data stream level.
You can only delete the CRM-related data from the Dimension Explorer. Complete the following steps to
delete the CRM related data:
11.. Navigate to Connect & Mix.
22.. Expand the Dimensions tab and select Dimension Explorer.
33.. In the search box, select the entity you want to delete, for example, Lead. Notice that there are two
‘Lead’ entities and two ‘Opportunity’ entities that appear. When deleting an entity, always pick the
bottom entity of the two.
44.. All the data that has been retrieved into the workspace for the selected entity, is displayed. For
example, if ‘Lead’ has been selected then all the leads that have been retrieved into the workspace are
displayed.
55.. Click Delete to delete all the keys within the selected entity, or select the specific key you want to
delete and then click Delete.
Suggested Best Practices
•
It’s recommended to rename the display names for the following dimensions so that you can easily
recognize what each dimension is:
- Message Send Name to Account Engagement Campaign (Email)
- Lead Custom Attribute 01 to Account Engagement Campaign (Leads)
- Opportunity Custom Attribute 01 to Account Engagement Campaign (Opportunities). Custom
Attribute 01 is preserved for longer values. If your values are short, then it’s recommended you map
to another custom attribute to avoid any issues.
•
In order to create interconnectivity at the Account Engagement Campaign level, between the different
data stream types, it’s recommended to create the following two fusions:
- Pardot Campaign (Email) <---> Pardot Campaign (Leads)
- Pardot Campaign (Leads) <---> Pardot Campaign (Opportunities)
These fusions will transitively also fuse between Pardot Campaign (Email) and Pardot Campaign
(Opportunities) meaning that for any Pardot Campaign, you are able to see measurements and
299

=== PAGE 304 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
attributes from all three data stream types.
However, the example assumes that you changed the display names as suggested above, if not, use
the original dimension names.
•
In Account Engagement, prospects don’t have a ‘Status’ dimension, however, the prospect’s status can
be determined from the Prospect’s Stage dimension. It’s recommended to not leave the Marketing
Cloud Intelligence Lead Status dimension unmapped. Instead, you can use a mapping formula to
create the lead status value based on the prospect stage. The following image shows an example of
how this can be set up:
When you set up the above logic yourself, it’s important to apply the appropriate logic according to
what prospect stage values are considered to be prospects that have been converted to opportunities
within your data, for example with a ‘QUALIFIED ’ status, and which are still just leads, for example an
‘UNQUALIFIED ’ status.
Salesforce Reports API Connector
The Salesforce Reports API connector retrieves reports created in Salesforce, in this connector you will
have access to all the reports in Salesforce to which you have permissions for. Since the reports in
Salesforce are customizable, once you save the stream you will be directed to the Mapping screen to
allow you the same level of customization in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
•
A valid Salesforce username and password.
Details
•
Since the date field in Salesforce Reports is customizable, Marketing Cloud Intelligence will not
automatically map the Date Dimension
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence will always process the most current version of the report from
Salesforce. Note that this means that when reprocessing Salesforce Reports streams, only the latest
version of the report will be retrieved and not the prior version.
•
Salesforce has a limit for the number of rows allowed in a single report (up to 2000 rows per report).
To make sure that all your data is retrieved properly it is advised that you create reports that are
narrowed down to a specific view using filters. A limited report will have a lesser chance of reaching
the row limit.
- You will only have the option to choose from 2,000 total reports. Therefore, if you have over this
amount, you need to either delete some reports or create a new user that will have access only to
the reports you want to fetch, so the list of reports is below the maximum.
•
If your report has changed, check the mapping of the stream and edit accordingly.
•
Data Stream Processing: LIFETIME. Marketing Cloud Intelligence will always pull the most current
version of the report from Salesforce in a LIFETIME method since oftentimes, many of the reports does
300

not contain date variables(reports such as leads or opportunities for example), hence processing using
a date range is irrelevant for this API.

The connector allows using SOQL to pull data from any Salesforce entity.

Search Ads 360 Custom API Connector
Search Ads 360
•
Channel: Search
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Search Ads 360 credentials with at least Advertiser viewer permissions
When Should I use the Google Search Ads 360 Custom Connector?
The Search Ads 360 API Connector is used to retrieve a predefined set of Dimensions and
Measurements, supplied by Search Ads 360’s API. These Dimensions are mapped within the Ads,
Conversion Tag, Conversion Tag with Keywords, Search Keywords or Conversion Tag Data Stream Types,
depending on the Extended Properties that are selected within the Data Stream. The pre-defined nature
of this API means that creating a Data Stream containing any additional fields other than the predefined
ones is limited and would require a Feature Request.
The Search Ads 360 Custom API Connector allows you to retrieve fields that are not available through
the Standard API, such as Floodlight Dimensions.
Keep in mind that unless you are intending to retrieve a custom set of fields, the standard GSA 360 API
Connector would be a better choice, as it is preset to be accurately mapped to the Data Model, while the
Custom Connector requires manual setup.

SEMrush API Connector
SEMrush is a competitive research platform that allows you to analyze your competitors' domains,
looking into their traffic, relevant keyword, and so on, SEMrush offers different types of data so you can
set up the connector to your liking.
•
Data Lagging: 0 Days
Prerequisites
SEMrush API key. An API key is available as part of the SEMrush API package. When purchasing a
package, note that Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses 'line' requests for live data only.
Details
•
You can validate the data by comparing the Marketing Cloud Intelligence view to the table view under
304

=== PAGE 309 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
each report type, according to the report you chose to retrieve.
•
SEMrush displays in its platform either live data or historical data aggregated to a month granularity.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence retrieves only live data from SEMrush, so data will start to aggregate from
the stream's creation date moving forward. Note, however, that unlike SEMrush, that doesn't enable
viewing the data in a daily granularity, Marketing Cloud Intelligence enables this view.
•
Since the report available in SEMrush displays only the current date's data, you won’t be able to
reprocess past dates in SEMrush streams.
•
The SEMrush data is different in structure from the 'Search Keyword' data model. For example, the
'Position' field is defined as a Dimension in SEMrush, but as a Measurement in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence. Furthermore, in SEMrush the 'Keyword Name' might be linked to several different
'Position' values. Mapping the data 'as is' might cause discrepancies. The best practice for mapping
SeEMrush data is to make sure the 'Keyword Name' and 'Position' fields are linked in a 'many to many'
relationship. Another option is to concatenate the two fields together to create a unique 'Keyword Key'
for every pairing instance.
csv['keyword_name']+ csv['position']
Then map the 'Keyword Name' to the 'Keyword Name' field in the 'Search Keyword' data model, and
map the 'Position' to the 'Keyword Custom Attribute' field.
General Setup
•
Choose the Report you want to retrieve:
- Domain_organic_search_Keywords - corresponding with the 'Positions' report in Semrush Buyer
untargeted Segment Performance report - corresponding with the 'Competitors' report in Semrush.
•
Enter the domain you want to retrieve data for. If no domain is entered, the stream fails .
•
Choose the Database Type. This refers to the Databases available at the top of the report.
•
Select the appropriate Data Stream Type.
SimilarWeb API Connector
SimilarWeb is a digital marketing platform offering a competitive analysis of site traffic.
•
Channel: Social Listening
•
Data Stream Type: Competitive
•
Data Lagging: Retrieves the last full month
Prerequisites
To connect to the SimilarWeb API, you need a valid API Key. Contact your SimilarWeb representative to
receive an API Key.
Details
•
SimilarWeb displays data up to the last month. For example, if the date is May 4 SimilarWeb displays
305

=== PAGE 310 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
data up until April.
•
When reprocessing a SimilarWeb Data Stream, the reprocess retrieves only whole months, even if you
entered only part of a month. For example, if you chose to reprocess Jan 8 to Feb 14, the whole month
of January and February will be retrieved in this reprocess.
•
Data can only be retrieved as far back as 16 months. All the Measurements pulled by the SimilarWeb
connector aren’t summable, except for Visits. The Measurements retrieved are either Rate
Measurements for example, Pages Per Visit or Average Measurements for example, Avg visit duration.
- You might experience inconsistencies when pulling these types of Measurements, as they’re
retrieved from SimilarWeb with calculated values, and not as ‘native’ Measurements.
- It’s recommended to view these Measurements in the lowest granularity available.
•
The Avg Visits Duration Measurement is retrieved from SimilarWeb as seconds. To ensure that it’s
displayed properly in Marketing Cloud Intelligence, change the Measurement type to Duration by
completing the following steps:
- Navigate to Connect & Mix > Measurements > Measurement List
- Filter your Measurements to Avg Visit Duration
- Under Type select Duration
- Click Save
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Transposes the data based on the Device entity - SimilarWeb metrics
have the Device Value and Metric Name merged into one. For example, Mobile Visits contains Mobile,
which is a Dimension value and Visits, which is a Measurement. Transposing the data separates the
two fields so that the Device value is mapped to the relevant Dimension, and the Measurement value
is mapped to the relevant Measurement.
- This process adds more rows depending on the number of Device values, but it’s the best way to
process the data so that you can filter the data by device, and so you can easily update any new
devices that are added in the future, without having to update the mapping each time.
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls worldwide data (if it’s available in the account) by default. If
worldwide data is not available, Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data only for the countries available
within the account.
General Setup
In this section you can define the general details. After entering your credentials, select the relevant
Account you want to retrieve data for.
Advanced Settings
This section allows you to define advanced settings for your API connector. These settings are the same
for all API connectors. For more information about the different settings, see API Connector - Advanced
Settings.
Extended Properties
•
Domain- Insert up to five domains you want to pull data for. The domains should be separated by a
comma.
306

- Ensure that the domain entered doesn’t include the ‘www’ prefix, for example, salesforce.com and
not www.salesforce.com. Inserting invalid domains results in an error.

Known Issues
•
Sizmek reports are run at a set time, and may not have processed by the time the API is run. This
occurrence could result in the query timing out, displaying an error such as: Provider Report Timeout
in the Data Stream Log. One way to resolve this issue is to overwrite the API runtime by using
Workflows.

in the Snapchat Ads platform, the Conversion Measurements in Intelligence are transposed.

Purchase, View Content, and so on.

Category Dimension, while the metric values of all conversions are available in the Total Conversions
metric in order to align with the Data Model and other platforms.

Social Studio API Connector
Salesforce Social Studio is a Management Center and Social Listening Platform for owned social accounts
on popular social networks, such as Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, Pinterest, and Google+. It’s
also a platform for Social Listening.
•
Channel: Social, Social Listening
•
Data Stream Type: Social Objects, Social Listening
•
Data Lagging: 0 Days
Note As of May 2, 2024, Marketing Cloud Social Studio Connector is no longer supported. After this
date, data streams can no longer be created or updated. Data pulled by existing data streams
continues to be available in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for reporting and use in dashboards. For
additional information, please contact our support team.
Prerequisites
To connect to Salesforce Social Studio, you need a valid username and password.
Details
•
The connector shows LIFETIME type Measurements. LIFETIME Measurements display the
accumulated value of the Measurement as of the current date.
•
When a Salesforce Social Studio Data Stream is created, all posts from the Creation Date of your Social
Accounts are retrieved. Topic Profiles allows reports to be exported up to 90 days back, while Managed
Accounts pulls all available posts - including those that were created further back than 90 days.
•
Entries in the Data Stream Log appear under the processing date of the extraction date (‘Today’).
•
When processing the Data Stream, there’s no date selection, as all data for all posts is retrieved until
today and assigned a date value that is identical to the day of processing.
•
Measurement data retrieved for all posts is accurate as of the date when processing takes place.
•
In order to prevent potential data inaccuracies, Data Streams in LIFETIME Aggregation don’t allow for
any failed retrievals to be reprocessed post-failure. For example, if a Data Stream retrieval failed on
January 1, 2019 at 8 AM, a minute later the data will be updated and refreshed, and the data available
for retrieval on the platform will no longer reflect what was attempted to be retrieved a minute prior.
Reprocessing the failed retrieval on January 2 would mean that the data didn’t exist in the platform on
January 1 and therefore would be attributed to January 1. This would be a misrepresentation and
could lead to serious inaccuracies.
•
Because of the nature of LIFETIME Measurements, after data is deleted from a Salesforce Social Studio
Data Stream, the deleted data is not accessible for retrieval through reprocessing.
•
In order to reprocess data retrieved in the past without actually deleting any data - for example, to
accommodate changes in mapping - the Re-Run Files option can be used, which is located under the
‘Log’ button in the Data Streams Management menu.
312

=== PAGE 317 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
General Setup
After a Workspace is selected, you can choose to retrieve a single or several Accounts and Topics. Leave
the field empty to retrieve all Accounts and Topics. Social listening data contains a high amount of data
by nature, therefore retrieving many Topics could cause the Data Stream to timeout. If this happens, try
reducing the number of Topics per Data Stream.

This section allows you to define advanced settings for your API connector. The settings are the same for
all API connectors.
Known Issues
Due to Twitter's legal limitations that block the transfer of Twitter posts to third party applications, certain
Twitter Dimensions aren’t retrieved by Marketing Cloud Intelligence. Nevertheless, Measurements
associated with these posts will be retrieved.

•
Click the down arrow to the right of View Results and then click Run Now.
•
Go back to your reports list and click the report you just created.
•
Click Download.
•
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they’re aligned.
Salesforce Social Studio Topic Profiles allows reports to be exported up to 90 days back, while Marketing
Cloud Intelligence pulls all available posts – including those that were created further back than 90 days.
So, when you compare Measurement Totals between Salesforce Topic Profiles and Intelligence, either
compare specific posts, or make sure you’re looking at the same date range.
Use Scheduled Reports to Integrate Social Studio with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Salesforce Social Studio is a Management Center and Social Listening Platform for owned social accounts
on popular social networks, such as Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, Pinterest.
This article goes over the recommended approach when using scheduled reports to push Social Studio
data into Marketing Cloud Intelligence using TotalConnect.
313

=== PAGE 318 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Social Studio API Versus Scheduled Reports: Social Studio API
•
Data ingested via the dedicated Social Studio API returns mentions over the past 30 days as a lifetime
measurement.
•
You would need dedicated Topic Profiles for Social Studio/Intelligence analysis, which would result in
double consumption of Social Studio EMV.
•
Keyed off Social Post ID, which means a 1:1 ratio of mentions vs. row consumption Intelligence
(expensive).
Social Studio API Versus Scheduled Reports: Scheduled Reports from Social
Studio
•
Greater control on data entering Marketing Cloud Intelligence - Can be scheduled from a Keyword
Group level, rather than the entire Topic profile.
•
Historical data can be consumed on the day the mentioned happened allowing for trending analysis.
•
Custom key aggregating post count at the day and custom dimension level - significant reduction in
row consumption.
•
Scheduled reports allow you to pull in the data at a Keyword Group level (KWG), which allows for
Share of Voice or Category trending that the API approach doesn't allow. The data on this dashboard
for scheduled reports is represented by the green segment, whereas the data returned from an API
connection returns all KWGs.
Social Studio Report Data Points: Data Selection from Social Studio
Let’s go over the setup on Social Studio’s end first.
To provide the maximum amount of control for data pushed into Marketing Cloud Intelligence, we’d
recommend using Social Studio reports. Using scheduled reports gives you greater control over the data
ingested into Intelligence, which then impacts the volume of rows consumed.
Selecting ‘CSV’ as the export format of your report allows you to customize the fields that you want set as
part of your report.
Here’s a breakdown of the suggested Social Studio Report Data Points and what they represent:
Social Studio Report Data Points What this data point represents
Language The language the post has been classified
Media Provider of Post Media origin
Post Type Type of engagement, limited to tweets (retweets,
quotes, replies - and so on)
Published Date The date the post/content was published,
different to harvest date that represents when
314

=== PAGE 319 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Social Studio Report Data Points What this data point represents
Social Studio ingested the post into our database.
Region Country of origin
Sentiment Sentiment as classified in Social Studio
Workflow - Author Tag Tags applied via social automate or manually
inside Social Studio engage or analyze.
Workflow - Post Tag Tags applied via social automate or manually
inside Social Studio engage or analyze.
Within the ‘filters’ section we would then set up an individual report for each of the keyword groups that
are set up inside of that topic profile.
A topic profile is a social media search tool created in Social Studio, you can learn more about it here.
It may also be that you don’t want all of the keyword group mentions being passed through into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence and this will be a great way of controlling that data flow without having to
build dedicated Topic Profiles inside of Social Studio which will cause duplicate mentions (which is one
of the primary license charges for Social Studio).
Within the ‘filters’ section we would then set up an individual report for each of the keyword groups that
are set up inside of that topic profile. A topic profile is a social media search tool created in Social Studio,
you can learn more about it here. It may also be that you don’t want all of the keyword group mentions
being passed through into Marketing Cloud Intelligence and this will be a great way of controlling that
data flow without having to build dedicated Topic Profiles inside of Social Studio, which will cause
duplicate mentions (which is one of the primary license charges for Social Studio).
Marketing Cloud Intelligence: Initial Data Mapping
Now that we know how to configure our reports in Social Studio, let’s look at how to ingest those reports
in Intelligence.
If you’ve built your Social Studio reports as suggested above, we’d recommend the following mapping
logic in Intelligence. Select TotalConnect and choose the ‘Social Listening’ data stream type.
Important: In case you have a Social Studio environment that operates across multiple timezones,
ensure that your data load mode is set to ‘Upsert’. The reason for this is that your Social Studio tenant
may have a different timezone set than the country you work in. As a result you’re likely to get some
overlap of data day-on-day, using upsert will overlook data matches and only update new data points.
This is also particularly useful if changes are made to the data inside of Social Studio. Maybe new labels,
author tags or sentiment changes have been made since the initial upload. Having your Social Studio
report set to the last 7 days and the data mode set to upsert, will accommodate these changes to the
data set for the rolling period.

Note Using the ‘FORMATDATE’ function strips out the timestamp, which is part of the Publish_Date
dimension. If you want to view the data on an hourly basis, you can leave this format function out
and just concatenate all Social Mention Fields. However, this would result in a significant increase in
row consumption, which will likely be 1:1 (1 SS mention = 1 Intelligence row). If you do apply the
‘FORMATDATE’ function, the result would be, for example, 4.5 K mentions over a 7 day period = 321
Intelligence rows. If you want to represent multiple keyword groups separately you need to schedule
individual reports by Keyword Group.

Synthesio API Connector
Synthesio is a social intelligence tool that provides the ability to listen to and analyze consumer
conversations across social and mainstream media.
•
Channel: Social Listening
•
Data Stream Type: Social Listening
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to the Synthesio API, you need a valid username and password.
Details
•
The connector retrieves Mentions data that is available under the Listen section in the Synthesio
platform.
•
Breakdowns available under the Analyze section can be generated using the Mentions attributes in the
platform. For example, an equivalent view of the Gender breakdown found in the ‘Demographic’ page
can be created using the ‘Social Mention Author Gender’ Dimension and the ‘Social Mention Count’
Measurement.
•
In some Mentions, some fields may be missing due to copyright rules:
- For Mentions from some websites, the Content field won’t be provided.
- For Twitter mentions, all information provided by Twitter is hidden. Only Synthesio additions are
available.
•
Due to these limitations and the fact that currently, the retrieved data has no corresponding view in
Synthesio, minor inconsistencies may occur.
•
To validate your data, compare it to the data exported from the ‘Mentions (beta)’ section. The
exported file includes all the relevant ‘Mentions’ which is closest to the data retrieved by the API.
General Setup
•
After you’ve logged into your Synthesio account, you can now select the Dashboard and Topics you’re
pulling data for.
•
After you select a Dashboard, all the Topics/Subtopics for that Dashboard appear in the Topics
dropdown list.
•
You can select a single topic, multiple topics, or none if you want to retrieve all the topics and
subtopics for the selected Dashboard.
Retrieving Topics that have a large number of posts can cause timeouts — try reducing the number of
topics per Data Stream.

unique in-app event is mapped to the total conversion Marketing Cloud Intelligence field. Unique
counts are available only for in-app events. Metrics for page events are empty. Values from the total
in-app events are unmapped, and the app_install and real_time_app_install metrics are
empty because they aren’t supported in TikTok.

retrieve data from SKAN in-app event metrics. The data is on the ad-group level.

The data is on the ad-group level.
••
To retrieve website page event stats, select Get Page Conversions Data.
The total event count isn't mapped by default. The unique metric, which is mapped to Total
Conversions by default, is empty for page events.

A Demand-Side Platform (DSP) that allows ad buyer teams to manage data-driven digital advertising

in addition to the standard data being pulled:
- Country - Select this to pull country data and add the Dimension ‘Country’ to the dataset.
- Region - Select this to pull Regional data and add the Dimension ‘Region’ to the dataset.
- Metro - Select this to pull Metro data and add the Dimension ‘Metro’ to the dataset.
- City - Select this to pull City data and add the Dimension ‘City’ to the dataset.
Note
- You can only select one type of Location Breakdown in any given TradeDesk My Reports API
Data Stream; however, each consecutive selection will pull the previously listed dataset. For
example, if you select Region the Data Stream will retrieve both Country and Region, if you
select Metro, the Data Stream will retrieve Country, Region, and Metro, etc.
- After a Location Breakdown is selected, Creative Data and Frequency are not pulled.

The Trade Desk (My Reports) Custom API Connector
The Trade Desk (TTD) is a Demand-Side Platform that allows ad buyer teams to manage data-driven
digital advertising campaigns across various advertising formats, including display, video, and social, on a
multitude of devices. The TTD Custom API connector allows you to retrieve a custom set of
Measurements and Dimensions from the API into Marketing Cloud Intelligence. It enables you to pull
data from any of the TTD (My Reports) reports. While the standard TTD (My Reports) API connector is
preset to be automatically mapped to the Data Model, the Custom connector requires manual setup.
•
Channel: Display, Search
•
Data Stream Type: Ads, Conversion Tag, Generic
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to The TradeDesk My Reports API, you need a valid username, password and Partner ID, that
has access to the My Reports reporting system. To enable API for your user, contact your TTD
representative and request API access for your Partner and third party API access to My Reports.
Details
•
You only need to define the Extended Properties, when pulling a non-standard report.
•
After you’ve defined the TTD (My Reports) Custom API Connector and click Save, you’ll be redirected
to the Mapping screen so you can map the Data Stream. The mapping process is the same as every
other TotalConnect Data Stream. Click here to learn more about Mapping. After you finish mapping
the relevant fields and save, data is retrieved for the period defined in the Initial Data Fetch section of
323

the connector.

Extended Properties
•
Data Stream Type - Select one of the available Data Stream Types you want to map your data to. You
can only select one type of Data Stream Type in any given The Trade Desk Custom API Data Stream.
You can map into the following Data Stream Types:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
- Generic
•

they appear in the TTD platform. This field is mandatory.
•

as they appear in the TTD platform. This field is only mandatory when mapping to the Ads Data
Stream Type. If any other Data Stream Type is selected, this field is ignored.
•
Report Conversion - Insert the Conversion Tags you want to retrieve, separated by a comma. When
inserting the Conversion Tags name, only include the tag name, without the tag number. It’s important
to insert ‘Conversion Tag Name’ into the Report Dimensions text box when retrieving Report
Conversions.
This field is only mandatory when mapping to the Conversion Tag Data Stream Type. If any other Data
Stream Type is selected, this field is ignored.

X Ads API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use the X Ads API connector to pull data that helps you target your audiences and promote tweet
campaigns. X Ads is a display channel connector.
The data stream type is ads and conversions, and the data lag is 1 day.
Additional Information on X Ads
•
If you want to pull Cards data, you must have access to view cards in your X account.
•
The sliding window for the X Ads API connector is 7.
•
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, the values displayed for Impressions, Social Follows, and Total
Engagements measurements are likely higher than the values displayed in the X Ads platform and
reporting due to limitations on the X platform.
- In X, there are three Impression measurements and three Follows measurements, and they include
subsets of these measurements.
- Impressions:
- promoted_account_impressions
- promoted_tweet_search_impressions
- promoted_tweet_timeline_impressions
- Follows:
- promoted_account_follows
- promoted_tweet_search_follows
- promoted_tweet_timeline_follows
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, the three Impression measurements are aggregated into one
measurement called Impressions, while the three Follows measurements are aggregated into one
measurement called Social Follows. In the X Ads platform and reporting, you can only see the
promoted_account_impressions and promoted_account_follows.
•
X doesn't support conversion data by device on a Tweet (Ad) level, either on the UI level or the API
level. The implication is that when 'Get Conversion Level Data' is selected, additional segmentation is
set to 'Device', and 'Get Tweet Level Data' is selected, conversion data isn’t populated. The proposed
solution is to create two data streams, one that has the tweet level data checked and another data
stream that has the additional segmentation set to 'Device'.
•
Due to an X Ads known issue, if you see your sales amount value in the millions and the amount isn’t
correct, you must divide the sales amount by 1 million. For more information see this X article.
Create an X Ads Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Create an X Ads data stream to pull ads data from your X accounts.

=== PAGE 336 ===

•
Get Conversions - When checked, an additional Measurement, Tracked Conversion will also be
retrieved and mapped to Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s Total Conversions Measurement using the

Name/Tag Dimension, but rather with the same set of Dimensions used in the Ads Data Stream type
seen above.

data is retrieved and mapped to the Conversion Tags Data Stream Type. When this box is checked, the
Conversion data that is retrieved will override the 'Get Conversions' report.
How to QA Your Data
•

•
After you select the relevant Campaign, you’ll be able to select between different Entities by which to
view your data.
•
When you compare your selected Weborama views to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, make sure you’re
looking at the same Date Range, Dimensions, and Measurements in the view you generate in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence - whether it be a Report, a Widget, or a Pivot Table.
•
Start off by simply comparing the Measurement totals and make sure they are aligned.
•
Next, filter the report for a certain Dimension value on both platforms, and make sure that the
Measurement totals are still aligned.

=== PAGE 340 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Intelligence. The values will be the same as in the Yandex platform.
•
The Total values are different for the Ad level and the Search Keyword level. This is the desired
behavior and is the case both in the Yandex platform and the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
•
Every API user has points that enable it to perform API actions, like pulling reports. The points spent
on pulling a report are determined by the amount of data included in the report.
•
In the Yandex API, when retrieving the basic report on the campaign level only, the API delivers 2
types of measurements for Clicks, Impressions, and Cost:
- A Search Measurement
- A Context Measurement
•
In the Yandex platform, as well as in Marketing Cloud Intelligence, the Measurement values are a sum
of both types of measurements. However, if you require special settings, these Measurements are
available as unmapped in the mapping screen of the data stream.
General Setup
•

you’re pulling data for.

If you’re an Agency client, you can select an Agency and the Advertisers you wish to retrieve data for.
•
If you're an Advertiser client, your Advertiser appears in both the Agency and Advertiser dropdown
lists.

•
Get Granular Data - This retrieves reports at the Media Buy level, and includes Search and Conversions
data. To create an identical view in the Yandex platform, choose a specific campaign to drill down,
then choose 'View Statistics'. For Search Measurements navigate to 'Search Queries', for Delivery
Measurements navigate to 'Report Wizard'. Create a report similar to the view in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.

YouTube Ads API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use YouTube ads to discover videos in various ways: by searching on the YouTube search page, clicking
suggested videos on the watch page, or choosing a video from the homepage feed. You can use YouTube
ads to advertise more effectively to people who are searching for your product or brand. The YouTube
ads connector is a display connector with a data lag of one day.
336

=== PAGE 341 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Additional Information for the YouTube Ads Connector
•
YouTube ads connector pulls data from the Google Ads API.
•
The sliding window for the YouTube Ads API connector is 7.
•
Precision issues could be present in Video Completion measurements resulting in slight discrepancies
in the data.
•
You could experience discrepancies in the video completion percentage (%) Measurement. Marketing
Cloud Intelligence calculates Video Completion % differently than YouTube. YouTube has two
TrueView Video Ad Formats – In-stream and In-discovery (FKA – In-display), and a different calculation
is performed for each format:
- For In-stream: % * impressions / 100
- For In-discovery: % * video views / 100
Marketing Cloud Intelligence calculates both of these formats as In-Stream format.
•
Use an IF formula based on the Media Buy Name to differentiate between the two streams, for
example:
Video Completion 75%:
IF(csv['media_buy_name'] contains 'InDiscovery',
NUMBER(csv['video_completions_75']*csv['video_views']/csv['impressions'],
csv['video_completions_75'])

•
Since the YouTube Public API retrieves public data such as Channel Likes, Channel Views, Video Views
etc., validating your data should be done by comparing it to those Measurements’ values for the
specific element - IE: Channel, Video, and so on - you’re tracking on the YouTube platform.
•
All the Measurements in the YouTube Public API are aggregated as LIFETIME Since this Connector only
retrieves data for the date on which the retrieval takes place, it’s impossible to reprocess historical data
in YouTube Public Data Streams.
Extended Properties
•
Channels: Enter the Channel Name you want to track. You can specify up to five channels. To retrieve
Channel IDs, select Channel IDs and then enter the required IDs. Separate each value with a comma.
Known Issues
•
As the YouTube Public API can only retrieve up to 256 Videos at once, only the 256 most recent Videos
are retrieved every time a YouTube Public API Data Stream runs. This limitation is per Data Stream, so
if you wish to retrieve Channels containing many Videos, choose to pull one Channel per Data Stream.
YouTube Reports API Connector
YouTube Reports
•
Data Lagging: 1 day
Prerequisites
•
YouTube username and password
•
Similar to YouTube Insights, the user should be a channel owner.
Details
•
To validate your data compare it to the relevant view in the YouTube Analytics platform. When in the
YouTube analytics platform, to the left, under the 'Analytics' section, there are three tabs:
- Traffic Sources
- Demographics
- Devices
•
In the Devices tab, there are 2 views: 'Devices' 'Operating Systems'
- 'Devices'
- 'Operating Systems'
•
Select the view that fits the report you’re validating.

=== PAGE 345 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
The Watch Time measurement is aggregated and displayed in minutes format.
How to set up YouTube Reports
11.. Choose the desired report from the list:
••
Traffic Sources - Includes viewing statistics based on the manner in which viewers reached your
video content.
••
Device and Operating System - Includes viewing statistics based on viewers' operating systems and
device types.
••
Viewer Demographics (Age + Gender) - Includes viewing statistics based on viewers' age group and
gender.
••
Playback Locations - Provides statistics related to the type of page or application where video
playbacks occurred.
••
User Activity - Provides user activity statistics related to a channel and its videos.
••
User Activity By Province - Provides user activity statistics for U.S. states and the District of Columbia.
••
Traffic Sources By Video - Includes viewing statistics based on the manner in which viewers reached
your video content per video.
••
Viewer Demographics By Video - Includes viewing statistics based on viewers' age group and gender
per video.
••
Device Type And Operating System By Video - Includes viewing statistics based on viewers'
operating systems and device types per video.
••
Playback Locations By Video - Provides statistics related to the type of page or application where
video playbacks occurred per video.
22.. Select the Report Context - Video or Playlist. The report context determines the scope of the data. For
instance, 'Playlist' will return data relevant to views made in the context of a playlist.
33.. Select the Data Stream Type - The Traffic Sources report can be mapped using the Social Element
Traffic Sources Data Stream Type. The Viewer Demographics and Device and OS reports include many
to many entities, it’s advised that you map them using the Generic Data Stream Type.

Creating Data Streams with LiteConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With LiteConnect, you can upload a file into Marketing Cloud Intelligence as is and visualize the data
automatically. You don’t need to consider the predefined structure and constraints of the data model or
map fields.
Use LiteConnect when you have a single data source and no harmonization is required. If you have
multiple data sources that must be harmonized, use other upload methods, such as TotalConnect or API
Connectors.
345

When uploading data, be aware of the data limits:
•
Direct upload: 40 MB / 150 million rows (subsequent)
• Email: 30 МB / 150 million rows (subsequent)
•
Retrieval: 500 GB / 150 million rows (subsequent)
Create a Data Stream with LiteConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Create a LiteConnect data stream to upload a single data source as is without any manipulations. You
can upload any flat file.

You can harmonize or integrate LiteConnect data streams with other data streams using Vlookup, data
fusion, or calculated dimensions. However, LiteConnect data streams are naturally harmonized at the
date level, so you can consolidate measurements by date.

Date Values in LiteConnect Data Streams
A LiteConnect data stream has two date columns. The date changes according to the source data.
The DAY column (1) contains the date value as interpreted by Marketing Cloud Intelligence. You can’t
edit this column. The DATE column (2) is the actual date column that comes from the source file.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence automatically assigns the DAY value to the first date column that it finds in
the source file.
If the source file has more than one date column, select a different date to go by instead of the one that
Marketing Cloud Intelligence selected.
If the uploaded source file doesn’t have a date column but has measurements, the Yesterday day value is
automatically assigned. A measurement must have a date.
If the uploaded file contains only dimensions, the day value is empty because it isn’t required.
Harmonizing LiteConnect with Other Data Sources
You can harmonize or integrate LiteConnect data streams with other data streams using Vlookup, data
fusion, or calculated dimensions. However, LiteConnect data streams are naturally harmonized at the
date level, so you can consolidate measurements by date.
Vlookup
With Vlookup, you can identify like values across data streams to populate one data stream with data
from another. For example, a pizza store wants to cross-match its delivery data from Facebook with
weather data to see if there’s a correlation. They can run the Vlookup command from the Facebook data
stream to find similar dates in the LiteConnect weather data. They can then pull the weather conditions
for matching date values. In the mapping of the API data stream, they can use the Strategy entity to
house this formula:
VLOOKUP(csv['Facebook Date], [‘Gsom Date’], [‘conditions’],true,false,{'Gsom
Weather'})
The pizza store created a widget to display the data.
348

=== PAGE 353 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can also source Vlookup in LiteConnect. For example, you can include the Vlookup command in a
formula in a LiteConnect data stream header to pull data from another data stream.
Data Fusion
Data fusion creates a link between two dimensions from different data stream types based on identical
values. You can unify identical campaign values and measurements from different data streams in a
single widget. Data fusion is easier to set up than Vlookup. It’s more appropriate for unifying
measurements from different data streams rather than pulling dimension values, which is done using
Vlookup.
For example, you have a LiteConnect data stream with survey data, and you want to match identical
values in a Facebook API data stream. You can then create a widget with the survey figures from
LiteConnect and the delivery figures from Facebook.
Calculated Dimensions
Use calculated dimensions for multiple data source unification. For example, let’s use the Vlookup
weather data and Facebook delivery data. If a calculated dimension is conditioned on the presence of W
or T in either dimension and assigns a new value accordingly, one value is created that is true for both
sources and unifies them.
Dimension from weather data:
Dimension from Facebook delivery data:
Here’s an example of a calculated dimension based on this principle:
if([EMSN_ATTRIBUTES] contains 'W' && [EMSN_] contains 'T')
{
return 'WT Activity';
}
else
{
if([Campaign_Name] contains 'W' && [Campaign_Name] contains 'T')
{
return 'WT Activity';
}
else
349

=== PAGE 354 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
{
return 'W Activity';
}
}
When viewing this calculated dimension in a widget, you can see metrics for either source per calculated
dimension.
Create Data Streams with DirectConnect
DirectConnect is a premium feature that allows you to retrieve data directly from an external database,
without having to store the data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s database. DirectConnect is used in
cases where you have data sensitivity issues, or you want to display and interact with your data from an
external source instead.
Whenever an instance relating to this source is initiated, data in DirectConnect is queried and retrieved
live from the external database. For example, when loading a widget that uses at least one DirectConnect
dimension or measurement, the data is visible and accessible for the duration of that instance only.
Unlike TotalConnect or LiteConnect connections, no data is retrieved with DirectConnect, therefore no
rows are added. Although data isn’t stored in Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s database, you can still use
data from DirectConnect in calculations and in data classifications. These external databases are
supported in DirectConnect:
•
PostgreSQL
•
Redshift
•
Vertica
•
Presto
•
AWS
•
Athena
•
MySQL
•
SQL
•
Server Snowflake
Create a Data Stream with DirectConnect
Use DirectConnect to create a data stream and retrieve data directly from an external database. You
can either configure a database, or connect to a database manually with a user and password. After
connecting to the database and testing the connection, you can create a query to determine how data
from the database is represented in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. Make sure the scope of the query is
as close to the consumption requirements as possible for faster processing and performance.
Connection and Performance Optimization in DirectConnect Data Streams
Setting up a DirectConnect data stream establishes a real-time connection to your database. Each
time an entity that uses dimensions or measurements sourced from DirectConnect is loaded, your
database is queried in real time. A real-time connection can potentially result in many calls being
350

=== PAGE 355 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
made to your database concurrently, leading to slower performance and exceeding rate limits.
Create a Data Stream with DirectConnect
Use DirectConnect to create a data stream and retrieve data directly from an external database. You can
either configure a database, or connect to a database manually with a user and password. After
connecting to the database and testing the connection, you can create a query to determine how data
from the database is represented in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. Make sure the scope of the query is as
close to the consumption requirements as possible for faster processing and performance.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED

99.. In the Configure tab, enter a name for the table. Make sure the table name is short, yet descriptive.
1100.. In the Data Type section, make sure that the dimensions and measurements were identified correctly.
1111.. To validate a dimension or measurement, hover over a category or an individual column name. The
corresponding field is highlighted in the table.

the Connect tab, edit the connection string.

Performance in DirectConnect Data Streams

See Also
Connection and Performance Optimization in DirectConnect Data Streams
Connection and Performance Optimization in DirectConnect Data Streams
Setting up a DirectConnect data stream establishes a real-time connection to your database. Each time
an entity that uses dimensions or measurements sourced from DirectConnect is loaded, your database is
queried in real time. A real-time connection can potentially result in many calls being made to your
351

=== PAGE 356 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
database concurrently, leading to slower performance and exceeding rate limits.
Use the data stream’s cache functionality to improve performance and temporarily save each query
result for the defined cache duration. Whenever you load the same entity, the data is retrieved from the
cache, rather than making an additional call to the external database.
Salesforce recommends setting the cache duration based on how frequently your database is updated.
For example, set the cache duration to 20 minutes if the database updates every 20 minutes. Setting to
any amount of time less than 20 minutes, can result in no data retrieval.
Pull Data 360 Data into Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data 360 unifies your data from multiple sources so that you can build a comprehensive view of your
customer. Pull in a small amount of data with TotalConnect or try the Granular Data Center connector for
larger amounts. You can also install the Data Cloud app to get predefined insights.
Note Not supported in Government Cloud. See Data 360 edition availability
Set Up Access to Pull Data 360 Data
Before you can pull in data from Data 360 into Marketing Cloud Intelligence, authenticate access
between the apps.
Use TotalConnect to Pull Data 360 Data into Intelligence
If you have Data 360 data and you want to bring it into Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can use the
TotalConnect connector. With this connector you can fetch up to 50,000 rows per query and run up to
1,000 queries per day. After you connect your data, a data stream is created and you can then get
insights on your data.
Use the Granular Data Center to Pull Data 360 Data into Intelligence
To analyze a lot of data, use the Granular Data Center connector to pull in your data. You can query up
to 15 million rows at one time.

Before you can pull in data from Data 360 into Marketing Cloud Intelligence, authenticate access
between the apps.

Mapping Your Source Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Mapping connects the source fields in your uploaded file to the corresponding Marketing Cloud
Intelligence fields. Intelligence uses machine learning algorithms to map your data. The mapping process
is similar regardless of which method you use to upload your data, with some minor differences. If you’re
using an API connector to upload your data, Marketing Cloud Intelligence automatically maps the fields
for you, but you can make changes to the mapping. If you’re using TotalConnect, Intelligence identifies
the most suitable data stream type according to your data and offers an initial mapping suggestion.
View How Your Data Is Mapped in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
355

=== PAGE 360 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
On the mapping page, you can see all the fields that were mapped and unmapped in your data
stream.
Map a Source Field to a Marketing Cloud Intelligence Field
Map source fields to Marketing Cloud Intelligence fields to connect the data and create a match
between your source data and Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Change the Data Stream Type
All data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence is categorized using data steam types. A data stream type is
assigned automatically when data is uploaded. For example, if the data includes advertising data, such
as clicks, impressions, and campaigns, Marketing Cloud Intelligence applies the Ads data stream type.
You can change the data stream type if it isn’t correct, or use a different type.
Customize a Field
You can customize an existing data model field.
Use the Formula Editor to Map
You can apply formulas directly to your field mappings to perform complex manipulations of your
data. The formulas are intuitive and use a similar syntax found in Excel. For more complex
manipulations, you can also use MVEL, a Java-based expression language. You apply formulas during
the mapping stage when creating data streams.
Mapping Actions
There are some handy actions you can apply when mapping. Some appear in the toolbar, while others
can be found in the More Actions dropdown list.
Reach Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Reach represents the number of unique visitors exposed to your content over a specified time period.
Reach differs from impressions, which includes multiple views by the same visitor.
Mapping Validations
TotalConnect and API data streams offer enhanced mapping validations during the Map to Model
stage of data ingestion. The mapping validations allow you to check if there are errors or irregularities
with your mapping. They also provide details on how you can rectify errors.
Mapping a File With No Date Column
To map a file with measurement data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Platform, the file must contain a
date column. The platform automatically identifies and pulls date information. If a date isn’t included
the file can’t be uploaded.
Mapping Formulas
Use the mapping formula editor to apply formulas directly in your mapping fields. Using functions in
your mapping formulas, such as text and date functions, allows you to perform more complex
manipulations to your data.
Date Format Syntax
When setting up date formats in TotalConnect, you may need to convert textual data to dates using
formulas. The PARSEDATE function can be used for the conversion. The function receives text
containing the date information, and an expression that specifies how to construct the date using the
text.
Formula Operators
A formula is a mathematical relationship or rule expressed in symbols. With Marketing Cloud
356

=== PAGE 361 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Intelligence, you can use formulas to create calculated measurements and dimensions when mapping
your data. Operators specify the type of calculation to perform on the terms in a formula—such as
addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.
Mapping Functions in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Functions are used when creating mapping formulas, allowing you to perform complex manipulations
to your data. These functions are available in Marketing Cloud Intelligence and are divided into
different groups to easily search for the function you’re looking for.
Control Flow Operators
Marketing Cloud Intelligence supports an assortment of control flow operators, so you can perform
advanced scripting operations. Aside from the custom functions that are supported in the formula
editor, you can write formulas based in C and Java Script syntax to make more complex decisions.
Mapping Geolocation Dimensions in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Geolocation refers to the geographical location of an internet-connected device. Found from the user's
IP address, geolocation includes information about the device user's location, such as country, region,
and city. After you map geolocation, you can create a Map widget so that you can see performance
broken down by region.
View How Your Data Is Mapped in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
On the mapping page, you can see all the fields that were mapped and unmapped in your data stream.

The Formula Editor contains these areas:
11.. Edit Area — Enter the formula. You can type your formula manually or select one from the available
functions or fields.
22.. Functions/Fields — Select a function or field from the list. These types are available:
••
Functions – A list of available functions that can be implemented on dimensions, measurements,
native values, and variables.
••
External fields – A list of all fields from the source file that were uploaded to the data stream.
••
Data model fields – A list of all fields available in the data stream type of the chosen data stream,
listed with their system native name. These fields can be used with dimensions or measurements
that already have a formula in the mapping. A value is given after the formula is processed instead
of the native CSV value from the original file. This value enhances ETL performance because it
doesn’t require more processing.
Note Only mapped fields can be used as Marketing Cloud Intelligence fields.
The practical difference between the two types of fields in the mapping stage is:
••
Csv[…] =pre-formula — Refers to the external fields and appears in this format. The value is
taken as it exists in the source file before any formula manipulations were performed.
••
Dat[…] = post-formula — Refers to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence field and appears in this
format. This field takes the value after the formula is applied. For example, if you map Media Buy
Name, you can use dat[media_buy_name] in another field formula without writing or evaluating
it again.
33.. Additional Options – These options are also available:
••
Auto Format Code – Formats your code into the standard structure.
Before Auto Format:

After Auto Format
••
Comment Out — Allows you to mark a comment in the code for the formula. This code is processed,
even if it has dimensions or measurements.
••
Ucomment — Allows you to remove the comment from the code.
••
Validate Syntax — Ensures that your syntax is free of errors.
••

Mapping Actions
There are some handy actions you can apply when mapping. Some appear in the toolbar, while others
can be found in the More Actions dropdown list.
•
Unmap all fields — Unmap all your mapped fields.
•
Rename all model fields — Change the display name of all the data model fields to their external
names.
•
Download mapping summary — Download a summary of all the mapped fields to an Excel file.
•
Preview — Preview how your mapped data looks after it’s done.
Note
- Preview isn’t available for API data streams.
- The preview shows the top 100 rows of data.
- Any changes made to the mapping are automatically applied to the preview after the system
validates that the mapping is correct. If any actions are made to the preview table (sort, filter,
group, and so on), the data in the table isn’t refreshed,
•
Validate — Check if all of your mapped fields are working as expected. Here you can see if there are
any errors or irregularities with the way your mapping is set up.
Note The system automatically validates your mapping after you save mapping changes.
Reach Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Reach represents the number of unique visitors exposed to your content over a specified time period.
Reach differs from impressions, which includes multiple views by the same visitor.
You can't sum Reach data. For example, when analyzing reach in 7-day increments, a user can view your
content twice in one week and twice the following week. Each week, the user is counted as a single user,
but you can’t add them up because the sum no longer conveys a unique user for the specified time
period. Total Reach isn’t a sum of reach calculated in shorter increments. Instead, total reach represents
361

=== PAGE 366 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
the reach of a page or an individual piece of content in a specific time-frame that includes various types
of reach. Cookie- level data is not available when ingesting specific data into Intelligence. To ensure that
your report displays the correct data, aggregate your data at the granularity that you want the report to
reflect.
Reporting Reach at Multiple Entity Levels
To see reach at multiple levels or different frequencies in Intelligence for example, daily, weekly, or
monthly, you create multiple APIs or TotalConnect data streams, differing the entity dimensions or
date range per data stream.
Processing Historical Data for Reach Data Streams
When manually processing data streams, Marketing Cloud Intelligence doesn’t consider the sliding
window. Data needs to be processed in one month increments to ensure integrity of reach data is not
compromised. You have to be conscious of how your regular retrieval is set up - to ensure that the
newly loaded historical data and what you will regularly receive in the data stream are even
comparable.
Field Mapping for Reach Data Streams
It’s important to map the day and reach fields correctly in a reach data stream. The mapping can vary
depending on the granularity that you’re setting up. Only map the required fields.
Reporting Reach at Multiple Entity Levels
To see reach at multiple levels or different frequencies in Intelligence for example, daily, weekly, or
monthly, you create multiple APIs or TotalConnect data streams, differing the entity dimensions or date
range per data stream.
To use one reach value across entities, you can create a calculated dimension using the CONTEXT
functions. CONTEXT functions return different measurements based on the dimensional values in the
query. With CONTEXT, you can use one measurement regardless of the dimensional or date range level
in a table or pivot—for example, a pivot table reporting Campaign Reach at the Campaign level, and
Media Buy Reach at the Media Buy level.
To create a calculated measurement using a CONTEXT_DIMENSIONS() formula:
if(CONTEXT_DIMENSIONS () contains ‘ [Media_Buy_Name] ’ ) {
return [Media_Buy_Reach];
}
else if (CONTEXT_DIMENSIONS () contains ‘[Campaign_Name]’ ){
return [Campaign_Reach];
}
NOTE - To pull reach beyond 31 days and up to 90 days, your account must have an API User. API User is
a premium feature available for purchase.
362

=== PAGE 367 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Processing Historical Data for Reach Data Streams
When manually processing data streams, Marketing Cloud Intelligence doesn’t consider the sliding
window. Data needs to be processed in one month increments to ensure integrity of reach data is not
compromised. You have to be conscious of how your regular retrieval is set up - to ensure that the newly
loaded historical data and what you will regularly receive in the data stream are even comparable.
When processing historical data:
•
Consider the sliding window date range, and select the entire date range for each date you process.
For example, if you want to process reach data for April 2, 2022, and you have a sliding window of 31
days, select 31 days before April 2, 2022:03/02/2022 to 04/02/2022.
•
To get a reach value for each individual date, manually set the sliding window for each day. For
example, process reach for April 1, 2022, select the date ranges from March 1, 2022, to April 1, 2022
(31 days).
Field Mapping for Reach Data Streams
It’s important to map the day and reach fields correctly in a reach data stream. The mapping can vary
depending on the granularity that you’re setting up. Only map the required fields.
Mapping the Day field for reach data:
•
For a 31-day granularity, the formula for the day field is:
Day = csv['fb.date_stop']
•
For a 7-day granularity, the formula for the day field is:
Day = var date = csv['fb.date_stop'];
var dayofweek = FORMATDATE(date, 'u');
if(dayofweek != 1)
{diff = dayofweek-1;
return date = DATEADD('d',-diff,date);}
else {return date;}
Mapping the Reach measurement for reach data:
•
Reach = csv['fb.reach']
Mapping Validations
TotalConnect and API data streams offer enhanced mapping validations during the Map to Model stage
of data ingestion. The mapping validations allow you to check if there are errors or irregularities with your
mapping. They also provide details on how you can rectify errors.
363

=== PAGE 368 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Three validation messages can appear during validation: mapping errors, mapping warnings, and model
relationship errors. Errors are in red and warnings are in yellow. You can see each of these messages in
the pop-up on the left of your mapping, under Validations.
Validation Mapping
Mapping errors indicate that one or more of your mapped fields has critical mapping issues that must be
fixed before you can save your data stream. To get more information and see how to fix an error, click the
error on the left side.
Here’s an example of a mapping error indicating that an entity key mapping is missing:
This error occurs when you have mapped attributes, but you haven’t mapped the entity key. In the above
example, you have mapped the Media Buy Size attribute but you’re missing the Media Buy Key or Media
Buy Name. In order to fix this error, you must map either the Media Buy Key or Media Buy Name.
Mapping errors also show you when there’s an issue with the syntax of a mapping formula you added.
Here’s an example of a mapping formula that’s missing a parenthesis at the end:
Note You can’t save a data stream mapping until you rectify its mapping errors.
Validation Mapping Warnings
Mapping warnings indicate less critical scenarios and affect data in your data stream. Unlike mapping
errors, which prevent you from saving a data stream, you can save your data stream with mapping
warnings.
Here’s an example of a mapping warning that indicates an external field that doesn’t exist in the source
file was mapped to a data model field:
If a measurement field doesn't exist in the source file, it’s replaced with nulls whenever not present. This
scenario doesn’t cause any critical issues, but it’s something that to be aware of.
364

=== PAGE 369 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Validation Model Relationship Errors
Model relationship errors indicate that there are critical entity-relationship errors with one or more of
your mapped fields that must be fixed before you can save your data stream. For example, the “Key
attribute relationship error” indicates that there are multiple Attribute Display Name values associated
with the same Key Display Name value, which causes data aggregation issues.
To get more information about a model relationship error and how to fix it, click the error on the left
side.
Validation After Fixing Errors
After you address mapping errors or warnings, you can ensure that your new mapping is correct by
clicking “Check for new issues”. As soon as you see the “No mapping issues were found” message, you
can rest assured that the changes you made rectified any mapping errors or warnings.
Mapping a File With No Date Column
To map a file with measurement data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Platform, the file must contain a
date column. The platform automatically identifies and pulls date information. If a date isn’t included the
file can’t be uploaded.
In some cases, the date appears in the file name. For example, a file named ‘File_06-01-2022’ contains a
date in the file name but not as a date column in the data. To use the file name as a date column, you
can apply a formula to the file and extract the date from the file name.
Extracting a Date From the File Name
If your file includes a date in the file name but not in the file itself, you can extract the date field from
the file name and use it as the date field.
Adding a Date in the Mapping Process
If your file doesn’t include a date in any of the fields, you can add a date in the mapping process,
without having an existing date field in your file.
Extracting a Date From the File Name
If your file includes a date in the file name but not in the file itself, you can extract the date field from the
file name and use it as the date field.
