=== PAGE 485 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
Measurements
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, measurements are quantitative numerical data that you can count, for
example, number of clicks and emails opened. Measurements are used to set KPIs and analyze your
data.
The Measurement list, found in the Connect & Mix tab under the measurement menu, shows which
measurements the data model uses and the entities the measurements are associated with.
The available measurement types are: Currency, Number, Duration, and Percentage.
The available aggregation types are: AVG, COUNT, FIRST, LAST, LIFETIME, MAX, MIN, and SUM.
From the Measurement list, you can:
•
Export the measurement list.
•
Edit the measurement display names, type, and aggregation.
•
Replace a measurement across your workspace.
•
View which data streams the measurement is mapped to.
•
View which measurements are mapped and unmapped.
Note If a calculated measurement is inherited from another workspace, you can edit it only in the
original workspace.
Calculated Measurements
A calculated measurement is a numerical custom calculation that you can create on top of your
existing data. The calculated measurement doesn’t physically exist in the database. Rather, it
represents a query that you can reference from any report, pivot table, or widget in your dashboard
pages.
Filtered Measurements
A filtered measurement is used to customize an existing measurement to include information relating
to a specific dimension attribute.
Calculated Measurements
A calculated measurement is a numerical custom calculation that you can create on top of your existing
data. The calculated measurement doesn’t physically exist in the database. Rather, it represents a query
that you can reference from any report, pivot table, or widget in your dashboard pages.
For example, you want to view your return on ad spend (ROAS) to better analyze your campaign
performance. Instead of manually calculating the ROAS per campaign, create a calculated measurement
to extract this information from your data and view it in your campaign’s goals or dashboards.
Create a Calculated Measurement From The Calculated Measurement Tab
Create a calculated measurement on top of existing data that you can refer to across your workspace.
481

=== PAGE 486 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
Create a Calculated Measurement from a Widget
Create calculated measurements on top of existing data so you can refer to them across your
workspace. Widgets can be used to display the calculated measurement data.
Aggregation and Total Aggregation Functions
An aggregation function computes numerical calculations on data, allowing the query to return
summarized information about a given column or result set. You can use an aggregation function to
calculate each row at the dimension level or the total value. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you
define the aggregation function when creating a calculated measurement.
Granularity in Calculated Measurements
Granularity is the level of detail considered and then represented in an analysis or query. The greater
the granularity, the deeper the level of detail. With more detail, you can better understand the smaller
components of your data and create a fuller picture.
System Calculated Measurement
Marketing Cloud Intelligence calculates popular industry measurements that you can use as data in
widgets, reports, and pivot tables. The measurements are calculated using fixed formulas using your
workspace’s data. A measurement is available only if its formula’s components are mapped in an
existing data stream. For example, if you uploaded a data source with MTC data, the MTC system
calculated measurements appear. This table describes the available system calculated measurements
and their formulas.
Calculated Measurement Functions
Use functions when creating a calculated measurement to make your functions more dynamic.
Create a Calculated Measurement From The Calculated Measurement Tab
Create a calculated measurement on top of existing data that you can refer to across your workspace.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and manage calculated measurements: Power User or Admin
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, expand Measurements, and then expand Calculated Measurements.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. In the General section, define the calculated measurement.
When setting the decimal places, the more places you specify, the more exact the calculation.
44.. In the Advanced section define advanced settings.
aa.. For Total Aggregation Function, select the function to calculate the total value of a query. For
example, SUM, AVG, MIN, or MAX. Values that appear in separate rows of a display are all
aggregated in the Total row. The Total Aggregation Function and the Aggregation Function—set in
the expression section— should be the same.
bb.. Set the granularity—Determine the level the calculations for the measurement takes place.
cc.. To let null values be used in the formulas,, select Treat Nulls as Zeros
dd.. To include the date in the granularity of the calculation, select Include Date in Granularity
482

=== PAGE 487 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
55.. In the Expression section, define the expression:
aa.. Set the aggregation function to determine the calculation result for all the produced rows. When a
granularity is set, the underlying calculation produces multiple calculation results for multiple rows.
These rows must be aggregated into a single row of the displayed dimension.
bb.. Create the expression that you want to base the calculated measurement on using the list of
available measurements and functions. For example, if you want to calculate email open rate that
uses email sends as the denominator instead of email deliveries, use this expression:
IFERROR([Email_Unique_Opens]/[Email_Sends], null) The IFERROR function results in
the calculated measurement returning null values when the email sends equal to 0.
cc.. To add a function to your expression, click it, or start typing for auto-complete.
66.. Click Save.
Create a Calculated Measurement from a Widget
Create calculated measurements on top of existing data so you can refer to them across your workspace.
Widgets can be used to display the calculated measurement data.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and manage calculated measurements: Power User or Admin
11.. On the Visualize tab, click a dashboard page.
22.. Click the pencil icon to enter edit mode.
33.. To add a widget, click the plus icon.
44.. Select the widget type, and then click the plus icon to add a field.
55.. Click the Add Calculated Field icon , and then click Calculated Measurement.
66.. In the General section, define the calculated measurement.
When setting the decimal places, the more places you specify, the more exact the calculation.
77.. In the Advanced section define advanced settings.
aa.. For Total Aggregation Function, select the function to calculate the total value of a query. For
example, SUM, AVG, MIN, or MAX. Values that appear in separate rows of a display are all
aggregated in the Total row. The Total Aggregation Function and the Aggregation Function—set in
the expression section— should be the same.
bb.. Set the granularity—Determine the level the calculations for the measurement takes place.
cc.. To let null values be used in the formulas, select Treat Nulls as Zeros
dd.. To include the date in the granularity of the calculation, select Include Date in Granularity
88.. In the Expression section, define the expression:
aa.. Set the aggregation function to determine the calculation result for all the produced rows. When a
granularity is set, the underlying calculation produces multiple calculation results for multiple rows.
These rows must be aggregated into a single row of the displayed dimension.
bb.. Create the expression that you want to base the calculated measurement on using the list of
available measurements and functions. For example, if you want to calculate email open rate that
uses email sends as the denominator instead of email deliveries, use this expression:
483

=== PAGE 488 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
IFERROR([Email_Unique_Opens]/[Email_Sends], null) The IFERROR function results in
the calculated measurement returning null values when the email sends equal to 0.
cc.. To add a function to your expression, click it, or start typing for auto-complete.
99.. Click Save.
Aggregation and Total Aggregation Functions
An aggregation function computes numerical calculations on data, allowing the query to return
summarized information about a given column or result set. You can use an aggregation function to
calculate each row at the dimension level or the total value. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you define
the aggregation function when creating a calculated measurement.
For example, to perform a different calculation per row, such as getting the average value for each
dimension while summing all the values in the total row, set the aggregation function to AVG and the
total aggregation function to SUM to sum the values in the different rows.
Use Case
You can use both the aggregated function and the total aggregation function in cases where you have a
calculated measurement with a formula that includes a dimension with an aggregation function set to
Auto, and the total value in the query for this calculated measurement is zero. The reason there’s no total
value is because the total row doesn’t include the formulas’ required dimension for this calculated
measurement. To solve this issue and display a total value, you can set a total aggregation function to
display a total value based on the value of each row in the query.
Granularity in Calculated Measurements
Granularity is the level of detail considered and then represented in an analysis or query. The greater the
granularity, the deeper the level of detail. With more detail, you can better understand the smaller
components of your data and create a fuller picture.
For example, you have a calculated measurement that calculates cost per click (CPC) per media buy. The
granularity is set to the media buy dimension, and the calculation is done on the values of each media
buy key. By setting the granularity of the calculated measurement to a lower level, such as the creative
level, the calculation now occurs at the creative key level when slicing the calculated measurement on
any relevant dimension.
System Calculated Measurement
Marketing Cloud Intelligence calculates popular industry measurements that you can use as data in
widgets, reports, and pivot tables. The measurements are calculated using fixed formulas using your
workspace’s data. A measurement is available only if its formula’s components are mapped in an existing
data stream. For example, if you uploaded a data source with MTC data, the MTC system calculated
measurements appear. This table describes the available system calculated measurements and their

Aggregation Functions
All aggregation functions look at a data range, as opposed to a single row. You can specify the data range
you want the calculated measurement to consider by defining the set of rows that the function operates
on, which is called a window function.
You can define a window function in two ways.
•
Use GroupByDims within your function, for example SUM([metric],[[groupByDims]])
•
Use a syntax that lets you choose in which row the function begins to apply and where it stops, such as
SUM(metric, first_index(), last_index())
If you don’t define the window function, aggregation functions consider all the data included in the
query.
Function Name Syntax Description

orderByDim, sortOrder) the metric grouped by the
dimensions, ordered by the
orderByDim with the given
sortOrder (‘ASC’ or ‘DESC’). For
example, ACC(‘Clicks’, [‘Media
Buy Key’], ‘Day’, ‘ASC’).
ACC ACC(metric, orderByDim, Gives an accumulative sum of
sortOrder, first_index(), the metric ordered by the
last_index()) orderByDim with the given
sortOrder (‘ASC’ or ‘DESC’). The
window is defined as an offset
from the current row. Use

AND AND(condition1, condition2) Logical AND, checks to see that
both conditions are true.
IF IF(condition,resultifTrue,resultifF Returns the second argument if
alse) the first argument is true, and
the third argument if otherwise.
IFERROR IFERROR(someExpression, Returns the someExpression if
number) it’s a valid number. Otherwise,
returns a number.
NOT NOT(condition) Logical NOT, reverses the logic of
its argument: true becomes false
and vice versa.
OR OR(condition) Logical OR, checks to see if
either one of the conditions are
true.

Filtered Measurements
A filtered measurement is used to customize an existing measurement to include information relating to
a specific dimension attribute.
For example, if you want to display impressions from an ads verification source, you can create a filtered
measurement based on impressions filtered only by the ads verification source. The filtered
measurement is a separate measurement and doesn’t affect the original measurement it’s based on.
Filtered measurements can only be based on system measurements, not calculated measurements.
Create filtered measurements with the Measurements tab, or inside a widget.
Create a Filtered Measurement
504

=== PAGE 509 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
Create a filtered measurement to customize a measurement to include specific information correlating
to certain dimensional attributes. For example, you want to display your impressions only from an ads
verification source. You can create a filtered measurement based on impressions that is filtered for
only the ads verification source. The filtered measurement is a separate measurement and does not
affect the original measurement it is based on. Filtered measurements can only be based on system
measurements, not calculated measurements.
Create a Filtered Measurement from a Widget
A filtered measurement is used to customize a measurement to include information relating to a
specific dimension attribute. You can create a filtered measurement directly from within a widget.

Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers many tools so you can get a real-time view of all your marketing data
in one place. You get everything from standard key performance indicator (KPI) reporting to complex
visualizations required for audience segmentation, customer journey analytics, and predictive modeling.
And it’s all done in the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Get Started
To get started, learn about visualization elements (collections, pages, and widgets), visualization tools,
and how to navigate the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Collections
After you connect your data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can start to organize and manage
your data for the different departments of your organization—for example, delivery data, cost data, or
social impact. The relevant data for each department is managed in separate visualization entities or
collections.
Pages
Pages segment your collections so you can divide up your data according to your preferences, such as
products, campaigns, or social platforms. Each page can be customized and filtered, so you can
modify the design and remove any unwanted data. To assist you in dividing the visualization into
manageable sections, you can have multiple pages within a collection.
506

=== PAGE 511 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Widgets
Widgets are the graphic elements that appear on the page and display your data through pie charts,
tables, and bar graphs. Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers multiple widget types: interactive, element,
and custom.
SmartLenses in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
SmartLenses are predefined dashboard pages that are created for you after a supported data source is
created for the first time. After the data source is processed and data is retrieved, a notification
appears with a link to the SmartLenses dashboard page. All SmartLenses dashboard pages are hosted
under one designated SmartLenses collection.
Share and Export Pages and Widgets
After you’re done creating and designing your page, you’re ready to publish and share it with others.
Use the Share option to share pages and widgets from the same place.
Get Started
To get started, learn about visualization elements (collections, pages, and widgets), visualization tools,
and how to navigate the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Get to Know Visualization Elements
The visualization process in Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three elements: collections, pages,
and widgets.
Get to Know Visualize Tools
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers an array of different tools to help you create professional, eye-
catching dashboards quickly and efficiently. View your data clearly and share it using multiple formats.
Navigate the Visualize Tab
Go to the Visualize tab to familiarize yourself with the available options and to find all the visualize
elements. The Visualize tab is dynamic and changes when you select different elements.
Get to Know Visualization Elements
The visualization process in Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three elements: collections, pages,
and widgets.
Collections
Collections synergize all your data and consume it in one place. No matter where data is coming
from—a data stream or an app—you can visualize, organize, and personalize it all together in a
collection.
Pages
Pages visualize your data. You can have multiple pages within your collection to assist you in
dividing the visualization into manageable sections. These page types are available in Marketing
Cloud Intelligence:
•
Blank dashboard pages – Used to create visualizations of your data without any predefined

settings or design.
•
Dashboard templates – Pages that are created based on a dashboard template saved to the
workspace, allowing you to replicate your dashboard pages across your collections and
workspace.
•
App pages –- Pages that display all the valuable data generated from an installed app, enabling
you to visualize and customize a marketplace app all within your collection.
Widgets
Widgets are the graphic elements that appear on the page, such as pie charts, tables, and bar
graphs.

Get to Know Visualize Tools
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers an array of different tools to help you create professional, eye-
catching dashboards quickly and efficiently. View your data clearly and share it using multiple formats.
•
Widgets – Use the vast library of widgets to bring your data to life.
•

relevant to you.
•
Themes – Create expressive visualizations and customize them with the utmost granular control.
•
InstaBrand – Generate beautiful, branded dashboard themes based on a logo or image uploaded by
you.
•
Export – Share your data with multiple stakeholders in an assortment of different formats.
Navigate the Visualize Tab
Go to the Visualize tab to familiarize yourself with the available options and to find all the visualize
elements. The Visualize tab is dynamic and changes when you select different elements.
•
The left page offers your editing options. Here, you see all the relevant tabs for the setting level you’re
in—whether you’re setting up a widget or designing the page.
•
The top bar is where you can administer page-level settings and actions.

data for the different departments of your organization—for example, delivery data, cost data, or social
impact. The relevant data for each department is managed in separate visualization entities or
collections.

Pages
Pages segment your collections so you can divide up your data according to your preferences, such as
products, campaigns, or social platforms. Each page can be customized and filtered, so you can modify
the design and remove any unwanted data. To assist you in dividing the visualization into manageable
sections, you can have multiple pages within a collection.
Create Blank Dashboard Pages
You can use a blank dashboard page to create visualizations of your data without any predefined
settings or design. For example, create a dashboard page to illustrate how campaigns are performing
to an executive overview. Divide dashboard pages according to products, campaigns, or social
platforms. And customize them to your preferences.
App Pages
An app page is a page added to your collection that displays all the valuable data generated from an
installed app. App pages enable you to visualize and customize a marketplace app within your
collection, without running over the app instance settings.
Dashboard Page Templates
Dashboard page templates are based on a dashboard template saved to the workspace. Dashboard
templates allow you to replicate your dashboard pages across your collections and workspace. When
you create a dashboard template, all the data and design, including theme, widgets, and filters, are
cloned into the new dashboard page. Using dashboard templates helps unify your workspace and save
valuable time because you don’t create the same dashboard page from scratch.
Create Blank Dashboard Pages
You can use a blank dashboard page to create visualizations of your data without any predefined settings
or design. For example, create a dashboard page to illustrate how campaigns are performing to an
executive overview. Divide dashboard pages according to products, campaigns, or social platforms. And
customize them to your preferences.

=== PAGE 515 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can add filters to your page when you want to filter the page by any dimension, measurement, or
date. Filters are defined in the Filters tab when in Edit Mode.
Create Interactive FilterSets
Interactive FilterSets enable admins and power users to save different views of their dashboard page
by saving the selections made to the interactive filter widgets. You can also apply interactive FilterSets
as a filter option within the dashboard page. For example, if your dashboard page displays all your
campaigns around the world but you want viewers to quickly see the most successful campaigns in
the US.
Design Your Page
In the Design tab when in Edit Mode, you can design your page to create beautiful and effective
dashboard pages.
Customize Dashboard Pages
After you create a dashboard page, you can customize it to meet your needs via Edit Mode.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To customize dashboard pages Admin and Power User
When you select the page you’re customizing and click the pencil icon to enter Edit mode, you have
these options:
11.. Name your page so you can find the relevant page in your collection.
22.. Set your date range. The date range sets the time range according to the data displayed on the page.
The page doesn’t display data that isn’t in its range. Set your date range by clicking the date. You can
set a predefined date range from the list or set a custom date range.
33.. Add widgets to the dashboard page by clicking the plus icon.
44.. Apply a filter to your page. To define a page-level filter, click the funnel icon. You can also apply filters
on the widget level, which filters according to values and dates.
55.. Save your changes. To discard the changes, click the x button. Unsaved changes aren’t available to
other users.
66.. Publish your dashboard page. When a dashboard page is unpublished, only you and authorized users
can view the dashboard page. Users aren’t able to view unpublished dashboard pages by default. By
publishing a dashboard page, you allow all users with access to the collection to view it. You can
choose to publish or unpublish your dashboard page by clicking the round icon at the top-left.
Unpublished dashboard pages appear with a gray icon, while published dashboard pages appear with
a green icon
77.. To return to view mode, click the eye icon.
88.. To access other options, such as exporting, sharing, or deleting your dashboard page, click the options
menu.

Add Filters to Your Page
You can add filters to your page when you want to filter the page by any dimension, measurement, or

To add filters to a page Admin and Power User
The two types of filters are:
Enable you to filter the page by any dimension,
Page Filters
measurement, or date.
Enables you to save different views of your
dashboard page by saving the selections made to
Interactive FilterSet the interactive filter widgets on the page. The

To create a page filter.
11.. In the dashboard page, click the pencil icon to enter edit mode.
22.. In the Edit Page pane on the left, click Page Filters. Here you can see if there are any existing page
filters.
33.. Click Add Filter.
44.. Select a dimension, measurement or date field. For example, select Campaign Name.
55.. (Optional) Define a condition. For example, select List Of and then select the name of the campaign
you are filtering by. Note that the condition options change according to the type of field you select.
66.. If you add more than one filter, click Group. This groups the filters using an AND/Or condition and
forms a single filter. Note that you can only create groups on dimension filters.
Create Interactive FilterSets
Interactive FilterSets enable admins and power users to save different views of their dashboard page by
saving the selections made to the interactive filter widgets. You can also apply interactive FilterSets as a
filter option within the dashboard page. For example, if your dashboard page displays all your campaigns
around the world but you want viewers to quickly see the most successful campaigns in the US.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create Interactive FilterSets Admin and Power User
Note All users can see created FilterSets regardless of their user role and permissions. To clear
selected interactive FilterSets, click the options menu when in edit mode, and select Clear

Interactive Filters.

interactive date and interactive compare widgets when the interactive FilterSet was saved.

use filters, make sure that you use the same names.

you can start configuring it.

•• - Sticky — Fixes the widget to the top of the page, where it
remains when users scroll.
•• - Float — Floats the widget on top of your existing widgets
on the page, where it remains when users scroll.
•• - Size — Adjust the size of a widget by clicking and dragging
its edge.

Advanced Customizations
Apply advanced customizations to your widgets such as forecasting, highlighting rules, and cascading
filters.
Forecasting
Forecasting allows you to activate sophisticated algorithms that predict the behavior of a specific
measurement.
Highlighting Rules
Highlighting rules enable you to highlight values in a widget, allowing you to track specific values
associated with measurements and dimensions. For example, you can use a highlighting rule to track
the click-through rate (CTR) of a specific campaign to make sure that you remain within your key
performance indicators (KPIs). Or you can use a highlighting rule to mark a specific media buy that
you want to monitor.
Cascading Filters
Cascading filters allow you to apply a filtering component such as a page filter or an interactive filter
widget to other interactive filter widgets. After you apply a cascading filter, the list of values in the
selected interactive filters widgets are filtered to only contain values that are relevant to the selected
value.
Forecasting
Forecasting allows you to activate sophisticated algorithms that predict the behavior of a specific
measurement.
522

=== PAGE 527 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Predicted values are displayed in their relevant dates, and the background color of the forecasting period
appears slightly darker, as you can see in this image:
For optimal forecasting, follow these key principles:
•
More data is better — The larger the window of time your forecast is based on, the greater the
likelihood for accuracy. For example, querying a weekly data slice from within a single month produces
a less accurate forecast than querying daily data from across an entire quarter.
•
Recurring patterns — Data that exhibit repeatable patterns yields more accurate and confident
forecasts.
•
Continuous data — Missing data periods in your query result in an estimation by the forecasting
algorithms, which can affect forecast accuracy.
•
Minimal number of outliers — Data sets with a minimum number of outlier values yield more accurate
and confident forecasts.
When activating forecasting, the platform automatically runs two different forecasting models and
selects the one with the highest quality.
The forecasting models use the R Forecasting Package and have two available algorithms:
•
ARIMA — Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average
•
ETS — Exponential Smoothing
The Forecasting Quality Score (FQC) is determined by calculating the Mean Absolute Scale Error (MASE)
for each of the algorithms and then normalizing it to get a score 1–100. The forecasting method with the
highest score is selected.
Activate Forecasting
Forecasting is available within the bar graph widget when using a date dimension (day, month, or
week).
Activate Forecasting
Forecasting is available within the bar graph widget when using a date dimension (day, month, or week).
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To activate forecasting Admin and Power User
11.. Click the measurement you want to add forecasting to.
22.. Enter the Options menu.
33.. To turn on forecasting, select Forecasting. These options are available:
••
Type Auto — Extend the widget time frame by 25%.
••
Exactly — Choose the number of days in the forecast length.
523

=== PAGE 528 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
••
Until — Choose the end date for the forecast period.
Forecast Length Defined automatically based on the data you have in your workspace.
The more data and repeating patterns you have in your workspace, the
better Marketing Cloud Intelligence’s forecasting capabilities.
Forecast Model The forecasting method with the highest-quality score is selected. You
can keep the automated process AUTO. Then, each time the widget is
loaded, the platform selects a different method depending on the
data retrieved and the method’s quality score. Alternatively, you can
specify a model that is always used for this measurement from the
dropdown list. The quality score for each method is displayed next to
the method’s name.
Ignore last X This option allows you to ignore the number of days inserted in the
forecasting calculation. This option is suitable for cases where the data
for the last number of days is incomplete and you don’t want to
include it in the calculation.
Show Description When checked, a tool-tip appears at the top-right side of the widget
displaying the selected forecasting method and its quality score for
each relevant measurement.
Highlighting Rules
Highlighting rules enable you to highlight values in a widget, allowing you to track specific values
associated with measurements and dimensions. For example, you can use a highlighting rule to track the
click-through rate (CTR) of a specific campaign to make sure that you remain within your key
performance indicators (KPIs). Or you can use a highlighting rule to mark a specific media buy that you
want to monitor.

To create a rule that applies to dimensions and measurements simultaneously, create two separate rules,
and then apply them individually to the widget as explained previously.
For example, to highlight all banner placements that made less than 1,000 impressions, first create a rule
that highlights all placement names that contain the word Banner. Then create another rule that
highlights all impressions that are under 1,000. Attach these rules separately to the widget as a whole
under the Design tab, or attach them to the measurement or dimension under the Data tab
Cascading Filters
Cascading filters allow you to apply a filtering component such as a page filter or an interactive filter
526

=== PAGE 531 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
widget to other interactive filter widgets. After you apply a cascading filter, the list of values in the
selected interactive filters widgets are filtered to only contain values that are relevant to the selected
value.
You can only apply cascading filters within the same entity hierarchy, from the top hierarchy to the lower
hierarchies. The campaign entity and its attributes (campaign name and campaign advisor) can filter the
media buy entity and its attributes (media buy name and type). However, the media buy entity and its
attributes can’t filter the campaign entity and its attributes.
In the table, the filtering entity can filter level 1 and level 2, and filtered entity level 1 can only filter level
2.
Filtering Entity Filtered Entity level 1 Filtered Entity level 2
Campaign Media Buy
Sites Media Buy
Channels Media Buy
Channels Web Analytics Site Web Analytics Tag
Channels Social Element Social Post
Channels Email Campaign
Channels Generic Entities 1–5
Products Media Buy
Products Web Analytics Site Web Analytics Tag
Products Social Element Social Post
Products Email Campaign
Products Generic Entities 1–5
Custom Classification 1 Media Buy
Custom Classification 1 Web Analytics Site Web Analytics Tag
Custom Classification 1 Social Element Social Post
Custom Classification 1 Email Campaign
Custom Classification 1 Generic Entities 1–5
Custom Classification 2 Media Buy
Custom Classification 2 Web Analytics Site Web Analytics Tag
Custom Classification 2 Social Element Social Post
Custom Classification 2 Email Campaign

Custom Classification 2 Generic Entities 1–5
Social Element Traffic Source Social Element Social Post
Products Opportunity
Client Company Opportunity
GEO Company Opportunity
GEO Lead
Here’s an example:
The Media Buy Size interactive filter widget has the Cascading Filter checkbox selected, and it’s included
in the Apply to Specific Widgets list in the Campaign Advertiser interactive filter widget. The value
selected in the Campaign Advertiser interactive filter widget affects the values in the Media Buy Size
interactive filter widget.
In the table above, the Media Buy Size interactive filter widget alone affects the widget. Media Buy Size is
the only widget in the Apply to Specific Widgets list in the Campaign Advertiser interactive filter widget.
In the table, you see campaign advertiser values other than Travelight, the filter in the Campaign
Advertiser' interactive filter widget. That’s because the Campaign Advertiser interactive filter is applied to
the Media Buy Size interactive filter alone. It doesn’t affect any other widgets on the page.
Note
•
Cascading filters don’t apply to interactive date filters.
•
Cascading filters don’t work if the interactive filter widget the filters are applied to is set to Set
Values Manually.

Widget Types
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers three different types of widgets so you can visualize your data. The
creation process is dynamic according to the type of widget you select.
Chart Widgets
A chart widget shows data in various forms, such as a bar graph, line chart, and pie chart.
Interactive Widgets
Interactive widgets allow users who browse the page to toggle between measurements and
dimensions without modifying the data on the page.
Element Widgets
Element widgets are static elements that you can add to a page, such as rich text, videos, images, and
custom widgets created using JavaScript, CSS, or HTML.
Additional Widgets
You can find an array of widgets in the Visualize tab.
Chart Widgets
A chart widget shows data in various forms, such as a bar graph, line chart, and pie chart.
Create a Time-Comparison Bidimensional Bar Widget
A time-comparison bidimensional bar widget shows the performance of two measurements using a
dimension breakdown over time.
Create a Time-Comparison Bidimensional Bar Widget
A time-comparison bidimensional bar widget shows the performance of two measurements using a
dimension breakdown over time.

To create a chart widget: Admin or Power User
11.. Navigate to the Visualize tab.
22.. Select the dashboard page that you want to add a widget to.
33.. Click the pencil icon, and then click the + icon to add the widget.
44.. In the widget search box, enter time-comparison bidimensional bar.

66.. Add the dimensions that you want to show. You must add one temporal dimension and one non-
temporal dimension.

or down.

want to show change in the widget.
99.. Select up to 10 decimal places.
1100.. To compare the data by week, select Compare by Week Number. For example, to compare the data
for the first week of each year.
1111.. To indicate that a drop in a measurement value is a negative change, select Revert Change Indicators.

Create an Interactive Filter Widget
The interactive filter widget manages the filter settings for other widgets in a dynamic, flexible manner.
It displays all values contained in a given dimension, and it can be applied to specific widgets on the
page.
Apply Interactive Filters via a URL
Marketing Cloud Intelligence allows you to include the interactive filter values you want to apply to a
page inside the page URL. You can use this feature when you want to send pages that are filtered by
different values to different users. For example, if your page is filtered at a high-level view, you can
send different market heads this high-level view filtered by specific markets.

entire page or for specific widgets of your choice. This option is available for admins, power users, and

viewers. But one of its main objectives is to enable viewers to filter the page according to a specific

Create an Interactive Filter Widget
The interactive filter widget manages the filter settings for other widgets in a dynamic, flexible manner. It
displays all values contained in a given dimension, and it can be applied to specific widgets on the page.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To an interactive filter widget Admin and Power User
In the interactive filter’s search box, search for values to filter widgets by. If you need multiple values,
enter the exact term and then add a comma between each value.
A page can have several interactive filters, allowing the user to select a combination of filter settings.
11.. Navigate to the Visualize tab.
22.. To enter edit mode, click the pencil icon.
33.. To add a widget, click the + icon.
44.. In the Interactive section, select Filter.
55.. To add data to your widget, click the + icon.
Note
••
The interactive filter widget supports calculated dimensions. Setting up the calculated
dimension with a related measurement isn’t necessary. You can select dimensions only.
••
When the chosen dimension has more than 300 values, the widget displays an error message.
You can use a filter to narrow down the value number.
After you select a dimension these options are available:
•
Auto Set Value — Automatically lists the filter values displayed in the widget according to the
dimension selected.
•
Manual Set Values — Allows you to define the filter values displayed in the widget. For example, to
provide the option to filter by a few specific campaigns and not all campaigns in the workspace, define
the specific campaigns here.
11..
To enable this option, click the arrow next to the Auto Set Values field, and select the Manual Set
Values option.
22..
To edit the values, click the pencil icon. The Set Values Manually dialog appears.
33..
Insert the value you require, and then click Add Value.
44..
Select Equals or Contains in the Filtering Operator area. This selection affects the dimension values
included in the widget.
55..

=== PAGE 536 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Set Default Values — Set default values for your filter widget so that each time the page loads these
values appear. Select the values you require from the dropdown list.
Note If you have a long list of values, you can use the Search option to search for the values you
require.
•
Apply to Specific Widgets — Select the widgets you want the filter to apply to instead of the default to
all widgets on the page.
Note If you delete a widget that has an interactive filter widget applied to it, the interactive filter
widget settings revert to the default settings: applying the filter to all the widgets on the page.
Filtering issues can result after the page is saved and refreshed.
•
Cascading Filters — If you want to enable one filtering component (a page or an interactive filter
widget) to apply to other interactive filter widgets, select this checkbox.
•
Retain User Selection — Select this checkbox to save the interactive filter widget settings that are
applied per user to a dashboard page or collection. Now, when switching between collections or
dashboard pages, the same settings appear.
More options are available in the Filter and Data tab.
•
Filtering Display — Select how to display the filter selections within the interactive filter widget. These
suboptions are available:
- List — Lists out all the filter values within the widget.
- Drop Down — Places all the filter values in a dropdown list within the widget.
- Search Box — Places a search box within the widget, where you can search for the filter to apply.
Depending on the filtering display selected above, these options are available:
•
Selection Method — Define whether users can select a single filter selection or multiple filter
selections.
•
List Display — Select how to display the list within the widget: vertically or horizontally. This option only
applies to the list filtering display.
•
Filtering Operator — Define how the search box searches for values. Equals requires an exact match to
the value you’re searching for, while Contains searches for a value based on what’s entered. This
option only applies to the search box filtering display.
•
Limit Selection — Select this checkbox to limit the number of selections a user can make within the
interactive filter widget. This option is helpful when the creator of the dashboard page wants to
prevent too many filters being used simultaneously. This option is only available when Multiple is
selected in the Selection Method box. Insert the number you want to limit the selections to.
•
Show Search Box — Select this checkbox to display a search box and the list of values in the widget.
•
Value(s) must be selected — Select this checkbox to make sure that there is at least one selected value
in the interactive filter widget.
After creating an interactive filter widget, you can search for values to filter widgets within the widget’s
search box. If you need multiple values, enter the exact term and then add a comma between each
value.

=== PAGE 537 ===

Apply Interactive Filters via a URL
Marketing Cloud Intelligence allows you to include the interactive filter values you want to apply to a
page inside the page URL. You can use this feature when you want to send pages that are filtered by
different values to different users. For example, if your page is filtered at a high-level view, you can send
different market heads this high-level view filtered by specific markets.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To apply an interactive filter widget Admin and Power User
11.. Go to the page you want to apply the filter to, and then copy the ID of the interactive filter widget.
Note If there are no interactive filter widgets on the page, create them according to your needs.
22.. To copy the interactive filter ID, open the widget’s Options Menu, and select Info.
33.. To copy the widget’s ID, click Copy.
44.. Insert the relevant details into this snippet, and add it to the end of the page’s URL:
&wf [INTERACTIVE_FILTER_WIDGET_ID]=[Comma separated filter values]
Note The values added here are case-sensitive and must be an exact match to the values as they
appear in the interactive filter.
The example below shows what it should look like after inserting the parameters, this is for display
only.
Example https://platform.datorama.com/1111/visualize/11111/page/v2/
111111?viewMode=VIEW&wf12345=NA,LATAM You can also include multiple interactive filters in
the URL: https://platform.datorama.com/1111/visualize/11111/page/v2/
111111viewMode=VIEW&wf12345=NA&wf56789=Campaign12

Use an Interactive Date Filter Widget
The interactive date filter widget offers an easy way to set and toggle between date ranges on your entire
page or for specific widgets of your choice. This option is available for admins, power users, and viewers.
But one of its main objectives is to enable viewers to filter the page according to a specific date range.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To use an interactive date filter widget Admin and Power User
11.. Navigate to the Visualize tab, and enter edit mode by clicking the pencil icon.
22.. Add a new widget by clicking the + icon.
33.. You can search for the interactive date widget in the search bar, or from the left panel, under
Interactive, select the Date widget type.
533

=== PAGE 538 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After you add the interactive date widget, you can configure the widget with these options:
•
Name — Name your widget.
•
Dimension — Select the dimension you want to filter by. You can choose any type of date dimension.
Note When setting a dimension other than the day dimension, the interactive date filter works
alongside the page’s date range. The interactive filter date range affects the widget's dimensions,
while the page’s date range affects the widget’s measurements.
For example, let’s say you’re setting the interactive date filter’s dimension to Campaign Start Date and
the date range to September 1 until September 7. The relevant widgets in the page display dimension
values that are linked to campaigns with a campaign start date within this date range. However, the
measurements displayed are according to the page’s date range.
•
Restrict Date Range — Select this checkbox to restrict the displayed data to a specific time period. The
chosen date range acts as a boundary for the user so that they can’t select any date range outside the
restricted time frame. If they do, they receive an error message.
•
Apply Default Values — Select this checkbox to set a default time range to the widget. The chosen
date range is applied to the widget by default whenever the page is loaded.
•
Apply to Specific Widgets — If you don’t want to apply the filter to the whole page, from the
dropdown list, select the widgets you want to apply the date filter to.
•
Retain User Selection — Select this checkbox to save the date widget settings that are applied per user
to a dashboard page or collection. Then, when switching between collections or dashboard pages, the
same settings appear.
Create a Switch Widget
A switch widget is an interactive filter widget that is available in the widget library. You can switch or add
measurements or dimensions.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create a switch widget Admin and Power User
The switch widget offers two work modes:
•
Switch — Instantly override dimensions and measurements in selected widgets or even at the page
level. For example, let’s say you want to toggle between several measurements among the widgets on
your page, such as total conversions and revenue. All you do is define the measurements or
dimensions you want to be able to switch, and decide at what page level or for specific widgets. After
adding a switch widget, you can change the widget view to display different fields while preserving the
widget’s settings. This mode is useful when you want to show static data from different points of view.
•
Add — Gain more page design flexibility by adding dimensions or measurements, rather than switching
them. In this mode, you can add dimensions or measurements one after the other without switching
out the existing dimensions and measurements. You can add dimensions or measurements from
multiple switch widgets in any order. You can also use multiple switch widgets in Add Mode and
organize dimensions or measurements into categories.
534

=== PAGE 539 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Note Using multiple switch widgets within the same target widgets is only supported in Add Mode.
Switch Mode works as a single switch per target widget.
11.. Navigate to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard, and then select the page you want to add a widget to.
33.. To edit the selected page, click the pencil icon.
44.. To add a new widget, click the + icon.
55.. Scroll down to the Interactive section and click Switch Dimensions or Switch Measurements.
A new switch widget is added to your page from which you can preview any change that is made to the
widget. These options are available in the Data tab:
•
Name — Name your widgets so that you can easily find it when using other features in the system, such
as advanced export. You can click the eye icon next to the widget name to hide your widget title from
displaying on the page.
•
Add data — To add data to the widget, click the + icon.
Note You can add up to 300 measurements or dimensions to a switch widget, allowing you to
present a large selection of options. Each widget has its own limit for the number of
measurements it can display.
•
Widget Mode — Select the switch widget mode you want to use:
- Switch — Select this mode to override dimensions or measurements within the target widgets, or to
all the widgets on the dashboard page.
- Add — Select this mode to add dimensions or measurements within the target widgets, or to all the
widgets on the dashboard page.
•
Set Default Values — Define values that can be set as the default-selected values for the switch widget
each time the page loads.
•
Apply to Specific Widgets — Apply the switch widget selections to specific widgets on the page. If no
widgets are selected, then the switch widget is applied to all the widgets on the page.
•
Retain User Selection — This checkbox is selected by default. It automatically saves the switch widget
settings that are applied per user to a page or dashboard. Then, when switching between dashboards
or pages, the same settings appear. If you don’t want to save the switch widget selections per user,
clear this checkbox.
After you finish configuring the Data tab, configure the Design tab. These options are available:
•
Filtering Display — Select how to display the filter selections, either in a list or in a dropdown box.
•
Selection Method — Define whether users can select one filter selection or multiple filter selections.
•
List Display — If you selected List as the filtering display, select how to display the list:
- Vertical — Lists the filter selections on individual rows.
- Horizontal — Lists the filter selections one after the other, on the same row.
•
Limit Selection — If you selected Multiple as the selection method, select this checkbox to limit the
number of selections a user can select within the switch widget. Then insert the number of options
you want to allow.
•
Show Search Box — This checkbox is selected by default. This option inserts a search box into the
widget so that you can search for specific dimensions or measurements to filter by. If you don’t want to
535

=== PAGE 540 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
add a search box to the widget, clear this checkbox.
•
Show Order Indicators — If you selected Multiple as the selection method, select this checkbox to
show the order of the dimensions or measurements that are applied to the switch widget. This
method is important when you have multiple dimensions and the order can affect the display, for
example in a table.
•
Use Date Dimensions As First Sort — This option is only relevant for switch dimensions widgets. Select
this checkbox to override the target widget first sort value, and set it as the date dimension instead.
•
Override Measurements Settings — This option is only relevant for switch measurement widgets.
Select this checkbox to override the default settings for the measurements in your workspace when
using switch mode. This option is useful for when you switch measurements in a chart and you want to
define the style of the measurements directly from the switch widget and not from the chart widget.
After you select the checkbox, navigate back to the Data tab and click on the measurement you want
to define the settings for. From here you can define the following:
- Color — The display color of the measurement.
- Display — The display of the measurement in the chart — bar, area, spline, or line.
- Type — The type of measurement — number, percentage, duration, or currency.
- Precision — The number of decimal places.
- Override Currency Symbol — The currency symbol to display.
- Friendly Formatting — The format to display large numbers, for example 1,000,000 as 1M.
- Highlighting Rules — The highlighting rule to apply to the measurement.

A switch currency widget is a type of interactive filter widget available in the widget library. With this
widget you can switch the currency for all currency-metrics on the page.
Create a Switch Date Widget
A switch date widget is a type of interactive filter widget available in the widget library. With this widget
you can switch the date granularity of widgets used in the page.

A new switch currency widget is added to your page from which you can preview any change that is
made to the widget. These options are available in the Data tab:
•
Source Currency — The default source currency is the currency setup in your workspace.
•
Target Currencies — These are the currencies you want to switch to. The default target currency is the

•
Set Default Values — Define the default currency used each time the page loads.
•
Apply to Specific Widgets — Apply the switch currency widget selections to specific widgets on the

page.
•

currency widget settings that are applied per user to a page or dashboard. Then, when switching
between dashboards or pages, the same settings appear. If you don’t want to save the switch widget
selections per user, clear this checkbox.
Note Currency conversions only apply to mapped currency type measurements. Therefore, for the
conversion to work on calculated measurements, they must contain mapped currency type
measurements.

•
List Display — If you selected List as the filtering display, select how to display the list:
- Vertical — Lists the filter selections on individual rows.
- Horizontal — Lists the filter selections one after the other, on the same row.
•
Show Search Box — Select to insert a search box into the widget so that you can search for specific
currencies to filter by.

available in the design tab.

Create a Switch Date Widget
A switch date widget is a type of interactive filter widget available in the widget library. With this widget
you can switch the date granularity of widgets used in the page.
537

=== PAGE 542 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a switch date widget: Admin or Power User
11.. Navigate to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard, and then select the page you want to add a widget to.
33.. To edit the selected page, click the pencil icon.
44.. To add a new widget, click the + icon.
55.. Scroll down to the Interactive section and click Switch Date.
A new switch date widget is added to your page from which you can preview any change that is made to
the widget. These options are available in the Data tab:
•
Add data to your widget — Select the date-type dimensions you want to add to the widget.
•
Set date granularity — Select the granularity level of the date field you want to switch to if more than
one date-type field is available in the target widgets.
- Least Granular — Select this to change the least granular date in the target widget. For example, if
day, week and year are in the target widget, day will be switched.
- Most granular — Select this to change the most granular date in the target widget. For example, if
day, week and year are in the target widget, year will be switched.
•
Set Default Values — Define the default date period used each time the page loads.
•
Apply to Specific Widgets — Apply the switch date widget selections to specific widgets on the page. If
no widgets are selected, then the switch date widget is applied to all the widgets on the page.
•
Retain User Selection — This checkbox is selected by default. It automatically saves the switch date
widget settings that are applied per user to a page or dashboard. Then, when switching between
dashboards or pages, the same settings appear. If you don’t want to save the switch widget selections
per user, clear this checkbox.
Note
•
The switch date widget only switches date type dimensions.
•
Only dimensions from the Date entity can be added to the switch date widget. However, when a
custom calendar is in use, you can also add Custom Date dimensions.
After you finish configuring the Data tab, configure the Design tab. These options are available:
•
List Display — If you selected List as the filtering display, select how to display the list:
- Vertical — Lists the filter selections on individual rows.
- Horizontal — Lists the filter selections one after the other, on the same row.
•
Show Search Box — Select to insert a search box into the widget so that you can search for specific
date periods to filter by.
You can further tailor the appearance of the switch date widgets on your page with the options available
in the design tab.

Design Your Widget
Element Widgets
Element widgets are static elements that you can add to a page, such as rich text, videos, images, and
custom widgets created using JavaScript, CSS, or HTML.
Create a Rich Text Widget
The rich text widget allows you to enter freestyle text in your page and edit it with an editor. You can
also make a query and then display the results of that query within the widget. You can also style the
text any way you want.
Create an Image Widget
The image widget provides you with the ability to upload an image into your page. You can either
upload a file or link to a URL.
Create a Headers Widget
A headers widget is a preconfigured custom widget that allows you to quickly and easily insert a
header on your page. The header can contain a logo and links to specific widgets on the page, links
within Marketing Cloud Intelligence, or links to external web pages. Use a headers widget to introduce
the content displayed on the page, to brand the page, to insert links to other pages, or to scroll to
specific areas on the page.
Create an Apply Filter Widget
Use the apply filter widget to update a page after you apply all the filters to the page, instead of
updating the page values after applying each filter. When using the apply filter widget, click Apply after
you select all the filters you want to apply to the page and the page updates.
Create a Map Widget
Map widgets illustrate the geographical location of a data variable. Color progression or bubble size is
used to represent value in each region of the map—the darker the color, or the bigger the bubble, the
higher the value. You can use map widgets to illustrate which areas within selected locations have the
most or least data. For example, where in the world do you gain the most page views.
Create a Custom Widget
Custom widget is a complete development interface within Marketing Cloud Intelligence collections
that allows nearly limitless dashboard page design and behavior. It includes HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
coding environments, with access to a full library of functions that provide ready-to-use page
visualization, navigation, and API solutions.
Create a Rich Text Widget
The rich text widget allows you to enter freestyle text in your page and edit it with an editor. You can also
make a query and then display the results of that query within the widget. You can also style the text any
way you want.
539

=== PAGE 544 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create a widget Admin and Power User
The most common uses of rich text widgets are to:
•
Write notes and add commentary to pages.
•
Dynamically return query performance results in a textual manner.
•
Create simple headlines or sections.
11.. Navigate to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard, and then the page you want to add a widget to.
33.. To edit the selected page, click the pencil icon.
44.. To add a new widget, click the + icon.
55.. Scroll down to the Elements section, and click Rich Text.
After you add a rich text widget, customize the information you want to display in the widget. The rich
text widget is set up just like all other widgets and offers basic editing options. If you want to create a
widget using HTML, CSS, or Server-Side JavaScript, use a custom widget instead.
Note It’s possible to switch a rich text widget to a custom widget under the Data tab when editing
the widget. Selecting the custom widget icon takes all the content from your rich text widget and
converts it into code in the custom widget. It’s important to note that this process is
irreversible—you can’t switch a custom widget back to a rich text widget.
66.. After you create a rich text widget, customize the information displayed in the widget by opening the
Rich Text Editor. Click the pencil icon next to Rich Text Editor.
From here you can customize the information displayed in the widget. These options are available:
•
Query — Queries your data based on the tokens selected and displays the result in the widget.
Syntax: {Query(Field Name, type, delimiter)}
Example: {Query(Campaign Name, none, @)} displays the following:
•
Filter — Displays the filters applied to the widget.
Syntax: {Filter(Field Name, type, delimiter)}
Example: {Filter(Campaign Name, list, )} displays the following:
•
Table — Displays a table of the fields selected in the FIELDS section. To display the table, you must
select whether to include a Total row by including true or false in the token. True displays the Total
row, false doesn’t.
Syntax: {Table(true/false)}
Example: {Table(true)} displays the following:
These query tokens can be used:
•
Dimension – {Query.Dimension Name} — Displays all the dimension values available in the widget.
•
Measurement – {Query.Measurement Name} — Displays all the measurement values available in the
540

=== PAGE 545 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
widget.
•
Table– {Query.Table} — Displays all the dimensions and measurements in a table format. For the type,
enter your preferred display method. These options are available:
- none (Concatenated)
- bullet
- list
- Number
For the delimiter, enter how you want the values to be separated when using concatenate values.
When using concatenating values, the display is a string of values separated by a separator of your
choice.
Note
•
Every dimension and measurement you include in the editor section must also be entered in the
FIELDS section.
•
A query token retrieves all the available values for that query. To narrow the results, add a filter to
the widget.

Design Your Widget
Create a Headers Widget
A headers widget is a preconfigured custom widget that allows you to quickly and easily insert a header
on your page. The header can contain a logo and links to specific widgets on the page, links within
Marketing Cloud Intelligence, or links to external web pages. Use a headers widget to introduce the
content displayed on the page, to brand the page, to insert links to other pages, or to scroll to specific
areas on the page.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create a widget Admin and Power User
11.. Navigate to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard and then the page you want to add a widget to.
33.. To edit the selected page, click the pencil icon.
44.. To add a new widget, click the + icon.
55.. Scroll down to the Elements section, and click Headers.
A new headers widget is added to your page. These editing options are available in the Data tab:
•
Name — Name your widgets so that you can easily find your widget when using other features in the
system, such as Advanced Export. You can click the eye icon next to the widget name to hide your
widget title from displaying on the page.
•
Select the type of header you want to use. To see additional options, click More. Each header type has
slightly different configurations.
•
To open the editor window, click the pencil icon next to Custom Widget Editor. From here you can
further customize the information displayed in the widget. The editor window has a predefined
configuration already set up within the Server-Side JavaScript (JS) box so you can easily configure the
widget. The areas to edit are either colored in brown or blue. These options are available:
- Gradient colors — Input the color hex code for the color you want to use for the gradient color of
the widget. For example: const firstGradientColor = '#243B55';
Note Use the colors from your page’s InstaBrand theme by copying the hex numbers within the
Color Palette section within the Design tab when editing the page. Click the color you want to
542

=== PAGE 547 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
use and copy the hex number.
- When using the Photo header type, you can change the background image instead of the gradient
colors and then customize the background size, position and repeat. Insert the URL of the image
you want to use as your background image . For example: const backgroundImage =
'https://datorama—public—resources.s3.amazonaws.com/
photo—header—background.png'
- Logo — Insert the URL of the image you want to use as your logo. For example: const
logoImageSrc = 'https://datorama—public—resources.s3.amazonaws.com/logo.svg'
If you don’t want a logo in the header, delete this code from the JS editor window: // Logo image
source address const logoImageSrc =
'https://datorama—public—resources.s3.amazonaws.com/logo.svg';
•
Links — You can add a link in your header to an external URL or to a widget on the page. The code is
divided into three areas, which you can customize according to your needs:
- The destination link:
To link to a URL, add the URL between the quotation marks. For example:
'https://salesforce.com'
To link to a widget on the page, insert the widget ID instead of the word ‘null. For example:
scrollToWidget: 123456
Note To find the widget ID, click the relevant widget’s options menu marked by the three dots
in the top-right corner of the widget, and select Info. To copy the widget’s ID, click the copy
icon.
- The display title:
Insert the name of choice between the quotation marks.
For example: title: 'Salesforce'
- The display color:
Inset the hex color code between the quotation marks, after the #.
For example: color: '#000'
•
You can then enter the relevant codes for the next two links.
The next two links contain the same code as the first link, just without the descriptions.
If you don’t want links in your header, delete this code from the JS editor window:
// Change this array to add or update links
const links = [
{
href: '#', // The link URL to be navigated on click
scrollToWidget: null, // Insert the widget ID
title: '01 | Link 1', // The name of the link
color: '#FFF' // The color of the link
},
{ href: '#', scrollToWidget: null, title: '02 | Link 2', color: '#FFF' },
{ href: '#', scrollToWidget: null, title: '03 | Link 3', color: '#FFF' }
If you want just one link, delete the code for the other two links:
543

=== PAGE 548 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
{ href: '#', scrollToWidget: null, title: '02 | Link 2', color: '#FFF' },
{ href: '#', scrollToWidget: null, title: '03 | Link 3', color: '#FFF' }
Note If you want to add more links, copy and paste the previous code within the same section,
and change accordingly.
•
After you insert all the required sections, click Apply, and then save your page. The rest of the widget
settings are the same as a custom widget.

Use the apply filter widget to update a page after you apply all the filters to the page, instead of updating

all the filters you want to apply to the page and the page updates.

Name — Name your widgets so that you can easily find your widget when using other features in the

Create a Map Widget
Map widgets illustrate the geographical location of a data variable. Color progression or bubble size is
used to represent value in each region of the map—the darker the color, or the bigger the bubble, the
higher the value. You can use map widgets to illustrate which areas within selected locations have the
most or least data. For example, where in the world do you gain the most page views.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create a widget Admin and Power User
11.. Navigate to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard and then the page you want to add a widget to.
33.. To edit the selected page, click the pencil icon.
44.. To add a new widget, click the + icon.
55.. Within the Charts section, click Maps.
A new map widget is added to your page. These editing options are available within the Data tab:
•
Name — Name your widgets so that you can easily find your widget when using other features in the
system, such as Advanced Export. You can click the eye icon next to the widget name to hide your
widget title from displaying on the page. Select the type of map you want to use. To see additional
options, such as different regions or countries, click More.
•
Map — Alternatively, you can select or search for the country or region you want to display.
•
Additional Granularity — Add further granularity to the map by breaking it down by City or Zip Code. If
selected, these options are available:
- Load when zoomed — Selecting this checkbox aggregates the data at the city or ZIP code level
when zoomed in to an area. You can select to load all the granularity levels even when you’re not
zoomed in. However, this selection adds a limit of up to 400 measurement values to ensure that the
widget’s performance stays intact.
- Include Partially Mapped Data — By default, Marketing Cloud Intelligence cleans unknown data.
When you select this checkbox, you add this data back in and include the data in the map widget.
•
Add data to the map widget. You can add one measurement and one dimension. Select the
measurement you want to display by clicking the + icon. A dimension is automatically selected
depending on the map you add. However, you can remove the selected dimension and add a different
one.
Note When you select a regional map, a filter is automatically added to the widget to filter the
data to the countries within the region. You can add or remove countries within the filter tab.
After you finish configuring the Data tab, you can configure the Design tab. These options are available:
•
Show Legend — Select this checkbox to display a bar in the Y axis that illustrates the meaning of the
color gradient within the map widget.
545

=== PAGE 550 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Floating Legend — Select this checkbox to make the legend float on top of the map. When this
checkbox isn’t selected, the legend appears static underneath the map.
•
Legend Position — Select where you want your legend to appear within the widget. You can position
the legend horizontally or vertically.
•
Data Layer Style — Select how the geolocations you have data for are displayed. These suboptions are
available:
- Area — Colors the entire area that has data. The more data you have in the geolocation, the stronger
the color is.
- Bubbles — Bubbles appear in the center of the area you have data. The more data you have in the
geolocation, the larger and more opaque the bubble is.
After you finish customizing your map widget, these options are available while viewing the widget:
•
You can double-click an area on the map to zoom in to that area. There are also zoom in and zoom out
buttons in the widget.
Note If an admin user saves the page while the widget is zoomed in, the widget remains zoomed
in.
•
You can search for specific areas within the search bar in the widget. The results in the dropdown list
are sorted by the measurement value, so you can always find the most impactful regions fast.

Create a Custom Widget
Custom widget is a complete development interface within Marketing Cloud Intelligence collections that
allows nearly limitless dashboard page design and behavior. It includes HTML, CSS, and JavaScript coding
environments, with access to a full library of functions that provide ready-to-use page visualization,
navigation, and API solutions.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create a widget Admin and Power User
This functionality allows admins and power users to create and configure design options that otherwise
wouldn’t be readily available in a custom widget. For example, you can add a color picker design setting,
allowing the end user to select colors from a predefined color palette to match brand colors.
If you’re an admin, you can install preconfigured custom widgets from the Marketplace. For more
information, see Install Custom Widgets.
11.. Navigate to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard and then the page you want to add a widget to.
33.. To edit the selected page, click the pencil icon.
44.. To add a new widget, click the + icon.
55.. Under Elements, select Custom.

66.. Classify your widget by selecting a widget type from the dropdown menu.
77.. Click the Custom Widget Editor icon to open the custom widget editor dialog panel.
88.. In the snippet library, click the JavaScript option.
99.. Click Custom Design Settings to open the custom design settings options panel. There are two main
options that are crucial to creating your custom design options:
Populates the functions you want displayed in
the design tab of your custom widget. To add
Set Custom Design
functions to your widget, you must add the Set
Custom Design API first.
Imports the design settings values from the
Get Custom Design panel into the custom widget, where they can be
applied.
1100.. To add Set Custom Design, click the plus + icon.
1111.. Now you can add the desired field controls into the Set Custom Design as an array by clicking the plus
+ icon next to the desired field. The input is displayed in the Widget Design tab. These functions are
available:

Add EMI APIs to Custom Widgets
Einstein Marketing Insights (EMI) is an insights discovery tool used to analyze a data set and uncover
key highlights that affect your performance in a positive or negative manner. This tool enables you to
become proactive with your marketing efforts. The EMI APIs allow you to pull your insight bots and
insights into custom widgets.
Add EMI APIs to Custom Widgets
Einstein Marketing Insights (EMI) is an insights discovery tool used to analyze a data set and uncover key
highlights that affect your performance in a positive or negative manner. This tool enables you to
become proactive with your marketing efforts. The EMI APIs allow you to pull your insight bots and
insights into custom widgets.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To add EMI APIs to widgets Admin and Power User
There are three EMI APIs created for custom widgets: Get Bot Details, List Bots, and Get Bot Results.
11.. In a custom widget, click the edit icon to open the Custom Widget Editor dialog panel.
22.. In the snipped library, click Javascript.
33.. At the bottom, click Einstein Marketing Insights API.
44.. To add the API, click the plus + icon next to the API name.
•
Get Bot Details — This API returns the bot parameters in JSON format. For example, bot name, types
of analyses it runs, and its scheduling. When adding the snippet, insert the bot ID in the code so the
query knows which bot information to pull. The bot ID can be found in the bot URL. Select the desired
bot, then copy the ID from the URL.
•
List Bots — This API returns a list of available bots and their general details in the current workspace in
JSON format. For example, bot name, KPI, date, and the types of analyses it runs.
Here is an example of JS code you can copy and paste to utilize the List Bots API:
Note Be sure to change the bot id to the bot id you want to retrieve data for. This code snippet
returns raw data. You can customize the code to create a visual display of your choice.
DA.api.EMI.getBotsSummary({cb: (err, data) => {
var toPrint = "";
data.bots.forEach((item, i) => {
toPrint+= JSON.stringify(data.bots[i]) + "<br><br>"
})
document.getElementById("demo").innerHTML = toPrint;
}});
548

=== PAGE 553 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Get Bot Results — This API extracts the actual insights from a specific bot in JSON format. It includes
data for each insight, such as the dimension and value of the insight, its performance, and its volume.
Insert the bot ID that you want to populate. The bot ID appears in the URL.
Here is an example of JS code you can copy and paste to utilize the Get Bot Results API:
Note Be sure to change the bot id to the bot id you want to retrieve data for. This code snippet
returns raw data. You can customize the code to create a visual display of your choice.
DA.api.EMI.getInsightsByBotId({botId: 55149, cb: (err, data) => {
var toPrint = "";
data.insights.forEach((item, i) => {
toPrint+= JSON.stringify(data.insights[i]) + "<br><br>"
})
document.getElementById("demo").innerHTML = toPrint;
}});
If you want to populate several bots, dedicate each API call to a single bot by inserting the desired ID
into each API.
Here’s a video about custom widgets:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/gtykw34Qi6SFSmgfgN2GxJ
