Create a Goal Widget
After you create a goal in your workspace, you can visualize the goal using a goal widget. A goal widget
can only be set up if you have a goal in your workspace.
Create a Goal Widget
After you create a goal in your workspace, you can visualize the goal using a goal widget. A goal widget
can only be set up if you have a goal in your workspace.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Goal values are calculated by the Average Daily Goal Value multiplied by Elapsed days and appear
differently depending on the selected widget.
In Goal Table Widgets, values are calculated using the number of days in the widget or page date range.
For example, if today is December 27, 2023, but you're viewing a date range of December 1-31, the goal
calculation uses 31 elapsed days.
In Goal Gauge Widget values never exceed the current number of elapsed days when calculating the
549

=== PAGE 554 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
goal. For example, if today is December 27, 2023, but you're viewing a date range of December 1-31, the
goal calculation uses 27 elapsed days
.
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a widget: Admin or Power User
11.. In the Visualize tab, double-click the page that you want to create a goal widget on.
22.. Click the pencil icon, and then click the plus icon.
33.. To add a widget, select the goals widget from the Add Widget menu. The default widget for goals is a
gauge.
44.. To change the widget type, click the suggested options next to the gauge (1).
55.. To view additional widget options, click more (2). Add the measurements and dimension, or add both
(3).
66.. To create a Goal Table widget, select the goal table from the goal widget menu.
77.. Name your goal, and add pacing fields. Pacing measurements allow you to add granularity and focus
on the performance of your goals. Adding a pacing field affects the visualization of the goal widget.
These pacing measurements are available:
The value of the measurement that the goal is
targeting. For example, if the goal measurement
Measurement Value
is clicks and the goal is set to reach 200 clicks,
the measurement value is 200.
Period Start The goal start date.
Period End The goal end date.
The target number the goal measurement is set
Goal Value
to achieve.
How much of target value is left in order to
Remaining Value
achieve the goal.
Period Days Elapsed How many days have passed since the start date.
Period Days Left How many days are left until the end date.
The number the measurement must reach daily
in order to reach the goal. (Total goal value -
Daily Target
current measurement value) / # of days left =
Daily Target
The daily average achieved up until now. To
Daily Average Value calculate this number, divide the measurement
value by the period days elapsed.

=== PAGE 555 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The number the measurement must reach every
Daily Average Goal
day in order to achieve the goal.
Calculates the predicted value that the
measurement reaches at the end of the goal
period or at the end of the page or widget’s date
range.
(Total relevant Measurement’s value until today *
Total Days for the goal’s period / # of days
Predicted Value
passed since the goal’s start period = Predicted)
If the goal’s period ends after the page or the
widget’s defined date range, the total days for the
goal’s period are calculated through the last day
of the widget or page’s date range.
Delivery rate The current performance rate of the goal.
88.. To apply filters to your goal, click the Filters tab on the Add Widget menu.
99.. To inherit the page filters, select Inherit Page Filters checkbox. This checkbox allows the goal widget
to take on the same date range as the page. However, data is only displayed if the page’s date range is
within the goal’s date range.
For example, your goal’s date range is between January 2022 and June 2022. If the goal is set to
inherit page filters, and the page is set to display data between January and February 2022, the goal
automatically updates and reflects that date range. If the page is set to display data for July 2022, the
goal doesn’t display any data because this date range is outside of the goal’s defined date range.
1100.. Save your Table Goal widget.

in the hierarchy.

SmartLenses in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
SmartLenses are predefined dashboard pages that are created for you after a supported data source is
created for the first time. After the data source is processed and data is retrieved, a notification appears
with a link to the SmartLenses dashboard page. All SmartLenses dashboard pages are hosted under one
designated SmartLenses collection.
SmartLenses are available for these data sources:
•
Adform
•
Adroll
•
Adwords
•
Airpush
•
AOL 1-Video
•
Appnexus
•
Atlas
•
Bing
•
Brandwatch DV360

•

•

•

•

•

•

•
Yahoo Gemini
•
YouTube Ads (by Adwords)
•
YouTube Insights
554

=== PAGE 559 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
SmartLenses View
SmartLenses visualizations are built to match industry standards, tailored for the data source in question.
The SmartLenses view changes according to the data source and, in some cases, according to the
extended properties set up in the data source. For example, a Facebook data stream with no extended
properties creates a different dashboard page view than a Facebook data stream with the get country
checkbox selected.
Some data sources generate multiple SmartLenses dashboard page to provide a more convenient view.
For example, the Google Ads data source creates a general view dashboard page and a keyword level
dashboard page with a more in-depth view.
SmartLenses Settings
The SmartLenses collection has sharing permissions set to admins only, and all dashboard pages are set
to unpublished. You can change these permissions to make the SmartLenses available to other users or
copy these dashboard pages to a collection accessible to them.
The SmartLenses dashboard pages are filtered on the relevant data source and show data from all data
streams created for that data source. The dashboard page date range is the previous month, but you can
change the page’s settings.
Deleting SmartLenses Pages and Collections
If you delete a SmartLenses dashboard page, the dashboard page isn’t created in the workspace again. If
you delete the SmartLenses collection, a collection is created the next time a SmartLenses dashboard
page is created.

Use Advanced Export
Advanced export gives you the power to design the layout of your page before exporting it and
customizing it for print, presentations, or emails. A layout includes a page’s structure and saved
settings so that you can reuse it in the future. You can maintain different layouts based on your use
case, including interactive filtersets, and only include relevant materials.
Scheduled Reports
Admin and power users can create a scheduled report so that a collection, dashboard page, or widget
is sent to them at a specific time. This feature is especially useful to send executive overviews
automatically or to send a weekly report to stakeholders without manually downloading and sending
the report.

aa.. Choose whether it’s by time or event (1).

100.

ee.. Define how often you want to check if the conditions are true (6).
66.. In the Filters section, you can see all the filters that are applied to your rule and edit the date range.

Note
•
When exporting to PPT, Marketing Cloud Intelligence automatically splits long pages into multiple
slides according to the PPT slide height and the margin width at the top and bottom.
•
When exporting to Excel, Marketing Cloud Intelligence automatically splits each widget on the
dashboard page into individual tabs within the spreadsheet.
•
When exporting to Advanced Export, you can customize the export layout, including widget size,
page size, and hide widgets.
Use Advanced Export
Advanced export gives you the power to design the layout of your page before exporting it and
customizing it for print, presentations, or emails. A layout includes a page’s structure and saved settings
so that you can reuse it in the future. You can maintain different layouts based on your use case,
including interactive filtersets, and only include relevant materials.
560

=== PAGE 565 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To use advanced export Admin and Power User
11.. Go to the Visualize tab.
22.. Select the dashboard page you want to export.
33.. Click the Options menu, and select Advanced Export.
44.. Use these options:
•
Format — Configure the file type, paper size, and rotation of the dashboard page. When changing the
paper size or rotation, the width remains at 1,600 pixels and only the height changes.
•
Design — Configure the display of the layout, such as showing or hiding the title, logo, date range, or
background. When Include Background is selected, you have these two options:
- Fit to Page — Applies the background image to the paper size you selected.
- Repeat Background — Applies the selected image to the whole report while scrolling. It’s helpful
when you have long pages with a background image that is smaller than the page length. Note that
Repeat Background can only be selected when exporting to PDF or PNG.
•
Widget Options — Control the widgets that appear in your layout. These two options are available:
- Hidden Widgets — Select the widgets you want to exclude from the export. Use the dropdown to
select the widgets you want to omit.
- Add New Widgets Automatically — When you save your layout, checking this checkbox ensures that
any new widgets added to your dashboard page are also added to your export layout.
•
Interactive FilterSet — Filter your advanced export layout based on one of the interactive filtersets you
created. When toggled on, you can select the interactive filterset you want to apply to the layout.
Note that:
- After you add an interactive filterset, you can hover over the i icon to see which filters are included
in the selected interactive filterset.
- You can create up to 200 layouts and filtersets per page.
- Admins and power users can add interactive filtersets to layouts.
- Viewers can see and use all advanced export layouts but can’t create any.
- There are currently no permission settings on filtersets or layouts, but user-level restrictions still
apply. If certain viewers have restrictions on specific dimensions or measurements, they can’t see
the data.
After you configure your layout settings, you can start to design your export by editing the layout
manually on the advanced export page itself. You can instantly see how your page looks before
exporting.
These options are available:
•
Widget resize — Hover over the widget borders, and click and drag to the size you want.
•
Page break — Hover over the left-hand side ruler, and click to add a separator line.
•
Drag and drop — Drag widgets into the position you want on the page.
Now that your layout looks how you want it to, you’re ready to export your file. You can Schedule your
export or Export it right away.
561

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence

REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create a scheduled report for a widget Admin and Power User
11.. Go to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard page where the widget is located.
33.. Select the widget you want to export.
44.. Click Share.
55.. Select Scheduled Report to be redirected to the Reports tab. A new report is created for you
according to the format you selected.
66.. Under Configuration, the Dashboard, Page, and Widget are automatically selected for you based on
the widget you exported.
77.. When you’re done defining your report, save it, or click Save & Run to run it.
Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers many tools for analyzing your data. All of these tools are found in the
Analyze and Act tab.

For example pivot tables ensure that your data is being loaded correctly from the source into Marketing
Cloud Intelligence and that the values are aggregating correctly. Pivot tables can be exported into reports
that can be set to run as part of a workflow, in real time, or according to a defined schedule. Goals are
monitoring tools that enable you to track KPIs, helping you improve your work strategies and outcome.
Activation Center
Use the Activation Center to connect to third-party platforms, such as Slack, Facebook Ads, and
Google Ads, to trigger alerts, collaborate, and automate workflows in other systems. For example, you
can create an action that sends a notification in Slack every time a certain campaign hits the target
value you set for it.
Einstein Marketing Insights
Einstein Marketing Insights analyzes your data to show key factors that affect your marketing
performance. Track your KPIs and verify that they perform as you intend by using Einstein Marketing
Insights bots. For example, if you track the average time on your website, the bot can point out the
path that increased the average time spent.
Goals
Use Marketing Cloud Intelligence Goals to track your performance KPIs. The goal tool helps you
monitor your progress against benchmarks you set. Marketing Cloud Intelligence goals bring you
closer to actionable insights and help you improve the efficiency and effectiveness of your data. You
can visualize your goals in your dashboards allowing you to view and monitor your KPIs in context, and
get notifications in real time for your KPIs.
Pivot Tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Explore your data from multiple perspectives using pivot tables. You can calculate, summarize, and
analyze data by seeing comparisons, patterns, and trends in your data. You can also create custom
reports without using query formulas, such as SQL queries.
Reports in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use reports to retrieve, view, and share a detailed analysis of the data that you’ve integrated into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence. You can save them locally or share them via email or the cloud. The data
uploaded into Intelligence is displayed as a flat file and includes selected dimensions and
measurements within a defined time range.
Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau
Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau to easily query your data. With the Marketing
Cloud Intelligence Tableau integration, you get harmonization capabilities with Intelligence’s ETL
solution and Tableau’s advanced visualization tools.
Use Database Exports to Export Datasets
Export data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence, and connect it to an enterprise database such as
Snowflake and PostgreSQL. With Database Exports, automate the export of Intelligence marketing
data. Enhance enterprise-wide analytics by making marketing insights accessible, and get a full view of
your company’s data.
Query Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data with the API Builder
Use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Query API Builder to perform advanced queries on your existing
data.

Activation Center
Use the Activation Center to connect to third-party platforms, such as Slack, Facebook Ads, and Google
Ads, to trigger alerts, collaborate, and automate workflows in other systems. For example, you can create
an action that sends a notification in Slack every time a certain campaign hits the target value you set for
it.
You can create actions and customize them using Webhooks and Python. And then you can
automatically trigger actions based on key performance indicators (KPIs) you set for your campaigns,
media buys, and creatives.
Actions and Action Rules
You can react, track, and manage your most important KPIs and benchmarks by using the activation
center data optimization tools. Access the tools from the Activation Center menu in the Analyze & Act
tab.
•
Actions — Interact with your channel or campaign. For example, send a Slack message to your
reporting channel, pause a campaign on Facebook, or send a custom email.
•
Action Rules — Define the triggers for your actions. Triggers for your stored actions are automatically
executed when the event you define occurs. You can trigger multiple actions for one action rule.
Action Types
These action types are available.
•
Email - send email to a list of contacts.
•
Webhook - trigger actions over the web in other applications.
•
IFTTT Connector - trigger an appellate in IFTTT.
•
Twilio Connector - send an SMS through Twilio.
•
Slack Connector - post to Slack.
•
Python - trigger python scripts.
•
Media Actions in Google, Twitter, and Facebook - trigger media actions such as stopping or starting
campaigns, ad sets, or ads.
Create Actions in the Activation Center
Create actions for third-party providers and channel platforms such as Facebook Ads and Slack.
Create Action Rules in the Activation Center
Action rules define how a rule is triggered. For example, the action can be triggered by time or by a
specific event. You can create a rule for several actions.
Trigger an Action Rule from the Management Screen
Triggering an action manually allows you to run an action rule at any time. If the action rule condition
is met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if your stakeholders ask for an
updated report in the middle of the week, you can trigger the action rule and see the latest
565

information.

drops below 100.
Einstein Marketing Insights
Einstein Marketing Insights analyzes your data to show key factors that affect your marketing
performance. Track your KPIs and verify that they perform as you intend by using Einstein Marketing
Insights bots. For example, if you track the average time on your website, the bot can point out the path
that increased the average time spent.
An Einstein Marketing Insights bot shows two types of insights.
•
Over- and under-performers
For example, a campaign’s click-through rate is 60% above average, with 11% of overall impressions
in 1 month.
•
Over- and under-performers
For example, a campaign’s click-through rate is 60% above average, with 11% of overall impressions
in 1 month.
For example, your click-through rate drops by 70% in a given week, but impressions increased from 2
million to 5 million, which caused the drop.
Here’s a video about Einstein Marketing Insights:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/YQkVputrU9BXZo5iUcE2KQ?

To analyze your datasets and uncover aspects that affect performance in a positive or negative way,
use an Einstein Marketing Insights bot.
Review Your Insights Bots
After you create your insights bots you can view and review the insights that are available.
Best Practices for Creating EMI Bots
Here are some best practices to consider when creating and using EMI bots.
Create an Einstein Marketing Insight Bot
To analyze your datasets and uncover aspects that affect performance in a positive or negative way, use
an Einstein Marketing Insights bot.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create Insights bots Admin or Power User
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab left navigation panel, click Einstein Marketing Insights.
22.. Click the plus icon.
33.. Select the KPI that you want to optimize such as Visitor Bounce or Click Through Rate. Not all
measurements are available as KPIs. A measurement is eligible to be used as a KPI in an Insights Bot if
it’s in the form of A/B, such as CPM, CPC, CTR, and other calculated metrics whose formulas are
aligned with this format (A divided by B).
44.. To add a filter, click Add Filter. Use a filter to narrow your bot results. For example, you can filter by
data stream, creative, or conversion tag.
55.. To optimize your KPI by increasing the value, click Increase. To optimize your KPI by decreasing the
value, click Decrease.
66.. To run your bot with default settings, click Save & Run.
77.. To further customize your bot, in the left navigation window, click Analysis Settings.
88.. From scheduling, set the frequency: how often you want the bot to run.
99.. Select the time period: the date range when you want the bot to run (2).
1100.. Select the dimensions that the bot examines (3).
1111.. To further customize your bot, click Advanced in the left sidebar (4). To better reflect the commercial
logic behind the selected measurements, change volume measurement. To send emails when new
insights are available, set email notifications.
1122.. Click Save and Run.
Review Your Insights Bots
After you create your insights bots you can view and review the insights that are available.
568

=== PAGE 573 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create Insights bots Admin or Power User
11.. From the Analyze & Act tab, on the left, click Einstein Marketing Insights.
22.. From the Insights Bots screen you can see the bots in your workspace. Each bot shows a data
summary.
33.. To group your bots by KPI, toggle Group by KPI. This view separates your bots based on matching
KPIs. For example, see all the bots that track bounce rate.
44.. To learn more about a bot, click it and review the four sections.
•
The bot name and objective (1).
•
What changed and a quick view of the insights for: Week over Week (WoW), Month over Month
(MoM), and overall change (2).
•
To narrow the insights, use the filters bar (3).
•
View a list of Bot insights, ordered by EMI’s evaluation of most valuable insights. Each insight includes
detailed information (4).
•
To learn more about an insight, click it. You can see a detailed breakdown of the KPI and the
components leading to positive or negative impact. And you can see comparisons of similar
campaigns.
Best Practices for Creating EMI Bots
Here are some best practices to consider when creating and using EMI bots.
•
Filter to a scope that you can easily control and monitor. Consider filtering your bots to a single
campaign. For example, the Budget measurement moves relatively easily within a single campaign,
but not so easily between more than one campaign.
•
Select dimensions that you can easily target or that are practical such as age, gender, or demographic.
•
Have at least two full quarters of historical data in your workspace for WoW, MoM, and Quarter-over-
Quarter analysis. EMI bots can process your custom date ranges if data is available.
•
EMI insights are a starting point . If you see something interesting, investigate it to discover more
insights.

your progress against benchmarks you set. Marketing Cloud Intelligence goals bring you closer to
actionable insights and help you improve the efficiency and effectiveness of your data. You can visualize
your goals in your dashboards allowing you to view and monitor your KPIs in context, and get
notifications in real time for your KPIs.

Create a Goal
Use goals to track your campaign KPIs and improve the outcome.
Set Your Goal Filters and Values
Filters can be set to customize your goal. You can filter goals according to a dimension or
measurement value. For example, you can choose to filter your clicks goal by a specific campaign.
Set Your Goal Notifications
While your goal is active, you can get notified on the progress of your goals and where they stand
compared to the benchmarks you set.
Set Your Goal Actions
In the Action section, define the actions you want taken for your goal.
Create a Goal
Use goals to track your campaign KPIs and improve the outcome.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create and manage goals Admin or Power User
11.. Navigate to the Analyze & Act tab.
22.. Expand Goals and then expand Goals List.
33.. Click Add New.
44.. In the General section:
aa.. Name your Goal - This name is used as the name of the goal in the Goals List and the Scorecards
subtabs.
bb.. Select the measurement you want to target in your goal.
cc.. Set the measurement type — Determine if your measurement is summable or non-summable. Non-
summable measurements usually relate to percentage measurements. The measurement type
affects how the goal is calculated.
dd.. Determine how the goal’s success is measured:
The goal is a success if the measurement is at
least the given value or higher than the goal’s
value. For example, when tracking CTR, set the
At Least
lowest value that is considered successful as the
baseline and any value higher than the baseline
will be displayed as a success.
The goal is a success if the measurement
Exactly matches the exact value set for the goal. This
goal type is suitable for measurements that
570

=== PAGE 575 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
calculate a budget.
The goal is a success if the measurement is
At Most lower than the goal’s set value. This goal type is
suitable for measurements like CPA.
ee.. Set the goal’s time period, and the days to include. When tracking a campaign, you might want to
set the goal’s date range to match the campaign’s start and end dates.
ff.. If you want to make the time period more granular, you can apply a target value to the time period.
For example, if you select Quarter, the goal monitors the results for every quarter in comparison to
the set target value.
gg.. Select a dimension for the goal to be affiliated with. For example, if you select Campaign Name, the
goal monitors each campaign name in comparison to the target value. When selecting a dimension,
you can set a Dimension Hierarchy that allows you to drill down your view when visualizing goals.
Note The target value applies only to the selected dimension and not to its lower hierarchies.
hh.. Define the goal’s granularity. For example, if your goal is monitoring five campaigns, you can apply a
different value to each campaign, or you can apply a single goal value for all the entities. If you
choose to apply multiple values, the Values section is populated with the relevant values.
Note When choosing either a Period Granularity or a Dimension, the field you choose has to
contain data relevant to the date range of the goal. If there’s no data included, the Values
section can’t be populated.
ii.. Set the ranges for the success rate of the goal.
jj.. Select whether to set the goal’s value manually or automatically. The Automatic option is typically
used for checking ongoing values against your initial planning, for example, comparing impressions
to booked impressions. If you select Automatic, select the fill measurement from the dropdown.
kk.. If you didn’t configure the period granularity, you can use prediction sampling to pace your goal by
defining the number of days being measured. Prediction sampling affects only the Average Daily
Value, Predicted, and Goal Delivery Rate fields.
ll.. Select the rate calculation:
Compares the number of impressions reached
so far at the given pace to your desired goal and
Target Until Today
provides a percentage of the success rate on the
dashboard.
Calculates your goal’s success rate by comparing
the current status to your ultimate target or the
point you would be at currently within the goal’s
Entire Goal date range. For example, a goal set to reach
3,000 impressions within 30 days would ideally
reach at least 100 impressions per day to be
successful.
mm.. If you want your goal to Run After a specific data stream is processed, select the data stream from
the dropdown.

Pivot Tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Explore your data from multiple perspectives using pivot tables. You can calculate, summarize, and
analyze data by seeing comparisons, patterns, and trends in your data. You can also create custom
reports without using query formulas, such as SQL queries.
A common use case for using pivot tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence is to validate your data and
ensure that it’s loading correctly from the source and that the values are aggregating correctly. After you
create a pivot table, you can export it locally or turn it into a scheduled report.
Create a Pivot Table from Scratch
Use a pivot table to explore and extract data.
Create a Pivot Table Using a Data Stream
Create a pivot table using a data stream to base it on already-defined measurements and dimensions.
Manage Pivot Tables
Edit, duplicate, and delete pivot tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Create a Pivot Table from Scratch
Use a pivot table to explore and extract data.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create and manage pivot tables Admin or Power User
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, click Pivot Tables.
22.. Click the plus (+) icon.
33.. Add the dimensions and measurements that you want to include in the pivot table by clicking + Add a
Field.
By default, dimensions are placed in rows, and measurements are placed in values. You can change
the format by manually dragging them to where you want. To hide a dimension or measurement from
the table, hover over the field name and click the eye icon.
44.. When you’re done, close the Add Data window.
55.. Select the date range that you want to view data for and how to display dates.

example, naming the first 4 weeks in January
“W1-W4.”

the pivot table. For example, 1 Jan 2022.

option.

click the filter icon in the relevant column header in the table, and select the filters.

A pivot table can include up to 8,000 rows, and you’re notified when you reach the limit. You can
either reduce the number of rows by updating the pivot table settings or adjusting the date range to
narrow the results or save the pivot table and download the full results.

Reports in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use reports to retrieve, view, and share a detailed analysis of the data that you’ve integrated into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence. You can save them locally or share them via email or the cloud. The data
uploaded into Intelligence is displayed as a flat file and includes selected dimensions and measurements
within a defined time range.
You can run reports as part of a workflow in real time, or according to a defined schedule. You can create
data reports and visual reports. Data reports display data in an Excel, CSV, TSV, or TXT file. Visual reports
create a screenshot of a dashboard page that you can export as a PNG, PDF, or PPT file.
Create a Data Report
A data report is based on the data that exists in your workspace. You can save it as an Excel, CSV, TSV,
or TXT file.
Create a Visual Report
A visual report is a screenshot of a dashboard page. You can export it as a PNG, PDF, or PPT file.
Configure Report Scheduling and Delivery
You can choose when and how to run and share a report. You can set up multiple schedules for a
report. For example, you can send the report to the management team on a weekly basis and to the
analyst team daily.
Manage Reports
After you create a report, it appears in your reports list. From your reports list, you can view all your
reports and edit, delete, and create a copy.
575

=== PAGE 580 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Create a Data Report
A data report is based on the data that exists in your workspace. You can save it as an Excel, CSV, TSV, or
TXT file.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To create and manage reports Admin or Power User
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, click Reports in the left panel.
22.. Click the plus (+) icon.
33.. Enter the general information:
aa.. Name the report, and select the export format. To pull your data into BigQuery, select CSV.
bb.. For Purge Report History, enter the number of days after which the report is deleted. For example,
if you enter 30, the report’s history is deleted after 30 days.
Note The default maximum number of days you can use the history is 180 days.
cc.. Optionally, select Include the Description in the report.
44.. Configure the report:
aa.. Select the time period that you want to pull data from or enter a custom date.
bb.. Select the measurements you want pulled into the report, for example, email sends. You can select
up to 100 measurements.
cc.. Select the dimensions you want pulled into your report, for example, send date. You can select up
to 80 dimensions.
dd.. Depending on the format that you select, you can choose how to sort the data.
ee.. (Optional) To apply a filter to the report, click Add Filters.
55.. (Optional) Add Advanced Report settings:
aa.. Assign the report a different name from the one you entered in the General section. For example,
you could create a general name for a report familiar to an admin role, but give the extracted report
a different name for the recipient of this report. You can also add tags in the report file name field.
For example, {DATA_START_DATE} and {DATA_END_DATE} automatically enter the report’s start and
end dates to the report name.
bb.. To include headers in the report, select Headers.
cc.. To split the pages when exporting, select an option from the Split Files By dropdown menu.
dd.. To create a unique encryption key to encrypt and decrypt your files, select GPG Encryption.
ee.. Define how to display unrelated measurements.
ff.. To only retrieve data created or updated during the selected time range, select Retrieve By Update
Date. Selecting this option replaces the date range defined in the Configuration section.
66.. Define scheduling and delivery methods for the report.
Unrelated Measurements in Data Reports
When you query entities from different data types with different measurements that are unlinked in
the platform, unrelated measurements appear in your report. For example, a report pulling Ads data—
Impressions and a measurement that isn’t in the Ads data type—visits, results in unrelated
measurements in your report.
576

=== PAGE 581 ===

Unrelated Measurements in Data Reports
When you query entities from different data types with different measurements that are unlinked in the
platform, unrelated measurements appear in your report. For example, a report pulling Ads data—
Impressions and a measurement that isn’t in the Ads data type—visits, results in unrelated measurements
in your report.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and manage data reports: Admin or Power User
There are two ways to show unrelated measurement data in your report:
•
Show unrelated measurements on default entity—shows the unrelated measurements’ total values in a
default entity of the dimension.
•
Duplicate unrelated measurements across rows—shows the unrelated measurements’ total values in
each row of the report.
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, in the left menu, select Reports.

33.. Scroll down, and expand Advanced Sheet "Sheet 1" Settings.

Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau
Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau to easily query your data. With the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Tableau integration, you get harmonization capabilities with Intelligence’s ETL solution and
Tableau’s advanced visualization tools.
You can connect your data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence to Tableau in two ways:
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence integration with Tableau (push method)
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence connector in Tableau (pull method)
Here’s a comparison of the two methods looking at which features are available:

Known Issues:
•
To prevent data source failures, all Tableau reports have unique names.
•
You can add or remove dimensions and measurements in reports that are part of an existing Tableau
data source. However, changing names of dimensions and measurements that are part of a data
source can cause processing failures.
•
If your Tableau Server instance is protected for external applications, ask your team to allow the
following IPs according to your Marketing Cloud Intelligence environment.

Use the push method to integrate your marketing data to create Tableau views, group them together,
and then download them to Tableau Desktop. With the push method, you work in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence. You can control where to send your data, which server to use, and which views you want
to make available to your users. You can push a single view or multiple views.

Push Reports from Marketing Cloud Intelligence to Tableau
Use the push method to integrate your marketing data to create Tableau views, group them together,
and then download them to Tableau Desktop. With the push method, you work in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence. You can control where to send your data, which server to use, and which views you want to
make available to your users. You can push a single view or multiple views.
You can also schedule automatic refreshes to Tableau Server or Tableau Online. All data is sent
automatically from Marketing Cloud Intelligence to Tableau, so you don’t have to refresh your data.
Push a Single View from Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Integrate Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau by pushing a single view. During the process, you
create a Tableau view, a Tableau feed, run the data source, and then visualize your data in Tableau
Online.
Push Multiple Views from Marketing Cloud Intelligence to Tableau
When you push multiple views, you match the values between the views in Tableau Desktop. You can
then publish to Tableau Server or Online.

Harmonize Your Data
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers multiple tools for harmonizing your data. Because you can upload
data from various sources, you need a way to merge the data and provide a complete view of your
marketing activity. In most cases, no single data source holds all relevant data on a marketing event or
campaign. The data is shared by the multiple platforms that digital marketers use to do things like
promoting the campaign on multiple channels. To analyze the full picture, harmonize data from all
sources into one. If data isn’t harmonized, you must analyze each source individually—making it a
challenge to gain insights quickly and efficiently.
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence data model is marketing oriented and facilitates natural harmonization
of marketing data from multiple sources. Another option is to use data harmonization tools, like the
harmonization center, parent-child, calculated dimensions, and measurements.

Parent-Child Connections in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A parent-child connection describes a case where two or more data streams are linked by a matching
entity key. This connection establishes hierarchical dependencies between entities, allowing for
grouping at various granular levels such as campaign, media buy, or creative. Matching entity keys
between the data streams are required to establish a parent-child relationship.
Fusing Data Between Datasets
Data fusion creates a link between dimensions that don’t already have a data model relationship
between them.
Custom Classifications in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Custom classifications are cross channel entities that are present in all data streams. They allow you to
link data streams together via an entity.

Naming Convention Patterns
In the distraction-filled world of a digital marketer, clarity is important. Therefore, when using different
marketing platforms, digital marketers often name an activity with all the information in the name.
Proper naming allows them to differentiate between the different campaigns, and understand campaign
goals and details at a glance without having to look elsewhere.
Digital marketers usually abide by a certain structure or naming convention for the campaign name.
Creating a naming convention pattern deconstructs your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and creates harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A naming
convention usually consists of the main details and contains a delimiter.
For example, in this naming convention each color represents an attribute and an underscore is used as
the delimiter. The naming convention can be divided into four attributes: Car Name, Car Category,
Promotion Name, and Site.
Here’s a video about naming convention patterns:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/beMMaYcnJS7QWghC4TMAeU
Create a Naming Convention Pattern
Create a naming convention pattern to deconstruct your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and create harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A harmonized
dimension acts like any regular calculated dimension and can be used in the platform, for example in
reports.
Manage and Monitor Harmonized Dimensions
After harmonized dimensions are created, they appear in a list so you can easily manage them. Add
classifications and validations, see previews, find and replace in bulk, and more.
Multi-Delimiter Patterns and Structure Compliance
A multi-delimiter pattern contains different types of delimiters in a pattern, such as an underscore,
colon, and comma. Structure compliance determines how and when extractions take place. With a
single delimiter pattern, an extraction occurs only for dimension values that adhere to the pattern’s
exact structure. For example, dimensions that have the same number of positions as the dimension
that you’re currently creating this pattern for. When working with multi-delimiter patterns, the logic is
594

different.

Multi-Delimiter Patterns and Structure Compliance
A multi-delimiter pattern contains different types of delimiters in a pattern, such as an underscore, colon,
and comma. Structure compliance determines how and when extractions take place. With a single
delimiter pattern, an extraction occurs only for dimension values that adhere to the pattern’s exact
structure. For example, dimensions that have the same number of positions as the dimension that you’re
currently creating this pattern for. When working with multi-delimiter patterns, the logic is different.
596

=== PAGE 601 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
Structure Compliant
When Structure-Compliant is toggled on, any dimensional value to which the pattern is applied is
compared to the pattern’s structure for an exact match. With multi-delimiter patterns, an exact match
occurs when a given dimension value and the applied pattern use the exact same type, quantity, and
order of delimiters.
This table describes what happens when a pattern structure is applied to the specified dimensions and
its results. The delimiters are in red. An extraction occurs only when there’s an exact match between the
pattern structure and the structure of the given dimension value. Any difference in the structure is
considered invalid, so no extractions occur.
In addition, there’s a sensitivity to whether extraction results are allowed to contain delimiter values. If an
extraction result contains one of the delimiters, it’s considered invalid. In this example, because the
second position extracts “35_50 HI,” which contains one of the defined delimiters, the entire extraction
is invalid.
Non-Structure Compliant
When Structure-Compliant is toggled off, a dimension value doesn’t have to match the applied pattern’s
structure in its entirety, from the first delimiter to the last. It follows the pattern structure up until the
extracted position. In the examples, although the dimension values don’t follow the entire pattern
structure from start to end, the values do follow it up until the extracted position. Extractions up until
this point are executed.
If the next delimiter in line is missing, the entire string that appears after the existing delimiter is
extracted as is and is attributed to the next position. In these examples, the second delimiter (/)
pertaining to position number 2 is missing. Everything that appears after the first delimiter (_) is
extracted and is attributed to position number 2.
If a position before the current extraction contains delimiters, the current extraction is considered
invalid. In these examples, there are no delimiters in any positions that come before positions 1 and 2.
Therefore, the extractions pertaining to these two positions are executed. Extractions from position 3
and onward are considered invalid because they contain delimiters.
The extraction result for position 2 contains a delimiter. Extraction results are only sensitive to delimiters
if they appear in positions before the current extraction. Delimiters that appear within the current
597

=== PAGE 602 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
extraction itself are considered valid, so the extraction is executed.
The string value that appears in the last position is extracted as is regardless of its contents, with or
without delimiters.

Status Bar
The status bar is split into these sections:
•
(1) An overall assessment of your data based on an average of the KPI totals.
•
(2) An assessment of your data based on source dimensions or harmonized dimensions.
•
(3) The percentage of source dimension values compliant with the data classifications you defined.
KPI Table
Breaks down the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for both harmonized dimensions and data
classification depending on the toggle setting. The default display shows the KPIs that are
underperforming at the top, allowing you to review the entities that need the most attention first.
You can sort the table by:
•
Status — Sorts the table to show the KPIs doing poorly at the top.
•
Type — Groups KPIs by type.
•
Name — Sorts the table alphabetically.
Use the search bar to locate a specific source dimension, harmonized dimension, or data classification
value. You can filter the table by selecting an option from the dropdown menu.

Fixing Data in Fix & Maintain
Use Fix & Maintain to make corrections to source dimensions, harmonized dimensions, and data
classification values. There are two ways to fix data. To make bulk changes, export exception reports
manually, fix incorrect values, and then import the fixed data. To make a few changes, use in-line
editing within Fix & Maintain and make changes directly in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.

Use Fix & Maintain to make corrections to source dimensions, harmonized dimensions, and data

within Fix & Maintain and make changes directly in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.

send to specific recipients.

Parent-Child Connections in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A parent-child connection describes a case where two or more data streams are linked by a matching
entity key. This connection establishes hierarchical dependencies between entities, allowing for grouping
at various granular levels such as campaign, media buy, or creative. Matching entity keys between the
data streams are required to establish a parent-child relationship.
Parent-child data streams are linked at the database level and are poured into one data pool, sharing
dimensional values. This connection allows for a complete view of the data sets from both data streams.
606

=== PAGE 611 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
Measurements aren’t shared across data streams in a parent-child relationship, and can be stored only in
their own data stream. For example, measurements from an ‘Ads’ data stream can’t be stored in a
‘conversion’ data stream. However, when both data streams are linked together in a parent-child
relationship, the measurements from both data streams can be viewed alongside each other.
In a parent-child relationship, both the parent and the child data streams have equal power in
contributing to the data pool to which they belong. Meaning, any data stream that is the first to
introduce an entity key to the parent-child data pool determines and dictates the hierarchy and
attributes for the introduced entity key.
Over time more sources can be added to a parent-child data pool that share the same entity keys but
have an updated hierarchy and attributes. You can configure which data streams update attributes and
hierarchy to keep up with the new updates. All entity keys and attributes are shared between all data
streams in the data pool. After the updated values are introduced, data streams that have permissions to
update, update the attributes or hierarchy for all shared entity keys.
Define a Parent Data Stream
Define a parent data stream to determine a connection between two data streams using a shared
entity.
Parent-Child Use Cases
You can use parent-child connections when there are multiple data streams that share the same keys,
but have unique attributes, and you must share a hierarchy between them. Here are examples of when
parent-child can be used.
Define a Parent Data Stream
Define a parent data stream to determine a connection between two data streams using a shared entity.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To define a parent data stream: Admin
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, click Data Streams and then Data Streams list.
22.. Select the data stream you’re assigning as a parent data stream and click Edit.
33.. Scroll down to Parent Child settings.
44.. From the Parent Data Stream dropdown list, select the data stream to set as the parent data stream.
55.. To view the streams in the parent-child data pool, click View All.
66.. In the Data Updates Permissions section, determine how your data stream feeds the parent-child
dimensional pool with these options:
Update Attributes (1) - Updates existing mapped attributes in the parent-child data pool every time
the data stream is ingested.
607

=== PAGE 612 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
Inherit Attributes and Hierarchy (2) - Prevents existing attributes and hierarchies from being updated.
Update Attributes and Hierarchy (3) - Updates existing mapped attributes and the media buy,
campaign, or site hierarchy in the parent-child data pool at every data ingestion.
Note Selecting this option enables the Override Media Buy Hierarchy option in the More
Options section and can’t be disabled.
77.. Save your changes.
88.. To apply the changes, the child data stream must be reprocessed. Recreating your data can have major
effects on the data. For more information see Re-Process, Re-Run and Re-Create.
99.. Select how to reprocess the data, and then click Recreate.
Parent-Child Use Cases
You can use parent-child connections when there are multiple data streams that share the same keys,
but have unique attributes, and you must share a hierarchy between them. Here are examples of when
parent-child can be used.
Use Case 1: Updating a parent data stream with measurements from a child
data stream
The parent data stream contains entity keys, attributes and measurements, and the child data stream
contains dimensions and measurements. You want to update the parent data stream with the
measurements that exist in the child data stream to match campaign keys. This means that you can view
campaign attributes from the parent data stream alongside measurements from the child data stream.
To do this, select the Updates Attributes and Hierarchy option for the Parent data stream.
This image shows how the data appears after the settings are defined for the parent data stream and the
child data stream:
Use Case 2: Classifying a parent data stream with attributes from a child
data stream
The parent data stream contains dimensions and measurements, and the child data stream contains
entity keys and attributes. You want the child data stream to update the attributes of the parent data
stream, while keeping the dimension hierarchy of the parent data stream. This means that you can view
attributes from the child data steam alongside measurements from the parent data stream. To do this,
apply the Update Attributes option to the Child data stream.
608

=== PAGE 613 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
This image shows how the data appears after the settings are defined for the parent data stream and the
child data stream:
Use Case 3: Entities Only
The parent data stream contains entity keys and attributes, and the child data stream contains
dimensions and measurements. You want the parent data stream to update the child's attributes for the
matching keys (Media Buy Key), while keeping its dimension hierarchy. This means that you can view
dimensions and measurements from the child, alongside the entity keys and attributes from the parent
data stream.
To do this, apply the Inherit Attributes and Hierarchy option to the Child data stream.
This image shows how the data appears after the settings are defined for the parent data stream and the
child data stream:
Fusing Data Between Datasets
Data fusion creates a link between dimensions that don’t already have a data model relationship
between them.
For example, campaign and site dimensions have a data model relationship, whereas campaign and a
generic dimension don’t. The link created by the data fusion is based on identical values of the fused
dimensions. Values that are similar are viewed with attributes and measurements that are associated
with one another. In this example, Dimension A is fused with Dimension B, resulting in a link between
the two dimensions.
Fusion is a workspace-level setting, so it doesn’t require data stream reprocessing to take effect. It’s
created dynamically at run time as part of the query processing, much like calculated measurements and
dimensions.
Data fusions in Marketing Cloud Intelligence are transitive. If Dimension A is fused with Dimension B,
and Dimension B is fused with Dimension C, then Dimension A is also fused with Dimension C because
of their chain relationship. Therefore, the measurements and attributes that are linked to Dimension C
are also linked to Dimension A, and vice versa for like values.
Additional information about data fusion:
609

=== PAGE 614 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
•
Data fusion implemented between the same two entities potentially won't work -- for example, two
media buy attributes fused with two social element attributes.
•
Multiple fusions between the same entities aren’t allowed, but can happen.
•
Transitive fusions between entities that already have a data model relationship between them don’t
work. For example, Site Name and Conversion Tag already have a relationship between them by virtue
of the data model.
When to Use Data Fusion
Use data fusion to unify data that doesn’t naturally harmonize by virtue of the data model based on
shared dimension values.
Create a Data Fusion
Create a data fusion to link two datasets with a shared dimension together.
Applying Filters to Fused Dimensions
When applying a filter to fused dimensions, the filtering impacts the datasets associated with all the
fused dimensions.
When to Use Data Fusion
Use data fusion to unify data that doesn’t naturally harmonize by virtue of the data model based on
shared dimension values.
For example, Ads and Fraud Detection datasets are mapped to a generic data stream type. You want to
see a unified dataset with Ads data next to Fraud Detection data. Create a data fusion between them
because they both share the Media Buy External Key dimension.
After you set up the data fusion, you can view each dimension to which the fusion was applied with
measurements related to the other dimension.
Additional Use Cases
Because data fusion is transitive, you can also apply a data classification rule to Dimension B, which is
already fused with Dimension A. In this case, the classification for B is also applied to A.
Data classification is a system-generated data fusion between the Classification Dimension and the
dimension to which it’s applied.
610

=== PAGE 615 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
The result is another case of a chain relationship between three dimensions connected by two data
fusions, with a linear connection between them.
Data fusion saves you from creating the classification rule for each dimension in a chain of fused
dimensions.
You can also apply one data classification to another data classification.
The first data classification is a system-generated data fusion between Dimension A and the classification
dimension applied to it, referred to as A1.
The second data classification is also a system-generated data fusion between A1 and the new
classification dimension applied to it in succession, referred to as B1.
The result is another case of three dimensions, connected by two data fusions, with a linear connection
between them. B1 classifications also now apply to Dimension A.
Use data fusion in cases where two different classifications must be applied to the same dimension.
Create two classification columns within the same classification file that is used in the data classification.
Sometimes these classifications aren’t available within the same file or are created by different teams. In
that case, you can use this solution for the same effect.
Create a Data Fusion
Create a data fusion to link two datasets with a shared dimension together.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a data fusion: Admin
Note You can’t select overarching entities, calculated dimensions, and Web Analytics Page Path
dimensions for a data fusion.
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, click Dimensions and then Data Fusion.
22.. Click Add New.

=== PAGE 616 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
aa.. Give the data fusion a unique name (1) and add a description (2).
bb.. On the left side, the Dimension dropdown (3) lists all the dimensions that are mapped in your
workspace. Select the first dimension that you want to create the data fusion for. For example,
select Media Buy Key.
cc.. After you select a dimension, the Dimension dropdown on the right (4) lists all the dimensions that
can be fused with the first dimension. Select the dimension to fuse. For example, select DF Media
Buy Key. The Case-Sensitive option is selected by default (5). Marketing Cloud Intelligence doesn’t
perform data fusions on dimensional values that don’t follow the same capitalization rules.
33.. Save your work.
Applying Filters to Fused Dimensions
When applying a filter to fused dimensions, the filtering impacts the datasets associated with all the
fused dimensions.
For example, when filtering on Unit Name “d” (from the generic data stream), values of the Media Buy
Name dimension from the Ads data stream are also filtered accordingly.
Custom Classifications in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Custom classifications are cross channel entities that are present in all data streams. They allow you to
link data streams together via an entity.
Custom classifications are attributes of the main entity in each data stream type. For example, in the Ads
data stream type, ‘Custom Classification 1’ is an attribute of ‘Media Buy,’ whereas in the Social Object
data stream type it’s an attribute of ‘Social Element’.
Custom classifications aren’t tied to a data stream, thus deleting the data stream doesn’t delete
dimensional values already uploaded by that data stream. The dimensional values are present in your
workspace, but aren’t connected to any measurement, and don’t affect your data or workspace row
usage. If you need to delete dimensions that are related to custom classifications, you must manually
remove them via the Dimension Explorer.
Custom Classification Use Cases
Use this example to see how custom classifications are used to connect data from different data
streams.
Custom Classification Use Cases
Use this example to see how custom classifications are used to connect data from different data streams.

The classification data stream is mapped in any data stream type, and the mapping looks like this:
You can now slice the data from the Ads data stream type using ‘Custom Classification 1’.
If something changes, for example the color of Product ID ‘222’ changes, change the classification file
and process it. You don’t need to reprocess the Ads data stream for the data to change.
Media Transparency Center
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center allows you to ingest, unify, and analyze
Media Plan and Delivery data to pace, track costs, and effectively optimize your media budgets and
campaigns.
For example, if you have a budget for an insertion order (IO), you can compare it to the generated media
cost and calculate how much of the budget has been spent and at what rate. If you are at 50% of the
allocated time frame and 60% of your budget is already spent on the campaign, you can see an over-
delivery and adjust as needed.
Note The Media Transparency Center is a premium feature available upon purchase

Media Transparency Center Setup
Learn how to set up the Media Transparency Center for the first time.
There are five steps to setting up the Media Transparency Center:
Step 1: Identify Essential IO (Insertion Order) Fields
Buy Data, the data containing all your planned activities and costs (sometimes also referred to as ‘Media
Plan’ or ‘Planned Data’), can come in through a variety of platforms, such as Salesforce, MediaOcean,
Nexelus, Google Sheets, and others. While Buy Data might arrive in different formats, it must contain a
core set of fields that are essential for an IO’s structure and function.
These are the essential fields:
•
ID
•
Name
•
Start/End Dates
•
Cost Types
•
Rate
•
Margin (optional)
•
Budget
Here are some examples of field these fields’ values:
STEP 2: Set up Custom Cost Types
Marketing Cloud Intelligence has a set of default Cost Types you can use for your IOs. These Cost Types
are as follows: CPM, CPC, CPCV, CPE, CPV, VCPM, CPA, dCPM, dCPC and FLAT. However, if your Buy Data
contains a Cost Type that isn’t one of these, you can add it using the ‘Custom Cost Types’ option in the
Workspace settings. Creating a Custom Cost Type can be done quickly and easily, as follows:
11.. Define the Cost Type in Media Transparency Center
614

=== PAGE 619 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
First navigate to the Cost Center by clicking the Intelligence (Datorama) logo on the top right, then
your User Name, then Workspace in the left pane menu, and finally on ‘Cost Center’.
Then set your Custom Cost Type by selecting the Measurement to which it applies, and assign a
Display Name exactly as it appears in the Buy Data file.
For example, GoingSolar’s digital marketing activity puts a strong emphasis on generating leads for
prospective clients. Because leads are an expensive yet important resource, their generation amid all
marketing costs is closely watched. GoingSolar’s Buy Data therefore has CPL (Cost per Lead) as one of
its main Cost Types. CPL isn’t on the list of default Cost Types, so it has to be set up as a Custom Cost
Type:
Things to consider when configuring a Custom Cost Type:
••
The Measurement you select to base the Custom Cost Type upon can only be a Mapped Field that
exists in your Delivery Data. It is impossible to base Custom Cost Types on Filtered or Calculated
Measurements.
••
The selected Measurement can only be Mapped in one of the following Data Stream Types: Ads,
Conversion Tags, or Ads Verification'.
••
Custom Cost Type for a given IO is calculated as follows: <Selected Measurement> X IO Rate.
So in our example, if a given IO has generated a total of 211 Leads within a certain date range, and
the IO Rate for CPL for that IO is $2.5, the calculation the system would perform is: 211 X $2.5 =
$527.50:
••
Since the calculation performed by the Media Transparency Center for a Custom Cost Type is always
a direct multiplication of the <Selected Measurement> by the ‘IO Rate’, calculations that require
additional steps, must have these steps applied from within the mapping of the Selected
Measurement. For example, if the Custom Cost Type required is the ‘Cost per 1,000 Leads’, then the
Leads Generated value has to be divided by 1,000 from within the mapping of the Leads Generated
measurement:
This way, when the Multiplication in the Transparency Center occurs, the Selected Measurement is
already pre-divided by 1,000.
••
Apply a Cost Type function in the Buy Data data stream mapping
22.. Apply a Cost Type function in the Buy Data data stream mapping.
For any Custom Cost Type created, in order for it to work, it has to be referenced from the Mapping of
the Buy Data Stream using a dedicated Custom Cost Type Function:
COSTTYPE(<Name of Cost Type Field in the Buy Data File>)
This function searches for the Cost Type value in the Cost Center, obtains the Selected Measurement
and uses that to multiply by the IO Rate.
615

=== PAGE 620 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Since Custom Cost Types are generally used in addition to Standard Cost Types, could handle both
cases in the Mapping formula of ‘IO Cost Type’. In such a case, the formula would be as follows:
if(csv['Cost Type']=='CPL')
{
return COSTTYPE(csv['Cost Type']);
}
else
{
return csv['Cost Type'];
}
STEP 3: Mapping your Buy Data
The following screenshot shows how each of the essential IO Fields should be mapped:
The IO Key or ID can either be found in the source file itself as the key that uniquely identifies each row
in question, as shown below:
If it isn’t present in the file as a unique identifier, you can create an IO Key using a mapping formula to
concatenate a group of fields that together identify each row uniquely:
The IO Name is the name given to the marketing activity in question. A common practice is to name the
row after the Campaign that this IO represents. Like the IO Key, the IO Name can also be created or
modified using a mapping formula
The IO Start Date and IO End Date indicate the date range during which an IO is active. Recalculations
aren’t carried out for dates on which an IO is NOT active.
The next fields refer to the IO Measurements:
•
IO Cost Type denotes the type of Recalculation that will be performed for a given IO. For example,
CPM means that if a certain IO has generated 20,000 Impressions, the Recalculated Cost is: (20,000 /
1000) * IO Rate = 20 * IO Rate
•
IO Rate is the Planned Cost or ‘price tag’ for every unit (or 1000 units). This is the rate that is used for
cost recalculations for a given IO. For example if a certain IO’s Cost Type is CPC and its IO Rate is
$0.20, then the Recalculated Cost would be: Total Number of Clicks X $0.2
•
IO Margin is optional. It denotes the Margin that is added to the Recalculated Cost (or to the Media
616

=== PAGE 621 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Cost in cases of Dynamic Cost recalculations).
•
IO Capped Cost indicates the maximum value for Actualized Cost and its subsequent calculations,
capping them out after this value has been reached.
STEP 4: Connecting your Buy Data to your Delivery Data
You can connect your buy data to your delivery data using one of the following four methods:
11.. Associating each individual IO with its Delivery Items from the UI:
Start from the IO management tab, locate the relevant IO from the list and double-click it in order to
edit. Then, scroll down to the tab at the bottom entitled ‘Items’ and expand it in order to enter
Delivery items that this IO is associated with. In the example below, the Delivery items added are
identified by their Media Buy Keys:
You can also link IOs to Delivery data using a combination of Site and Campaign Keys, or by
‘Classification’ as explained separately in method 4. Linking your IOS to your Delivery data from within
each individual IO however, isn’t the best method to use when having to create this association at
scale.
22.. Mapping your Delivery data as part of your Buy Data:
Your Delivery Item Keys might appear in the Buy Data file, as shown here:
In this case, you can associate the IO Keys with the Delivery Item Keys by simply mapping the relevant
Delivery Field to the matching Intelligence Field in the Buy Data data stream:
The availability of the Delivery Items in your Buy Data however is rare and in most cases you won’t be
able to use this solution.
33.. Using an additional Buy Data Stream
You can associate your IOs with your Delivery Data by creating an additional Buy Data, data stream (in
addition to the original Buy Data Stream you already have) which includes your IO Keys alongside your
Delivery Item Keys:
Since MTC data is at the workspace level rather than at the data stream level, all ‘buy data’ data
streams created under the same workspace are part of the same pool of buy data. Therefore, any
617

=== PAGE 622 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
matching IO Key values ingested by different ‘buy data’ data streams are effectively considered as the
same IO entity, thus creating an association between IO keys and Media Buy Key Delivery Items in the
above example.
44.. Using the ‘MTC Classification’ method
IOs can be associated with Delivery Items by virtue of a shared MTC classification. In the example
below, IO Key cvb123 in the buy data and three media buy keys within the delivery data, all share the
same classification value 'term1'. The result is that IO key cvb123 is associated with the three delivery
items that have the same classification:
When using the MTC Classification method, in order to assign a classification value within the Buy
Data data stream, use the ‘IO Item Classification Term’ and in order to assign a classification value
within the Ads (Delivery) data, use the ‘Media Buy IO Classification’
STEP 5: QA Your Data
The first step is to ensure that the uploaded Buy Data has been converted successfully into IOs. To do
this, navigate to the IO Management tab, where you should see all the IOs displayed:
Next, access a few IOs randomly to make sure the IO Attributes such as IO Cost Type, IO Start Date, IO
End Date, IO Rate etc. are correct.
Additionally, verify that the IO's associated Delivery Keys are correct:
You can now visualize the connection between the Buy Data and the Delivery Data by creating a Table
Widget or a Pivot Table that has the IO Name with some of its Main Attributes of Interest. Also make sure
you include the Measurements related to the IO Cost Types, and the IO Recalculated Cost as shown in
the following example:
Finally, using the visualized data above, test the following:
•
IO Recalculated Cost for selected IOs as per the Measurement to which the Cost Type applies and the
IO Rate.
•
If you have any Custom Cost Types, test the calculation for those as well.
•
Look out for any “Other” values in the IO Name column. Any such values would suggest that the row
in question pertains to Delivery activity for which no IO has been defined, or one that has been
defined incorrectly with wrong Delivery Item Keys, or an incorrect date range.
618

=== PAGE 623 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Creating an IO in the Media Transparency Center
Learn how to create an IO in the Media Transparency Center.
Note The Media Transparency Center is a premium feature available upon purchase
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center allows integration of media buying information
from a media buying system into your Marketing Cloud Intelligence account.
After the data is added to your account, Intelligence does all the complicated calculations for the defined
date ranges, according to the specified cost type and given rate, taking into account parameters like
monthly budget, booked units, and margins. This financial information is stored as IOs in Intelligence
and uses the data that comes from the various delivery systems to recalculate the actual media cost.
There are two ways to create an IO key in the platform:
11.. Add it through the IO Management sub tab under the Media Transparency Center tab.
22.. Uploading Buy Data into the platform, through TotalConnect
Method 1: IO Management
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, click Media Transparency Center.
22.. Select the IO Management sub tab.
33.. Click Add New.
In the General, Target Configuration, and Data sections:
11.. Enter the IO Key. The IO key is the unique id according to which the platform identifies the IO.
22.. Enter the IO Name.
33.. Choose the IO Cost Type – This is how your recalculated cost is calculated for the relevant delivery
items.
44.. IO Start Date — The start and end date determines the time period of the delivery data to which the
calculations in the IO are relevant. You can’t set a time range of more than three years for the IO — the
date picker won’t allow you to set an end date that exceeds this time range.
55.. Select the IO Target Type - This defines the target for the IO.
66.. Enter the IO Target Value that is used to measure the actual value against the margin.
77.. IO Rate — Enter the rate that defines the cost of a single unit your cost type refers to (for example, a
Click in the case of a CPC). However, if your cost type is dCPM or dCPC you don't need to add a rate
value.
88.. IO Capped Cost — Enter the capped cost for this IO that defines its budget limits.
99.. IO Booked Units — Enter the booked units for this IO. This sets its delivery limits (for example, booked
impressions).
1100.. IO Margin Rate — If a margin exists in your calculations, add it here. This margin is applied on top of
the recalculated cost.
1111.. IO Group Name — If you want to include this IO in a group, enter the group name here. Groups allow
619

=== PAGE 624 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
you to view the IO Measurements on a higher level using IO Group Measurements.
1122.. IO Group Capped Cost is the Capped Cost of the group level. It’s unrelated to the IO Capped Cost,
meaning it won’t sum up the IO capped Costs, but apply a separate capping at the IO Group level.
Method 2: Upload Buy Data with TotalConnect
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, add a data stream through Total Connect.
22.. For Data Stream type select Buy Data.
33.. Enter the Data Stream Source. The file should have the fields specified in the IO Management subtab
as described earlier.
44.. Concerning the IO start and end dates, the limit for three years’ time range for an IO still stands. If the
source file contains IO with a time range that exceeds three years, the processing of the file will be
successful, however, the Problematic end date will be altered.
The Buy Data bucket maps the IO fields and create the IO entities automatically in the Media
Transparency Center.
Sometimes the cost type value in the original file doesn’t match the preset ones in the platform. In these
cases you can use the following formula (in this example for FLAT cost type):
IF(csv['RATE_TYPE'] == 'Flat Rate','FLAT',csv['RATE_TYPE'])
Where:
•
RATE_TYPE: is the name of the field in your file.
•
"Flat Rate": is how it appears in your file.
•
"FLAT": is the Marketing Cloud Intelligence value to convert to.
Now you can:
•
Create an IO in the Media Transparency Center IO Management screen.
•
Create an IO in the Media Transparency Center using a file upload.
Attaching a Delivery Item to an IO in the Media Transparency
Center
Learn how to attach a delivery item to an IO in the Media Transparency Center.
An IO holds financial data which together with delivery data enables recalculating media cost. In order to
attribute delivery data to an IO you will need to attach delivery items to an IO.
A delivery item can be a Media Buy, Site, Campaign, Vendor or Classification. These items hold Delivery
Data like Impressions, Clicks and other measurements which enables Cost Calculation.
Note For the MTC correlation between Delivery Items and IO's to take place correctly, you must add
your Buy Data and your Delivery Data to the same Workspace.
620

=== PAGE 625 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
There are two ways to add an item to an IO:
11.. Add the item through the Media Transparency Center (MTC) IO Management sub tab .
22.. Add a data stream of Buy Data into the platform with TotalConnect.
Method 1: IO Management
In the Media Transparency Center (MTC), under the Connect & Mix tab, select IO Management and
choose the IO you want to add an item to. In the Items section, click Add Item.
Then insert the appropriate key in its assigned field.
Each row should contain one combination of keys that describes a delivery item (for example. a Media
Buy Key or a Site key together with a Campaign Key).
In the Classification field enter the relevant value of the Media Buy IO Classification Dimension. When
adding an item based on Classification you must enter a Campaign Key as well.
Method 2: TotalConnect
To connect between an IO and an Item you can add a file through the TotalConnect Connector. The file
should be Dimension only and include the IO key and the relevant item key.
When uploading, the file automatically appears in the IO screen (there is no need to connect the two
Data Streams with a parent-child connection).
Notes:
•
If an IO's Delivery Items don't have Delivery Data for its Time period and filters, that IO is considered
never to have been fulfilled in terms of Delivery, and will not be visible in a Database Query. The
planned, or Buy Data, has to be matched with Measurements data in order to show up in a Widget,
Report, or Pivot Table. You will be able to see the IO planned data in Media Cost Center, but not in
queries.
•
When you attach a Delivery Item that doesn’t bring in any Data for the relevant IO date range it will
receive the 'Other' Value.
