Media Transparency Center FAQ
Read our FAQ to learn about the most common Media Transparency Center questions and answers.
•
What's the difference between Actualized Cost and IO Actualized Cost (By Day)? What's the use case
for using each one?
•
How can I view the cost of entities on a level lower than IO in case of a FLAT rate type IO?
•
How do I create an IO with a Cost Type / Target Type that doesn’t exist in the platform?
•
When slicing Delivery Data on IO Dimensions it seems the Delivery Data values are higher than what
would be expected. Why is this happening?
•
When slicing delivery data on IO Dimensions I see data under an IO named 'Other'. What caused this
situation and why is there Delivery Data connected to this IO?
•
What is the calculation for Recalculated Cost in the Datorama platform? Based on the margin rate I'm
charging, I'm expecting to see different results.
•
How do I connect delivery data to an IO?
•
What is the Actual Value column in the Margin Adjustment Tool and how is it being calculated?
What's the difference between Actualized Cost and IO Actualized Cost (By
Day)? What's the use case for using each one?
The Actualized Cost Measurement is used to display actualized cost for entities on levels lower than an
IO, for example, Media Buy. Its calculation is based on maintaining the ratio between the entity's
recalculated cost to the recalculated cost of the entire IO. The calculation is as follows:
632

=== PAGE 637 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
The Actualized Cost by day Measurement presents the Recalculated cost until the point when the IO
reached its budget, and from that point and on it will show 0 cost.
The choice between the two actualized Measurements depends on the type of Entity you’re using in your
view and the information you’re trying to visualize:
•
For Date Entities (Day, Week, and so on) you can use either one of the Measurements, depending on
what you want to see. Remember that currently IO Actualized Cost (By day) must be presented with a
Date Dimension.
•
For any other Entity (Media Buy, Site, and so on) use Actualized Cost.
How can I view the cost of entities on a level lower than IO in case of a FLAT
rate type IO?
Since FLAT Rate Type IOs don't base their cost on Delivery Data, the cost for each entity is approximated
by the ratio between the number of the relevant Entity's Impressions and the total number of
Impressions delivered during the IO's date range.
The calculation is as following:
Note that 'IO Recalculated Cost' doesn't hold any value in case of a 'FLAT' Rate Type IO. If you want to
aggregate the cost of a 'FLAT' Rate Type IO with other IOs, use 'Actualized Cost'.
How do I create an IO with a Cost Type / Target Type that doesn’t exist in the
platform?
First, set a Custom Cost Type from the Media Transparency Center tab under the Workspace's settings.
Choose the relevant Measurement from the drop-down list.
The calculation is Measurement*Rate. Select the display name for this Custom Cost Type / Target Type
and click Save.
Custom Cost Types can only be based on Native Measurements (that is, Measurements that are mapped
via a Data Stream). In case you want your Cost Type to be based on a Calculated Measurement, you need
to create it during the mapping process with the TotalConnect formulas.
The new Custom Cost Type is available in the drop-down list in the IO Management tab along with
platform Cost Types. In case you are using TotalConnect to create your IOs, you will need to use the
function COSTTYPE () or TARGETTYPE() in the relevant fields in order to map your IO correctly. These
functions perform a lookup to the available Cost Type / Target Type in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
For example, if my data is as following:
633

=== PAGE 638 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
IO Key
IO Name Cost Type IO Rate
Generic Key no. 1 Generic IO no. 1 CPCV 1.5 100
The formula to use to map the 'Cost Type' dimension is: COSTTYPE(csv['Cost Type'])
It isn’t possible to verify a formula that contains the COSTTYPE function by clicking 'Validate Formula' in
the mapping screen. Test the logic of your formula before adding the COSTTYPE function and then
testing the results of the full formula in a Pivot Table.
When slicing Delivery Data on IO Dimensions it seems the Delivery Data
values are higher than what would be expected. Why is this happening?
This can happen if a Media Buy is connected to more than one IO at a given point in time. This isn’t an
expected behavior, but if this does occur, the Delivery Data of the Media Buy will be used to calculate the
cost for any IO that it's connected to.
When slicing delivery data on IO Dimensions I see data under an IO named
'Other'. What caused this situation and why is there Delivery Data connected
to this IO?
The IO named 'Other' is an entity's instance created just for the sake of the view, it represents the
Delivery Data that isn't connected to any IO at the requested date range.
What is the calculation for Recalculated Cost in the Datorama platform?
Based on the margin rate I'm charging, I'm expecting to see different results.
First, you need to know the differences between Markup and Margin. These two terms are commonly
being misused and are the key to understanding the calculation that Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses
in the 'Media Transparency Center'.
Margin
The ratio between the additional costs added on top of the basic Media Cost and the
Recalculated Cost, the final amount the client pays (considering the capped cost, if there’s one)
Markup
The ratio between the additional costs added on top of the basic Media Cost, and the basic
Media Cost.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses Margin to calculate the additional costs added over the Media Cost. If
you want to calculate according to Markup, please contact our support team with the relevant details
(Workspace ID).
634

=== PAGE 639 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
The calculation for Recalculated Cost in the Intelligence platform is as follows:
For example, if your data is:
IO Name Media Cost IO Margin Rate
Generic IO no. 1 100 $ 0.2
The 'Recalculated Cost' will be calculated as follows:
How do I connect delivery data to an IO?
There are two common ways to connect Delivery Data to an IO, both of which are based on creating a
connection between the 'IO Key' and the relevant Entity in the Delivery Data. You can either connect the
keys with Media Transparency Center or with TotalConnect.
What is the Actual Value column in the Margin Adjustment Tool and how is
it being calculated?
The Actual Value is calculated as follows:
The Delivery Measurement is inherited from the Target Cost Type set for the IO. For example, if the Target
Cost Type is CPA, the Delivery Measurement would be Conversions. The actual value is colored according
to the Target Value set in the IO settings.
For example, if you had a Target Value of 1 and your Cost Type is CPC, your Recalculated cost is $10 and
you have 5 clicks, your actual value will be 2 more than your target value. Therefore it is colored red.
Grouping Delivery Data in the Media Transparency Center
Learn about grouping delivery data in the Media Transparency Center.
When looking at Buy Data in the IO or Media buy level, the delivery data can sometimes be grouped
under the 'Other' Entity.
This happens when the delivery data is relevant for a date range no IO is linked to.
635

=== PAGE 640 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
To further inspect the matter and make sure this is indeed an IO date range matter, create a report, pivot
table, or table widget with the following fields: Day, Media Buy Name/Key, IO key, IO start date, IO end
date, and Impressions.
You get the following:
In this example, you can see that for the same media buy key, the data in some of the days is attributed
to a real IO, and starting September 11, the data is attributed to 'Other'. Notice that the IO end date is
September 10, therefore, this data is out of the IO's date range.
Actualized and Recalculated Costs in the Media Transparency
Center
Learn about actualized and recalculated costs in the Media Transparency Center.
In working with Media Transparency Center, you’ll have noticed that quite a few new Cost Measurements
have been added to your Workspace, most are variants of two main Cost Type Measurements:
IO Recalculated Cost
IO Recalculated Cost is a recalculation of the cost, based on the IO Cost Type, IO Rate, and
actual units delivered, irrespective of any Budget limits.
Actualized Cost
Actualized Cost is the same recalculation of the IO Recalculated Cost, but with adherence to the
Budget (IO Capped Cost) as its limit, ‘capping out’ as soon as that budget has been fulfilled.
Definitions
IO Cost Type
Cost Type is usually an abbreviation of letters symbolizing both the calculation method used to
calculate the cost of digital marketing activity in question. Common Cost Type examples are CPC
(Cost per Click) and CPM (Cost per 1,000 Impressions).
IO Rate
The actual amount that each delivered unit will be charged in the cost recalculation. For
example, the IO Rate used for a CPC Cost Type, of a certain IO is $0.2 for each Click.
IO Margin Rate
An additional charge, defined as percentage, added on top of the cost recalculation. Typically
used by parties such as agencies, to factor in an additional commission charge.
636

=== PAGE 641 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Accumulative Costs
For example, IO Recalculated Cost (Accumulative), are cost recalculations that are date-based,
whose value on any particular day, is the total of all the days which preceded it. Furthermore,
Accumulative costs are always at the entire IO level, for all associated delivery items combined —
that is, even if viewed for a single delivery item, the value shown will be that of all delivery items
associated with the IO. Accumulative costs always start their accumulation from the IO Start
Date, even if that date falls outside the filtered date range of any particular view.
IO Group Costs
For example, IO Group Actualized Cost, are cost recalculations processed at the group level, for
multiple IOs defined as belonging to the same group.
IO Capped Cost
The value at which the IO’s Recalculated Cost caps — that is, the IO’s Budget. This Capping is
observed by Actualized Cost calculations.
Prorated Actualized Cost Value
While the IO Capped Cost is defined at the entire IO level, some users may wish to view the
Actualized Cost at the level of the IO’s Delivery Items, for example Media Buys, Campaigns and
so on. Since Actualized Cost is dependent upon the IO Capped Cost, which itself is defined for
the IO as a whole and not for each Delivery Item, there’s no way to derive the Actualized Cost for
each Delivery Item. Therefore when the IO Capped Cost is exceeded, the only way to assign an
Actualized Cost value to each Delivery item, is to prorate the entire IO Capped Cost by each of its
associated Delivery Item’s weight. This value is hence an estimation.
Re-Calculated Cost Measurements
Measurement Name Description
IO Recalculated Cost This measurement is a recalculation of the Cost
based on the Measurement value and Cost Type.
The recalculation varies depending on the IO’s
Cost Type, as follows:
CPM: IO Rate * Impressions / 1000
CPC: IO Rate * Clicks
CPA: IO Rate * Conversions(from the conversion
tags defined in the IO)
CPCV: IO Rate * Video Fully Played
637

=== PAGE 642 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Measurement Name Description
CPE: IO Rate * Social Total Engagements
VCPM: IO Rate * Viewed IAB Impressions / 1000
CPV: IO Rate * Video Views
dCPM: Media Cost / ( 1- IO Margin Rate )
dCPC: Media Cost / ( 1 - IO Margin Rate )
FLAT: will show no value
If the ‘IO Margin Rate’ field contains a value, that
value will be added to the IO Recalculated Cost as
follows: IO Recalculated Cost / (1 - Margin)
Can be used with: IO dimensions, IO items (Media
Buy, Site, Campaign), Dates
IO Recalculated Cost (Accumulative)
For any particular date, this Measurement shows
the total ‘IO Recalculated Cost’ for all the days
which preceded it, from the IO Start Date (even if
the IO Start Date falls outside the filtered date
range of any particular view).
Can be used with: IO dimensions, Dates
IO Group Recalculated Cost (Accumulative)
For any particular date, this Measurement shows
the total ‘IO Recalculated Cost’ for all the IOs that
belong to the same Group, for all the days which
preceded it, from the IO Start Date.
Can be used with: IO Dimensions, Dates
Actualized Cost Measurements
Measurement Name Description
IO Actualized Cost (Accumulative) For any particular date, this Measurement shows
the sum of all the Actualized Cost values for all the
days which preceded it, from the IO Start Date.
IO Actualized Cost (Total) The total sum of the Actualized Cost with a
maximum value of the IO Capped Cost.
638

=== PAGE 643 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Measurement Name Description
Actualized Cost
The total cost actualized (or used) out of the ‘IO
Capped Cost’. This Measurement is mostly
intended for use at a Delivery Item level. This
Measurement shows an assumed (or prorated)
value.
Calculation:
IO Share of Total X IO Actualized Cost (Total)
Can be used with: IO Dimensions, Dates, IO items
(Media Buy, Site, Campaign)
IO Actualized Cost By Day
On each day, shows the IO Recalculated Cost
while keeping track of its accumulated total. The
moment the accumulated total reaches or
surpasses the IO Capped Cost, the IO Actualized
Cost by Day will show a zero value on any
consecutive day from that point forth.
IO Actualized Cost By Day is calculated using the
following formula:
Recalculated Cost - IO Recalculated Cost
(Accumulative) + Actualized Cost (Accumulative)
If a negative value is returned, the IO Actualized
Cost By Day value shows as ‘0’.
Can be used with: IO Dimensions, Dates, IO items
(Media Buy, Site, Campaign)
IO Group Actualized Cost
The total cost actualized (or used) out of the total
‘IO Capped Cost’ of all the IOs that belong to the
same group. This Measurement is intended for
use at a Delivery Item level and its value is
assumed (or prorated).
Calculation:
IO Group Share of Total * IO Group Actualized
Cost (Accumulative)
Can be used with: IO Dimensions, Delivery

Workspace Settings in the Media Transparency Center
Learn about the workspace settings in the Media Transparency Center.
To access the Media Transparency Center Settings:
11.. Open Marketing Cloud Intelligence and access your account settings by clicking the Account
dropdown menu.
22.. Select the account for which you want to adjust the MTC settings.
33.. Click Workspaces and then select the specific Workspace.
44.. In the left pane, under the workspace settings menu click Cost Center.
As with other Workspace settings, MTC settings can also be pushed or imported across Workspaces in
the same account.
55.. In the MTC settings you can set Custom Cost Types and Custom Target Types.
If you create Custom Cost Types, their display names must be different than the System Cost Type
names. The system always uses the system calculations (the predefined measurements per cost type)
rather than the selected measurements in the Custom Cost Type fields.
66.. In the Measurement column select the Measurement you want to calculate your cost according to. For
example, if you chose Video Completes, the Cost/Target type should be Cost Per Video Completes.
77.. Under Margin Adjustment, you can choose what happens when you edit yesterday's margin by
selecting:
Never change IO’s default margin

Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To enhance your marketing intelligence solutions, check out the apps and tools offered on the Marketing
Cloud Intelligence Marketplace. Anyone can access the Marketplace, but depending on your user role,
640

=== PAGE 645 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
your access might be limited.
You can download apps to get marketing intelligence views within seconds. When you install an app,
Marketing Cloud Intelligence components, such as data streams, calculations, or dashboards, are added
to your workspace. These out-of-the-box solutions have no learning curve or code requirements.
Get Started with the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace is a repository of tools and apps for anyone creating a
marketing intelligence solution. Easily download and customize apps, connectors, and more from the
Marketplace. While anyone can access the Marketplace, each user role has different permissions.
Marketplace Apps
The Marketplace offers easy down-loadable apps that you can customize to your own needs. No
coding knowledge is required. After an app is installed, underlying entities are installed in your
workspace. You also get a run mode of the app with configured dashboards allowing you full control of
your views. It’s important to note that apps are installed for an entire workspace and only admins can
install them. If you’re a power user or viewer, you can add the app to your wishlist and your admin
installs the app for you.
Marketplace Connectors
The Marketplace offers a Connectors library with a number of customized connectors. With
connectors, connect to an external environment, instead of creating a custom connector. You can find
connectors that were developed by partners, third-party vendors, and Intelligence.
Marketplace Add-On Features
The Add-On Features tab is a centralized location for all of Marketing Cloud Intelligence add-ons. Add-
on features extend the capabilities of the platform, and can be enabled for free, or at an extra cost.
Marketplace Widgets
Install and use Marketplace widgets in your workspace to get the full functionality of pre-defined
custom widgets. Custom widgets include ready-to-use page visualization, navigation, and API
solutions. After installing a custom widget, you can edit it and change your dashboard page’s design
and behavior.
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Developer Portal
The Developer Portal is an easy-to-use and centralized location for developers who want to develop
tools, use cases, and examples. The Developer Portal offers these tools: Query API, Platform API,
Custom Data Connectors, and Apps.
Get Started with the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace is a repository of tools and apps for anyone creating a
marketing intelligence solution. Easily download and customize apps, connectors, and more from the
Marketplace. While anyone can access the Marketplace, each user role has different permissions.

Underlying Entities in Marketplace Apps
Underlying entities are created behind the scenes after Marketplace apps install. They enable the app
to function properly. Each app has its own set or combination of underlying entities depending on the
app’s purpose and functionality.
Understanding App Installations
What happens when Marketplace apps are installed? What determines whether an app installs
successfully or fails? Is there anything I can do to make sure the app installs successfully? If an app fails
during an installation, is there a way to see why and even fix it? These are only some questions you’re
probably asking yourself when installing Marketplace apps. This section explains how to troubleshoot
and fix errors that occur when installing apps.
Available Marketplace Apps
Here you can find information about all the available apps in the Marketplace. Click an app to view more
details about what it does, how to install it and how to use it after it’s installed in your workspace.
Audience Insights for Advertising Studio App
Optimize your audience strategy with a single, cross-channel view to analyze the effectiveness of your
Facebook and Twitter audiences. The Audience Insights for Advertising Studio app connects Marketing
Cloud Advertising to your paid media channels.
Customer Insights for Marketing Cloud Customer Data Platform App
Unify all your data from anywhere for a complete view of your customers. With the Customer Insights
for Marketing Cloud Customer Data Platform app, you can pull calculated insights, ingest predefined
insights on demographics, engagement, and conversion, and deploy out-of-the-box dashboards in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Cross-Channel Marketing Insights
The Cross-Channel Marketing Insights App (CCMI) connects your cross channel marketing data
including Facebook Ads, Instagram Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Pinterest Ads, Twitter Ads, and Google Ads. The
app allows you to optimize your paid campaigns across every major channel. This app is a great
starting point for users who are still going through an implementation process, as it enables you to
quickly connect your data sources with just one click. The app is also useful for customers who are
looking for a quick and clear overview of their campaign performance.
Data Dictionary App
The Data Dictionary app provides access to a list of all available fields in your workspace, including
calculated fields, such as calculated dimensions and measurements. Use this app to understand how
fields are built and how they relate to your data.
Data Readiness App
The Data Readiness app monitors the quality of your data harmonization efforts across all designated
dimensions. The app focuses on calculated, harmonized, and mapped dimensions. Use the Data
Readiness app to view the naming convention compliance of your dimensions and identify
noncompliant data sources.

Ecommerce Marketing Insights App
The Ecommerce Marketing Insights app shows you how your marketing efforts are driving ecommerce

eCommerce Optimizer App
The eCommerce Optimizer app provides a complete interactive picture of your Return On Ad Spend
(ROAS) by connecting ecommerce spend and revenue. This app provides a detailed visual breakdown
of where your spend is performing best and how individual platform and channel performance
updates. Discover which first-party marketing touchpoints led to conversions or brought in the highest
revenue according to attribution models.
Email to Web Conversion GA4 App
Connect your web analytics and email data into one dashboard using the Email to Web Conversion
GA4 app. The app simplifies the process of data harmonization between emails and traffic and
addresses the email use case for multiple email vendors.
Implementation Templates App
Use the Implementation Templates app to download QA templates for testing and validating data
sources. When you download a template, a new page is added to your collection so that you can easily
access the template.
Lead Generation App
The Lead Generation app tracks the relationship between Lead Generations and Opportunities, and it
harmonizes and connects your lead generation data.
Marketing Assistant App
Interact with Marketing Cloud Intelligence using voice or text to ask simple questions about marketing
data. The Marketing Assistant app provides detailed answers in both voice and visuals.
Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud App
The Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app helps marketers and sales understand how their marketing
efforts and spend convert into revenue. The app provides various views to analyze data based on
objectives. For example, find the weak links in a sales funnel or the business justifications to allocate a
certain percentage of your marketing budget to a campaign.
Media Naming Builder App
Create consistent campaign, placement, and creative names that match your naming conventions
using the Media Naming Builder app. Instead of manually generating names with other third-party
tools, add hundreds of names instantly based on your naming convention patterns and avoid data
discrepancies.
Media Pacing App
The Media Pacing app is an instant pacing package for your Media Transparency Center. It provides
you with visualization into your Planned Spend and Booked Units against your Actual Spend and
Delivered Units.
Media Planning Center App
The Media Planning Center enables advertisers to create plans for strategic allocation of advertising
budget. It facilitates budget allocation at a high level, for entities such as channels, platforms, or
strategy.
Multi-Touch Attribution App
Discover which first-party marketing touchpoints led to conversions or brought in the highest revenue

cc.. Click Next.
44.. Classify your campaign strategies. By default, Marketing Cloud Intelligence creates strategies and

aa.. To change the classifications, add or remove strategies.
bb.. Add classification rules to the strategies to accommodate your naming conventions.

audience data from your advertising platforms to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence data model.
aa.. To change the mapping, click Edit.
bb.. Select the data stream type from the dropdown.

•
Data Streams - If you added data streams during the installation process, the app automatically
creates these fields and definitions:
- Marketing Cloud Advertising
- Facebook Ads Four data model fields and one custom field. All fields are automatically mapped.

=== PAGE 652 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Existing data stream definitions:
- Under Extended Properties, the Get Conversions toggle is turned on.
- Under Breakdown, Placement or Placement & Impressions Device is set.
New data streams definitions:
- Under Extended Properties - the Get Conversions toggle is turned on.
- Under Breakdown, Placement is set.
•
Twitter Ads
Four data model fields and one custom field. All fields are automatically mapped. For both existing
and new data streams definitions:
- Under Extended Properties - the Get Conversions and Get Tailored Audience Data toggles are
turned on.
•
Patterns -These patterns are created:
- Platform of Facebook and Twitter
- Adwords Site Name for Platform
- Platform According to Data Source Name
•
Harmonized dimensions - One harmonized dimension is created for all patterns.
•
Classification rules - These classification rules are created:
- Social Campaign Strategies
- Platform for 1st Party Audiences
- Included Classification Rule
- Excluded Classification Rule
- All Audiences Classifications
•
Calculated dimensions - The calculated dimension Audience from Ad Studio Flag is created.
•
Calculated measurements - These calculated measurements are created:
- Count Of Ad Studio Audiences in active Campaigns
- Campaigns per Ad Studio Audiences
- Count Of Ad Studio Audiences (All)
•
Report - A report is created that exports the relevant fields from Marketing Cloud Advertising. The
report is updated each time the data stream is updated and shows the status of the audiences in
Marketing Cloud Advertising.
Run the Intelligence App for Marketing Cloud Advertising
After installing the Intelligence App for Marketing Cloud Advertising, the Audience Insights for Marketing
Cloud Advertising dashboard appears on the Visualize tab. Use the dashboard to see how your
campaigns using first-party audiences are performing.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To view installed Marketplace apps Admin, Power User, or Viewer
You can filter the dashboard by data stream, platform, and date. The dashboard contains these sections:
•
Overall View for Campaigns Using First-Party Audiences - A snapshot of how all your campaigns are
performing in relation to impressions, conversions, and spend. This section contains these widgets:
648

=== PAGE 653 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Total Spend - The total amount of money spent in all campaigns using first-party audiences.
- Total Impressions - The total number of impressions for all campaigns using first-party audiences.
- Total Conversions - The total number of conversions for all campaigns using first-party audiences.
(This is according to the “Total Conversions” field in the Advertising Platforms data streams.)
- Total # of Audiences - The total number of audiences that exist in Marketing Cloud Advertising.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence counts audiences that are either active or have errors. (Audiences in
“draft” status don’t appear in the total calculation.)
- Total # of Audiences Active in Campaigns - The total number of audiences that are active in
campaigns displayed in the current timeframe. These audiences are either included or excluded
from these campaigns.
•
Audience Usage - An overview of which audiences were used the most and which were used the least
in your campaigns. This section contains these widgets:
- Top Used Audiences - The audiences that are most used in your campaigns, either as included or
excluded.
- Least Used Audiences -The audiences least used in your campaigns, either as included or excluded.
Audiences appear here if they were used at least one time.
•
Top Campaigns - Information about your top-performing campaigns. You can select the relevant
audience and then filter by a measurement to investigate the performance of the top-10 campaigns.
This section contains these widgets:
- Audiences Included in Campaigns - Filter by which audiences were included in the campaign to
see which campaign performed the best per the selected audience.
- Audiences Excluded in Campaigns - Filter by which audiences were excluded from the campaign.
(Google Ads doesn’t pull excluded audience data.)
- Top 10 Campaigns for Selected Audience - Filter the widget by audiences included or excluded in
the campaigns. You can organize the campaigns according to impressions, clicks, CPM, media cost,
conversion CPA, and conversion rate.
•
Which Strategy of Audience Performs Best - You can filter by strategy, and audiences used in the
different campaigns to see how they react to different strategies.
•
Detailed Display All - Campaign for Each Audience - Displays all your campaigns and audiences.

Demographics Dashboard
The demographics predefined insights aggregate data from your Individual or Unified Individual data
model objects in Data 360. This dashboard creates widgets with different customer breakdowns, to
identify trends of your audiences. This dashboard contains these widgets:
•
Customers—Shows the total number of customers
•
Unified Customers —Shows the total number of unique customers.
•
Customers by Location—Shows a breakdown of customers by geographic location.
•
Customers by Gender—Shows a breakdown of customers by their gender.
•
Customers by Age—Shows a breakdown of customers by age.
•
Population over time—Shows trends of customers during the defined time period. Select up to two
dimensions to change the filter of the population over time widget.
•
Table—A detailed view of the customer data, including age group, country, day, gender and more.
Engagement Dashboard
The engagement predefined insights aggregate data from your different engagement data model objects
and unifies it into a single dashboard. You can track engagement over time and compare different
channels and campaigns.
This dashboard contains these widgets:
•
Highest Open Rate (Push) —Shows the highest open rate of push messages.
•
Highest Open Rate (Email)—Shows the highest open rate of email messages.
•
Total Engagements—Shows the total number of engagements.
•
Channel—Shows a breakdown of engagements by the different channels.
•
Campaign—Shows a breakdown of engagements by the different campaigns.
•
Engagement Over Time—Shows the engagement during the defined time period. Select a
651

measurement to change the data that is shown in the Engagement Over Time widget.
•
Engagement Drilldown—Shows the total engagements drilled down into different categories.
•
Details—Shows a detailed view of engagements by channel, campaign name, day and more.
Conversion Dashboard
The conversion predefined insights aggregate data from your Sales Order data model objects to show
how your customers convert. This dashboard provides a view of purchases over time with product and
product category breakdowns. You can filter this dashboard by product category, product name, and
business unit.
This dashboard contains these widgets:
•
Avg. Spend Per Customer—Shows the average amount of money spent per customer.
•
Total Orders—Shows the total number of orders for all customers.
•
Avg Orders Per Customer—Shows the average number of orders per customer.
Click a dimension to change the data you see in these breakdown widgets:
•
Order—Shows the total number of orders per product name or category.
•
Revenue over time—Shows the total revenue per product name or category.
•
Details—Shows a detailed view of conversions by product name, product category, day and more.
Cross-Channel Marketing Insights
The Cross-Channel Marketing Insights App (CCMI) connects your cross channel marketing data including
Facebook Ads, Instagram Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Pinterest Ads, Twitter Ads, and Google Ads. The app allows
you to optimize your paid campaigns across every major channel. This app is a great starting point for
users who are still going through an implementation process, as it enables you to quickly connect your
data sources with just one click. The app is also useful for customers who are looking for a quick and
clear overview of their campaign performance.
Note The Cross-Channel Marketing Insights app now supports GA4 and can be selected alongside
UA360 profiles during the installation. In order to pull in GA4 data you must first uninstall and then
reinstall the app.
With the Cross-Channel Marketing Insights app you can:
•
Compare your campaigns and creatives cross-channel.
•
View and act on suggestions made by the Einstein Marketing Insights bots to optimize your marketing
efforts.
•
Learn about your top and worst performing campaigns and their characteristics.
•
Start and stop campaigns directly from the app.

Data Dictionary App
The Data Dictionary app provides access to a list of all available fields in your workspace, including
calculated fields, such as calculated dimensions and measurements. Use this app to understand how
fields are built and how they relate to your data.
If you’re an admin, create and update data field definitions, and then share the app with viewers and
power users so that they can analyze the data. This app doesn’t create, update, or install any underlying
entities. It also doesn’t automatically filter out any fields based on user restriction.

them to make sure that you included the correct fields.

=== PAGE 660 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
bb.. To exclude fields that don’t have descriptions defined for them on the workspace level, toggle Hide
fields with no description.
cc.. To add an expression column to the table, toggle Include Expression for Calculated Fields. This
option only appears for fields that contain a calculation, such as calculated measurements.
dd.. To apply filters to include or exclude fields with specific names, descriptions, or expression values,
toggle on Filter. Use this action when including or excluding specific naming conventions. For
example, if all old and unused fields are marked as internal, filter by excluding any fields containing
the word internal.
33.. To preview the fields that were found in your workspace, click Review. All the fields appear in a table.
aa.. To search for a specific field, enter a term in the Search bar (1).
bb.. Show or hide field types by selecting the checkboxes of the fields that you want to show (2).
cc.. Show or hide columns by selecting the checkboxes of the column names that you want to show (3).
dd.. To return to the setup page, click the settings icon (4).
ee.. To view updated data, click the refresh icon (5).
Note The data updates based on the data in the workspace according to this logic:
••
If you removed fields from the workspace, they don’t display anymore.
••
If you added a description to a field in the workspace, the description appears in the app.
••
If you updated fields, all the attributes of the fields are updated, except for the descriptions.
••
If you updated the description in the workspace, the new description overrides the description
in the app.
••
If you removed the description or changed it, the description remains as it is in the app
44.. Click Finish

Data Readiness App
The Data Readiness app monitors the quality of your data harmonization efforts across all designated
dimensions. The app focuses on calculated, harmonized, and mapped dimensions. Use the Data
Readiness app to view the naming convention compliance of your dimensions and identify
noncompliant data sources.
This app complements Fix & Maintain because it captures data governance for dimensions outside of the
Harmonization Center. For more information, see Validate and Maintain your Harmonized Data.

your data is classified based on the naming convention that you defined in your workspace.

analyze the success rates for each harmonization dimension, and filter the data by data sources and
data streams. After the app installs, two new app pages are created for you and appear in your
dashboards.

=== PAGE 670 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
11.. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, on the Marketplace tab, select Apps.
22.. Click Installed Solutions, and then select the eCommerce Optimizer app.
33.. Filter the app page by date.
44.. View the data:
•
Conversion data:
- Conversions – The total conversions for the time period. A conversion is an event that occurred as a
result of a touchpoint event.
- Revenue – The total revenue from the conversion for the time period. (If a revenue KPI doesn’t
exist, no data appears here. )
- Avg Customer Journey – The average time it took for all customers from the first touchpoint to the
conversion.
- Avg Events Until Conversion – The average number of events that occurred from the touchpoint to
the conversion.
- Conversion Number and Sum of Revenue Over Time – A comparison between the total
conversions and sum of revenue over time. It also identifies the dates when your revenue increased
or decreased.
•
Touchpoint data:
- Touchpoint name – Select a touchpoint to view data for.
- Breakdown – Select a breakdown you defined during setup. For example, if you define a breakdown
for a campaign, it appears here. You can then select a specific campaign that you want to focus on.
- Number of Conversions Over Touchpoints – A comparison between the number of conversions
per touchpoint.
- Sum of Revenue Over Touchpoints – The sum of revenue per touchpoint
•
Breakdown data:
- Breakdown selection – Select a breakdown to drill down to view specific data. (If you didn’t define
breakdowns during setup, this section appears with no data.)
- Number of Conversions Over Breakdown – The number of conversions for the breakdown that you
selected.
- Sum of Revenue Over Breakdowns – The revenue for the breakdown.
- Sum of Revenue – A comparison between the different touchpoints and breakdowns according to
the total revenue. For example, you can see which campaigns had the highest revenue via the Email
touchpoint and which had the highest revenue for the SMS touchpoint.
- Top 10 Breakdowns – A list of the top 10 breakdowns.

Requirements for Harmonizing Your Data in the Email to Web Conversion G4 App
To harmonize your data, the Email to Web Conversion GA4 app matches your Google Analytics and
email campaign names. Therefore, if you’re using different naming conventions for Google Analytics
and your email data, classify your campaign names so that the app can locate the data from each
source.
Set Up the Email to Web Conversion GA4 App
The Email to Web Conversion GA4 app connects your web analytics and email data into one
dashboard. The app simplifies data harmonization between emails and traffic. Setting up the app
involves classifying your Google Analytics campaign names and email campaign names so that they
match.

visualizations: the Email Campaign Performance dashboard and the Email to Web Conversion app
page. The dashboard provides a comprehensive view of your email campaign’s performance.
GA4 Fields Available in the Email to Web Conversion App
The Email to Web Conversion GA4 app was rebuilt to work with GA4, and therefore some fields were

Requirements for Harmonizing Your Data in the Email to Web Conversion G4 App
To harmonize your data, the Email to Web Conversion GA4 app matches your Google Analytics and email
campaign names. Therefore, if you’re using different naming conventions for Google Analytics and your
email data, classify your campaign names so that the app can locate the data from each source.
Many times a campaign name contains irrelevant information. For example, if your campaign name is
Summer_2021_Shoes_Sale and you want it to say Summer Sale, create a rule that removes the
unneeded information.
You can create a final campaign name in two ways.
•
Use Contains — Add a rule to create the final campaign name. For example, if the name contains the
letters “ABC,” the final campaign name is “ABC Campaign.”
•
Use a Pattern — Extract a specific term according to a position and delimiter in the naming convention.
You can then use the extracted term to classify the campaigns. For example, if your naming
convention is “summer_sale_1,” extract the first position, which is “summer.” Then use the term
“summer” as the contains term, and classify the campaigns according to the term.
Set Up the Email to Web Conversion GA4 App
The Email to Web Conversion GA4 app connects your web analytics and email data into one dashboard.
The app simplifies data harmonization between emails and traffic. Setting up the app involves classifying

The app page is divided into these tabs: Conversion, Traffic, and Performance.
Conversion - Get details about conversion performance per email campaign.
Traffic - See which day of the week had the most email traffic.
Which day of the week has the most email traffic? Shows days of the week and the average time spent
on the site. To see insights for that specific day, click a day.
•
Best-performing channel of the day.
•
The average lag from when the email was delivered to when the site was visited. This metric is the
670

=== PAGE 675 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
difference between the average date the traffic from a specific campaign appeared on the website and
the average date the campaign was sent.
•
The average time on the site for the email channel.
•
The average time on site for all the other channels.
Performance - See overall campaign performance by looking at two measurements: Message Open Rate
from the email marketing side and Visitor Bounce Rate from the web analytics side. A high Message
Open Rate and low Visitor Bounce Rate indicates that your campaign performed well.
•
Campaign Performance — Filter by Message Open Rate or Visitor Bounce Rate.
•
Badges — How successful your campaign was according to Marketing Cloud Intelligence badges.
Badges look at your campaign’s average Message Open Rate and Visitor Bounce Rate to determine the
campaign’s performance. There are three badges available:
- Message Quality — Examines the quality of your message. A high message open rate suggests that
the message subject, audience reach, and time the message was sent appealed to your users and
encouraged them to open the message.
- Quality of Content — Examines the quality of the content of the message. For example, the
campaign has a clear and effective Call to Action for the audience it was sent to, or the landing page
is relevant and interested users.
- Audience Targeting — Examines how you targeted your audience for the campaign. For example, if
your campaign is for women sports shoes and you targeted women aged 20–50, then your target
audience is correct. However, if your target audience is men aged 50–80, you didn’t target your
audience correctly.

Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud App
The Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app helps marketers and sales understand how their marketing
efforts and spend convert into revenue. The app provides various views to analyze data based on
objectives. For example, find the weak links in a sales funnel or the business justifications to allocate a
certain percentage of your marketing budget to a campaign.
Here’s a video about the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/VQ75NzGz2W1fHNk3bD7o7o?
Set Up the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud App
The Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app provides an overview of marketing efforts and spend and
how they convert into revenue. Setting up the app involves connecting CRM data, connecting ad data,
and harmonizing CRM and ad data.
Run the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud App
Use the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app to review your marketing efforts and spend and how
they convert into revenue.

Set Up the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud App
The Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app provides an overview of marketing efforts and spend and
how they convert into revenue. Setting up the app involves connecting CRM data, connecting ad data,
and harmonizing CRM and ad data.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To install Marketplace apps Admin
To install the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app requires:
•
Salesforce Sales Cloud as the CRM and these objects
- Leads
- Opportunities
- Accounts
- Contacts
- Campaigns
- Campaign Members
•
No CRM data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence (the app supports only new CRM data stream creation)
•
Running paid ad campaigns on common advertising platforms
These advertising platforms are supported in the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app:
•
Google AdWords
•
Facebook Ads
•
Bing Ads
•
Twitter Ads
•
Instagram
•
YouTube Ads
•
Linkedin Ads
•
Pinterest
11.. Install the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app.
aa.. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, on the Marketplace tab, select Apps.
bb.. Select the app, and click Get it Now.
cc.. Click Start.
22.. Connect the CRM data.
aa.. Connect to a Salesforce CRM data source or create one.
bb.. Select the time period for retrieving CRM data, and click Apply.
cc.. A preview of all the CRM tables appears on the right side. To view which data points are extracted,
hover over a table.
dd.. Click Next. A list of fields for harmonizing the CRM data appears.
ee.. Click Next.
33.. Classify the CRM statuses and stages.
aa.. Drag each displayed status according to the correct bucket: Lead or Qualified Lead.
680

=== PAGE 685 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
bb.. Click Next.
cc.. Drag each displayed stage to the correct bucket: Opportunities or Closed Won.
dd.. Click Next.
44.. Connect the ad data.
aa.. Select an existing ad data stream. If a data stream doesn’t exist, create one on the Connect & Mix
tab.
bb.. Save a draft of the app before creating the data stream to continue from the same stage.
cc.. Click Next.
55.. Harmonize the CRM and ad data.
The harmonization takes place between the name field in the CRM’s campaign object and the
campaign name field in the Ads data stream.
aa.. Determine whether the name of the field that connects your CRM and campaign data together are
identical. If they aren’t identical, you must create a pattern or a classification. If the names are
identical, from the Usage dropdown, select Identical. For example, if the name is “My Product
Summer_Sale” in Ads, and “My Product Summer_Sale” in the CRM.
bb.. If the name of the field that connects your CRM and campaign data contain the same naming
conventions but aren’t identical from the Usage dropdown, select Pattern. Enter the delimiter,
select the position, and select which Ads data you want to apply it to. For example, if the name is
“Summer_Sale” in Ads, and “My Product Summer_Sale” in the CRM, you can use a pattern with a
space delimiter and select position 3.
cc.. If the name of the field that connects your CRM and campaign data is different, from the Usage
dropdown, select Classification. For example, if the name is “Summer-campaign” in Ads, and “My
Product Summer_Sale” in the CRM, upload a classification file.
66.. Review the data in the preview tables for each data source, and then click Finish.
After the Marketing Insights for Sales Cloud app installs, these underlying entities are created:
•
Six data streams for your CRM data
•
34 calculated measurements
•
Harmonized dimensions

It shows a part of your sales funnel and how your spend and revenue are doing in correlation to it. It
681

=== PAGE 686 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
also displays the conversion rate between opportunities and closed-wons and the average time it took,
in weeks, for an opportunity to convert to a closed-won.
44.. Click the Weak Links tab to see the weak points within your sales funnel.
It shows the conversion rates of the different stages in your funnel and the average time it takes for a
lead to move to the next step.
55.. Click the Budget Justification tab to help you justify the allocation of your budget to your stakeholders
and to yourself.
The funnel indicates how much spend went in and how it converted throughout the cycle to leads,
opportunities, revenue, and ROI. You can also see the cost per lead and the cost per opportunity.
The Cost Per Lead, Closed-Won Rate, and ROI columns are three critical elements in recognizing
whether a campaign was successful. For example, a campaign that uses 40% of your budget has a low
cost per lead, high closed-won rate, and a high ROI.

the Media Naming Builder app. Instead of manually generating names with other third-party tools, add
hundreds of names instantly based on your naming convention patterns and avoid data discrepancies.

see Generate Media Names with the Media Naming Builder.

Media Pacing App
The Media Pacing app is an instant pacing package for your Media Transparency Center. It provides you
with visualization into your Planned Spend and Booked Units against your Actual Spend and Delivered
Units.
With visualizations that use color coding and effective filtering options, you can identify where your
attention is needed in real time, so that you can take the necessary actions to get back on track. The
Media Pacing app is for anyone that needs a tool for analyzing, monitoring, and optimizing media
planning activities and planning.
Here’s a video about the Media Pacing app:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/PHVYXgxpvSJ5K42Hpum5N7?
Install the Media Pacing App
The Media Pacing app provides an overview of how well your media campaigns are pacing based on
already defined data.
Run the Media Pacing App
After you install the Media Pacing app you can start visualizing your IO data. This allows you to easily
and efficiently identify under and over pacing campaigns.
Install the Media Pacing App
The Media Pacing app provides an overview of how well your media campaigns are pacing based on
already defined data.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To install Marketplace apps: Admin
In order to install this app, you must have the Media Transparency Center (MTC) enabled in your
account. MTC is a premium feature. If you’re interested in purchasing MTC, contact your Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Account Manager. No configuration is needed with this app. You only download it to your
workspace.
11.. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, on the Marketplace tab, select Apps.
22.. Select the app and click Get it Now.
After the app installs it’s immediately ready to use, no further configuration is required.

To customize the calculations and align them to your own business logic, modify the underlying entities.
For example, if you want to only consider a result of the calculation to be 'over pacing’ when it’s greater
or equal to 115%, edit the calculated measurement.
Run the Media Pacing App
After you install the Media Pacing app you can start visualizing your IO data. This allows you to easily and

USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED

685

=== PAGE 690 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The IOs are presented using color coding, allowing you to immediately identify how well each IO is
pacing. The color coding is based on a calculation of the Actual Spend to date, as a percentage of the
Expected Spend to date, and is governed by the following key:
< 70% = ‘Under pacing’, color: Red
> 70% and < 90% = ‘Pacing Risk’, color: Orange
> 90% and < 110% = ’Pacing On Track’, color: Green
>110% = ‘Over pacing’, color: Purple
11.. To open the app, go to your Workspace Apps and double-click. Notice in this example that the overall
actual spend to date isn’t in line with the originally planned spend, resulting in under pacing.
22.. To view only Under Pacing and Over Pacing IOs, change the filters under the overall score.
33.. Change the date range at the top-right corner of the page if necessary. A few notes about the date
range:
Note
••
Out of all the IOs that were active within the selected range, the earliest IO Start Date, and the
latest IO End Date serve as the two extremities for the IO visualization.
••
All IOs that had any activity within the date range are visualized on the timeline view, each with
its own Start and End Date.
••
There’s an option Show Only Live IOs. An IO is considered to be Live only if it’s active on the
Current Date on which you’ve accessed the app. If the current date resides between an IO’s
start and end dates, it’s visualized. Each IO is visualized with its own Start and End dates. This
option allows you to instantly focus your view on IOs that are currently active and therefore
require your attention more urgently than others.
44.. In the Timeline view, focus on the IOs that require further monitoring to see what’s causing
misalignment between the actual and planned data. Here you can see information about the IO,
including IO Name, Capped Cost (budget), and IO Cost Type by default.
55.. To change the columns, click the cogwheel and select the names you require. You can display up to
three columns.
66.. By default, the timeline display is at a Monthly breakdown, but you can change it to Weekly or Daily
breakdowns.
77.. In IOs Remaining Days, filter by remaining days, for example, view IOs that have less than 3 days left.
88.. To drill down, click a specific IO.
Note
••
There are two important filter options here, which dynamically change the view and the title of
the graph (3). (1) Spend/Units and (2) Daily/Total.
••
The app considers this IO to be over pacing because the actual cost is higher than the
expected cost. After analyzing the Total aggregation mode, switch to Daily view and see on
686

=== PAGE 691 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
which actual days the spend exceeded what was planned, in order to try and understand what
caused the over pacing.
••
To see the absolute values of each of the measurements and the difference between them,
hover over a specific day.
99.. Click the stacked view and view the actual media buys that generated activity on each day. They
appear stacked on the bars of the graph.
1100.. Hover over an IO and view the media buy name. When media buy stacking is on, you can also see the

1111.. Investigate an under pacing IO. Notice there’s an IO with no media buy associated with it, therefore it’s

more information about a specific insight.
Media Planning Center App
The Media Planning Center enables advertisers to create plans for strategic allocation of advertising
budget. It facilitates budget allocation at a high level, for entities such as channels, platforms, or strategy.
With the Media Planning Center you get full and immediate access to all historical actuals related to
budgeted entities. This app uses past performance in order to forecast what the future performance is
likely to be, and suggests more cost effective budgeting decisions. There’s no need to import or export
data, since all of your historical actuals and planned budget are connected in one place.
It’s important to note that the Media Planning Center app is used at the first stage of the media planning
process. It doesn’t replace platforms or tools that are used at a later stage to create media plans that are
at the IO (Insertion Order) or IO-Like level.
In this diagram you can see the Media Planning Center’s positioning in the media planning process:
There are no technical prerequisites for using the Media Planning Center app and you don’t need the
Media Transparency Center or the Media Pacing app to use it.
Note The Media Planning Center app is a paid app and appears in demo mode until purchased.
Contact your account manager for more information.
Here’s a video about the Media Planning Center app Scenario Planning:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/xv4v6SVHNsNvh5fcQSvsZT?
687

=== PAGE 692 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence

A maximum of 9 underlying entities are created with the Media Planning Center app.

Use the Media Planning Center to create a strategic budget allocation plan.
EMI Budget Allocator
The Media Planning Center EMI Budget allocator is a tool used to automatically produce a
recommended media budget scenario. Using AI-based forecasting and optimization algorithms, the
EMI Budget Allocator offers an optimal suggestion on how to split your allocated budget, across your
channels, platforms, and overall strategy.

The Media Planning Center app manages media plans, analyzes spending, and optimizes budget.

44.. Define the measurement you use to visualize media cost in your workspace.
The Media Planning Center must know how you measure cost because all of its comparisons,
forecasts, and optimization calculations are cost-based.
These calculations treat your selected spend measurement as the historical ‘actual’ spend value, which
is aligned with your ‘planned’ spend for example, your budget.
You can use any measurement that you have in the system, such as mapped measurements,
calculated measurements, or even system-generated calculated measurements.

aa.. Select Single KPI, if you have one objective.

=== PAGE 697 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Underlying Entities in the Media Planning Center App
A maximum of 9 underlying entities are created with the Media Planning Center app.
•
1 Generic data stream
- The budgeting grid is turned into a report, which is uploaded into this data stream. Upon
installation, this report only has dimension values with ‘nulls’ for the budget figures. After
installation, every time the grid is updated with budget figures, a new report is created with the
latest figures, which replaces the previous matching data that was there before.
•
A maximum of 6 calculated dimensions
- The total number is per the number of selected dimensions (levels). Each of these calculated
dimensions (one for each budgeting level) harmonizes the budget data sourced from the generic
data stream. It correlates delivery data (historical actuals) sourced from the relevant data streams in
the workspace, and aligns figures from both sources by the same calculated dimension value.
•
2 Calculated measurements
- Calculated Cost Per Measure—Calculates the average cost per unit as per the relevant KPI defined
for each objective (if multiple KPIs are used). It divides the selected Spend measurement by the
relevant KPI measurement for each case.
- Calc Budget— Used to apply a LIFETIME aggregation to the mapped budget figure. This is required
because the mapped budget figure undergoes a certain calculation from within the mapping, so it
can apply to any date within the selected date range. This ensures that there’s always a figure, which
correctly shows the latest number that isn’t impacted by the mapping calculation.
Customize the Calculated Cost Per Measure calculated measurement if you want to apply your own logic
to this measurement. This measurement calculates cost per unit as a straightforward division of the total
cost by total KPI regardless of the cost type as can be seen in this example:
693

=== PAGE 698 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
If you prefer to calculate the average CPM and not the average cost of a single impression, change the
calculated measurement. For example, the average cost per 1,0000 impressions.
Run the Media Planning Center App
Use the Media Planning Center to create a strategic budget allocation plan.
The run mode of the app contains three tabs. Each tab allows you to plan your budget, optimize it and
then see a simulation of it. All the dimensions you selected during the app installation are used. You
can’t modify any dimensions at this stage. To do so, you must reinstall the app.
Plan Your Budget Allocation
After you install the app, you can see the outline, or grid, of your budgeting plan. This grid contains all
of the dimensions (in the hierarchy) alongside the timeframe, which was defined during the
installation process.
Analyze Your Budget By Dimension
Whenever you highlight an entity on the grid, an analytic display appears at the bottom presenting two
analytic types, Forecast, and YoY (Year-over-Year). The data that displays is per the level that you’re
currently highlighting, meaning if you drill up or down, the numbers change accordingly.
Optimize Your Budget With Analysis Tools
The Optimize section looks at different entities in terms of past performance and planned budget. It
then recommends how you can change your allocated budget to improve performance. It uses a
scatter plot quadrant display, which shows a selected KPI against a certain dimension associated with
the KPI in question.
Calculation of Optimization Recommendations
The Media Planning Center performs a calculation to determine the performance of a specific
dimension in terms of past performance and planned budget. It uses a scatter plot quadrant display to
perform this calculation.

then copy and paste to the row or column you require.

aa.. Define how these top-level budgets trickle down to lower levels in the hierarchy. Use this option if

The budget is reallocated accordingly all the way down to the lowest level.
Analyze Your Budget By Dimension
Whenever you highlight an entity on the grid, an analytic display appears at the bottom presenting two
analytic types, Forecast, and YoY (Year-over-Year). The data that displays is per the level that you’re
currently highlighting, meaning if you drill up or down, the numbers change accordingly.
Forecast
The forecast section displays the effect that the budget has had on performance in the past, and it
predicts what the performance is likely to be in the future. This prediction is based on a computation of
past performance data and future budget. It offers a selection of forecasting methods from a dropdown
at the top right-hand side of the display:
To view an explanation of each forecasting method, click the icon beside the method itself.
The forecasting methods vary in complexity from simple to complex. The first two methods predict
696

=== PAGE 701 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
results even with relatively little data, however, the advanced methods require at least 10 historical
points of reference. If you use monthly increments, when using one of the advanced methods, you need
at least 10 months’ worth of past data.
Forecasting isn’t displayed as an absolute number, but rather as a range in which the true number
assuringly lies, for example, the confidence interval. The confidence interval range is always spread over
two standard deviations from the mean for all methods. In general, the more data you have and the
more accurate it is for the period scoped in the past, the more accurate the forecasting is likely to be.
Forecasting aligns your budgeting decisions with specific targets. For example, in cases where you must
achieve a certain number of conversions, but the selected forecasting method suggests that this target
will be missed. You can try to reallocate budget in-line with the forecasting at the expense of other
allocations that are less target sensitive.
YoY Analysis
This analysis shows you the previous year’s spend for the selected highlighted entity in the selected time
frame. This analysis can serve as a benchmark. If for example, you decide to spend 15% more this year
than you did the year before, you can immediately see how much you spent on every budgeted entity. It
also provides you with a seasonal trendline. You can see all of the previous year’s peaks and troughs,
which can be related to recurring events and are worth considering this time around.
Optimize Your Budget With Analysis Tools
The Optimize section looks at different entities in terms of past performance and planned budget. It
then recommends how you can change your allocated budget to improve performance. It uses a scatter
plot quadrant display, which shows a selected KPI against a certain dimension associated with the KPI in
question.

granularity where there’s more than one entity.
Calculation of Optimization Recommendations
The Media Planning Center performs a calculation to determine the performance of a specific dimension
in terms of past performance and planned budget. It uses a scatter plot quadrant display to perform this
calculation.
At any particular point in time, the display shows a selected KPI against a certain dimension associated
698

=== PAGE 703 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
with the KPI in question and uses this logic.
•
The average cost per unit (based on the previous year’s activity) is calculated. The maximum result
achieved for this calculation makes the extremity value on the Y-axis with 0 being the lowest value.
•
At the same time, it also takes the highest budget value for the same entities and makes that the
extremity on the X-axis, with 0 being the minimum.
•
Any average cost per unit that’s below the median is considered ‘cost-effective’, and any that’s above is
considered too expensive. Likewise, any budget above the median is considered to be on the high
side, and any that’s below is considered to be on the low side.
699

=== PAGE 704 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Any entity that’s recognized as being cost-effective yet budgeted on the low side, is considered to be
badly budgeted. In this example, the entity has already proven itself to be cost-effective so it makes
sense to budget it relatively high. The Media Planning Center recommends considering spending more
on something that has a proven track record of being cost-effective, therefore the entity is moved to
the adjacent quadrant:
•
Similarly, any entity that’s recognized as being expensive yet budgeted on the high side, is also
considered to be badly budgeted. This entity has already proven itself to not be cost-effective, so it’s
recommended to budget it relatively low.

EMI Budget Allocator
The Media Planning Center EMI Budget allocator is a tool used to automatically produce a
recommended media budget scenario. Using AI-based forecasting and optimization algorithms, the EMI
Budget Allocator offers an optimal suggestion on how to split your allocated budget, across your
channels, platforms, and overall strategy.
The allocator considers past spending and performance data, the KPI you set as the goal of the
investments, and the current budget allocation from your main scenario. The EMI budget allocator takes
the existing budget and splits it in every possible way to produce an optimal budget allocation. It
considers the highest number of conversions predicted and the lowest spend.
EMI scenarios are created daily when sufficient data is available. You can manually run the EMI scenario
at any time. The latest EMI scenario is added to your app when available.
Here’s a video about the Media Planning Center EMI Budget allocator:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/LuJs3ATsFEbz1GEPfD47Lz?
Use The EMI Scenario Plan
The EMI scenario plan is a read-only plan, created automatically by the EMI Budget Allocator tool. The
latest scenario is displayed in the Media Planning Center app.
Set EMI Budget Constraints
From the Budget constraints tab, you can apply and remove optional settings. For example, turn off
the EMI Budget allocator, set limitations, or expand how the EMI budget allocator splits your budget.
This allows you to customize your budget and see more results.

Set Up the Multi-Touch Attribution App
The Multi-Touch Attribution app provides an analysis of first-party marketing touchpoint data. Setting
up the app involves ingesting first-party user-level data into the Granular Data Center and defining
touchpoints, conversions, and attribution models. The fields that appear during setup are derived
from Granular Data Centers. Changes to data must be done in the Granular Data Center.
Run the Multi-Touch Attribution App
Run the Multi-Touch Attribution app to see which first-party marketing touchpoints led to conversions
or brought in the highest revenue according to the attribution model. Each attribution model selected
during setup has its own dashboard page.
Set Up the Multi-Touch Attribution App
The Multi-Touch Attribution app provides an analysis of first-party marketing touchpoint data. Setting up
the app involves ingesting first-party user-level data into the Granular Data Center and defining
touchpoints, conversions, and attribution models. The fields that appear during setup are derived from
Granular Data Centers. Changes to data must be done in the Granular Data Center.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To install Marketplace apps Admin
To install the Multi-Touch Attribution app requires:
•
Granular Data Center add-on enabled in your workspace
•
First-party data ingested and harmonized in the Granular Data Center
•
First-party data with user-level identifiers across multiple channels
11.. Install the Multi-Touch Attribution app.
aa.. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, on the Marketplace tab, select Apps.
bb.. Select the app, and click Get it Now.
If the Granular Data Center isn’t enabled in your workspace, you can’t install this app. Contact a
Marketing Cloud Intelligence representative for more information.
22.. Define marketing touchpoints. A marketing touchpoint is the method used to reach an audience, for
example, email or SMS. Define at least two touchpoints.
aa.. Enter the touchpoint name, for example, Email.
bb.. Select the Granular Data Center table that contains the email events for the touchpoint. For
example, email_touchpoint_table.
cc.. Select the column name that represents the user ID, for example, user_ID.
dd.. Select the column name that represents the time and date that the event occurred, for example,
timestamp.
ee.. Click Save Touchpoint. The touchpoint appears on the left side under Marketing Touchpoints.
ff.. Define the next touchpoint using the same steps.

44.. Define conversions. A conversion is the outcome of the touchpoint that you’re trying to optimize. You
can add multiple conversion events in one app instance. For example, the user signed up for your

the conversion. If you’re not looking for insights of revenue, you don’t have to define a KPI. For
example, you only want to expand your end-user numbers even if no purchases are made.
ff.. Click Save Event. To add another conversion event, repeat the above steps. The conversion appears
on the left side under Conversion Events.
gg.. Click Next.
55.. Add attribution models.
An attribution model is a set of rules that determines how credits are assigned to touchpoints in a
conversion path. Depending on the model, weights are assigned to the various events in the path. For
example, this path: first Email, second SMS, and third Display. Four attribution models are available.
You can also create a custom model to meet specific business needs.
Gives 100% credit to the first touchpoint seen in
First Touch
the attribution lookback window.
Gives 100% credit to the touchpoint occurring
Last Touch
most recently before conversion.
Gives equal credit to every touchpoint seen
Linear Touch
leading up to a conversion.
Goes according to an exponential decay with a
custom half-life parameter. The default is 7 days.
The weight of each channel depends on the
Time Decay
amount of time that passed between the
touchpoint initiation and the eventual
conversion.
aa.. Select the attribution models. A dashboard page is created for each attribution model that you
choose.
bb.. To determine the number of days from when a conversion occurred to count the marketing event,
modify the Lookback Window. For example, you want to only include marketing events (such as

emails) that were sent a maximum of 30 days before the conversion occurred.

44.. View the data.
•
Conversion data
- Conversions–The total conversions for the time period. A conversion is an event that took place as a
result of a touchpoint event.
- Revenue–The total revenue from the conversion for the time period. (If a revenue KPI doesn’t exist,
no data appears here.)
- Avg Customer Journey–The average time it took for all customers from the first touchpoint to the
conversion.
- Avg Events Until Conversion–The average number of events that took place from the touchpoint to
the conversion.
- Conversion Number and Sum of Revenue Over Time–A comparison between the total conversions
and sum of revenue over time, which helps you identify the dates when your revenue increased or
decreased.
•
Touchpoint data
- Touchpoint name–Select a touchpoint to drill down to view data.
- Breakdown–Select a breakdown you defined during setup. For example, if you defined a
breakdown for campaign, it appears here and you can then select a specific campaign you want to
focus on.
- Number of Conversions Over Touchpoints–A comparison between the number of conversions per
touchpoint.
- Sum of Revenue Over Touchpoints–The sum of revenue per touchpoint.
•
Breakdown data
- Breakdown selection–Select a breakdown to drill down to view specific data. Note: If no
breakdowns were defined during setup, this section appears with no data.
- Number of Conversions Over Breakdown–The number of conversions for the breakdown that you
selected.
- Sum of Revenue Over Breakdowns–The revenue for the breakdown.
707

=== PAGE 712 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Sum of Revenue–A comparison between the different touchpoints and breakdowns according to
the total revenue. For example, you can see which campaigns had the highest revenue via the Email
touchpoint and which had the highest revenue for the SMS touchpoint.
- Top 10 Breakdowns–Displays the top 10 breakdowns.

Search Keyword Insights App
Connect your organic and paid search data to view overall search performance and keyword insights.
Automatically identify what is and isn’t covered by your paid campaigns and use that information in
future campaigns to drive growth.
Set Up the Search Keyword Insights App
To optimize keywords based on their organic insights, the Search Keyword Insights app shows which
organic search terms are included in paid keywords and which aren’t. Setting up the app involves
connecting your SEO and SEM data and applying filters.
Run the Search Keyword Insights App
Installing the Search Keyword Insights app creates an app page that shows all search terms that
weren’t covered by your SEM. It also shows organic and paid data side by side so that you can compare
their performance.
Set Up the Search Keyword Insights App
To optimize keywords based on their organic insights, the Search Keyword Insights app shows which
organic search terms are included in paid keywords and which aren’t. Setting up the app involves
connecting your SEO and SEM data and applying filters.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To install Marketplace apps Admin
To install the Search Keyword Insights app, you need:
•
For the organic side (SEO) — Google Search Console data stream with query level data. You can also
add this data stream via the app.
Note If the data stream doesn’t have query level data, Marketing Cloud Intelligence automatically
reprocesses it during the installation so it includes query level data.
•
For the paid side (SEM) — Any keyword-level data, including match type. Common supported sources
include Google Ads, Google Search 360, and TotalConnect.

Run the Search Keyword Insights App
Installing the Search Keyword Insights app creates an app page that shows all search terms that weren’t
covered by your SEM. It also shows organic and paid data side by side so that you can compare their
performance.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To view Marketplace apps Admin, Power User, or Viewer
This app uses a predefined Marketing Cloud Intelligence calculated measurement called Magic. You can
sort your results using Magic in a few different places in run mode. Magic identifies which search terms
have a high probability of clicks per their position. The magic formula is based on these assumptions:
•
A position higher than 5 is considered bad and has a lower chance of getting clicks.
•
Click Through Rate (CTR) is a personal metric, so it’s calculated differently for each customer. A good
CTR is higher than the average CTR for the specific client.
Typically Magic performs this calculation: If the term has a bad position but a high CTR, the position, the
CTR, and a ratio of the impressions (to normalize) are multiplied to produce a score.
711

Set Up the Social Intelligence App
The Social Intelligence app provides an analysis of social competitive data from Twitter, Facebook,
Instagram, and YouTube. Setting up the app involves connecting your brand and competitor data.
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To install Marketplace apps Admin
To install the Social Intelligence app you need a username and password for the platform you want to
pull data for. You can use your own login details because you’re pulling public data. However, each
platform works a bit differently, so keep these considerations in mind:
713

=== PAGE 718 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Twitter — You can log into any Twitter account.
•
Facebook — Your user must have permissions to manage at least one Facebook page.
•
YouTube — Your user must be associated with a channel.
•
Instagram — You must have a valid Facebook username and password that are connected to a
Facebook page linked to an Instagram business account.
11.. Install the Social Intelligence app.
aa.. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, on the Marketplace tab, select Apps.
bb.. Select the app, and click Get it Now.
22.. Authenticate social networks.
