can connect to all networks.
bb.. Click Save & Continue.
33.. Define your brand. To avoid displaying irrelevant data, we recommend that you look at the social
network’s site and find the relevant page first. When searching for social pages, select that page here.
aa.. Enter a keyword in the Search box.
If you’re searching for a page in Instagram, make sure that you enter the full business name,
otherwise the page can’t be found. All relevant pages appear grouped by the different social
networks.
bb.. Select the relevant pages. The Brand Name card at the top of the page appears with the name of
the brand that you selected. Each time a page is added, it’s also added to the relevant social
network.
cc.. To remove a page, hover over it and click Delete.
dd.. To view the actual page, click the link.
ee.. Click Save & Continue.
44.. Define competitors. You can define up to four different competitors.
aa.. Enter a keyword in the Search box.
bb.. Select the relevant pages.
The Competitor Name card appears with the brand that you selected. And each time a page is
added, it’s added to the relevant social network.
cc.. To define the brand of the next competitor, click the next card and repeat the previous steps.
55.. Click Finish Setup to complete the installation.

•

=== PAGE 719 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
REQUIRED USER ROLE
To view Marketplace apps Admin, Power User, or Viewer
11.. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, on the Marketplace tab, select Apps.
22.. Click Installed Solutions, and then select the Social Intelligence app.
33.. Filter the app page by date, competitors, and social networks.
To view the different sections, scroll up or down or use the bookmarks on the left-hand side of the page.
•
You Vs Competitors — Get overall social presence by looking at your score. The score is calculated by
comparing the number of likes per brand across each social network. The number of followers also
plays a role, adding more weight to the social network with more followers. For example, if most
followers are on Facebook, likes on Facebook have more weight on the overall score.
•
Follower Growth — View how well your follower base is growing compared to your competitor’s. A
dynamic graph is used to show this information by looking at how your follower growth over time. This
graph changes as the app gathers more data. After there’s enough data, you can see the growth rate
for you and your competitors. Note: You can only see data from the date of installation. You can’t view
prior data.
•
Frequency — Determine how frequently to post per social network. You can customize these
calculations using calculated measurements. For example, you can adjust the industry value like in the
example to change the numbers used for the general benchmark column.
•
Post Timing — Get insights about the best day of the week or time of day to post to your social
networks. You get a score based on a number of factors and see the times with the highest number of
highly rated posts.
•
Top Posts — Shows which posts were the most successful per social network according to the number
of likes and shares and, if available comments.
•
Messaging — View how each brand uses different messaging tactics. Scan posts and find the terms or
hashtags that are used the most. See where messages overlap, which means more than one company
uses the same term. Determine which terms to use and which make your marketing tactics more
unique.
- Each competitor appears on its own in a different location on the page.
- If the term is connected to more than one competitor, the term appears closest to the competitor
that uses it the most.
- If you hover over a term, you can see if another competitor uses the term.
- The colors used represent the uniqueness of the words. Font size also plays a role. Terms that
appear with a larger font are used more often per competitor.

Uninstall an App
If for some reason you no longer need an app and you want to remove it, you can use the uninstall
option.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To uninstall an app: Admin
When uninstalling you must decide what to do with the app’s underlying entities. You can either keep
them or delete them. Before you decide, keep this in mind.
•
If you chose to delete underlying entities, if any of the underlying entities are in use, they aren’t
deleted.
•
If you decide to keep underlying entities they remain in the system, but they aren’t connected to any
app.
•
All underlying entities created by apps contain the app name and version in the entity name.
11.. Access the Marketplace main page from the Marketplace tab in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
22.. Click Go to Installed Solutions.
33.. Select the tile of the app you’re uninstalling.
44.. Click the options button and then select Uninstall.
55.. Select if you want to delete or keep the underlying entities.
66.. Click Uninstall.
Underlying Entities in Marketplace Apps
Underlying entities are created behind the scenes after Marketplace apps install. They enable the app to
function properly. Each app has its own set or combination of underlying entities depending on the app’s
purpose and functionality.
You can edit these entities outside of the app to change the app’s behavior. These are the types of
underlying entities available:
•
Data streams
•
Filtered measurements
•
Calculated measurements
•
Calculated dimensions
•
Insights bots
•
Goals
•
Data source authentications
It’s important to mention that underlying entities can’t be deleted as they’re vital for the app’s behavior. If
722

=== PAGE 727 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
for some reason you must delete an underlying entity then you must uninstall the app.
Identifying Underlying Entities
You can differentiate between different types of entities, by looking at the naming convention of the
entity.
Editing Underlying Entities
Underlying Entities are editable so that you can apply your own business logic to the app’s entities.
Identifying Underlying Entities
You can differentiate between different types of entities, by looking at the naming convention of the
entity.
For example, here you can see 5 calculated dimensions were created for this app.
The number appearing after the underscore in the entity is the installation ID of the related app.
If you open an installed app, for example, Social Intelligence, you can see this number in the URL of the
related app.
You can also use this number to search for all the underlying entities related to a specific app. For
example, if you want to find the calculated measurements that were created for app ID 1873, filter the
name to 1873 and then search for all the calculated measurements.
Editing Underlying Entities
Underlying Entities are editable so that you can apply your own business logic to the app’s entities.
Note Any change you make to an app’s underlying entity can affect its functionality. It’s
recommended that you first make a copy of the entity before changing it.
For example, if you’re using the Lead Generation app and you want to change the dates that are used in
the Cohort table to a date that suits you, edit the Lead Time to Convert calculated dimension.
If you’re using the Social Intelligence app and you want to change the General Benchmark value for post
frequency, adjust the ‘Industry Value’ of the calculated measurement General Benchmark.
723

=== PAGE 728 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Understanding App Installations
What happens when Marketplace apps are installed? What determines whether an app installs
successfully or fails? Is there anything I can do to make sure the app installs successfully? If an app fails
during an installation, is there a way to see why and even fix it? These are only some questions you’re
probably asking yourself when installing Marketplace apps. This section explains how to troubleshoot
and fix errors that occur when installing apps.
App Installation Statuses
When an app starts to install, two synchronous steps occur during installation. These steps affect the
installation status of the app.
Failed Apps and Their Implications
Apps don’t always install successfully. Sometimes there’s an issue with the app, or the processing of
the underlying entities. For example, the provider timed out, the app installation timed out before the
underlying entities were processed, or even due to invalid mapping.
Fix a Failed App
If an app fails during installation you can explore and find out why this happened. You can see which
processes must be fixed to install the app, and those that can be fixed at a later date.
App Installation Statuses
When an app starts to install, two synchronous steps occur during installation. These steps affect the
installation status of the app.
•
All underlying entities are created.
•
Underlying entities that require processing are processed (for example data streams and insights bots).
The successful installation of an app depends on the successful completion of the above two processes.
There are three possible statuses of an app’s installation.
•
Successful
•
Draft
•
Failed
This table summarizes of all the different statuses and the different stages of underlying entity creation.
App Status Description Entities are Entities are App Ready for Use
Created Processed
Success The app was
installed
successfully
724

=== PAGE 729 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
App Status Description Entities are Entities are App Ready for Use
Created Processed
Draft User left the
installation
process before it
Some or all Never initiated
completed
entities were
created
Failed The app wasn’t
installed
successfully
Failed Apps and Their Implications
Apps don’t always install successfully. Sometimes there’s an issue with the app, or the processing of the
underlying entities. For example, the provider timed out, the app installation timed out before the
underlying entities were processed, or even due to invalid mapping.
What happens, if for example, the app installation timed out, but the data streams are still processing?
Tolerance determines how the system tolerates failed processes of underlying entities that require
processing. Currently, this refers to data streams and insight bots.
There are three levels of tolerances:
•
None: Not a single processing failure is tolerated.
•
All: All processing failures are tolerated.
•
Any: At least one successful processing is tolerated.
The level of tolerance can be different per underlying entity. For example, in certain apps, data streams
have a tolerance level set to None. If the data stream fails during installation, the app doesn’t install
successfully. If insight bots have a tolerance level set to All and they fail during installation, the app
installs successfully.
Each app has different tolerance levels for the same underlying entity type. For example, in the Lead
Generation app, there are two data stream types, one for Leads and one for Opportunities. The Leads
data stream type has a level of tolerance set to None. If the Leads data stream fails during installation the
app doesn’t install successfully. The Opportunities data stream has a level of tolerance set to All. If the
Opportunities data stream fails during installation, the app doesn't install successfully. To understand this
better, if the Opportunities data stream type fails, as long as the Leads data stream type is successful, the
Lead Generation app installs successfully.
Fix a Failed App
If an app fails during installation you can explore and find out why this happened. You can see which
725

=== PAGE 730 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
processes must be fixed to install the app, and those that can be fixed at a later date.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To fix a failed app: Admin
To help you understand the different failed processes and which must be fixed, these sections are
available:
•
Red - (Required Fixes) Fixes are required in order to install the app successfully.
•
Orange - (Recommended Fixes) It’s recommended you fix the failed processes.
•
Green - (Successful Jobs) Shows all processes that ran successfully.
11.. Access the Marketplace main page from the Marketplace tab in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
22.. Click Go to Installed Solutions.
If an app failed, the status appears as Failed.
33.. To view details as to why the app failed, click on the app.
In this example, there are a few required fixes, and one recommended fix.
44.. To fix this app, you must go to the three data streams that failed, and fix them. Go to the row of the
failed process and then click Edit.
55.. You can also fix the recommended data stream that failed, but it isn’t mandatory.
66.. When you’re done fixing the failed processes, click Validate & Retry. If the failed processes are fixed,
the app installs successfully.
Marketplace Connectors
The Marketplace offers a Connectors library with a number of customized connectors. With connectors,
connect to an external environment, instead of creating a custom connector. You can find connectors
that were developed by partners, third-party vendors, and Intelligence.
It’s important to note that none of your account data is provided to the partners when installing a
connector. After you install a connector you can use it to create a data stream, with no extra
customizations needed. Only admins can install connectors. If you’re a power user or viewer, you can add
the connector to your wishlist and your admin installs the connector for you. No rows are created after
installing connectors.
View Marketplace Connectors
Explore the available connectors in the Marketplace to find the one that works best for your business
needs. Here you can view the available connectors, get more information, and install a connector in
your account.
Install a Connector
Install a connector to get a custom connector in your account. Connectors are installed for the entire

account, meaning all workspaces in the account can use and see the connector.

about the connector, uninstall, and report an issue.

If you decide to keep the related entities, the following happens:
•
The connector doesn’t appear in the Connect & Mix tab anymore.
•

•
The data streams are disabled automatically.

handle issues with partner developed connectors, and Intelligence developers handle issues with
Intelligence developed connectors.

Marketplace Add-On Features
The Add-On Features tab is a centralized location for all of Marketing Cloud Intelligence add-ons. Add-on
features extend the capabilities of the platform, and can be enabled for free, or at an extra cost.
Explore the available add-ons by watching short explanatory videos or read a one pager, which outlines
all you need to know about the add-on. If you’re interested, you can enable the add-on in your account
directly from here too.
These add-ons are available:
•
API Access—Connect to your data from anywhere and give your users more automation power using
Marketing Cloud Intelligence APIs. For more information, see Developer Portal.
•
Granular Data Center—Connect granular, high volume data into your single source of truth. For more
information, see Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Lakes.
•
Direct Connect—Create real-time connections to your databases. For more information, see Create
730

=== PAGE 735 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Streams with DirectConnect.
•
Executive Workspace—Connect the data from all of your workspaces across clients, regions, and
business units into one view.
•
Media Transparency Center—Gain transparency and save time with automated media costs or
revenue. For more information, see Media Transparency Center.
•
Sandbox—Sandbox is a separate mode where you can safely experiment and test changes before
applying them to your live environment. For more information, see Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Sandbox Testing Environment.
•
White Labeling—Customize the look and feel of your Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Marketplace Widgets
Install and use Marketplace widgets in your workspace to get the full functionality of pre-defined custom
widgets. Custom widgets include ready-to-use page visualization, navigation, and API solutions. After
installing a custom widget, you can edit it and change your dashboard page’s design and behavior.
Install a Custom Widget
Add functionality to your workspace’s dashboard pages with a custom widget. After the custom widget
installs it appears in your Installed Solutions page, and also becomes available in the Visualize tab just
like other widget types.
Add a Custom Widget to a Dashboard
Add a custom widget to apply more advanced use cases to your dashboard page. For example, use an
EMI custom widget to surface AI-powered insights in your dashboards.

The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Developer Portal
The Developer Portal is an easy-to-use and centralized location for developers who want to develop tools,
use cases, and examples. The Developer Portal offers these tools: Query API, Platform API, Custom Data
Connectors, and Apps.
733

=== PAGE 738 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
API (Application Programming Interface) is a set of definitions, communication protocols, and tools for
building software. API doesn’t use a GUI (Graphic User Interface), rather allows developers to call specific
functions they require via API calls. Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses platform and query APIs, and a
custom connector is used to integrate between Marketing Cloud Intelligence and other third parties. An
app is a web application that lives in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace and is built using
Intelligence APIs and SDKs. For more information about the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace,
see Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace and Tools.
Any Marketing Cloud Intelligence user can access the Developer Portal; however, your user must be API
enabled in order to make successful API calls. Contact your Marketing Cloud Intelligence representative
for more information. API is a programmatic interface and calls are executed from the developer’s code.
It’s recommended you use a tool called Postman to explore.
Note All Marketing Cloud Intelligence authenticated users can access the API documentation, but
only API-enabled users can execute API calls.
Access the Developer Portal
Access the Developer Portal to develop tools, use cases, and examples.
Developer Portal Tools
The Developer Portal offers different tools for developers to create their own apps, queries, and build
tools.
Access the Developer Portal
Access the Developer Portal to develop tools, use cases, and examples.

platform.

Developer Portal Tools
The Developer Portal offers different tools for developers to create their own apps, queries, and build
tools.
API Docs
Is divided into two subtabs: Platform API and Query API.
Platform API
Here you can find all the API documentation you need for all available endpoints, including Data
Streams, Measurements, Dimensions, Calculated Measurements, Data Lakes, Patterns, and Harmonized
Dimensions. You can automate processes or build tools to manage Marketing Cloud Intelligence
installation. For example, you can use the Platform API to update Data Streams in bulk.
To execute API calls in Sandbox mode, a sandbox-mode header must be passed with the value ‘true’.
Supported endpoints are marked with a purple ‘Sandbox’ tag.
Query API
The Query API allows you to pull marketing data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence into other tools and
platforms or to show in-house visual platforms you developed. If you click a query, you can see more
information about the query so you can try it out for yourself:
735

=== PAGE 740 ===
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace Apps and Tools in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Build
This tab is divided into two subtabs: Apps and Connectors.
Note To develop apps and connectors you must be enabled as a developer. For more information,
contact Marketing Cloud Intelligence support.
Apps
An app is a web application that’s built using Marketing Cloud Intelligence API and SDKs. An app can be
submitted to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Marketplace for other users to install or even purchase, or
it can be available to your account only. All apps can use Marketing Cloud Intelligence functionalities,
including visualization, data integration, data activation and more. An app can be developed using CLI
(Command Line Interface). For more information, see Quick Start.
Connectors
Custom connectors are API connectors, developed independently from the Marketing Cloud Intelligence
infrastructure. These custom API connectors integrate with external code based on a set of
configurations managed from inside the platform. Custom connectors can be used to develop API
integrations for any platform. For example, you can use custom connectors to integrate with marketing
vendors or internal client platforms. Documentation on how to build a Custom Connector appears under
the Build section in the Developer Portal. All connectors can be found in the Marketplace under
Connectors.
Note All Marketing Cloud Intelligence authenticated users can access the documentation, but only
allowed users can access the form to upload and manage custom connectors and apps.
Partners
This section is for partners who are interested in developing and submitting their own apps. Here you
can find detailed information about the process for building, publishing, and submitting apps and more.
736
